### Projects Overview

> 📄 Source: [Projects_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Projects_Overview.htm)

#### Projects Overview


A project is a set of items that are used together. You can organize related data files, guidelines, maps, configuration files, error severity files, and other associated files in folders within your project.


By default, projects are stored on your hard drive in folders under My Documents but you can specify another location.


Projects can also be managed by a version control system and stored in a remote subversion. Multiple users can then access and manage the same project, taking advantage of the version control and tracking features of the subversion.


The [Project Explorer](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Project_Explorer_Pane.htm) pane shows the user interface for managing projects.

---

<a id="create-a-new-project"></a>

### Create a New Project

> 📄 Source: [Create_a_New_Project.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Create_a_New_Project.htm)

#### Create a New Project


With project, you can work with interrelated files, storing them on your computer and (optionally) on a remote server.


To create a new project:

- Highlight the **Project Explorer** pane by clicking on it.**Note** To have the Project Explorer pane displayed, enable it in the **View** tab.
- On the Home tab of the ribbon, click New.
- In the New Project dialog box, type a name and location for the new project, and then click OK.

---

<a id="work-with-project-packages"></a>

### Work with Project Packages

> 📄 Source: [Book_Work_with_Project_Packages.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Work_with_Project_Packages.htm)

#### Work with Project Packages


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="create-a-project-package"></a>

#### Create a Project Package

> 📄 Source: [Create_a_Project_Package.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Create_a_Project_Package.htm)

##### Create a Project Package


You can use a package to archive your projects. Projects are archived in .sbpck package files.


To archive a project:

- Click a project in the Project Explorer.
- On the Home tab, in the Package group, click Create.
- On the Welcome to Create Project Package Wizard wizard page, click Next.
- In the Destination Path dialog box, browse to the location where you want to store the package.
- Click Next. The wizard archives the selected project.
- Click Finish to exit the wizard.

---

<a id="extract-files-from-a-project-package"></a>

#### Extract Files from a Project Package

> 📄 Source: [Extract_Files_from_a_Project_Package.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Extract_Files_from_a_Project_Package.htm)

##### Extract Files from a Project Package


You can extract files from an archived project package to a current project.


Note Before extracting files from a package, make sure that you close the project that you want to extract files to.


To extract files from a project package:

1. On the Home tab of the ribbon, click Extract.
2. In the Welcome to Extract Project Package Wizard, click Next.
3. In the Source/Destination Path dialog box, browse to the location of the project package to extract, and to the destination for the extracted files.
4. Click Open Extracted Project to open the project after the extraction is complete.
5. Click Next. The wizard extracts the selected project.
6. Click Finish to exit the wizard.

---

<a id="work-with-subversion"></a>

### Work with Subversion

> 📄 Source: [Book_Work_with_Subversion.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Work_with_Subversion.htm)

#### Work with Subversion


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="remote-subversion"></a>

#### Remote Subversion

> 📄 Source: [Remote_Subversion.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Remote_Subversion.htm)

##### Remote Subversion


A remote subversion makes it possible to store projects that are shared by multiple people. This is the preferred way to maintain a common library of files (guidelines, maps, data files, and so on).


When working with the subversion, you can:

- [Connect to the remote subversion](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Set_Connections_to_the_Subversion.htm).


To connect to the subversion, you should obtain the login information for the server, including Connection ID, URL link, user name and password (set by the administrator).

- Browse the subversion.
- Add project items to the subversion.
- [Download items from the subversion to your computer](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Download_Projects_from_the_Subversion.htm).
- Commit the changes to the subversion from your computer.
- [Update items from the subversion to your computer](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Update_Items_from_Subversion.htm).


To view other options of working with the Subversion, see [Project Explorer Pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Project_Explorer_Pane.htm).

---

<a id="set-connections-to-the-subversion"></a>

#### Set Connections to the Subversion

> 📄 Source: [Set_Connections_to_the_Subversion.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Set_Connections_to_the_Subversion.htm)

##### Set Connections to the Subversion


Creating a connection to the subversion enables you to work with the remote server from your computer knowing the subversion location and registration information. With the Connections dialog box, you can manage the connections to the remote servers.


To view the connections:

- On the Advanced tab of the ribbon, click Subversion Connections. The Connections dialog box is displayed:
- Click Modify () to change the connection information.
- Click Delete () to remove the connection to the certain subversion.
- Click Set Default () to make the connection appear by default when working with projects.


To connect to an existing subversion:

1. Click Add () to create the connection with an existing subversion.
2. On the Connection Properties dialog box, enter the subversion Connection ID and URL:
3. Select the type of authentication.
4. Click Test connection to test the connection with the remote subversion.
5. Click OK.


To set the connection to the remote subversion while going through the project wizards:

1. In the Add Server Connection dialog box, specify connection details:
2. Select the type of authentication to use. If you choose Server Authentication, type a User Name and Password for accessing the server.
3. Click Next.

---

<a id="subversion-browser"></a>

#### Subversion Browser

> 📄 Source: [Book_Subversion_Browser.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Subversion_Browser.htm)

##### Subversion Browser


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="browse-the-subversion"></a>

##### Browse the Subversion

> 📄 Source: [Browse_the_Subversion.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Browse_the_Subversion.htm)

###### Browse the Subversion


With the Subversion Browser, you can view the contents of your repositories, view the history of items and maintain the contents.


To browse a subversion:

- On the Advanced tab of the ribbon, click Subversion Browser.

---

<a id="view-the-history-log"></a>

##### View the History Log

> 📄 Source: [View_the_History_Log.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/View_the_History_Log.htm)

###### View the History Log


The Version History Log lets you view the dates and sequence numbers of revisions to the project.


To view the history log for the operation with the subversion items:

1. On the Advanced tab of the ribbon, click Subversion Browser.
2. On the Subversion Browser toolbar, click History ().
3. Set the time period for the actions that you want to view.
4. Click Save () to save selected revision to the disk.
5. Click Close to exit.

---

<a id="view-the-subversion-content-revision"></a>

##### View the Subversion Content Revision

> 📄 Source: [View_the_Subversion_Content_Revision.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/View_the_Subversion_Content_Revision.htm)

###### View the Subversion Content Revision


All revisions of the subversion content version have sequence numbers. You can find the needed revision and open an earlier content version.


To view a revision of subversion content:

1. On the Advanced tab of the ribbon, click Subversion Browser.
2. In the Connection field, click the subversion to view.
3. On the Subversion Browser toolbar, click Revision ().
4. Click Latest to view the latest version of the subversion content.
5. Click OK.


The requested content is displayed in the Subversion Browser.

---

<a id="add-project-items-to-the-repository"></a>

#### Add Project Items to the Repository

> 📄 Source: [Add_Project_Items_to_the_Repository.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Add_Project_Items_to_the_Repository.htm)

##### Add Project Items to the Repository


When you create a new project, it is stored on your computer, but you may want to upload the project contents to a repository where it is under version control.


To add local items to the repository:

1. On the Project tab of the ribbon, click Add to Repository.
2. On the Welcome to Add to Repository Wizard wizard page, click Next.
3. [Set the connection to a remote repository](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Set_Connections_to_the_Subversion.htm), if not set earlier.
4. Click Next.
5. On the Select Remote Folder wizard page, to store your items in a location other than the root folder, click the repository folder where you want to store the items. The location is displayed in the Path field.
6. Click the Connection drop-down list to change to another remote repository.
7. Click Next.
8. On the Commit Set wizard page, select the items to be committed (stored) to the selected folder on the repository, and then click Next.
9. (Optional) In the Log Message dialog box, type a description of the changes made.
10. Click Next.
11. Click Finish to exit the wizard.

---

<a id="commit-changes-to-the-subversion"></a>

#### Commit Changes to the Subversion

> 📄 Source: [Commit_Changes_to_the_Subversion.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Commit_Changes_to_the_Subversion.htm)

##### Commit Changes to the Subversion


Any additions or changes made to projects can be committed to the subversion. If changes have been made to a project, an indicator is shown next to the project name:


*[Image: Changes indicator]*


To commit changes to the remote subversion:

1. On the Home tab of the ribbon, click Commit to Subversion.
2. Click Next.
3. On the Commit Set wizard page, select the items you want to commit.
4. Click Next.
5. (Optional) In the Log Message dialog box, enter the log message for the changes made.
6. To commit the changes to the subversion, click Next.
7. Click Finish.


If the local content of the project is outdated because a newer version was uploaded to the subversion from another computer, the Conflict Resolution Confirmation dialog box is displayed.

---

<a id="download-projects-from-the-subversion"></a>

#### Download Projects from the Subversion

> 📄 Source: [Download_Projects_from_the_Subversion.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Download_Projects_from_the_Subversion.htm)

##### Download Projects from the Subversion


You can download a project from the subversion to your local computer. All of the project files, along with version information are saved in the project folder. If the project already exists locally, you are not able to download it.


To download a project stored on the remote server:

1. On the Project tab of the ribbon, click Open Remote.
2. On the Welcome to Open Remote Project Wizard wizard page, click Next.
3. [Set the connection to a remote subversion](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Set_Connections_to_the_Subversion.htm) if not set earlier.
4. Click Next.
5. On the Select Remote Project wizard page, do one of the following:
6. Click Next.
7. In the Destination Path dialog box, browse to the location on your local drive where you want to store the downloaded project.
8. Click Next to begin the download.
9. Click Finish to exit the wizard.


To save subversion items on your computer:

- On the Advanced tab of the ribbon, click Subversion Browser.
- On the Subversion Browser toolbar, click Save (). The Browse For Folder dialog box is displayed.
- Navigate to the location where you want to save the folders or click Make New Folder to create a separate folder for the items from the subversion.
- Click OK.

---

<a id="add-remote-items-from-the-subversion"></a>

#### Add Remote Items from the Subversion

> 📄 Source: [Add_Remote_Items_from_the_Subversion.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Add_Remote_Items_from_the_Subversion.htm)

##### Add Remote Items from the Subversion


You can download a remote files and folders from the subversion to your computer. All of the items are saved in the project folder.


To add a remote item from the remote server:

1. In the Project Explorer pane, on the project tree, right-click the folder where you want to download files from the subversion, and then click Add Remote File(s).
2. On the Welcome to Add Remote Resource Wizard wizard page, click Next.
3. [Set the connection to a remote subversion](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Set_Connections_to_the_Subversion.htm), if not set earlier.
4. Click Next.
5. In the Select Remote Location dialog box, in the left pane, navigate to a folder containing the project file and, in the right pane, click the file name. The location displays in the Path field.
6. Click Next to begin the download.
7. Click Finish to exit the wizard.

---

<a id="update-items-from-the-subversion"></a>

#### Update Items from the Subversion

> 📄 Source: [Update_Items_from_Subversion.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Update_Items_from_Subversion.htm)

##### Update Items from the Subversion


Any items of the projects can be updated from the subversion.


To update changes from the remote subversion:


On the Project tab of the ribbon, click Update from Subversion ().

---

<a id="data-explorer"></a>

## Data Explorer

> 📄 Source: [Book_Data_Explorer.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Data_Explorer.htm)

### Data Explorer


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="data-explorer-overview"></a>

### Data Explorer Overview

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Overview.htm)

| # Data Explorer Overview | |
| --- | --- |



Data Explorer is a data editor that is designed to work with EDI, HL7, and other data files of delimited standards. With Data Explorer, you can create new or modify existing EDI data files. EDI standards supported by Data Explorer include X12, EDIFACT, and all subsets of these standards such as UCS, VICS, and EANCOM.


In addition to creating and modifying data files, you can also use the Data De-identification function to change specific data in the data file. With the Data Replace Rules editor, you can select a match pattern and specify a replacement value. This allows you to de-identify data and send de-identified transactions to others without including any confidential information.


You can perform the following by using the Data Explorer:

- View analyzed data file content with highlighted segments names, separators, and reference information.
- View file starting each segment from new line.
- View multiple files with meta data (file name, size, and the rest) displayed on the status bar and add custom meta data from file content.
- Edit a data file by copying and pasting multiple lines.
- Insert bookmarks.
- Edit delimiters.
- Perform searches and replace data in multiple data files by using replace rules.
- Insert bookmarks across all files to highlight key information in data.
- Comment on a required piece of data.
- Validate data files by using SpecBuilder Analyzer.


Note Data files up to 50 MB can be analyzed by Data Explorer. Data files up to 150 MB can be open for editing by default. You can set this option on the File tab of the ribbon. Data files up to 2 GB can be open in the read-only mode.


You can work with multiple files which are displayed in the File List and perform analysis across the weekly or daily workload of production data. By using Data Explorer, you can do the following:

- Sort and filter data files in the File List based on meta information.
- Search for the patterns across multiple files.
- Perform search and replace in several files simultaneously.
- Export the key segments to CSV from the list of selected files.

---

<a id="explore-the-workspace"></a>

### Explore the Workspace

> 📄 Source: [Book_DataExplorer_Explore_the_Workspace.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_DataExplorer_Explore_the_Workspace.htm)

#### Explore the Workspace


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="the-workspace-layout"></a>

#### The Workspace Layout

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_The_Workspace_Layout.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_The_Workspace_Layout.htm)

##### The Workspace Layout


The **Data Explorer** workspace has a number of sections that show information about the data files you currently work with.


*[Image: Data Explorer Interface]*


The default workspace layout consists of the following panes:

- The central pane is the Data View. It shows currently open data file. For more information, see [Data View Pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Data_View_Pane.htm).
- The left pane is the File List pane. It shows the list of data files you work with. For more information, see [File List Pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_File_List_Pane.htm).


On the View tab of the ribbon, in the Views group, additional panes are available. You can move panes and customize the workspace. For more information about customizing a workspace, see [Move a Pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Move_a_Pane.htm).


The status bar in the bottom shows file encoding, offset, hexadecimal and decimal values, position in line, and number for the current character in a data file.

---

<a id="ribbon"></a>

#### Ribbon

> 📄 Source: [Book_DataExplorer_Ribbon.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_DataExplorer_Ribbon.htm)

##### Ribbon


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="ribbon-overview"></a>

##### Ribbon Overview

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Ribbon_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Ribbon_Overview.htm)

###### Ribbon Overview


The Data Explorer features are organized into groups within five different tabs: File, Home, View, File List, and Find/Replace.


The following options are available from the File tab, in the Backstage view:


| Option | Icon | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Save | | Use to save currently open documents. |
| Save As | | Use to save documents with a different document name or file type. |
| Open | | Use to open previously created documents. |
| Save to File List | | Use to save the currently open data file to the File List. |
| Info |   | Use to view detailed information about the open data file. |
| New |   | Use to create new data. You can create the following data files by using this option. For more information, see [Create a Data File](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Create_Data_Files.htm). |
| Recent |   | Use to open recently viewed documents. |
| Print |   | Use to print a data file. You can preview the printing and set print options. For more information, see [Print Data Files](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Print_Data_Files.htm). |
| Help |   | Use to register Data Explorer and get support. |
| Options |   | Use to set the large file thresholds in megabytes. For more information about how the threshold affects Data Explorer actions, see [Large Data Files Peculiarities](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Large_Data_Files_Peculiarities.htm). Using Options, you can also enable the advanced mode for search and replace using match patterns. For more information about using the advanced mode, see [Find/Replace Pane.](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Find_Replace_Pane.htm) |
| Close | | Use to close currently open documents. |
| Close All | | Use to close all open documents. |
| Exit | | Use to close Data Explorer. You are prompted to save changes to any open document. |

---

<a id="home-tab"></a>

##### Home Tab

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Home_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Home_Tab.htm)

###### Home Tab


The following options are available on the Home tab:


*[Image: Home tab]*


| Group | Option | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Clipboard | Paste | Use to cut, copy, and paste. |
| Cut | | |
| Copy | | |
| Editing | Undo | Use to undo the changes. |
| Redo | Use to redo the changes. | |
| Find/Replace | Use to find or replace code or data. For more information, see [Find/Replace Pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Find_Replace_Pane.htm). | |
| Go To | Use to jump to the required position based on line or offset. | |
| Re-parse | Use to re-parse a document. Syntax errors are marked in the Data View pane. | |
| Split | Use to split the file that was not successfully parsed. | |
| Un-split | Use to unsplit the file that was not successfully parsed. | |
| Edit Delimiters | Use to customize the delimiters. For more information, see [Edit Delimiters](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Edit_Delimiters.htm).  | |
| Mode | Data | Use to switch the **Data View** pane to the native data view mode. |
| Text | Use to switch the **Data View** pane to the text view mode. | |
| Binary | Use to switch the **Data View** pane to the binary view mode. | |
| Comments | New | Use to add a new comment. |
| Delete | Use to delete the existing comment. | |
| Bookmarks | Limit to Document | Use to limit the bookmarks to the currently open document. |
| Toggle | Use to create a bookmark for a selected line in the file. | |
| Previous | Use to navigate between the bookmarks. | |
| Next | | |
| Export Segments to CSV | Use to export the lines containing the bookmarks. For more information, see [Export Segments to a CSV File](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Export_Segments_to_a_CSV_File.htm). | |
| Remove Lines | Use to remove the lines containing the bookmarks. | |
| Data Replace Rules | Rules Editor | Use to open and edit the rules. For more information, see [Modify Data Replace Rules](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Modify_Data_Replace_Rules.htm). |
| Data De-identification | Use to de-identify data in the data file. For more information, see [Choose De-Identification and Specify Rules File](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Select_De_Identification_and_Specify_Rules_File.htm). | |
| Tools | Data Analyzer | Use to validate a data file against a guideline. For more information, see [Validate Data Files](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Validate_a_Data_File.htm). |
| Export Segments to CSV | Use to export the selected segments of the currently open data file and save them as a comma separated file. For more information, see [Export Segments to a CSV File](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Export_Segments_to_a_CSV_File.htm). | |



Note If there are no open data files, some of the options are not displayed.

---

<a id="panes-overview"></a>

#### Panes Overview

> 📄 Source: [Book_DataExplorer_ Panes_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_DataExplorer_ Panes_Overview.htm)

##### Panes Overview


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="file-list-pane"></a>

##### File List Pane

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_File_List_Pane.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_File_List_Pane.htm)

###### File List Pane


The File List pane allows you to work with several data files simultaneously. You can copy content from one file to another, perform searches and replace content in multiple files. The File List pane contains the list of all files that are imported for editing. You can edit only files that have been imported. This protects original files from being modified.


*[Image: File List pane]*


The following information is displayed in the File List pane:


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Status | The current status of the data file. The following statuses can be observed:Pending: Indicates that the data file is not imported yet. Processing: Indicates that the data file is being processed.Ready: Indicates that the data file is successfully imported.Failed: Indicated that the data is not successfully parsed. |
| File Name | The name of the data file. |
| Original File Location | The location of the original file. |
| Size | The size of the data file. |
| Created | The date when the data file was created. |
| **Modified** | The data and time of the last modification. |



You can filter data files in the list by a file name, file size, date of creation, or modification. To filter data files, click the corresponding column header.


To open a file from the File List pane:

1. Double-click the item you need.


For more information about configuring the file list, see [Customize a File List Pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Customize_a_File_List_Pane.htm).

---

<a id="data-view-pane"></a>

##### Data View Pane

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Data_View_Pane.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Data_View_Pane.htm)

###### Data View Pane


The Data View pane displays the currently open data files in one of the three modes: **Data**, **Text**, and **Binary**.


To switch between these modes go to the **Home** tab and choose the view in the **Mode** group.


*[Image: Data tab]*


In the **Data** mode you can easily edit the data files, re-parse them, and edit the delimiters. After you edited a data file, added or removed segments or separators, it needs to be re-parsed so that segments and separators are found.


*[Image: Text tab]*


The **Text** mode allows you to edit data files in a human-readable format. Switch to the **Data** mode after you edited the data file in order for it to be re-parsed.



The **Binary** mode displays the successfully parsed data files in an efficient binary format. You can edit binary content in this mode and after the content is edited, switch to the **Data** mode for the data file to be re-parsed.


Note Split functionality, editing delimiters, data de-identification, and export to CSV are not available in the **Text** and **Binary** modes.

---

<a id="de-identification-results-pane"></a>

##### De-Identification Results Pane

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Identification_Results_Pane.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Identification_Results_Pane.htm)

###### De-Identification Results Pane


The De-Identification Results pane contains the results of the de-identification process. De-identification is the way to change several data fields automatically. Data that gets changed as a result of this process is shown in this pane.


*[Image: De-Identification Results tab]*


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| # | The result number. |
| Original Data | The original data that is replaced. |
| Replaced Data | The result data. |
| Match Pattern | The pattern according to which the data is matched. |
| Replace Pattern | The pattern according to which the data is replaced. |
| Description | The description that is given to this match/replace in the replacement rules file. |



The selected result is highlighted in the Data View pane. Data Explorer allows saving the de-identification results as a CSV file.


To save the de-identification results as a CSV file:

1. On the File tab, click Save as CSV.
2. Provide the file name and the location of the destination folder.
3. Click Save.

---

<a id="bookmarks-pane"></a>

##### Bookmarks Pane

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Bookmark_Pane.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Bookmark_Pane.htm)

###### Bookmarks Pane


Bookmark is a marker which can be added to the selected line in a data file. Use the Bookmarks pane to create a list of lines you want to remember in a data file.


*[Image: Bookmarks tab]*


The following information is displayed in the Bookmarks pane:


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| # | The number of the bookmark. |
| Bookmark | The name of the bookmark. |
| Perspective | The perspective a bookmark is related to. |
| File Location | The name of the file containing the corresponding bookmark. |
| Line Number | The number of the line containing the corresponding bookmark. |

---

<a id="findreplace-pane"></a>

##### Find/Replace Pane

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Find_Replace_Pane.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Find_Replace_Pane.htm)

###### Find/Replace Pane


In the Find/Replace pane, you can search for and replace the text in a data file.


The following information is displayed in the Find/Replace pane:


| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| # | Search result ID. |
| Item | The type of the found item. |
| Location | The line and character numbers in the following format: (<line>, <character>). |
| Text | The search result. The found item is highlighted. |



In the Find/Replace pane, there are three available search mode tabs:

- The Simple tab: by using this tab, you can search for specific symbols, cases, or words and replace them with the specified elements.
- The Match Pattern tab: this tab allows you to perform the search through identifiers, such as system and custom templates, search for elements, and their values and qualifiers. The match pattern search also has the advanced mode.
- The Regular Expression tab: this tab allows searching through generic symbols used to create an expression and replacing them with the specified elements.


To search for information and view the results by using the Simple tab:

1. In the Find/Replace pane, on the Simple tab, in Find what, type the text to search for, and then in Replace with, type the replacement text.
2. In the Look in drop-down list, choose whether you want the search to be restricted to the currently open document, all data files from the File List, only to those files that are selected in the File List pane, or to the bookmarked lines in the currently open document.
3. In Options, select the Match case check box to search for case-sensitive data, and/or select the Match word check box to search for the entire word. Select the **HEX input** check box to use HEX values as a search criteria.
4. In Direction, specify the search direction. Click Down to start from the beginning of the document, or Up to start from the end.
5. Click Find Next or Find All to find all possible matches according to the settings.


To search for information and view the results by using the Match Pattern tab:

1. In the Find/Replace pane, click the Match Pattern tab and in the Template drop-down list, select System Template or Custom Template to refine the search. The following options are available:
2. In Target Element, provide an element for custom template. You cannot change this element for system templates.
3. In Original Value, type a value to search for, and then in Replace Value, type the replacement value.
4. In the Qualifiers table, you can add qualifiers to the selected custom template. The following options are available:
5. Click Find Next or Find All to find all possible matches according to the settings.


To search for information and view the results by using the advanced mode of the Match Pattern tab:


Note To enable the advanced mode, in the File menu, in Options, in Use the advanced mode for Match Pattern search in the Find/Replace pane, click Yes.

1. In the Find/Replace pane, on the Match Pattern tab, in Find what, type the pattern to search for, and then in Replace with, type the replacement pattern. Click (), to use predefined patterns.
2. In Options, select the Match case check box to search for case-sensitive data, and/or select the Match word check box to search for the entire word.
3. In Direction, specify the search direction. Click Down to start from the beginning of the document, or Up to start from the end.
4. Click Find Next or Find All to find all possible matches according to the settings.


To search for information and view the results by using the Regular Expression tab:

1. In the Find/Replace pane, click the Regular Expression tab and in Find what, type a regular expression you want to use for a search. You can click () to choose the necessary generic symbol from the list.
2. In Replace with, type the replacement text.
3. In the Look in drop-down list, choose whether you want the search to be restricted to the currently open document, all data files from the File List, only to those files that are selected in the File List pane, or to the bookmarked lines in the currently open document.
4. In Options, select the Match case check box to search for case-sensitive data, and/or select the Match word check box to search for the entire word.
5. In Direction, specify the search direction. Click Down to start from the beginning of the document, or Up to start from the end.
6. Click Find Next or Find All to find all possible matches according to the settings.

---

<a id="comments-pane"></a>

##### Comments Pane

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Comments_Pane.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Comments_Pane.htm)

###### Comments Pane


In the Comments pane, you can view, add, edit, and delete the comments by using the options in the Comments group of the Home tab of the ribbon. Comments doesn't affect the data file content.


Note You can save data files with comments you added. In this case, in the Save As dialog box, in Save As Type field, select the *.datx extension for the file you want to save.


To access the Comments pane:

1. On the Home tab of the ribbon, select the Comments check box. The Comments pane is displayed.


The comments can be saved together with the data file. For more information, see [Save Data Files](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Save_Data_Files.htm).

---

<a id="customize-the-workspace"></a>

#### Customize the Workspace

> 📄 Source: [Book_DataExplorer_Customize_the_Workspace.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_DataExplorer_Customize_the_Workspace.htm)

##### Customize the Workspace

---

<a id="customize-the-workspace-overview"></a>

##### Customize the Workspace Overview

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Customize_the_Workspace_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Customize_the_Workspace_Overview.htm)

###### Customize Workspace Overview


You can customize the Data Explorer interface to meet your needs. On a day-to-day basis, you may want to include only a limited number of functions. You can decide what functionality you want to see and hide the rest.


For customizing the workspace procedure, the following tools are used:

- 1 - Inner Dock Guides
- 2 - Outer Dock Guides


*[Image: Dock Guides]*


To customize the workspace layout:

1. On the View tab of the ribbon, select or clear the check boxes of the panes you would like to see in the workspace.
2. To change the location of panes in the workspace, use:


To close a pane so it is not displayed in the workspace:

- Click Close on the right of the pane's header:
- On the Home tab of the ribbon, clear the check box with the name of the pane.


To restore the default workspace layout:

1. On the Home tab, click Reset Layout.

<a id="move-a-pane"></a>

##### Move a Pane

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Move_a_Pane.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Move_a_Pane.htm)

###### Move a Pane


You can customize the workspace by moving any pane to another place.


To move a pane:

1. Click a pane's header and hold it.
2. Move a pane to the appropriate place on the Workspace.
3. Move the mouse to the appropriate button of the either the Inner Dock Guides or Outer Dock Guides.

<a id="work-with-data-files"></a>

### Work with Data Files

> 📄 Source: [Book_DataExplorer_Work_with_ Data_Files.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_DataExplorer_Work_with_ Data_Files.htm)

#### Work with Data Files


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="create-a-data-file"></a>

#### Create a Data File

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Create_Data_Files.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Create_Data_Files.htm)

##### Create a Data File


You can create a data file by using templates that are available in Data Explorer.


To create a data file:

1. On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click New.
2. Select from the following options:


A new data file is displayed in the Data view pane.

---

<a id="open-existing-data-files"></a>

#### Open Existing Data Files

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Open_Existing_Data_Files.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Open_Existing_Data_Files.htm)

##### Open Existing Data Files


You can open data files that have already been created.


To open an existing data file:

- On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click Open, and then navigate to the file you want to open.
- Click Open.


The open file is displayed in the Data View pane. An analyzed data file is structured by using highlighted segments names, separators, and reference information. Note that data files up to 50 MB can be analyzed by Data Explorer. Data files of up to 150 MB can be opened for editing by default. You can set this option on the File tab of the ribbon. Data files up to 2 GB can be opened in the read-only mode.

---

<a id="import-data-files"></a>

#### Import Data Files

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Import_Data_Files.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Import_Data_Files.htm)

##### Import Data Files


Data Explorer allows you to import data files or a folder with data files. The list of the imported data files is displayed in the File List pane that allows working with several data file simultaneously. For more information, see [*File List Pane*.](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_File_List_Pane.htm)


To import a data file:

1. On the File List tab of the ribbon, click Import Files.
2. Navigate to the location of the file you want to import, and then click Open.


The data file is displayed in the Data View pane. A list of all imported files is displayed in the File List pane.


You can also import a folder with data files.


To import a folder:

1. On the File List tab of the ribbon, click Import Folder.
2. In the Browse For Folder dialog box, select a folder to import, and then click OK.

---

<a id="export-data-files"></a>

#### Export Data Files

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Export_Data_Files.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Export_Data_Files.htm)

##### Export Data Files


You can export imported data files to apply changes you made to the original data files or to save edited data files.


To export data files:

1. In the File List pane, select the data files you want to export.
2. On the File List tab of the ribbon, click the Export Files.
3. Navigate to the destination folder for exported files.
4. Click OK.

---

<a id="export-segments-to-a-csv-file"></a>

#### Export Segments to a CSV File

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Export_Segments_to_a_CSV_File.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Export_Segments_to_a_CSV_File.htm)

##### Export Segments to a CSV File


You can export specific segments from the data files to a comma-separated value (CSV) file.


To export segments to a CSV file:

1. In the File List pane, select data files you want to export specific segments from. On the File List tab of the ribbon, click Export Segments to CSV.
2. In the Export Segments to CSV dialog box, provide the following information:
3. Click Next.
4. Click Finish.
5. In the Open File dialog box, click Yes if you want to view the file with the exported segments.

---

<a id="validate-data-files"></a>

#### Validate Data Files

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Validate_a_Data_File.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Validate_a_Data_File.htm)

##### Validate Data Files


You can use the Analyzer to validate data files against a certain guideline. The SpecBuilder Analyzer wizar guides you.


To validate a data file:

1. Open the data file you want to validate.
2. On the Home tab of the ribbon, click Data Analyzer.
3. In the Guideline selection dialog box, specify the guideline to use for validation. This can be either from the database or from a guideline file.
4. Click Next.
5. In the Envelope Segments dialog box, select one of the following options:
6. In the Envelope Segments dialog box, specify a guideline file for the Interchange level and one for the Functional Group.
7. Click Next.
8. In the Envelope Segments dialog box, specify the usage of the Selected Envelope Segments. Select from the following options:
9. Click Next.


After the wizard is finished, the data file is displayed in the Analyzer/Data pane of SpecBuilder. You can verify the validation results in the Data Errors pane.

---

<a id="save-data-files"></a>

#### Save Data Files

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Save_Data_Files.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Save_Data_Files.htm)

##### Save Data Files


You can save data files you edited.


To save a data file:

- On the File tab of the ribbon, click Save As to save a new file or an existing one with another name or extension.
- In the Save As dialog box, navigate to the destination folder, and provide a file name.


Note You can save data files with comments you added. In this case, in the Save As dialog box, in Save As Type field, select the *.datx extension for the file you want to save.

---

<a id="customize-a-file-list-pane"></a>

#### Customize a File List Pane

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Customize_a_File_List_Pane.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Customize_a_File_List_Pane.htm)

##### Customize a File List Pane


You can configure the columns in the File List pane.


To customize columns:

1. In the File List pane, click Configure Columns. The Columns Configuration dialog box is displayed.
2. In the Column Configuration dialog box, the following options are available:
3. Click OK.


To add a predefined column:

1. In the File List pane, click Configure Columns.
2. In the Column Configuration dialog box, click Add Predefined Column (). The Add Predefined Column dialog box is displayed.
3. Choose the standards group and the column template according to your business needs, and then click OK.
4. You can add HL7 or EDI data section by using the Add data section option () and change the data sections order by using Move Up () and Move Down () options.
5. Click OK.

<a id="edit-delimiters"></a>

#### Edit Delimiters

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Edit_Delimiters.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Edit_Delimiters.htm)

##### Edit Delimiters


You can change delimiters to new values. Delimiters apply to a specific type of guideline and are used for a more visual representation of the data file.


To edit delimiter characters:

1. On the Home tab of the ribbon, click Edit Delimiters.
2. Type or choose new Ascii or Hex Values for any of the delimiters.
3.  After you have changed delimiters, you can do one of the following:
4. Click OK.


Note Press Enter on your keyboard to insert a segment terminator.


You can add delimiters manually by typing its value in the data file. In this case, on the Home pane of the ribbon, click Re-parse to re-format the file.


You can change terminators in data files which cannot be parsed and are open in plain mode. For those data the Edit Delimiters dialog box is the following:


*[Image: Edit Delimiters]*


In the Edit Delimiters dialog box, type or choose new Ascii or Hex Values for terminators. You can also specify a terminator and split a data file that was not successfully parsed. In this case, place a cursor before the symbol you want to use as terminator and on the Home tab, in the Editing group, click Split.

---

<a id="set-preprocessing-options"></a>

#### Set Preprocessing Options

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Data_Editor_Set_Preprocessing_Options.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Data_Editor_Set_Preprocessing_Options.htm)

##### Set Preprocessing Options


If you want the opportunity to clean up the data file before running the Data Explorer, you can use the Preprocessor. With the Preprocessor, you can eliminate any terminator string or block characters in the data.


To set preprocessing options:

1. Open the Data Open Wizard.
2. On the Clean Up Data File wizard page, select the Use Preprocessor check box.
3. In the Parameters dialog box, choose a saved parameter file to use, if applicable.
4. Do one of the following:
5. To save these settings, click Save As and, in the Preprocessor Parameters Name box, type a name or select an existing parameter name to modify its definition.
6. Type a location to save the modified data file, or click Browse to locate a folder in which to save the modified data file.
7. Click Next.
8. On the Parsing result wizard page, do one of the following:
9. Click Next to go to the [Choose De-Identification and Specify Rules File](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Select_De_Identification_and_Specify_Rules_File.htm) wizards.

---

<a id="work-with-bookmarks"></a>

#### Work with Bookmarks

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Work_with_Bookmarks.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Work_with_Bookmarks.htm)

##### Work with Bookmarks


You can add a bookmark to a certain line to the data file. Bookmarks are displayed in the [Bookmarks Pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Bookmark_Pane.htm). You can create bookmarks and navigate them not only within the scope of a single file but also within the files in the File List. To limit the scope, on the Home tab of the ribbon, in the Bookmarks group, click Limit to Document.


To add a bookmark:

1. In the Data view pane, click a line that you want to bookmark.
2. On the Home tab of the ribbon, click Toggle.


The bookmark is created and marked in the Data view pane.


You can remove a bookmark by clicking the marker.


Data Explorer allows exporting bookmarked lines to CSV format.


To export bookmark lines to a CSV file:

1. On the Home tab of the ribbon, click Export to CSV.
2. In the Export Segments to CSV dialog box, click Browse.
3. In the Specify file to export segments dialog box, navigate to the export destination folder, and then provide the name for the resulted file.
4. Click Save.
5. Click Next.
6. Click Finish.

---

<a id="print-data-files"></a>

#### Print Data Files

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Print_Data_Files.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Print_Data_Files.htm)

##### Print Data Files


You can print data files. Printing of data files can be customized by changing the settings in the Print option of the file menu.


Note Use the Print Preview feature to view your data file or data replacement results before printing or publishing.


To set printing options:


On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click Print. The following options are available:


| Option | Icon | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Uncollated | | Select this option not to collate pages of the document. |
| Print line number | | Select this option to print a line number for each data line. |
| Print grid lines | | Select this option to print a grid. |
| Use alternating shading | | Select this option to print the alternate shading for each data file. |
| Show header nodes | | Select this option to show header nodes. |
| Show node information | | Select this information to show node information. The Show header nodes should be selected. |
| Show comments | | Select this option to display node comments in printed document. |
| Print in indented format | | Print a data tree in the indented format |
| Indent step size |   | Specify the indent step size here. |
| Margins |   | Set the page margins. |
| Header/ Footer position |   | Specify the Header and Footer details. |
| Header content |   | Specify the Header content. |
| Footer content |   | Specify the Footer content. |

---

<a id="data-replace-and-de-identify"></a>

### Data Replace and De-Identify

> 📄 Source: [Book_DataExplorer_Data_Replace_and_De_Identify.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_DataExplorer_Data_Replace_and_De_Identify.htm)

#### Data Replace and De-Identify


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="data-replace-overview"></a>

#### Data Replace Overview

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Data_Replace_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Data_Replace_Overview.htm)

##### Data Replace Overview


Data Replace is very similar to a search and replace function for multiple values. It allows you to make many changes to your data file quickly and efficiently. You can do this by first describing a pattern that best matches the data in the data file, and then specifying how you would like the data to be changed by using a replacement pattern.


Both match and replace patterns are stored in a configuration file. New patterns can be added or existing patterns can be modified by using the Data Replace Rules Editor.

---

<a id="modify-data-replace-rules"></a>

#### Modify Data Replace Rules

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Modify_Data_Replace_Rules.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Modify_Data_Replace_Rules.htm)

##### Modify Data Replace Rules


You can view and modify the Rules file by using the Data Replace Rules editor. To view the editor, on the Home tab of the ribbon, click Rules Editor. The Data Replace Rules editor is displayed.


*[Image: Data Replace Rules Editor]*


The following information provides descriptions for the columns in the Data Replace Rules dialog box:


Enabled: Select this check box to apply the pattern.


Match Pattern: Specify the items that you want to match.


Example


If you want to change the reference number on all REF segments, the match pattern should read: REF*.


If you want to change the reference number only on REF segments that have a qualifier IN then the match pattern should read: REF*IN*


Replace Pattern: Specify the pattern for the replacement data.

- Using wildcards


You can change the reference number for all REF segments.


Example


If you want to change the reference number on all REF segments to "12345", the match pattern should read: REF* and replace pattern should read: REF**12345.


In this example, the first element of the REF segment does not change, nor does any element after the second element. Only the value of the second element changes. So if the original data was REF*IN*Invoice Number, then the new value is REF*IN*12345.


To change the reference number for REF segments containing the qualifier IN, the match pattern changes, but the replacement pattern can remain the same.


Example


The match pattern becomes REF*IN* and the replace pattern remains REF**12345. In this case, only those REF segments that have the qualifier IN get a new reference number of "12345".

- Using {Empty} macro


To remove all data values from the data file, you can specify a replacement pattern with the macro "{Empty}".


Example


If you want to remove the reference number for all REF segments that have a reference number qualifier IN, then the match pattern should read: REF*IN* and replace pattern should read: REF*IN*{Empty}

- Using {Counter} macro


When you de-identify identical guideline nodes, a pattern naturally produces the same output with equal IDs. It makes the resulting data invalid against HIPAA rules. The {Counter(Key)} macro maintains a counter for each key in order to generate a unique ID for every guideline node. The Key parameter is a unique counter that increments the ID for every node. Using the macro without the Key parameter is the same as using the macro with an empty string Key.


You can use a segment ID to set the context. You can also use more than one counter at a time. You can use different counters for different segments.


Example


If you want to generate unique IDs for every guideline node you de-identify, then the match pattern should read: REF*IN* and replacement pattern should read: REF*IN*{Counter}


For more information about match and replace patterns, see [Data Replace Rules Syntax](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Data_Replace_Rules_Syntax.htm).


Rule Description: You can add your notes about the rule in this field.


###### Skipping Complex Fields in HL7 Data


You can skip complex fields when applying data replace rules. To do this, you must provide fields separators with no information between them (<field separator><field separator>). For example, if you want to skip information about one complex field, use the following sample that shows the node you need to skip.


Example


123456^ABC^|SO^Namespac&Universa&DNS^AM^NAmespac&Universal


This node has several components inside separated by a caret (^) and the field separator is a pipe (|) for this file. In this particular case, the rule should resemble the example in the following dialog box:


*[Image: Data Replace Rule]*


If you want to skip fields that you are not interested in, you need to remove the content between the pipe (|) field separators. In this case, the rule should resemble the example in the following dialog box:


*[Image: Data Replace Rule]*

---

<a id="data-replace-rules-syntax"></a>

#### Data Replace Rules Syntax

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Data_Replace_Rules_Syntax.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Data_Replace_Rules_Syntax.htm)

##### Data Replace Rules Syntax


Data replace rule syntax consists of two parts: match pattern and replace pattern. The match pattern specifies the data to search for. The replace pattern specifies the data to be used for replacement. Both patterns have the same format. The following table explains the syntax by using examples.


The following delimiters are used for examples in the table:

- segment terminator: '
- element separator: ~
- sub-element separator: +
- repetition separator: *
- release character: ?
- decimal indicator: .


| Match Pattern | Replace Pattern | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| NM1~Name | NM1~John | Every segment that has the ID NM1 and the first element Name, changes the ID to NM1 and the first element to John. Note This pattern starts from data, not from a delimiter. That is why data is interpreted as the segment ID. This pattern should start from the segment, so the Name should be the first element to be selected. |
| NM1~~Name | ~~John | Every segment with the ID NM1, any first data element, and the second data element Name changes its second element to John. The segment ID and the first data element remain the same. |
| NM1~{Empty}~Name | ~~{Empty} | Every segment with the ID NM1, empty first data element, and second data element Name have the second data element cleared. {Empty} in the match pattern defines the empty data. In the replace pattern it cleared the appropriate data. |
| ~Name | ~John | Every segment with the element Name is changed to John. This pattern starts from the element separator ~. The data after the separator is the data element. The match pattern is not bound to the start of the segment, it is important where the data element Name is located in a segment. So the data: NM1~Name, NM1~~Name, or NM2~~~~Name is changed. |
| ~Name~ | ~John | Every element Name with another element after it of any content is changed to John. Note ~Name~ is more constraining than ~Name. |
| NM1~~Name' | ~~John | Every segment with the ID NM1, any first element, the second element Name, and two data elements change the Name to John. Note Match pattern is bound to the right end of the data by using the segment terminator '. |
| REF~Description~01+Ref Info+01+Ref Info | ~~1+John+2+Smith | Every segment with the ID REF, the first element Description, the composite second element whose the first sub-element is 01, the second is Ref Info, and so on. Have the composite sub-elements changed. Note + is used to refer to composites. |
| ~Name+ | ~John+ | Every data sub-element Name, that is the first in the composite, is changed to John. Note This way you may bound to the beginning of the composite. |
| +Name | +John | Every sub-element Name regardless its position in the composite is changed to John. |
| +Name+ | +John | Every sub-element Name, that is not the last in the composite, is changed to John. Note +Name+ is more constraining than +Name. |
| +Name~ | +John | Every sub-element Name, that is the last in the composite and has an element after, is changed to John. |
| +Name' | +John | Every sub element Name, that is the last in the composite and that last in the segment, is changed to John. |

---

<a id="select-de-identification-and-specify-rules-file"></a>

#### Select De-Identification and Specify Rules File

> 📄 Source: [DataExplorer_Select_De_Identification_and_Specify_Rules_File.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Select_De_Identification_and_Specify_Rules_File.htm)

##### Select De-Identification and Specify Rules File


With this wizard, you can select the file that contains the rules for replacing confidential data with generic information.


To view the Data Explorer wizard:

1. On the Home tab of the ribbon, click Data De-identification. The Data Explorer wizard wizard page is displayed.


*[Image: Choose De-Identification and Specify Rules File]*


The following list provides descriptions for the user interface elements in the Choose De-identification and Specify Rules File wizard.

- Select replacement rules File: Browse to select a path to the document.
- Skip empty elements: Select this option not to apply replacement rules to empty elements in a data file.
- Customize Rules: Launches Data Replace Rules Editor.


*[Image: Data Replace Rules Editor]*


The Match Pattern specifies how to select data to be changed. The Replace Pattern displays the actual data that replaces the original data. Additionally, you can choose which patterns to enable and disable.


For more information about match and replace patterns, see [Data Replace Rules Syntax](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Data_Replace_Rules_Syntax.htm).

- After editing the rules, click OK, and then click Next to complete the wizard.


Note You can also [de-identify the file from the command prompt](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Perform_De_Identification_from_a_Command_Prompt.htm).


Edifecs De-identification rules are in compliance with Safe Harbor rules. The following identifiers of the individual or of relatives, employers, or household members of the individual, are removed:


| Criteria | Edifecs Support |
| --- | --- |
| Names | Yes |
| All geographic subdivisions smaller than a state, including street address, city, county, precinct, ZIP code, and their equivalent geocodes, except for the initial three digits of the ZIP code if, according to the current publicly available data from the Bureau of the Census: (1) The geographic unit formed by combining all ZIP codes with the same three initial digits contains more than 20,000 people; and (2) The initial three digits of a ZIP code for all such geographic units containing 20,000 or fewer people is changed to 000 | Yes |
| All elements of dates (except year) for dates that are directly related to an individual, including birth date, admission date, discharge date, death date, and all ages over 89 and all elements of dates (including year) indicative of such age, except that such ages and elements may be aggregated into a single category of age 90 or older | Yes |
| Telephone numbers | Yes |
| Fax number | Yes |
| Email addresses | Yes |
| Social security numbers | Yes |
| Medical record numbers | Yes |
| Health plan beneficiary numbers | Yes |
| Account number | Yes |
| Certificate/license numbers | *N/A |
| Vehicle identifiers and serial numbers, including license plate numbers | *N/A |
| Device identifiers and serial numbers | *N/A |
| Web Universal Resource Locators (URLs) | *N/A |
| Internet Protocol (IP) addresses | *N/A |
| Biometric identifiers, including finger and voice prints | *N/A |
| Full-face photographs and any comparable images | *N/A |
| Any other unique identifying number, characteristic, or code, except as permitted by paragraph (c) of the Safe Harbor Method Guidance Document by HHS. | *N/A |



Note *N/A - Not applicable for HIPAA transactions.

---

<a id="large-data-files-peculiarities"></a>

### Large Data Files Peculiarities

> 📄 Source: [Large_Data_Files_Peculiarities.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Large_Data_Files_Peculiarities.htm)

#### Large Data Files Peculiarities


Data Explorer has the following capabilities when working with data files:


| Action | Data files up to 50 MB | Data files up to the specified threshold(*) | Data files up to 2 GB |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Read | + | + | + |
| Edit | + | + | - |
| Edit delimiters | + | + | - |
| Split | + | + | + |
| Parse/Colorize | + | - | - |
| Simple Search | + | + | + |
| Simple Replace | + | + | + (in the File List only) |
| Match Pattern search | + | - | - |
| Match Pattern replace | + | - | - |
| Regex Search | + | + | + |
| Regex Replace | + | + | + (in the File List only) |
| Export Segments to CSV | + | + (in the File List only) | - |
| Print | + | - | - |



(*) - You can set the threshold size on the File tab, in Options. By default, the threshold is set to 100 MB, the maxim value is 150 MB.


Data files that are available in the read only mode, has a marker on the corresponding tab in the Data View pane.

---

<a id="standards-library"></a>

## Standards Library

> 📄 Source: [Book_Standards_Library.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Standards_Library.htm)

### Standards Library


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="standards-reference-overview"></a>

### Standards Reference Overview

> 📄 Source: [Standards_Reference_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Standards_Reference_Overview.htm)

#### Standards Reference Overview


Use the Standards Reference to research the available standards. Standards Reference is only available for standards that can use the same components but have different structures within one version. All components that can be used in one standard version are joined and shown in an encyclopedia view available in read-only mode. The information explains the structure of the standard as well as all the details associated with each node within the standard.


Standards available for browsing:

- EDIFACT
- UCS
- X12
- WINS
- TDCC


To launch the Standards Reference, on the Tools tab, click Standards Reference.


The Standards Reference workspace consists of four panes:

- Standard (left) pane - displays the transaction sets and transaction set structure. 
- Details (upper right) pane - displays the explanation of the selected node.
- Children pane (middle right) - displays the nested nodes (including loop structures) of the node selected in the Standard pane.


Of a code list node is selected in the Standard pane, the list of codes within that node is displayed in the Children pane.

- Cross-References pane (bottom right) - displays all the places where the node is used.


*[Image: Standards Reference]*

---

<a id="browse-standards"></a>

### Browse Standards

> 📄 Source: [Browse_Standards.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Browse_Standards.htm)

#### Browse Standards


The following standards are available in the Standards Reference:

- X12
- UCS
- EDIFACT
- WINS
- TDCC


Note All standards are available in read-only mode.


To browse a standard:

1. On the Tools tab, click Standards Reference.
2. Double-click the standard you want to browse to see available versions of the selected standard.
3. Click the version of the standard you want to browse.
4. Click OK. The Standards Reference workspace is displayed. For more information, see [Standards Reference Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Standards_Reference_Overview.htm).

---

#### Find Codes


When browsing standards, you can search for a specific code within a code list.


To find a code:

1. In the Standard pane, click a code list node.
2. On the Tools menu, click Find Codes.
3. In the Find What box, type the code to look for.
4. Click any of the following options:
5. After you have found one code, you can use the same parameters to search for the next code within the same node by doing one of the following:

<a id="de-identification-utility"></a>

## De-Identification Utility

> 📄 Source: [Book_DE_De-identification_Utility.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_DE_De-identification_Utility.htm)

### Data De-identification Utility


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="de-identification-utility-overview"></a>

### De-identification Utility Overview

> 📄 Source: [De-identification_Utility_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/De-identification_Utility_Overview.htm)

#### De-Identification Utility Overview


Healthcare and other EDI data often contain confidential information such as social security numbers, credit card numbers, and protected healthcare information. This confidential information can be automatically replaced and de-identified in your file based on a set of customizable replacement rules.


With the De-identification Utility, you can replace the confidential information in the original data file or a folder by using data replace rules. For more information, see [Data Replace Rules Syntax](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Replace_Rules_Syntax.htm).


The De-identification Utility is located in the SpecBuilder install path, usually – *C:\*Edifecs*\*SpecBuilder*\deidentify.exe* directory.


*[Image: De-identification Tool]*


The following list provides descriptions for the user interface elements in the **De-identification Utility** window.

1. **Original Data Path**: Provide a path to the original data file or folder, or click **File** or **Folder** to navigate to the file or folder location.
2. **Process subfolders**: Select this check box to allow **De-identification Utility** to process subfolders in case the original data folder includes it.
3. **Destination Path**: Type a path and a file name for the processed file or folder, or click **Browse** to navigate to the location you want to save the result data, and then type a name for a file.
4. **Replace Rules Path**: Browse to select a data replace rules file (.drr).
5. Skip empty elements in data file: Select this option not to apply replacement rules to empty elements in a data file.
6. **Edit**: Launches **Data Replace Rules Editor**. For more information, see [Data Replace Rules Editor](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Replace_Rules_Editor.htm).

---

<a id="data-replace-rules-editor"></a>

### Data Replace Rules Editor

> 📄 Source: [Data_Replace_Rules_Editor.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Replace_Rules_Editor.htm)

#### Data Replace Rules Editor


Data Replace is very similar to a search and replace function for multiple values. It allows you to make many changes to your data file quickly and efficiently. You can do this by first describing a pattern that best matches the data in the data file, and then specifying how you would like the data to be changed by using a replacement pattern.


Both match and replace patterns are stored in a configuration file. New patterns can be added or existing patterns can be modified by using the Data Replace Rules Editor.


*[Image: Data Replace Editor]*


The following information provides descriptions for the columns in the Data Replace Rules dialog box:


Enabled: Select this check box to apply the pattern.


Match Pattern: Specify the items that you would like to match.


Example


If you want to change the reference number on all REF segments, the match pattern should read: REF*.


If you want to change the reference number only on REF segments that have a qualifier IN then the match pattern should read: REF*IN*


The **Match Pattern** tells you how to select the data to be changed. The **Replace Pattern** displays the actual data that replaces the original data. Additionally, you can chose which patterns to enable and disable.


For more information about match and replace patterns, see [Data Replace Rules Syntax](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Replace_Rules_Syntax.htm).


Replace Pattern: Specify the pattern for the replacement data.

- Using wildcards
- Using {Empty} macro
- Using {Counter} macro


For more information about match and replace patterns, see [Data Replace Rules Syntax](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Replace_Rules_Syntax.htm).


Note: You can also [de-identify the file from the command prompt](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Perform_De_Identification_from_a_Command_Prompt.htm).


Edifecs De-identification rules are in compliance with Safe Harbor rules. The following identifiers of the individual or of relatives, employers, or household members of the individual, are removed:


| Criteria | Edifecs Support |
| --- | --- |
| Names | Yes |
| All geographic subdivisions smaller than a state, including street address, city, county, precinct, ZIP code, and their equivalent geocodes, except for the initial three digits of the ZIP code if, according to the current publicly available data from the Bureau of the Census: (1) The geographic unit formed by combining all ZIP codes with the same three initial digits contains more than 20,000 people; and (2) The initial three digits of a ZIP code for all such geographic units containing 20,000 or fewer people is changed to 000 | Yes |
| All elements of dates (except year) for dates that are directly related to an individual, including birth date, admission date, discharge date, death date, and all ages over 89 and all elements of dates (including year) indicative of such age, except that such ages and elements may be aggregated into a single category of age 90 or older | Yes |
| Telephone numbers | Yes |
| Fax number | Yes |
| Email addresses | Yes |
| Social security numbers | Yes |
| Medical record numbers | Yes |
| Health plan beneficiary numbers | Yes |
| Account number | Yes |
| Certificate/license numbers | *N/A |
| Vehicle identifiers and serial numbers, including license plate numbers | *N/A |
| Device identifiers and serial numbers | *N/A |
| Web Universal Resource Locators (URLs) | *N/A |
| Internet Protocol (IP) addresses | *N/A |
| Biometric identifiers, including finger and voice prints | *N/A |
| Full-face photographs and any comparable images | *N/A |
| Any other unique identifying number, characteristic, or code, except as permitted by paragraph (c) of the Safe Harbor Method Guidance Document by HHS. | *N/A |



Note *N/A - Not applicable for HIPAA transactions.

<a id="data-replace-rules-syntax"></a>

### Data Replace Rules Syntax

> 📄 Source: [Data_Replace_Rules_Syntax.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Replace_Rules_Syntax.htm)

#### Data Replace Rules Syntax


Data replace rule syntax consists of two parts: match pattern and replace pattern. The match pattern specifies the data to search for. The replace pattern specifies the data to be used for replacement. Both patterns have the same format. The following table explains the syntax by using examples.


The following delimiters are used for examples in the table:

- segment terminator: '
- element separator: ~
- sub-element separator: +
- repetition separator: *
- release character: ?
- decimal indicator: .


| Match Pattern | Replace Pattern | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| NM1~Name | NM1~John | Every segment that has the ID NM1 and the first element Name, changes the ID to NM1 and the first element to John. Note This pattern starts from data, not from a delimiter. That is why data is interpreted as the segment ID. This pattern should start from the segment, so the Name should be the first element to be selected. |
| NM1~~Name | ~~John | Every segment with the ID NM1, any first data element, and the second data element Name changes its second element to John. The segment ID and the first data element remain the same. |
| NM1~{Empty}~Name | ~~{Empty} | Every segment with the ID NM1, empty first data element, and second data element Name have the second data element cleared. {Empty} in the match pattern defines the empty data. In the replace pattern it cleared the appropriate data. |
| ~Name | ~John | Every segment with the element Name is changed to John. This pattern starts from the element separator ~. The data after the separator is the data element. The match pattern is not bound to the start of the segment, it is important where the data element Name is located in a segment. So the data: NM1~Name, NM1~~Name, or NM2~~~~Name is changed. |
| ~Name~ | ~John | Every element Name with another element after it of any content is changed to John. Note ~Name~ is more constraining than ~Name. |
| NM1~~Name' | ~~John | Every segment with the ID NM1, any first element, the second element Name, and two data elements change the Name to John. Note Match pattern is bound to the right end of the data by using the segment terminator '. |
| REF~Description~01+Ref Info+01+Ref Info | ~~1+John+2+Smith | Every segment with the ID REF, the first element Description, the composite second element whose the first sub-element is 01, the second is Ref Info, and so on. Have the composite sub-elements changed. Note + is used to refer to composites. |
| ~Name+ | ~John+ | Every data sub-element Name, that is the first in the composite, is changed to John. Note This way you may bound to the beginning of the composite. |
| +Name | +John | Every sub-element Name regardless its position in the composite is changed to John. |
| +Name+ | +John | Every sub-element Name, that is not the last in the composite, is changed to John. Note +Name+ is more constraining than +Name. |
| +Name~ | +John | Every sub-element Name, that is the last in the composite and has an element after, is changed to John. |
| +Name' | +John | Every sub element Name, that is the last in the composite and that last in the segment, is changed to John. |

---

| Criteria | Edifecs Support |
| --- | --- |
| Names | Yes |
| All geographic subdivisions smaller than a state, including street address, city, county, precinct, ZIP code, and their equivalent geocodes, except for the initial three digits of the ZIP code if, according to the current publicly available data from the Bureau of the Census: (1) The geographic unit formed by combining all ZIP codes with the same three initial digits contains more than 20,000 people; and (2) The initial three digits of a ZIP code for all such geographic units containing 20,000 or fewer people is changed to 000 | Yes |
| All elements of dates (except year) for dates that are directly related to an individual, including birth date, admission date, discharge date, death date, and all ages over 89 and all elements of dates (including year) indicative of such age, except that such ages and elements may be aggregated into a single category of age 90 or older | Yes |
| Telephone numbers | Yes |

<a id="perform-de-identification-from-a-command-prompt"></a>

> 📄 Source: [Perform_De_Identification_from_a_Command_Prompt.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Perform_De_Identification_from_a_Command_Prompt.htm)

#### Perform De-Identification from a Command Prompt


To perform de-identification from a command prompt:

1. Open a command prompt and change the current directory to where the deidentifyconsole.exe is installed.
2. To de-identify a file by using the Replacement Rules .drr file, type:
3. Press Enter.


De-identification of the file starts, and the output file is created in the output folder.


Note You can optionally use /v to view the output in the console window.
