This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="ec-objects-and-variables-overview"></a>

##### EC Objects and Variables Overview

> 📄 Source: [EC_Objects_and_Variables_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EC_Objects_and_Variables_Overview.htm)

###### EC Objects and Variables Overview


Almost all internal objects and variables begin with the characters "ec". Depending on the usage, the prefix may or may not be capitalized. Ec objects are special objects which make it easier to code business rules and also allow you to reduce the amount of script you have to write.


Ec objects are defined so that they can be used as part of your own custom JavaScript.  However, variables defined with "ec" (as opposed to "EC") as the prefix should be used with care as they are subject to change in future releases. A full list of "ec" variables is available from technical support.  As a best practice, you should use a company-specific naming convention for your objects and variables.


| Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| ec_Bookmark | Allows you to assign bookmarks to nodes and to access data values and guideline properties from the current bookmark location. |
| ec_DataLink | Provides access to the data node from the data file. |
| ec_ECGlobal | Provides access to properties that were captured from the envelope segments. |
| ec_ExtCodes | Provides access to external code list database. |
| ec_CheckNCCIEdit | Allows you to perform CCI/NCCI-initiative verification. |
| EC_List | Similar to an Array object with additional methods to make using lists easier and more useful. |
| EC_PatternChecker | Ability to match data against a list of patterns. |
| EC_ConditionChecker | Check value based on the specified condition. |
| ec_ErrorContainer | Allows you to pass your error messages to be reported as part of the validation error report. |

---

<a id="translator-javascript-object-crosswalkengine"></a>

##### Translator JavaScript Object CrosswalkEngine

> 📄 Source: [Translator_JavaScript_CrosswalkEngine.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_JavaScript_CrosswalkEngine.htm)

###### Translator JavaScript CrosswalkEngine


The CrosswalkEngine object is used for Crosswalk lookups.

 match


Does a lookup for an array of keys. The result is a CrosswalkResult object. Returns the nearest result, even if not all keys were matched. Result should be valid for the passed date. The format of the date is yyyymmdd. For example, 19990512 is 05/12/1999.


###### Syntax


CrosswalkResult match(string[] keys, integer date)


###### Return Value


Returns a IXECrosswalkResult object.


Example


This code snippet demonstrates how to use CrosswalkEngine in your code.


// get crosswalk engine by crosswalk ref


var engine = ec_CrosswalkRegistry.getCrosswalkEngine(ref);


// prepare keys for lookup


var keys = new Array();


keys[0] = "0001";


keys[1] = "9-to-10";


keys[2] = "pcs";


// do match with keys and date 02/08/2001


var result = engine.match(keys, 20010208);





 fullMatch


Does a lookup for an array of keys. The result is CrosswalkResult object. Returns result matching of all of the keys. Result should be valid for the passed date. The format of the date is yyyymmdd. For example, 19990512 is 05/12/1999.


###### Syntax


CrosswalkResult fullMatch(string[] keys, integer date)


###### Return Value


Returns a IXECrosswalkResult object.


Example


This code snippet demonstrates how to use CrosswalkEngine in your code.


// get crosswalk engine by crosswalk ref


var engine = ec_CrosswalkRegistry.getCrosswalkEngine(ref);


// prepare keys for lookup


var keys = new Array();


keys[0] = "0001";


keys[1] = "9-to-10";


keys[2] = "pcs";


// do match with keys and date 02/08/2001


var result = engine.fullMatch(keys, 20010208);

---

<a id="translator-javascript-object-crosswalkref"></a>

##### Translator JavaScript Object CrosswalkRef

> 📄 Source: [Translator_JavaScript_CrosswalkRef.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_JavaScript_CrosswalkRef.htm)

###### Translator JavaScript CrosswalkRef


Note The CrosswalkRef class will not be supported in future releases.


Note This interface and the methods contained are also applicable for Validator actions.


The following table lists the members of the CrosswalkRef object, which holds all the information for the Crosswalk.

 getId()


Note The method is not supported in future releases.


###### Syntax


int getId()


###### Return Value


Returns -1.





 getName()


###### Syntax


String getName()


###### Return Value


Returns a Crosswalk name.





 getCacheType()


NoteThe method is not supported in future releases.


###### Syntax


int getCacheType()


###### Return Value


Returns the Crosswalk type of caching: 0 – Full, 1 – Refreshable, 2 – None.





 getType()


NoteThe method is not supported in future releases.


###### Syntax


int getType()


###### Return Value


Returns the Crosswalk type. Possible value: 0 – Database. Custom crosswalks (Java classes) are no longer supported.





 getCrosswalkLink()


NoteThe method is not supported in future releases.


###### Syntax


String getCrosswalkLink()


###### Return Value


Returns a path to the Crosswalk database file. Custom crosswalks (Java classes) are no longer supported.





 getTPKeys()


###### Syntax


String[] getTPKeys()


###### Return Value


Returns Crosswalk TPKeys (TPNames).





 getCriteria()


###### Syntax


String[][] getCriteria()


###### Return Value


Returns Crosswalk criteria in the form of a name-value pairs array.

---

<a id="translator-javascript-object-crosswalkresult"></a>

##### Translator JavaScript Object CrosswalkResult

> 📄 Source: [Translator_JavaScript_CrosswalkResult.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_JavaScript_CrosswalkResult.htm)

###### Translator JavaScript CrosswalkResult


The CrosswalkResult object holds the record set of values returned by the CrosswalkEngine match methods. Use the CrosswalkResult methods to work with the results.

 getMatchedKeysCount()


**Syntax**


Int getMatchedKeysCount()


###### Return Value


Returns the count of matched keys.





 getAll()


**Syntax**


String [][] getAll()


###### Return Value


Returns all results in form of String[][].





 getFirst()


**Syntax**


String getFirst()


###### Return Value


Returns the first element of the first row – [0][0].





 getLast()


**Syntax**


String getLast()


###### Return Value


Returns the last element of the first row – [n][0].





 select(String[] values)


**Syntax**


CrosswalkResult select(String[] values)


###### Return Value


Returns a clarified result by first values of it.






Example


This code snippet demonstrates how to use CrosswalkResult in your code.


// do match with keys and date 02/08/2001


var result = engine.match(keys, 20010208);


// get all result as array of array


var arrRes = result.getAll();


// print result


ec_ECGlobal.println("first result:");


for(i = 0; i < arrRes.length; i++)


{


ec_ECGlobal.println(arrRes[i][0] + " " + arrRes[i][1] + " " + arrRes[i][2]);


}


ec_ECGlobal.println("Matched count = " + result.getMatchedKeysCount());

---

<a id="translator-javascript-object-crosswalkrefmutable"></a>

##### Translator JavaScript Object CrosswalkRefMutable

> 📄 Source: [Translator_JavaScript_CrosswalkRefMutable.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_JavaScript_CrosswalkRefMutable.htm)

###### Translator JavaScript CrosswalkRefMutable


Note The CrosswalkRefMutable class will not be supported in future releases.


Note This interface and the methods contained are also applicable for Validator actions.


The following table lists the members of the CrosswalkRefMutable object, which holds all the information for the Crosswalk.

 setId(int)


Note The method is not supported in future releases. In this release, XEngine does not use the values you provide for this method, but does not throw an exception.


###### Syntax


void setId(int id)


###### Parameters


id: the Crosswalk identifier


###### Return Value


This method does not return a value.





 setName(String)


###### Syntax


setName(String name)


###### Parameters


name: The Crosswalk name


###### Return Value


This method does not return a value.





 setCacheType(int)


Note The method is not supported in future releases. In this release, XEngine does not use the values you provide for this method, but does not throw an exception.


###### Syntax


void setCacheType(int cacheType)


###### Parameters


cacheType: The Crosswalk type of caching: 0 – Full, 1 – Refreshable, 2 – None


###### Return Value


This method does not return a value.





 setType(int)


Note The method is not supported in future releases. In this release, XEngine does not use the values you provide for this method, but does not throw an exception.


###### Syntax


void setType(int type)


###### Parameters


type: The Crosswalk type. Available value is 0 – Database. Custom crosswalks (Java classes) are no longer supported.


###### Return Value


This method does not return a value.





 setCrosswalkLink(String)


Note The method is not supported in future releases. In this release, XEngine does not use the values you provide for this method, but does not throw an exception.


###### Syntax


void setCrosswalkLink(String crosswalkLink)


###### Parameters


crosswalkLink: a path to the Crosswalk database file. Custom crosswalks (Java classes) are no longer supported.


###### Return Value


This method does not return a value.





 setTPKeys(String[])


###### Syntax


void setTPKeys(String[] tpKeys)


###### Parameters


tpKeys: Crosswalk TPKeys (TPNames)


###### Return Value


This method does not return a value.





 setCriteria(String[][])


###### Syntax


void setCriteria(String[][] criteria)


###### Parameters


criteria: The Crosswalk criteria in the form of a name-value pairs array.


###### Return Value


This method does not return a value.






Example



// create new crosswalk ref


var ref = ec_CrosswalkRegistry.createRef();


 


// set crosswalk information


ref.setName("myname1");


var criterion = new Array();


criterion[0] = "C1";


criterion[1] = "C2";


var criteria = new Array();


criteria[0] = criterion;


ref.setCriteria(criteria);


var TPKeys = new Array();


TPKeys[0] = "TP1";


TPKeys[1] = "TP2";


ref.setTPKeys(TPKeys);

<a id="translator-javascript-object-ec_crosswalkregistry"></a>

##### Translator JavaScript Object ec_CrosswalkRegistry

> 📄 Source: [Translator_JavaScript_ec_CrosswalkRegistry.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_JavaScript_ec_CrosswalkRegistry.htm)

###### Translator JavaScript ec_CrosswalkRegistry


Note This interface and the methods contained are also applicable for Validator actions.

 getCrosswalkEngine(String)

###### Parameters

- name: the Crosswalk name


###### Return Value


Returns null or a CrosswalkEngine by Crosswalk name.





 getCrosswalkEngine(String, String)

CrosswalkEngine getCrosswalkEngine(String name, String TPName)

- name: the Crosswalk name
- TPName: the Trading Partner name


###### Return Value


Returns null or a CrosswalkEngine object that matches the specified parameters.





 getCrosswalkEngine(String, String, String[][])


###### Syntax


CrosswalkEngine getCrosswalkEngine(String name, String TPName, string [] [] criteria)


###### Parameters

- name: the Crosswalk name
- TPName: the Trading Partner name
- criteria: the Crosswalk criteria


###### Return Value


Returns null or a CrosswalkEngine object that matches the specified parameters.


Example


var criteria = new Array();


var engine = ec_CrosswalkRegistry.getCrosswalkEngine("test", "test tp", criteria);





 getCrosswalkRefs(string name, string TPName, string [] [] criteria)


NoteThe method is not supported in future releases.


###### Syntax


CrosswalkRef [] getCrosswalkRefs(string name, string TPName, string [] [] criteria)


###### Return Value


Returns an array of CrosswalkRef objects by name, TPname, and criteria parameters.





 getCrosswalkEngine(CrosswalkRef)


NoteThe method is not supported in future releases.


###### Syntax


CrosswalkEngine getCrosswalkEngine(CrosswalkRef)


###### Return Value


Returns a CrosswalkEngine by name, TPname, and criteria parameters from the CrosswalkRef object.





 createRef()


NoteThe method is not supported in future releases.


###### Syntax


CrosswalkRefMutable createRef()


###### Return Value


Returns a new clean CrosswalkRefMutable.





 createRef(CrosswalkRef)


NoteThe method is not supported in future releases.


###### Syntax


CrosswalkRefMutable createRef(CrosswalkRef prototype)


###### Return Value


Returns a new CrosswalkRefMutable with information from a prototype.





 getCrosswalks(String, String, string [] [])


NoteThe method is not supported in future releases.


###### Syntax


Crosswalk[] getCrosswalks(String name, String TPName, string [] [] criteria)


###### Parameters

- name: Crosswalk name
- TPName (optional parameter): Trading Partner Name
- criteria (optional parameter): Crosswalk criteria


###### Return Value


Returns an empty array or an array with one Crosswalk object that matches the specified parameters.






Note The following methods are no longer supported: getCrosswalk(int), getCrosswalkRef(int), updateCrosswalk(CrosswalkRef), registerCrosswalk(CrosswalkRefMutable), registerCrosswalk(CrosswalkRefMutable, boolean), registerCrosswalk(CrosswalkRefMutable, String, String), unregisterCrosswalk(int).
