<a id="ec_bookmark-object"></a>

##### ec_Bookmark Object

> 📄 Source: [ec_Bookmark_object.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ec_Bookmark_object.htm)

###### ec_Bookmark Object


ec_Bookmark Methods


The ec_Bookmark object contains methods that can be used to assign bookmarks to nodes and to access data values and guideline properties from the current bookmark location. ec_Bookmark is a global static object. You can add as many bookmarks as you want, but only one bookmark is accessible at a time. If you place more than one bookmark in the data node structure, the current bookmark is considered to be either the last one you added or the last one you found by calling ec_Bookmark.Find.


Bookmarks are useful if you want to access the current node later with JavaScript. By creating a bookmark on the node, you can access various properties of the current node without having to keep each property of the current node in a temporary JavaScript variable. Bookmarks can help reduce memory and optimize JavaScript performance.


###### ec_Bookmark Methods

 ec_Bookmark.Add()


Use this object and method to create a bookmark for the current node with a specified name.


###### Syntax


ec_Bookmark.Add(bmName)


###### Parameters


bmName: Required; Type - string


Example


ec_Bookmark.Add ("ISA05")





 ec_Bookmark.Find()


Use this object and method to return true if the specific bookmark name is found; otherwise returns false. The found bookmark becomes active, so its properties can be accessed through other functions such as IsDataPresent, GetData, and the rest.


###### Syntax


ec_Bookmark.Find(bmName)


###### Parameters


bmName: Required; Type - string; User-assigned bookmark name; it should have previously been used to add a bookmark.


###### Return Value


true: if the specified bookmark is found


false: if the specified bookmark is not found


Example


if ((ec_Bookmark.Find ("NM108")) &&(ec_Bookmark.GetData () == "XX"))





 ec_Bookmark.GetData()


Use this object and method to obtain the data value for the active bookmark.


###### Syntax


ec_Bookmark.GetData()


###### Parameters


No parameters supported.


###### Return Value


Data value: if a value is present


Empty string: if no value is present


Example


if ((ec_Bookmark.Find ("NM108")) && (ec_Bookmark.GetData () == "XX"))





 ec_Bookmark.GetID()


Use this object and method to return the ID for the active bookmark.


###### Syntax


ec_Bookmark.GetID();


###### Parameters


No parameters supported.


Example


var ElementID = ec_BookMark.GetID();





 ec_Bookmark.GetName()


Use this method to return the Name for the active bookmark.


###### Syntax


ec_Bookmark.GetName();


###### Parameters


No parameters supported.


Example


var ElementName = ec_BookMark.GetName();





 ec_Bookmark.GetPosition()


Use this object and method to return the Position for the active bookmark.


###### Syntax


ec_Bookmark.GetPosition();


###### Parameters


No parameters supported.


Example


var ElementPosition = ec_BookMark.GetPosition();





 ec_Bookmark.IsDataPresent()


Use this object and method to return true if a data value is present for the active bookmark; otherwise it returns false.


###### Syntax


ec_Bookmark.IsDataPresent();


###### Parameters


No parameters supported.


###### Return Value


true: if a data value is present for the active bookmark


false: if a data value is not present


Example


if ((ec_Bookmark.Find (NodeRef)) && (ec_Bookmark.IsDataPresent ()))





 ec_Bookmark.Remove()


Use this object and method to remove a bookmark with a specified name. It is good to remove bookmarks after you are finished using them, as this frees memory.


###### Syntax


ec_Bookmark.Remove(bmName)


###### Parameters


bmName: Required; Type - string; User-assigned bookmark name; it should have previously been used to add a bookmark.


Example


ec_Bookmark.Remove ("PRV_2000A");

---

<a id="ec_checkcncciedit_object"></a>

##### ec_CheckCNCCIEdit_Object

> 📄 Source: [ec_CheckCNCCIEdit_Object.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ec_CheckCNCCIEdit_Object.htm)

###### ec_CheckNCCIEdit Object


The ec_CheckNCCIEdit object contains methods that allow you to perform CCI/NCCI-initiative verification.

 ec_CheckCNCCIEdit.checkCode()


Use this method to perform Correct Coding Initiative (CCI) validation such as comprehensive and mutually exclusive, add-on, or medically unbelievable. Validation is performed on every service line within a claim, taking into account all previous service lines. When an error is detected, it is reported for a specific procedure by using the procedureCode bookmark.


###### Syntax


ec_CheckCNCCIEdit.checkCode(bitMask,procedureCode,date,usageCount, modifierVal1, modifierVal2, modifierVal3, modifierVal4)


###### Parameters


bitMask: Required; Type - integer; Defines what kind of validation should be performed. Bit values by positions:

1 - Exclusive validation (value: 1);

2 - AddOns validation (value: 2);

3 - Unbelievable validation (value: 4).


Bits can be summed - for example, if you need to perform Exclusive and Unbelievable validation, the bitMask should have a value of 5 (4 + 1).


procedureCode: Required; Type - bookmark; Procedure code taken from the data that is common for all types of validation.


date: - Required; Type - string in format CCYYMMDD; Date from the data file.


usageCount: Optional; Type - bookmark; Current value of usage count for the particular procedureCode. Used for Unbelievable Edit validation only.


modifierValn: Optional; Type - bookmark; Additional modifiers. Used for Exclusive Edit validation only.


Example


xAdd-on validation


ec_CheckCNCCIEdit.checkCode("2", "100000", "20053030")


Exclusive validation


ec_CheckCNCCIEdit.checkCode("1", "100000", "20053030", "0", "KM");


Mixed mode validation


ec_CheckCNCCIEdit.checkCode("7", "100000", "20053030", "KM", ec_Bookmark.GetData());






 

 ec_CheckCNCCIEdit.reset()


Use this method to reset a CCI object to initial state; all captured procedures are removed.


###### Syntax


ec_CheckCNCCIEdit.Reset()


###### Parameters


No parameters supported.


Example


ec_CheckCNCCIEdit.reset();






###### ec_CheckNCCIEdit Script Global Variable Name


ec_CheckNCCIEdit


###### ec_CheckNCCIEdit Object Properties


None


###### ec_CheckNCCIEdit Exceptions


A JavaScript exception is raised in the following situations:

- A CCI table that cannot be unpacked or interpreted is encountered.
- A "StandardRegistry.xml" file that cannot be interpreted is encountered.
- The size of the unpacked CCI table is greater than 500 megabytes.

---

<a id="ec_conditionchecker-object"></a>

##### EC_ConditionChecker Object

> 📄 Source: [EC_ConditionChecker_Object.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EC_ConditionChecker_Object.htm)

###### EC_ConditionChecker Object


The object consists of the following methods:

 EC_ConditionChecker()


Use this method like an if/then statement, where values and conditional operators are provided and an error is reported based on the comparison of the values.


###### Syntax


    new EC_ConditionChecker(Value, ID)


###### Parameters


Value - the value of the node


ID - a reference to the node which is used in the default error message.


###### Return Value


Object - an EC_ConditionChecker object is set according to the arguments passed. If no arguments are passed, then an empty EC_ConditionChecker object is returned.


Example


    ec_ccBPR04 = new EC_ConditionChecker (ec_DataLink.m_sData, "BPR04"); ec_ccBPR04.m_strOperator = "EQ"; ec_ccBPR04.m_vConditionValue = "NON"; var ErrorMsg = "Value of element BPR04 is incorrect. Expected value is 'NON' when BPR01='H'." +        ec_strErrorElemMsg; ec_ccBPR04.Verify(ErrorMsg);





 EC_ConditionChecker.m_strValue


Use this method and property to provide a value if a value was not provided during the creation of the object.


###### Syntax


    EC_ConditionChecker.m_strValueList = Value;


###### Parameters


Value - The parameter used in the function EC_ConditionChecker(Value, ID).





 EC_ConditionChecker.m_strID


Use this method and property to provide an ID when one was not provided during the creation of the object.


###### Syntax


    EC_ConditionChecker.m_strID = ID;


Parameters


ID - The parameter used in the function EC_ConditionChecker(Value,ID)





 EC_ConditionChecker.m_strOperator


Use this method and property to indicate what type of comparison should be done.


###### Syntax


     EC_ConditionChecker.m_strOperator = Value;


Value must be one of the following operators:

- EQ: equal to condition value
- NQ: not equal to condition value
- EX: exists (used)
- NX: not exists (not used)
- INLIST: in list
- NOTINLIST: not in list
- BYIND: in list at indexed position
- PAT: formatted according pattern from list
- NOTPAT: all patterns from list are failed
- PATBYIND: formatted according indexed pattern from list
- LESS: less than specified condition value
- MORE: more than specified condition value





 EC_ConditionChecker.m_vConditionValue


Use this method and property to indicate what the comparison value should be based on the operator.


###### Syntax


    EC_ConditionChecker.m_vConditionValue = Value;


###### Parameters


Value - used for comparison. Value must be used in conjunction with the Operator.

- For operators EQ, NQ: value to compare must be provided. - For operators EX, NX: value should not be specified. - For operators INLIST, NOTINLIST, BYIND (list of values to check): EC_List object must be provided. - For operators PAT, NOTPAT, PATBYIND (list of patterns to check): EC_PatternChecker object must be provided. - For operators LESS, MORE: value should be a numeric value.





 EC_ConditionChecker.m_nIndex


Use this method and property when EC-ConditionChecker.m_strOperator is BYIND (in the list at the indexed position) or PATBYIND (formatted based on a pattern at the indexed position). This property can be used to indicate to the EC_ConditionChecker.Verify or EC_ConditionChecker. VerifyDependency methods that the value must match a specific pattern.


###### Syntax


    EC_ConditionChecker.m_nIndex = Index;


###### Parameters


Index - the position in a list.





 EC_ConditionChecker.Verify()


Use this method and property to compare the value set with the conditional value based on the specified operator. If the result of the comparison is false then an error message is reported on the error report.


###### Syntax


    EC_ConditionChecker.Verify(ErrorMsg)


###### Parameters


ErrorMsg - a string containing the error description to report if the condition fails. If the ErrorMsg contains the string "STDERR" then the default error message is reported.


###### Return Value


The return value differs depending upon the operator. When an operator such as EQ orNE is used, the value returned is Boolean. When an operator such as INLIST is used, the return value is the index of the matching value.


Example


    ec_ccBPR04 = new EC_ConditionChecker (ec_DataLink.m_sData, "BPR04"); ec_ccBPR04.m_strOperator = "EQ"; ec_ccBPR04.m_vConditionValue = "NON";


    var ErrorMsg = "Value of element BPR04 is incorrect. Expected value is 'NON' when BPR01='H'." +    ec_strErrorElemMsg; ec_ccBPR04.Verify(ErrorMsg);





 EC_ConditionChecker.VerifyAndPlace()


Use this method and property to compare the value set with the conditional value based on the specified operator. If condition is not met, error is reported on ErrorKey node with message provided by ErrorMsg, ErrorLevel and Severity.


###### Syntax


EC_ConditionChecker.VerifyAndPlace (ErrorKey, ErrorMsg, ErrorLevel, Severity)


###### Parameters


ErrorKey - required, type - string


ErrorMsg - optional, type - string


ErrorLevel - optional, type - string


Severity - optional, type - string


###### Return values depend on operator


all operators except INLIST: true/false is condition is met or not


INLIST: -1 if value is not found, otherwise numeric index of found value





 EC_ConditionChecker.VerifyDependency()


Use this method and property to perform a check of the condition between two nodes depending on the result of an initial condition check. In the following example, the value of the second field BPR04 must be NON only if the value of the field BPR01 is H.


###### Syntax


    EC_ConditionChecker.VerifyDependency(EC_ConditionChecker, ErrorMsg)


###### Parameters


EC_ConditionChecker - another object of the same type which allows more sophisticated dependency conditions to be checked.


ErrorMsg - a string containing the error description to report if the condition fails. If the ErrorMsg contains the string "STDERR" then the default error message is reported.


###### Return Value


The return value differs depending upon the operator. When an operator such as EQ, NE is used, the value returned is Boolean. When an operator such as INLIST is used, the value returned is the index of the matching value.


Example


ec_ccBPR01 = new EC_ConditionChecker (ec_DataLink.m_sData, "BPR01"); ec_ccBPR04 = new EC_ConditionChecker (ec_DataLink.m_sData, "BPR04"); ec_ccBPR01.m_strOperator = "EQ"; ec_ccBPR01.m_vConditionValue = "H"; ec_ccBPR04.m_strOperator = "EQ"; ec_ccBPR04.m_vConditionValue = "NON"; var ErrorMsg = "Value of element BPR04 is incorrect. Expected value is 'NON' when BPR01='H'." + ec_strErrorElemMsg; ec_ccBPR04.VerifyDependency(ec_ccBPR01, ErrorMsg);

---

<a id="ec_datalink-object"></a>

##### ec_DataLink Object

> 📄 Source: [ec_DataLink_Object.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ec_DataLink_Object.htm)

###### ec_DataLink Object


The ec_DataLink object accesses information in the current data token/guideline node pair and makes it available to the JavaScript handler. The ec_DataLink is a global variable, which can be accessed in place of any JavaScript code.


This object consists of the following methods:

 ec_DataLink.getDataOffset


Syntax


ec_DataLink.getDataOffset();


###### Return Value


Return string type is long.


Example


var ec_DataOffset = ec_DataLink.getDataOffset();





 ec_DataLink.getDataSize


###### Syntax


ec_DataLink.getDataSize();


###### Return Value


Return string type is long.


Example


var ec_DataLink.getDataSize = ec_DataLink.getDataSize();






###### ec_DataLink Properties

 ec_DataLink.m_sData


This property returns the current data value of the node. If the data node is missing, it returns an empty string.


###### Syntax


ec_DataLink.m_sData;


###### Return Value


Data node is present - Data value of the current node.


Data node is missing - Empty string.


Example


var currentNodeValue = ec_DataLink.m_sData;





 ec_DataLink.m_bDataPresent


This property returns true if there is a data value or false if not.


###### Syntax


ec_DataLink.m_bDataPresent;


###### Return Value


true: data value is present


false: data value is missing


Example


if((ec_DataLink.m_bDataPresent)


{


....


}





 ec_DataLink.m_nIID


This property returns the unique identifier for the current node in the guideline.


###### Syntax


ec_DataLink.m_nIID;


###### Return Value


Unique ID for the guideline node.


Example


var GuidelineNodeID = ec_DataLink.m_nIID;

---

<a id="ec_ecglobal-object"></a>

##### ec_ECGlobal Object

> 📄 Source: [ec_ECGlobal_Object.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ec_ECGlobal_Object.htm)

###### ec_ECGlobal Object


This object provides support functionality.


The object consists of the following methods:

 ec_ECGlobal.GetDNValue()


Returns resolved macro with regard to specified locale.


###### Syntax


ec_ECGlobal.GetDNValue() with appropriate severity macro


Example 1


This code snippet demonstrates how to use this method fro extracting rejecting category errors from report.


ec_ECGlobal.println("Rejecting category:");


if (ec_ECGlobal.GetDNValue("sv.err.cnt") > 0)


{


var sStat = ec_ECGlobal.GetDNValue("sv.err.for(\"Name: [nam] Count: [val]\", \"\n\")");


ec_ECGlobal.println(sStat);


}


else


{


ec_ECGlobal.println("none");


}


Example 2


You can use this method to get the Transaction node GUID attribute from the following LDNS report:


`<GroupNode Name="Transaction" GUID="{BFCB7214-CF12-4FD3-BCE5-485A6D095B51}"`


Use the following code:


ec_ECGlobal.SetGlobalProp("dns.fnm.EntireDocument.prp.TransactionId", ec_ECGlobal.GetDNValue("dns.id"));





 ec_ECGlobal.Exception()


Use this method to add a user-defined exception to JavaScript actions. When the exception is encountered during data processing, execution of the specified JavaScript action is stopped and the custom exception is raised with the specified ID and description.


###### Syntax


ec_ECGlobal.Exception(ExceptionID, ExceptionReason)


###### Parameters


ExceptionID: Required; Type - integer; User-assigned ID for the exception.


ExceptionReason: Required; Type - string; User-defined reason for the exception.


Example


ec_ECGlobal.Exception(1, "exception reason: database connection failed");





 ec_ECGlobal.GetGlobalProp()


Replaced by ec_ECGlobal.GetGlobalPropEx().


Use this method to search through all parent data nodes starting from the current and return a value of the property sPropertyName. If the property is not found, an empty string is returned.


Provides access to values that are captured from the envelope segment.


###### Syntax


ec_ECGlobal.GetGlobalProp(sPropertyName);


###### Parameters


sPropName: the property name to get the value for. Properties name can be found in the Analyzer tree which is presented in the Analyzer wizard. The following list for EDI is also provided:


X12

- InterchangeAuthorizationInfoQual
- InterchangeAuthorizationInfo
- InterchangeSecurityInfoQual
- InterchangeSecurityInfo
- InterchangeSenderQual
- InterchangeSenderID
- InterchangeReceiverQual
- InterchangeReceiverID
- InterchangeDate
- InterchangeTime
- InterchangeControlStandard
- InterchangeControlVersion
- InterchangeControlNumber
- InterchangeAckRequest
- InterchangeUsageIndicator
- InterchangeComponentElementSep
- GroupID
- GroupSenderID
- GroupReceiverID
- GroupDate
- GroupTime
- GroupControlNumber
- GroupAgencyCode
- GroupVersionNumber
- TransactionID
- TransactionControlNumber


UN/EDIFACT

- InterchangeSenderID
- InterchangeSenderQual
- InterchangeReceiverID
- InterchangeReceiverQual
- InterchangeDate
- InterchangeTime
- InterchangeControlNumber
- InterchangeAckRequest
- GroupID
- GroupSenderID
- GroupSenderQual
- GroupReceiverID
- GroupReceiverQual
- GroupDate
- GroupTime
- GroupControlNumber
- GroupVersion
- GroupReleaseNumber
- TransactionID
- TransactionControlNumber
- TransactionMessageVersionNumber
- TransactionMessageReleaseNumber


Example


var ec_GroupID = ec_ECGlobal.GetGlobalProp("GroupID");





 ec_ECGlobal.GetGlobalPropEx()


Use this method to search through all parent data nodes starting from the current and return a value of the property/link sPropName. If the property/link is not found, an empty string is returned.


Example X12 and EDIFACT property names include:


X12

- InterchangeAuthorizationInfoQual
- InterchangeAuthorizationInfo
- InterchangeSecurityInfoQual
- InterchangeSecurityInfo
- InterchangeSenderQual
- InterchangeSenderID
- InterchangeReceiverQual
- InterchangeReceiverID
- InterchangeDate
- InterchangeTime
- InterchangeControlStandard
- InterchangeControlVersion
- InterchangeControlNumber
- InterchangeAckRequest
- InterchangeUsageIndicator
- InterchangeComponentElementSep
- GroupID
- GroupSenderID
- GroupReceiverID
- GroupDate
- GroupTime
- GroupControlNumber
- GroupAgencyCode
- GroupVersionNumber
- TransactionID
- TransactionControlNumber


UN/EDIFACT

- InterchangeSenderID
- InterchangeSenderQual
- InterchangeReceiverID
- InterchangeReceiverQual
- InterchangeDate
- InterchangeTime
- InterchangeControlNumber
- InterchangeAckRequest
- GroupID
- GroupSenderID
- GroupSenderQual
- GroupReceiverID
- GroupReceiverQual
- GroupDate
- GroupTime
- GroupControlNumber
- GroupVersion
- GroupReleaseNumber
- TransactionID
- TransactionControlNumber
- TransactionMessageVersionNumber
- TransactionMessageReleaseNumber


###### Syntax


ec_ECGlobal.GetGlobalPropEx(sPropName)


###### Parameters


sPropName: string with the name of the property.


Example


var myGroupID = ec_ECGlobal.GetGlobalPropEx("GroupID");





 ec_ECGlobal.GetGlobalLink


Use this method to retrieve the value of the specified sLinkName captured from envelope segments (Interchange, group, or transaction). If the link name is not found, an empty string is returned.


###### Syntax


ec_ECGlobal.GetGlobalLink (sLinkName)


###### Parameters


sLinkName: Required; Type - string; The name of the property from which the lookup value is retrieved.


Example


ec_ECGlobal.GetGlobalLink("GroupSenderID");





 ec_ECGlobal.GetGlobalLinkEx


Use this method to return the value of link property sLinkName captured from envelope segments (interchange, group or transaction). If the look-up value is not found on the current data node, GetGlobalLinkEx continues searching through all the children nodes of the current data node to retrieve the look-up value. If the property is not found, empty string is returned.


###### Syntax


ec_ECGlobal.GetGlobalLinkEx (sLinkName)


###### Parameters


sLinkName: Required; Type - string; The name of the property from which the lookup value is retrieved.


Example


ec_ECGlobal.GetGlobalLinkEx("GroupSenderID");





 ec_ECGlobal.localtime()


Use this method to convert the specified universal time value and corrects it to the local time zone. You can apply the following fields to the return value in order to retrieve a more meaningful result:

- tm_sec - seconds
- tm_min - minutes
- tm_hour - hours
- tm_mday month day
- tm_mon - month
- tm_year - year
- tm_wday - week day
- tm_yday - year day


###### Syntax


ec_ECGlobal.localtime(nTime)


###### Parameters


nTime: Required; Type - integer; Time value in numeric UTC format.


Example


var t = ec_ECGlobal.localtime (ec_ECGlobal.time ());


var t_year = t.tm_year + 1900;


var t_mon = t.tm_mon +1;


t_mon = t_mon.toString ();


if (t_mon.length < 2)


{


t_mon = "0" + t_mon;


}


var t_day = t.tm_mday.toString ();


if (t_day.length < 2)


{


t_day = "0" + t_day;


}


ec_TrDate = t_year.toString () + t_mon + t_day;





 ec_ECGlobal.m_nEventCount


Use this method to get a count of events that are reported up to this point. The method counts all events occurred, for example, informational events, warnings, errors. For more information, see the Error Severity Settings Overview topic in XEngine Help.


###### Syntax


ec_ECGlobal.m_nEventCount;


###### Parameters


Method takes no input.


Example


var NbrOfEvents = ec_ECGlobal.m_nEventCount;





 ec_ECGlobal.m_nErrorCount


Use this method to get a count of errors that are reported up to this point. The method counts only errors, excluding other event types. For more information, see the Error Severity Settings Overview topic in XEngine Help.


###### Syntax


ec_ECGlobal.m_nErrorCount;


###### Parameters


Method takes no input.


Example


var NbrOfErrors = ec_ECGlobal.m_nErrorCount;





 ec_ECGlobal.SetGlobalProp()


Use this method to apply the specified name/value pair to the report for the current data node or to create/replace a property throughout the entire document. The value is applied to the logical data node if it is the current node.


###### Syntax


ec_ECGlobal.SetGlobalProp(sPropName, sPropValue)


###### Parameters


sPropName: Required; Type - string; Name of the property.


sPropValue: Required; Type - string; Value of the property.


Example


ec_ECGlobal.SetGlobalProp("MyUniqueIDForTransaction", "123456");


ec_ECGlobal.SetGlobalProp("dns.fnm.EntireDocument.prp.Receiver", "MyReceiver"); this creates or replace the MyReceiver property at all levels of EntireDocument





 ec_ECGlobal.StopExec()


Use this method to stop validation.


###### Syntax


ec_ECGlobal.StopExec();


###### Parameters


Method takes no input.


Example


ec_ECGlobal.StopExec()





 ec_ECGlobal.time()


Use this method to return a long integer value (system time_t type) that is the standard UNIX time format representing the number of seconds that have elapsed since 00:00 January 1, 1970, coordinated universal time.





 ec_ECGlobal.println


Use this method to print the line specified.


###### Syntax


ec_ECGlobal.println


Example


ec_ECGlobal.println("hello world");





 ec_ECGlobal.getSystemDate()


Use this method to return the current date in the local time zone. The date is returned in the yyyyMMdd format. If XERegistry uses GMT, then GMT date is returned in the yyyyMMdd format.


###### Syntax


ec_ECGlobal.getSystemDate()






The ec_ECGlobal object contains the following attributes:


###### ec_ECGlobal.m_sEngineType


###### ec_ECGlobal.m_nErrorCount


Returns the count of errors/events that have been reported up to this point.

---

<a id="ec_errorcontainer-object"></a>

##### ec_ErrorContainer Object

> 📄 Source: [ec_ErrorContainer_object.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ec_ErrorContainer_object.htm)

###### ec_ErrorContainer Object


The ec_ErrorContainer object contains methods that create, reset, and act on the error cache used during JavaScript action validation.


###### ec_ErrorContainer Methods

 ec_ErrorContainer.AddBookMarkParam()


Use this method to add error parameters directly to the error object from previously bookmarked items, based on the bookmark name. Please refer to the bookmark objects to understand more about using bookmarks.


###### Syntax


ec_ErrorContainer.AddBookmarkParam(sBmName, nOption, sBmLabel)


###### Parameters

 sBmName


Required; Type - string.





 nOption


Optional; Type - integer; see possible values later in the topic; specifies error parameters.


###### Possible values for nOption


| data value | 0x00001 | ec_[bookmark_name/substitute]_DVal |
| --- | --- | --- |
| data counter | 0x00002 | ec_[bookmark_name/substitute]_Counter_[level] |
| guideline ID | 0x00004 | ec_[bookmark_name/substitute]_G_ID_[level] |
| guideline Position | 0x00008 | ec_[bookmark_name/substitute]_G_POS_[level] |
| guideline Name | 0x00010 | ec_[bookmark_name/substitute]_G_Name_[level] |
| guideline(counter) parent 1 | 0x00020 |   |
| guideline(counter) parent 2 | 0x00040 |   |



Note Up to 10 parents can be specified. The desired option value is obtained by adding all the values together. All the parameters created for the node is created for the parent - see the following example:


For example, if the option parameter is 0x00001|0x00002|0x00004|0x00010|0x00040, the following parameters are added to the error object (assuming that the bookmark name is set to bm):

- ec_bm_DVal with the data value of the token
- ec_bm_Counter_0 with the counter value of the token (for example, the number of the segment)
- ec_bm_G_ID_0 with the guideline node ID for the matching guideline node
- ec_bm_G_Name_0 with the guideline node Name for the matching guideline node
- ec_bm_Counter_2 with the counter value of the token for the parent of the parent of the matching guideline node (as long as it exists in the guideline)
- ec_bm_G_ID_2 with the guideline node ID for the parent of the parent of the matching guideline node (as long as it exists in the guideline)
- ec_bm_G_Name_2 with the guideline node Name for the parent of the parent of matching guideline node (as long as it exists in the guideline)





 sBMLabel


Optional; Type - string; when specified, the parameter name is generated with this substitute value rather than the bookmark name.


###### Possible values for sBMLabel


For acknowledgement creation, you can use the following labels depending on the node type you are going to reference to:

- Element: DepE, DepE1, DepE2 (to add a reference for more than one element)
- Sub-element: DepSE, DepSE1, DepSE2 (to add a reference for more than one sub-element)
- Segment: DepS, DepS1, DepS2 (to add a reference for more than one segment)
- Loop: DepL, DepL1, DepL2 (to add a reference for more than one loop)






Example


ec_Bookmark.Add("NM102");


 


if (ec_Bookmark.Find ("NM102"))


{


ec_ErrorContainer.AddBookmarkParam("NM102", 0x00001);


ec_ErrorContainer.AddEventParamBM("NM102");


}





 ec_ErrorContainer.Activate()


Use this method to activate an error previously identified by sKey through use of PlaceError. Optionally, the specified error message (sDescription) is added to the error report produced during validation.


###### Syntax


    ec_ErrorContainer.Activate(sKey, sDescription, sGUID)


###### Parameters


sKey - Required; Type - string; Error key that should be activated.


sDescription - Optional; Type - string; Should be specified in either the PlaceError or the Activate function.


sGUID - Optional; Type - string.


###### Return Values


true: if sKey placeholder is found and error is successfully created in error report


false: otherwise


Examples


Create a placeholder on AAA segment:


b_AAA = ec_DataLink.m_sDataPresent;


ec_ErrorContainer.PlaceError("AAA");


 


Activate an error on AAA segment when DTP is present, but AAA is missing:


if (ec_DataLink.m_bDataPresent && (!(b_AAA)))


{


ec_ErrorContainer.Activate ("AAA", "Segment AAA is missing. It is required when segment DTP is used.");


}





 ec_ErrorContainer.Activate2()


Use this method to activate an error previously identified by sKey through use of PlaceError. Up to 10 parameters may be used during error message generation. For proper error message generation, at least one of following parameters must be specified:


_ec_CaseID - 4-digit value; specifies a previously-identified error ID


_ec_DescriptionTemplate - string; specifies the error message template directly


###### Syntax


    ec_ErrorContainer.Activate2(sKey, sParamName1, sParamValue1, ....)


###### Parameters


sKey - Required; Type - string; Error key that should be activated.


sParamNamen - Optional; Type - string; Parameter name to add to the error object. Possible names: _ec_DescriptionTemplate, Description, _ec_CaseID, ErrorLevel, Brief, SeverityName, SeverityID.


sParamValuen - Required; Type - string; Parameter value to be added to the error object.


###### Return Values


true : if sKey placeholder is found and an error is successfully created


false : otherwise


Example


ec_ErrorContainer.PlaceError("AAA");


 


if (ec_DataLink.m_bDataPresent)


{


ec_ErrorContainer.Activate2 ("AAA", "_ec_DescriptionTemplate", "Segment AAA is missing. It is required when segment DTP is present.");


}





 ec_ErrorContainer.AddError()


Note Only supported for backward compatibility, please use AddEvent.


Use this method to add the specified error message to the error report produced during validation.


###### Syntax


    ec_ErrorContainer.AddError( sDescription, sGUID);


###### Parameters


sDescription - Required; Type - string; The string containing the error message.


sGUID - Optional; Type - string; Reference to a pre-defined error message.





 ec_ErrorContainer.AddError2()


Note Only supported for backward compatibility; please use AddEvent.


Use this method to add an error with the specified parameters to the error report produced during validation. Up to 10 parameters may be used during error message generation. For proper error message generation, at least one of the following parameters must be specified:


_ec_CaseID - 4-digit value; Specifies a previously-identified error ID. _ec_DescriptionTemplate - string; Specifies error message template directly.





 ec_ErrorContainer.AddErrorParam()


Note Only supported for backward compatibility, please use AddEvent.


Use this method to define the parameter to be used during error message generation and assign the value to the parameter.





 ec_ErrorContainer.AddErrorParams()


Note Only supported for backward compatibility, please use AddEvent.


Defines up to 10 parameters to be used during error message generation and assigns values to those parameters.





 ec_ErrorContainer.AddEvent()


Use this method to add an event with a specified ID (EID) to the error report produced during validation.


Note The event IDs that can be used for custom events are between 0x000001 to 0x099999.


###### Syntax


    ec_ErrorContainer.AddEvent(EID[,sDescription[,sParamName1, sParamValue1, sParamName2, sParamValue2…]])


###### Parameters


EID - Required; Type - Hex integer; Event id of the error message.


sDescription - Optional; Type -string.


sParamNamen - Optional; Type - string; Parameter name to add to the error object. Possible names: _ec_DescriptionTemplate, Description, _ec_CaseID, ErrorLevel, Brief, SeverityName, SeverityID.


sParamValuen - Optional; Type - string; Parameter value associated to the parameter name.


Example


    ec_ErrorContainer.AddEvent(0x000001, "This is my error message");





 ec_ErrorContainer.AddEventByBM()


Use this method to add an event with the specified ID (EID) for the node specified by the bookmark (sBookmarkName) to the error report produced during validation. The description and a maximum of 10 parameters may be used during error message generation. This method associates the error to the bookmarked node rather than to the node where the error occurred.


###### Syntax


    ec_ErrorContainer.AddEventByBM(sBookmarkName, EID[, sDescription[, param_name, param_val, …]])


###### Parameters


sBookmark Name - Required; Type - string; Name of the bookmark node.


EID - Required; Type - Hex integer; ID associated with a particular error or event.


sDescription - Optional; Type - string; Event or error description.


sParamNamen - Optional; Type - string; Parameter name to add to the error object. Possible names: _ec_DescriptionTemplate, Description, _ec_CaseID, ErrorLevel, Brief, SeverityName, SeverityID.


sParamValuen - Optional; Type - string; Parameter value associated to the parameter name.


Example


ec_Bookmark.Add("Loop2000B");


 


if(ec_Bookmark.Find("Loop2000B"))


{


ec_ErrorContainer.AddEventByBM ("Loop2000B", 0x9999, "My error message");


}





 ec_ErrorContainer.AddEventParams()


Use this method to define parameters to be added to the previously-created event and assign values to parameters.


###### Syntax


    ec_ErrorContainer.AddEventParams( sName1, sValue1...);


###### Parameters


sName - Required; Type - string; Name of the parameter


sValue - Required; Type - string; Value for the parameter


_ec_CaseID - set CaseID


ErrorLevel - set SNIP level


SeverityName - set Severity name


SeverityID - set Severity ID


Description - set event description


_ec_DescriptionTemplate - set event description


Brief - set event brief


Example


    ec_ErrorContainer.AddEventParams ("gid", "ec_GroupID");





 ec_ErrorContainer.AddEventParamBM()


Use this method to add event parameters directly from the bookmark based on the bookmark’s name.


###### Syntax


    ec_ErrorContainer.AddEventParamBM(sBmName, sBmLabel);


###### Parameters


sBmName - Required; Type - string; Name of the bookmark.


sBMLabel - Optional; Type - string; Optional substitute parameter. When specified, the parameter name is generated with this substitute value instead of the bookmark name.


Example


    ec_ErrorContainer.AddEventParamBM ("Loop2000B");





 ec_ErrorContainer.PlaceError()


Use this method to create a placeholder for a future error, uniquely identified by sKey. Optionally, specify the error message (sDescription) to be added to the error report produced during validation.  This should be used if you want the error to be associated with this guideline node but the actual check for the error occurs in a different location.


###### Syntax


    ec_ErrorContainer.PlaceError(sKey, sDescription, sGUID)


###### Parameters


sKey - Required; Type - string; Create a placeholder for a future error uniquely identified by sKey


sDescription - Optional; Type - string


sGUID- Optional; Type - string


Example


    ec_ErrorContainer.PlaceError ("CUR02");





 ec_ErrorContainer.Remove()


Use this method to remove a placeholder previously identified by sKey through usage of PlaceError.


###### Syntax


ec_ErrorContainer.Remove(sKey)


###### Parameters


sKey - Required; Type - string


Example


ec_ErrorContainer.Remove("CUR02");





 ec_ErrorContainer.Reset


Note This method is obsolete and is removed in next versions of XEngine.


Resets and empties the error cache.


Example


ec_ErrorContainer.Reset(); 





 ec_ErrorContainer.FlushEvents()


Use this method to apply error severity on all events fired within the scope of the current JavaScript. This resolves the problem where ec_ECGlobal.m_nErrorCount returns an incorrect value when a script error is fired, the error's severity is changed from Normal to Warning, and the error counter is checked within one section of JavaScript.


###### Syntax


ec_ErrorContainer.FlushEvents()


###### Parameters


None


###### Return Values


None


Example


     ec_ErrorContainer.AddEvent (0, "", "_ec_CaseID", "1051", "ec_SeverityCriteria", ec_strSeverity, "ErrorLevel", 6);


ec_ErrorContainer.FlushEvents();


if(ec_ECGlobal.m_nErrorCount != 0) { ... }


 


In the previous example, the error severity is applied after the FlushEvents() method executes. Ec_ECGlobal.m_nErrorCount returns 0 or 1, depending on error's severity.






###### ec_ErrorContainer Exceptions


A JavaScript exception is raised if an incorrect parameter is encountered or if calls appear in the wrong order (for example, calling AddErrorParam without first calling AddError).

---

<a id="ec_extcodelist-object"></a>

##### ec_ExtCodeList Object

> 📄 Source: [ec_ExtCodes_Object.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ec_ExtCodes_Object.htm)

###### ec_ExtCodes Object


This object provides access to the external code list database.


The object consists of the following methods:

 ec_ExtCodes.CheckCode


Use this method to check the data values in the code list against the code list name, code list date, and/or other criteria to ensure that you are using the correct version of the code list for validation.


###### Syntax


function ec_ExtCodes.CheckCode(sCodeID, sCodeListID, sDate, sCriteriaName1, sCriteriaValue1 ... )


###### Parameters


sCodeID: Required; Type - string.


sCodeListID: Required; Type - string.


sDate: Required; Type - string in CCYYMMDD format.


sCriteriaNamen: Optional; Type - string - A string specifying the name of the criteria to search for. Criteria can be any standard node names within the code list.


sCriteriaValuen: Optional; Type - string - A string specifying the value of the criteria name you are searching for. For example, "segment", "PartA".


###### Return Value

1 - The specified code within the code list was found.

0 - The specified code was not found in the code list.


-1 - The specified code list was not found. This result could be caused if the code list is suppressed in the registry settings file.


-2 - A invalid non-numeric character was specified in sDate.


-3 - The specified date format is invalid.


-4 - The date specified is invalid.


Example


result = ec_ExtCodes.CheckCode("CodeID", "CodeListName", "20020524");


result = ec_ExtCodes.CheckCode("CodeID", "CodeListName", "", "segment", "part1", "area", "A");





 ec_ExtCodes.CheckCode2


Use this method to check the data values in the code list against the code list name, code list version, and/or other criteria to ensure that you are using the correct version of the code list for validation.


###### Syntax


function ec_ExtCodes.CheckCode2(sCode, sCodelist, sVersion, arrCriteria,arrParams);


###### Parameters


sCode: Required; Type - string.


sCodelist: Required; Type - string.


sVersion: -Required; Type - string.


arrCriteria: Optional; Type - array - A vector of criterion names and values.


sParams: Optional; Type - array - A vector of rule validation parameters.


###### Return Value

1 - The specified code was found within the code list.

0 - The specified code was not found in the code list.


-1 - The specified code list was not found.


-2 - The parameters format is invalid. Only one parameter can be provided, its value cannot be empty.


-4 - The specified version was not found in the ECL subversion.


Example


The following example shows how to use this method to validate the code against version and rule parameters.


// validates the code 22 from the code list 101 against criteria


var arrCriteria = ["CriteriaName","CriteriaValue"];


var nResult1 = ec_ExtCodes.CheckCode2("22","101","", arrCriteria);


 


// validates the code 22 from the code list 101 against version C4


var nResult2 = ec_ExtCodes.CheckCode2("22","101","C4");


 


// validates the code 22 from the code list 101 against version C4 by using the parameters


var arrParameters = ["466-EZ"];


var nResult3 =ec_ExtCodes.CheckCode2("22","101","C4", null, arrParameters);

---

<a id="ec_extcodescheckcodebyuid"></a>

##### ec_ExtCodes.CheckCodeByUID

> 📄 Source: [ec_ExtCodes.CheckCodeByUID.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ec_ExtCodes.CheckCodeByUID.htm)

###### ec_ExtCodes.CheckCodeByUID


Use this method to check the specified data string against the code list UID to ensure that you are using the correct version of the code list for validation.


This method is generally not used. See [ec_ExtCodes Object](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ec_ExtCodes_Object.htm).


###### Syntax


function ec_ExtCodes.CheckCodeByUID (sCodeID, sCodeListUID)


###### Parameters


sCodeID - Required; Type - string - A string containing the data to be validated.


sCodeListUID - Required; Type - string - A string specifying the UID of the code list.


###### Return Values

1 - The specified code within the code list was found.

0 - The specified code was not found in the code list.


-1 - The specified code list was not found. This result can result if the code list is suppressed in the registry.

---

<a id="ec_functions_overview"></a>

##### EC_Functions_Overview

> 📄 Source: [EC_Functions_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EC_Functions_Overview.htm)

###### EC Functions Overview


EC functions are designed to make it easier to code business rules.

---

<a id="ec_list-object"></a>

##### EC_List Object

> 📄 Source: [EC_List_Object.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EC_List_Object.htm)

###### EC_List Object


The object consists of the following methods:

 EC_List()


Use this method to create an object containing a specified list of values.


###### Syntax


    new EC_List(List entry)


###### Parameter


List entry - A string containing values that are comma delimited.


###### Return Value


Object - an EC_List object set according to the arguments passed. If no arguments are passed, then an empty EC_List object is returned.


Example


var d = new EC_List(); //Creates an empty list object var ec_ccPER03.m_vConditionValue = new EC_List ("EM","TE","FX");  //Creates a list object with 3 values - EM, TE, and FX





 EC_List.m_astrValueList


Uses the array to populate the value list in the list object.


###### Syntax


    EC_List.m_astrValueList = Array;


Array is a pre-defined array.





 EC_List Add()


Use this method to add a value to the end of the list.


###### Syntax


    EC_List.Add(Value);


###### Parameters


Value - the value to be added to the list


###### Return Value


Boolean. True indicates the method was successful; false indicates that it failed.


Example


    var d = new EC_List(); //Creates an empty list object d.Add ("EM");  //Adds the value "EM" to the end of the list.





 EC_List Insert()


Use this method to add a value at a specified position in the list.


###### Syntax


    EC_List.Insert(Value, Index);


###### Parameters


Value -  is the value to be added to the list. Index indicates where to add the value. This parameter is optional, if it is skipped then value is added to the end of the list.


###### Return Value


Boolean. True indicates that method was successful or false if it failed.


Example


var d = new EC_List(); //Creates an empty list object ..... d.Insert ("EM", 3);  //Inserts the value "EM" at the 3rd position.





 EC_List Remove()


Use this method to remove a value from the list, item to remove is indicated by the index.


###### Syntax


    EC_List.Remove(Index);


###### Parameter


Index - indicates the position in the list which should be removed.


###### Return Value


Boolean. True indicates method was successful or false if it failed.


Example


var d = new EC_List(); //Creates an empty list object ..... d.Remove (3);  //Removes the 3rd item from the list.





 EC_List Clear()


Use this method to remove all the items from the list.


###### Syntax


    EC_List.Clear();


###### Return Value


Boolean. True indicates method was successful or false if it failed.


Example


var d = new EC_List(); //Creates an empty list object ..... d.Clear();  //Removes all item from the list.





 EC_List GetAt()


Use this method to return a value of the item from the list based on the provided index.


###### Syntax


    EC_List.GetAt(Index);


###### Parameter


Index - the position of the item in the list.


###### Return Value


Value of the item from the list.


Example


var d = new EC_List(); //Creates an empty list object ..... var x = d.GetAt(3);  //Gets the value of the 3rd item from the list.





 EC_List SetAt()


Use this method to assign a new value to an existing item in the list.


###### Syntax


EC_List.SetAt(Index, Value);


###### Parameter


Index - is the position of the item in the list. Value is the new value to be given to the item in the list.


###### Return Value


Boolean. True indicates add method was successful or false if it failed.


Example


var d = new EC_List(); //Creates an empty list object ..... d.SetAt(3,"New");  //Assigns the value "New" to the 3rd item in the list. 





 EC_List_SetFromArray


Use this method to replace list of values for an object from specified list of values.


###### Syntax


EC_List_SetFromArray (arValue)


###### Parameters


arValue - required, type - array


###### Return Values


true: if no syntax error is detected.





 EC_List Find()


Use this method to search for the specified value in the list.


###### Syntax


        EC_List.Find(Value);


###### Parameter


Value to search for in the string.


###### Return Value


Position in the list where the item was found or -1 if no match was found.


Example


var d = new EC_List(); //Creates an empty list object ..... var pos = d.Find("New");  //Searches for the value "New" in the entire list.





 EC_List FindAll


Use this method to search for all the values in a list of values for an object.


###### Syntax


        EC_List.FindAll (Value)


###### Parameter


Value - Required; Type - string


###### Return Value


EC_List with indexes (0 and up) of value within a list.


Example


var d = new EC_List(); //Creates an empty list object ..... var pos = d.FindAll("New");  //Searches for all the values "New" in the entire list. 





 EC_List VerifyInList()


Similar to find in the list this method also searches for the specified value in the list, but if no match is found the error is reported on the error report during validation.


###### Syntax


EC_List.VerifyInList(Value, ErrorMsg);


###### Parameters


Value to search for in the string.


ErrorMsg - is a string containing the error description to report if no match is found in the list. If the ErrorMsg contains the string "STDERR" then the default error message is reported.


###### Return Value


Boolean. True indicates method was successful or false if it failed.


Example


var d = new EC_List(); //Creates an empty list object ..... var ErrorMsg = "Segment CAS is not used with CAS01 as 'PR' in this loop 2100. It's expected to be used when element CLP05 is used."; d.VerifyInList ("PR", ErrorMsg);





 EC_List VerifyNotInList()


Similar to find in the list this method also searches for the specified value in the list, but if match is found the error is reported on the error report during validation.


###### Syntax


    EC_List.VerifyNotInList(Value, ErrorMsg);


###### Parameters


Value to search for in the string.


ErrorMsg - is a string containing the error description to report if match is found in the list. If the ErrorMsg contains the string "STDERR" then the default error message is reported.


###### Return Value


Boolean. True indicates method was successful or false if it failed.


Example


var d = new EC_List(); //Creates an empty list object ..... var ErrorMsg = "Segment CAS is not used with CAS01 as 'PR' in this loop 2100. It's expected to be used when element CLP05 is used."; d.VerifyNotInList ("PR", ErrorMsg);





 EC_List VerifyByIndex()


Use this method to compare the value to the specified item in the list and if it does not match, an error is returned.


###### Syntax


    EC_List.VerifyByIndex(Value, Index, ErrorMsg);


###### Parameter


Value to search for in the string.


Index - is the position of the item in the list to which the value is compared.


ErrorMsg - is a string containing the error description to report if the value does not match the specified item in the list. If the ErrorMsg contains the string "STDERR" then the default error message is reported.


###### Return Value


Boolean. True indicates method was successful or false if it failed.


Example


var d = new EC_List(); //Creates an empty list object ..... var ErrorMsg = "Segment CAS is not used with CAS01 as 'PR' in this loop 2100. It's expected to be used when element CLP05 is used."; d.VerifyByIndex("PR", 3, ErrorMsg); //Value "PR" must match with the 3rd item in the list.





 EC_List Length()


Use this method to provide the total number of value list items.


###### Syntax


    EC_List.Length();


###### Return Value


Numeric value indicating the number of items in the value list.


Example


var d = new EC_List(); //Creates an empty list object ..... var totalItems = d.Length();





 EC_List VerifyPatternInList()


Use this method to verify that at least one value from the list formatted according to the pattern specified in the [EC_PatternChecker](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EC_PatternChecker_Object.htm) object.


###### Syntax


    EC_List.VerifyPatternInList(EC_PatternChecker, ErrorMsg);


###### Parameters


EC_PatternChecker - An object which can contain multiple regular expression objects.


ErrorMsg - A string containing the error description to report if match is found in the list. If the ErrorMsg contains the string "STDERR" then the default error message is reported.


###### Return Value


true : indicates method was successful


false : if it failed


Example


var d = new EC_List(); //Creates an empty list object var PatternObject = new EC_PatternChecker(); ..... var ErrorMsg = "Segment CAS is not used with CAS01 as 'PR' in this loop 2100. It's expected to be used when element CLP05 is used."; d.VerifyPatternInList (PatternObject, ErrorMsg);





 EC_List VerifyListInList()


Use this method to verify that at least one value from the list provided as the parameter is present in this list.


###### Syntax


    EC_List.VerifyPatternInList(EC_List, ErrorMsg);


###### Parameters


EC_List - Object of type EC_List.


ErrorMsg - A string containing the error description to report if match is found in the list. If the ErrorMsg contains the string "STDERR" then the default error message is reported.


###### Return Value


Boolean. True indicates method was successful or false if it failed.


Example


var d = new EC_List(); //Creates an empty list object var e = new EC_List("01","02"); ..... var ErrorMsg = "Segment CAS is not used with CAS01 as 'PR' in this loop 2100. It's expected to be used when element CLP05 is used."; d.VerifyListInList (e, ErrorMsg); //Verifies that value "01'" or "02" are in the list labeled d.

---

<a id="ec_patternchecker-object"></a>

##### EC_PatternChecker Object

> 📄 Source: [EC_PatternChecker_Object.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EC_PatternChecker_Object.htm)

###### EC_PatternChecker Object


The object consists of the following methods:

 EC_PatternChecker()


Use this object to check a value against a list of patterns or a specific pattern in the list. This should be used rather than using regular expressions in rules.


###### Syntax


    new EC_PatternChecker(List entry)


###### Parameters


List entry is a string containing regular expression values that are comma delimited.


###### Return Values


Object - an EC_PatternChecker object set according to the arguments passed. If no arguments are passed, then an empty EC_PatternChecker object is returned.


Example


ec_ccPER04 = new EC_PatternChecker("@", "^\\d{10}$", "^\\d{10}$"); var ErrorMsg = "Error Message"; ec_ccPER04.VerifyMatch(ec_ccPER03, ErrorMsg);





 EC_PatternChecker.m_astrPatternList


Populates the patterns from the provided EC_List.


###### Syntax


    EC_PatternChecker.m_astrPatternList = EC_List;


EC_List is a previously defined object of type EC_List.





 EC_PatternChecker VerifyMatch()


Use this method to match the provided value against the list of patterns that have been setup in the EC_PatternChecker object. If no pattern match returns true then error is reported on the error report, even if one pattern match is successful then no error is reported.


###### Syntax


    EC_PatternChecker.VerifyMatch(Value, ErrorMsg);


###### Parameters


Value - Value that should be matched against the list of patterns.


ErrorMsg - A string containing the error description to report if no pattern match is found against the list of patterns. If the ErrorMsg contains the string "STDERR" then the default error message is reported.


###### Return Value


Boolean. True indicates method was successful or false if it failed.


Example


ec_ccPER04 = new EC_PatternChecker("@", "^\\d{10}$", "^\\d{10}$"); var ErrorMsg = "Error Message"; ec_ccPER04.VerifyMatch(ec_ccPER03, ErrorMsg);





 EC_PatternChecker VerifyMatch2()


Use this method to match the provided value against the list of patterns that have been setup in the EC_PatternChecker object.


###### Syntax


EC_PatternChecker.VerifyMatch2 (Value, ErrorID, Severity)


###### Parameters


Value - Value that should be matched against the list of patterns.


ErrorID - An identifier of the reported error.


Severity - Severity of the error.


###### Return Values

- an index of a matched pattern (starting from zero); or
- -1 if there is no match and an error is reported if ErrorID is specified; or
- -2 iа a pattern list is empty with the “service” error message.





 EC_PatternChecker VerifyMismatch()


Use this method to match the value against the list of patterns that have been setup in the EC_PatternChecker object. If any pattern match returns true then an error is reported on the error report.


###### Syntax


    EC_PatternChecker.VerifyMismatch(Value, ErrorMsg);


###### Parameters


Value - Value that should be matched against the list of patterns.


ErrorMsg - A string containing the error description to report if pattern match is found against the list of patterns. If the ErrorMsg contains the string "STDERR" then the default error message is reported.


###### Return Value


Boolean. True indicates method was successful or false if it failed.


Example


ec_ccPER04 = new EC_PatternChecker("@", "^\\d{10}$", "^\\d{10}$"); var ErrorMsg = "Error Message"; ec_ccPER04.VerifyMismatch(ec_ccPER03, ErrorMsg);





 EC_PatternChecker VerifyIndexMatch()


Value provided is matched against the specified pattern based on the provided index. If pattern match returns false then error is reported on the error report.


###### Syntax


    EC_PatternChecker.VerifyIndexMatch(Value, Index, ErrorMsg);


###### Parameters


Value - should be matched against the list of patterns.


Index - is the position of the pattern to match against.


ErrorMsg - is a string containing the error description to report if pattern match is not found against the specific pattern. If the ErrorMsg contains the string "STDERR" then the default error message is reported.


###### Return Value


Boolean. True indicates method was successful or false if it failed.


Example


ec_ccPER04 = new EC_PatternChecker("@", "^\\d{10}$", "^\\d{10}$"); var ErrorMsg = "Error Message"; ec_ccPER04.VerifyIndexMatch(ec_ccPER03, 2, ErrorMsg);

---

<a id="ec-tool-overview"></a>

##### EC Tool Overview

> 📄 Source: [EC_Tool_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EC_Tool_Overview.htm)

###### EC Tool Overview


EC Tool is provided to generate an .ecs or XSD file directly from the Standards database. It also allows to collect specific guideline or map information necessary to avoid errors. There are three main classes in this API:

- StandardsDB
- GuidelineInfo
- MapInfo

---

<a id="ec-functions"></a>

##### EC Functions

> 📄 Source: [Book_EC_Functions.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_EC_Functions.htm)

###### EC Functions


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="eccheck"></a>

##### ECCheck

> 📄 Source: [ECCheck.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECCheck.htm)

###### ECCheck

 ECCheck782


This function verifies that the m_strValue parameter of Node does not exceed a maximum length of 10 characters including reported or implied places for cents (implied value of 00 after the decimal point).


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECCheck782 (Node, Severity)


###### Parameters


Node - Required, Type - [EC_ConditionChecker](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EC_ConditionChecker_Object.htm)


Severity - Optional, Type - string


###### Returns


true: value complies with requirements


false: value does not comply with requirements





 ECCheck782_2


This function verifies that the value of the current element does not exceed a maximum length of 10 characters including reported or implied places for cents (implied value of 00 after the decimal point).


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECCheck782_2 (Severity, ErrorID)


###### Parameters


Severity - Optional; Type - string


ErrorID - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


true: value complies with requirements


false: value does not comply with requirements





 ECCheckPositive


This function verifies that the m_strValue parameter of Node is zero or a positive number.


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECCheckPositive (Node, Severity)


###### Parameters


Node - Required, Type - [EC_ConditionChecker](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EC_ConditionChecker_Object.htm)


Severity - Optional, Type - string


###### Returns


true: value complies with requirements


false: value does not comply with requirements





 ECCheckPositive2


This function verifies that the value of the current element or sub-element is zero or a positive number.


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECCheckPositive2 (Severity, Indicator)


###### Parameters


Severity - Optional, Type - string


Indicator - Optional, Value - ‘SE’ or ‘E’  Used to indicate whether the function is checking a sub-element or an element.


###### Returns


true: value complies with requirements


false: value does not comply with requirements

---

<a id="ecdate"></a>

##### ECDate

> 📄 Source: [ECDate.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECDate.htm)

###### ECDate

 ECDataPeriodVerify


Use this method to verify if Start Date of Period in prior or the same as End Date. If Start Date is after End date, error is reported with message provided by ErrorMsg and Severity.


###### Syntax


ECDataPeriodVerify (Period, ErrorMsg, Severity)


###### Parameters


Period - required, type - string in format ‘CCYYMMDD-CCYYMMDD’


ErrorMsg - optional, type - string


Severity - optional, type - string


###### Return Values


true: condition is met


false: condition is not met


###### Parameters


Qualifier - Required; Type - [EC_ConditionChecker](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EC_ConditionChecker_Object.htm).


DateName - Required; Type - string  Used in error reporting to make clear which date (for example, Procedure Date or Admission Date) is in error.


Severity - Optional; Type - string.


###### Returns


true: value complies with requirements


false: value does not comply with requirements





 ECDateVerify


This function is used to verify Date format in DTP02/03.


The value of the current element is verified to be formatted according to Qualifier type:

- ‘D8’: CCYYMMDD
- ‘RD8’: CCYYMMDD- CCYYMMDD
- ‘DT’: CCYYMMDDHHMM
- ‘TM’: HHMM


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function  ECDateVerify  (Qualifier, DateName, Severity)


###### Parameters


Qualifier - Required; Type - [EC_ConditionChecker](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EC_ConditionChecker_Object.htm).


DateName - Required; Type - string  Used in error reporting to make clear which date (for example, Procedure Date or Admission Date) is in error.


Severity - Optional; Type - string.


###### Returns


true: value complies with requirements


false: value does not comply with requirements





 ECDateVerify2


This function is used to verify Date format in DTP02/03.


The value of the current element is verified to be formatted according to Bookmark type:

- ‘D8’: CCYYMMDD
- ‘RD8’: CCYYMMDD- CCYYMMDD
- ‘DT’: CCYYMMDDHHMM
- ‘TM’: HHMM
- ‘CY’: CCYY
- ‘CM’: CCYYMM
- ‘RD5’: CCYYMM-CCYYMM
- ‘RD’: MMDDCCYY-MMDDCCYY


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function  ECDateVerify2  (Bookmark, Severity, Indicator)


###### Parameters


Bookmark - Required; Type - string.


Severity - Optional; Type - string.


Indicator - Optional, Value - ‘SE’ or ‘E’  Used to indicate whether the function is checking a sub-element or an element.


###### Returns


N/A

---

<a id="ecnumberverify"></a>

##### ECNumberVerify

> 📄 Source: [ECNumberVerify.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECNumberVerify.htm)

###### ECNumberVerify


This function verifies that the value of the current element is a positive whole number without leading zeros.


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECNumberVerify (Severity)


###### Parameters


Severity - Optional; Type - string.


###### Returns


true: value complies with requirements


false: value does not comply with requirements

---

##### ECSecondaryIDType

> 📄 Source: [ECSecondaryIDType.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECSecondaryIDType.htm)

###### ECSecondaryIDType


Use this method to return short name of qualifier for secondary ID code of provider.


###### Syntax


ECSecondaryIDType (Value)


###### Parameters


Value: required; Type - string;


###### Returns


string with short name of qualifier.

<a id="ecstate"></a>

##### ECState

> 📄 Source: [ECState.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECState.htm)

###### ECState

 ECStateCodeCheck


This function verifies that the m_strValue parameter of Node is a valid State code from external code list 22, based on the Qualifier value representing country:

- ‘CA’ - Canada
- ‘US’ or empty - USA


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECStateCodeCheck (Node, Qualifier, ErrorKey, Severity)


###### Parameters


Node - Required; Type - [EC_ConditionChecker](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EC_ConditionChecker_Object.htm)


Qualifier - Required; Type - string


ErrorKey - Optional; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


true: value is valid code


false: value is not valid or cannot be verified





 ECStateCodeCheck2


This function verifies that the value of bookmark NodeRef is a valid State code from external code list 22, based on the  value of current element representing country:

- ‘CA’ - Canada
- ‘US’ or empty - USA


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECStateCodeCheck2 (NodeRef, ErrorID, Severity)


###### Parameters


NodeRef - Required; Type - string


ErrorID - Optional; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


N/A





 ECStateCodeUsage


This function is used within the N4 segment. State is required when specified city is within the USA or Canada. State is defined by StateBM bookmark, city by CityBM bookmark. Country is the value of the current element. If state is missing, an error is reported on StateBM node with provided Severity.


Note Added in 6.0.2 release of HIPAA.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECStateCodeUsage(CityBM,StateBM, Severity)


###### Parameters


CityBM - Required; Type - string


StateBM - Required; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


N/A





 ECStateCodeUsageCC


This function is used within the N4 segment. State is required when the specified City is within the USA or Canada. State is defined by StateCC, city by bCity. Country is the value of the current element. If state is missing, an error is reported on ErrorKey node with provided Severity.


Note Added in 5.2.21/6.0.2 release of HIPAA. It is no longer used, but is provided for historical purposes.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECStateCodeUsageCC (bcity, StateCC, ErrorKey, Severity)


###### Parameters


bCity - Required; Type - boolean


StateCC - Required; Type - EC_ConditionChecker


ErrorKey - Required; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


N/A





 ECStateUse


Use this method to verify if value of current element is one of: - ‘CA’ - Canada - ‘US’ or empty - USA and Bookmark is not used, error is reported with provided Severity. Otherwise if value of current element is different from specified ones and Bookmark is used, error is reported with provided Severity.  


###### Syntax


ECStateUse (Bookmark, Severity)


###### Parameters


Bookmark: required; Type - string


Severity: optional; Type - string

##### ECSubdivisionUse

> 📄 Source: [ECSubdivisionUse.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECSubdivisionUse.htm)

###### ECSubdivisionUse


Use this method to verify if value of Bookmark is one of: - ‘CA’ - Canada - ‘US’ or empty - USA and current element is used, error is reported with provided Severity.  


###### Syntax


ECSubdivisionUse (Bookmark, Severity)

Bookmark : required; Type - string
