<a id="ectrdatecompare"></a>

##### ECTrDateCompare

> 📄 Source: [ECTrDateCompare.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECTrDateCompare.htm)

###### ECTrDateCompare


This function verifies that the value of the current element (CCYMMDD) is on or prior to Transaction Creation Date.


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function  ECTrDateCompare (Name, Severity, Indicator)


###### Parameters


Name - Required; Type - string  Used in error reporting to make clear which date (for example, Procedure Date or Admission Date) is in error.


Severity - Optional; Type - string


Indicator - Optional, Value - ‘SE’ or ‘E’  Used to indicate whether the function is checking a sub-element or an element.


###### Returns


true: value complies with requirements


false: value does not comply with requirements

---

<a id="ecverify"></a>

##### ECVerify

> 📄 Source: [ECVerify.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECVerify.htm)

###### ECVerify

 ECVerifyAPID


Use this method to recognize an atypical provider. Value is verified to be a valid code of specified PayerID and IDType from the user defined external code list that has legacy IDs of atypical providers registered by payer (Name=‘APID’), Star value (‘*’) may be used as PayerID to show applicability to all payers.


###### Syntax


function ECVerifyAPID (Value, PayerID, IDType)


###### Parameters:


Value: required; Type - string;


PayerID: required; Type - string;


IDType: required; Type - string;


###### Returns:


true: value is valid code


false: value is not valid or cannot be verified





 ECVerifyComNumber


This function is used to verify Communication number format in PER03/04,PER05/06, PER07/08.


The value of current element is verified to be formatted according to Qualifier type:

- ‘TE’, ‘FX’, ‘WP’, ‘HP’: phone number
- ‘EM’: email
- ‘EX’: phone number extension


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECVerifyComNumber (Qualifier, Severity)


###### Parameters


Qualifier - Required; Type - [EC_ConditionChecker](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EC_ConditionChecker_Object.htm)


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


true: value complies with requirements


false: value does not comply with requirements





 ECVerifyComNumberBM


This function is used to verify Communication number format in PER03/04,PER05/06, PER07/08.


Value of current element is verified to be formatted according to Bookmark type:

- ‘TE’, ‘FX’, ‘WP’, ‘HP’ : phone number
- ‘EM’: email
- ‘EX’: phone number extension


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECVerifyComNumberBM (Bookmark, Severity)


###### Parameters


Bookmark - Required; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


true: value complies with requirements


false: value does not comply with requirements





 ECVerifyDateFormat


Use this method to verify if century of Date is not one of 18, 19 or 20, error with provided Severity is reported with message with default or provided ErrorID error ID.  


###### Syntax


ECVerifyDateFormat (Date, Name, ErrorID, Severity)


###### Parameters


Date: required; Type - string


Name: required; Type - string


ErrorID: required; Type - string


Severity: optional; Type - string





 ECVerifyFormatHIN


This function verifies that the m_strValue parameter of Node is formatted as valid HIN code with 7th check digit.


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECVerifyFormatHIN (Node, ErrorMsg, Severity)


###### Parameters


Node - Required; Type - [EC_ConditionChecker](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EC_ConditionChecker_Object.htm)


ErrorMsg - Required; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


true: value complies with requirements


false: value does not comply with requirements





 ECVerifyFormatHIN2


This function verifies that the value of the current element is formatted as a valid HIN code with 7th check digit.


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECVerifyFormatHIN2 (ErrorID, Severity)


###### Parameters


ErrorID - Optional; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


true: value complies with requirements


false: value does not comply with requirements





 ECVerifyFormatUPC


This function verifies that the m_strValue parameter of Node is formatted as a valid UPC code with length of 12-14 and last check digit.


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECVerifyFormatUPC (Node, ErrorMsg, Severity)


###### Parameters


Node - Required; Type - [EC_ConditionChecker](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EC_ConditionChecker_Object.htm)


ErrorMsg - Required; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


true: value complies with requirements


false: value does not comply with requirements





 ECVerifyFormatUPC2


This function verifies that the value of the current element is formatted as a valid UPC code with length of 12-14 and last check digit.


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECVerifyFormatUPC2 (ErrorID, Severity)


###### Parameters


ErrorID - Optional; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


true: value complies with requirements


false: value does not comply with requirements





 ECVerifyLanguage


This function verifies that the value of the current element is a valid language code based on Qualifier type:

- ‘LD’: from external code set 457
- ‘LE’: from external code set 102


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECVerifyLanguage (Qualifier, CurNodeID, Severity)


###### Parameters


Qualifier - Required; Type - [EC_ConditionChecker](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EC_ConditionChecker_Object.htm)


CurNodeID - Required; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


true: value complies with requirements


false: value does not comply with requirements





 ECVerifyLanguage2


This function verifies that the value of the current element is a valid language code based on QualifierBM type:

- ‘LD’: from external code set 457
- ‘LE’: from external code set 102


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECVerifyLanguage2 (QualifierBM, Severity)


###### Parameters


QualifierBM - Required; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


N/A





 ECVerifyPatientIDUsage


This function verifies the validity of Patient ID usage by comparing the current element value and the ExpValue after availability date (ec_strPatientIDAvailableDate):

- Type = 0 : used at sender's discretion
- Type = 1 : required when mandated (ec_strPatientIDComplianceDate)


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECVerifyPatientIDUsage (ExpValue, Type, Severity)


###### Parameters


ExpValue - Required; Type - string


Type - Required: Value - 0, 1


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


N/A





 ECVerifyPlanIDUsage


This function verifies validity of PlanID usage by comparing the current element value and the ExpValue after availability date (ec_strPlanIDAvailableDate):

- Type = 0 : used at sender's discretion
- Type = 1 : required when mandated (ec_strPlanIDComplianceDate)


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECVerifyPlanIDUsage (Type, Severity)


###### Parameters


Type - Required, Value - 0, 1


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


N/A





 ECVerifyPrimSecID


This function is used to verify that primary ID (Bookmark) and Secondary ID (current element) do not duplicate each other.


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECVerifyPrimSecID (Bookmark, Severity)


###### Parameters


Bookmark - Required; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


N/A





 ECVerifyPrimSecIDCC


This function verifies that primary ID (Qualifier) and Secondary ID (current element) do not duplicate each other.


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECVerifyPrimSecIDCC (Qualifier, Severity)


###### Parameters


Qualifier - Required; Type - [EC_ConditionChecker](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EC_ConditionChecker_Object.htm)


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


N/A





 ECVerifyProviderIDUsage


This function verifies validity of NPI usage by comparing the current element value and the ExpValue after availability date (ec_strProviderIDAvailableDate):

- Type = 0 : used at sender's discretion
- Type = 1 : required when mandated (ec_strProviderIDComplianceDate)
- Type = 2 : may not be used after mandated (ec_strProviderIDComplianceDate)


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECVerifyProviderIDUsage (ExpValue, Type, Severity)


###### Parameters


ExpValue - Required; Type - string


Type - Required: Value - 0, 1, 2


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


N/A





 ECVerifyProviderIDUsageBM


Use this method to verify validity of NPI usage by comparison of Bookmark element value and ExpValue after availability date (ec_strProviderIDAvailableDate):


Type = 0 : used at sender's discretion


Type = 1 : required when mandated (ec_strProviderIDComplianceDate)


Type = 2 : may not be used after mandated (ec_strProviderIDComplianceDate)


If value does not meet the requirements, error is reported on Bookmark node with provided Severity.  


###### Syntax


ECVerifyProviderIDUsageBM (Bookmark, ExpValue, Type, Severity)


###### Parameters


Bookmark: required; Type - string


ExpValue: required; Type - string


Type: required ; value - 0, 1, 2


Severity: optional; Type - string





 ECVerifySecondaryID


This function is used to verify Secondary ID sent in REF01/02.


The function verifies that the value of the current element is a valid ID of the type defined by Qualifier:

- ‘EI’: EIN
- ’SY’: SSN
- ‘TJ’: Federal Tax ID
- ‘1G’: UPIN
- ‘HPI’: National Provider ID
- ‘NF’: NAIC
- ‘X4’: CLIA Number
- ‘HI’: HIN
- ‘D3’: National Association of Boards of Pharmacy Number
- ‘ML’: Military Rank/Civilian Pay Grade Number


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECVerifySecondaryID (Qualifier, EntityName, Severity)


###### Parameters


Qualifier - Required; Type - [EC_ConditionChecker](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EC_ConditionChecker_Object.htm)


EntityName - Optional; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


true: value complies with requirements


false: value does not comply with requirements





 ECVerifySecondaryID2


This function is used to verify Secondary ID sent in REF01/02.


The function verifies that the value of the current element is a valid ID of the type defined by Bookmark:

- ‘EI’: EIN
- ’SY’: SSN
- ‘TJ’: Federal Tax ID
- ‘1G’: UPIN
- ‘HPI’: National Provider ID
- ‘NF’: NAIC
- ‘X4’: CLIA Number
- ‘HI’: HIN
- ‘D3’: National Association of Boards of Pharmacy Number
- ‘ML’: Military Rank/Civilian Pay Grade Number
- ‘DH’, ‘P2’, ‘QD’: Drug Enforcement Administration Number
- ‘APC’: Ambulatory Payment Classification Number


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECVerifySecondaryID2 (Bookmark, EntityName, Severity)


###### Parameters


Bookmark - Required; Type - string


EntityName - Optional; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


true: value complies with requirements


false: value does not comply with requirements

<a id="ecverifyvalue"></a>

##### ECVerifyValue

> 📄 Source: [ECVerifyValue.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECVerifyValue.htm)

###### ECVerifyValue

 ECVerifyValue2


This function verifies that the value of the current node is valid from the specified external code list with ID of CodeListID. If the value is not valid, an error is reported with message specified by case ID equal to ErrorID and Severity. 


###### Syntax


  function ECVerifyValue2 (Value, CodeListID, Date, ErrorID, Severity)


###### Parameters


Value - Required; Type - string


CodeListID - Required; Type - string


Date - Required; Type - string (CCYYMMDD or YYMMDD)


ErrorID - Optional; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


1: value is a valid code


0: value is not valid


-1: code list is not found for specified date


-2/-3/-4: specified date is invalid





 ECVerifyValueByCriteria2


This function verifies that the value of the current node is valid from the specified external code list with ID of CodeListID and with the criteria of CName and CValue. If the value is not valid, an error is reported with message specified by case ID equal to ErrorID and Severity. 


###### Syntax


  function ECVerifyValueByCriteria2 (Value, CodeListID, Date, CName, CValue, ErrorID, Severity)


###### Parameters


Value - Required; Type - string


CodeListID - Required; Type - string


Date- Required; Type - string (CCYYMMDD or YYMMDD)


CName, CValue - Required; Type - string 


ErrorID - Optional; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


1: value is a valid code


0: value is not valid


-1: code list is not found for specified date


-2/-3/-4: specified date is invalid





 ECVerifyValueGrace


This function verifies that the value is a valid code from the external code set specified by code listID within a grace period.


If the value is valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an informational message is reported on the ErrorKey node with the provided Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the informational message is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECVerifyValueGrace (Value, CodeListID, LDate, Indicator, ErrorKey, Severity)


###### Parameters


Value - Required; Type - string


code listID - Required; Type - string


LDate - Required; Type - string in format CCYYMMDD or YYMMDD


Indicator - Optional, Value - ‘SE’ or ‘E’


ErrorKey - Optional; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


true: value is valid code


false: value is not valid or cannot be verified





 ECVerifyValueGrace2


This function verifies that the value is a valid code from the external code set specified by code listID within a grace period.


If the value is valid, an informational message is reported with the provided Severity. If the value is not valid, an error is optionally reported with the provided ErrorID and Severity.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECVerifyValueGrace2 (Value, CodeListID, Date, Indicator, ErrorID, Severity)


###### Parameters


Value - Required; Type - string


CodeListID - Required; Type - string


Date - Required; Type - string in format CCYYMMDD or YYMMDD


Indicator - Required, Value - ‘SE’ or ‘E’


ErrorID - Required; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


1: value is valid code


0: value is not valid or cannot be verified


-1: value cannot be verified





 ECVerifyValueMECL


Use this method to check Value data value against the external CodeListID, code list date to ensure that the real (not placeholder) version of the code list is used for validation.


###### Syntax


ECVerifyValueMECL (Value, CodeListID, Date)


###### Parameters


Value - Required; Type - string


CodeListID - Required; Type - string


Date - Required; Type - string in format CCYYMMDD or YYMMDD


###### Returns


true: value is valid code, that is code list is real


false: value is not valid or cannot be verified, that is code list is a placeholder only

---

<a id="ecverifycode"></a>

##### ECVerifyCode

> 📄 Source: [ECVerifyCode.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECVerifyCode.htm)

###### ECVerifyCode

 ECVerifyCode()


This function verifies that code exists in an external code file.


###### Syntax


    function ECVerifyCode (Value, CodeListID, Date, ErrorMsg)


###### Parameters


Value - The code value to lookup.


CodeListID - code list to be searched. If multiple files for code lists are specified and they are based on date, then Date should be provided.


Date - required, type - string in format CCYYMMDD or YYMMDD


ErrorMsg - Specifies the string that is displayed on the error report in case the lookup is not successful.


###### Returns


true: value is a valid code


false: value is not valid or cannot be verified


Example


    var ErrorMsg = "Value of element N104 is incorrect. Expected value is HCFA National Plan ID." + ec_strErrorElemMsg; ECVerifyCode (ec_DataLink.m_sData, "540", ec_TrDate, ErrorMsg);





 ECVerifyCode130


Use this method to verify the value to be a valid code of specified Type from external code set 130, HCPCS Code. If value is not valid, error is reported with message provided by ErrorMsg and Severity.


###### Syntax


ECVerifyCode130 (Value, Type, Date, ErrorMsg)


###### Parameters


Value - required, type - string


Type - required, value - ‘Code’ or ‘Modifier’


Date - required, type - string in format CCYYMMDD or YYMMDD


ErrorMsg - required, type - string


###### Returns


true: value is a valid code


false: value is not valid or cannot be verified





 ECVerifyCode130BM


This function verifies that the value of Bookmark is a valid code of specified Type from the external code set 130, HCPCS Code.


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


Local codes handling is based on the value of the ec_nLocalCodesMethod variable, which is set in the User Global Declaration rule of every guideline:

- 0: no local codes are allowed
- 1: codes of Level III (starts from ‘w’-‘Z’) are allowed before compliance date specified in the User Global Declaration rule through variable ec_strComplianceDate


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECVerifyCode130BM (Bookmark, Type, Date, Indicator, ErrorKey, ErrorID, Severity)


###### Parameters


Bookmark - Required; Type - string


Type - Required, Value - ‘Code’ or ‘Modifier’


Date - Required; Type - string in format CCYYMMDD or YYMMDD


Indicator - Optional, Value - ‘SE’ or ‘E’


ErrorKey - Optional; Type - string


ErrorID - Optional; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


1: value is valid code


0: value is not valid


-1: value cannot be verified

This function verifies that the m_strValue parameter of Node is a valid code of specified Type from the external code set 130, HCPCS Code.


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


Local codes handling is based on the value of the ec_nLocalCodesMethod variable, which is set in the User Global Declaration rule of every guideline:

- 0: no local codes are allowed
- 1: codes of Level III (starts from ‘w’-‘Z’) are allowed before compliance date specified in the User Global Declaration rule through variable ec_strComplianceDate


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty

Date - Required; Type - string in format CCYYMMDD or YYMMDD


ErrorMsg - Required; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


true: value is valid code


false: value is not valid or cannot be verified

 ECVerifyCode131BM


This function verifies that the value of Bookmark is a valid code of specified Type from external code set 131 (131P or 131D), ICD-9-CM Code.


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


Codes may be verified by using a grace period set up by the ec_nGracePeriod variable in the User Global Declaration rule of every guideline.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECVerifyCode131BM (Bookmark, Date, Type, Indicator, ErrorKey, ErrorID, Severity)


###### Parameters


Bookmark - Required; Type - string


Date - Required; Type - string in format CCYYMMDD or YYMMDD


Type - Required, Value - ‘P’ for Procedure or ‘D’ for Diagnosis


Indicator - Optional, Value - ‘SE’ or ‘E’


ErrorKey - Optional; Type - string


ErrorID - Optional; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


1: value is valid code


0: value is not valid


-1: value cannot be verified

If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorMsg and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


Codes may be verified by using a grace period set up by the ec_nGracePeriod variable in the User Global Declaration rule of every guideline.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care

- X12N - Property & Casualty

 ECVerifyCode132

If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


Local codes handling is based on the value of the ec_nLocalCodesMethod variable, which is set in the User Global Declaration rule of every guideline:

- 0 : no local codes are allowed
- 1 : local codes are allowed before compliance date specified in the User Global Declaration rule through variable ec_strComplianceDate:


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty

Date - Required; Type - string in format CCYYMMDD or YYMMDD


Indicator - Optional, Value - ‘SE’ or ‘E  Used to indicate whether the function is checking a sub-element or an element


ErrorID - Optional; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


1: value is valid code


0: value is not valid


-1: value cannot be verified

If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is optionally reported on the current location/node.


Local codes handling is based on the value of the ec_nLocalCodesMethod variable, which is set in the User Global Declaration rule of every guideline:

 ECVerifyCode132CC


This function verifies that the m_strValue parameter of Node is a valid code of specified Type from external code set 132, NUBC Code.


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


Local codes handling is based on the value of the ec_nLocalCodesMethod variable, which is set in the User Global Declaration rule of every guideline:

- 0: no local codes are allowed
- 1: local codes are allowed before compliance date specified in the User Global Declaration rule through variable ec_strComplianceDate:

- Revenue : ‘0070’-‘0099’
- Span: ‘80’-‘99’, ‘X0’-‘ZZ’
- Occurrence: ‘50’-‘69’, ‘J0’-‘L9’
- Value: ‘80’-‘99’, ‘X0’-‘ZZ’
- Condition : ‘80’-‘99’, ‘X0’-‘ZZ’


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECVerifyCode132CC (Node, Type, Date, ErrorMsg, Severity)


###### Parameters


Node - Required; Type - [EC_ConditionChecker](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EC_ConditionChecker_Object.htm)


Type - Required, Value - ‘Revenue’, ‘Span’, ‘Occurrence’, ‘Value’, ‘Condition’ or  ‘UB82’


Date - Required; Type - string in format CCYYMMDD or YYMMDD


ErrorMsg - Required; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


true: value is valid code


false: value is not valid or cannot be verified





 ECVerifyCode132CCAndPlace


This function verifies that the m_strValue parameter of Node is a valid code of specified Type from external code set 132, NUBC Code.


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorMsg and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


Local codes handling is based on the value of the ec_nLocalCodesMethod variable, which is set in the User Global Declaration rule of every guideline:

- 0: no local codes are allowed
- 1: local codes are allowed before compliance date specified in the User Global Declaration rule through variable ec_strComplianceDate:
- Revenue : ‘0070’-‘0099’
- Span: ‘80’-‘99’, ‘X0’-‘ZZ’
- Occurrence: ‘50’-‘69’, ‘J0’-‘L9’
- Value: ‘80’-‘99’, ‘X0’-‘ZZ’
- Condition : ‘80’-‘99’, ‘X0’-‘ZZ’


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

 ECVerifyCode139


Use this method to verify if value of current node is from external code set 139, Claim Adjustment Reason Code, effective on date of transaction. If value is not valid, error is reported with message provided by ErrorID (or 3105 if ErrorID is missing) and Severity.


###### Syntax


ECVerifyCode139 (ErrorID, Severity)


###### Parameters


ErrorID - optional; Type - string


Severity - optional; Type - string


###### Returns


true: when date of transaction is more or equal to 1th of April 2007 and value matches the pattern ec_str139RemCodes. (Value is one of ‘16’, ‘17’, ‘96’, ‘125’, ‘A1’)


false: in other case  





 ECVerifyCode2


This function verifies that the value of the current node is a valid code from the specified external code list with ID of CodeListID. If the value is not valid, an error is reported with message specified by case ID as ErrorID and Severity.


###### Syntax


  function ECVerifyCode2 (CodeListID, Date, ErrorID, Severity)


###### Parameters


CodeListID - Required; Type - string


Date - Required; Type - string (CCYYMMDD or YYMMDD)


ErrorID - Optional; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


1: value is a valid code


0: value is not valid


-1: code list is not found for specified date


-2/-3/-4: specified date is invalid

0: value is not valid

 ECVerifyCode2CatGrace


This function verifies that the m_strValue parameter of Node is a valid code from the external code set specified by code listID, CName1, CValue1, CName2, CValue2 within a grace period.


If the value is valid, and an ErrorKey was specified, an informational message is reported on the ErrorKey node with the provided Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the informational message is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECVerifyCode2CatGrace (Node, code listID, LDate, CName1, CValue1, CName2, CValue2, ErrorKey, Severity)


###### Parameters


Node - Required; Type - [EC_ConditionChecker](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EC_ConditionChecker_Object.htm)


CodeListID - Required; Type - string


LDate - Required; Type - string in format CCYYMMDD or YYMMDD


CName1, CValue1, CName2, CValue2 - Required; Type - string


ErrorKey - Optional; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


true: value is valid code


false: value is not valid or cannot be verified





 ECVerifyCode77


Use this method to verify if Value is a valid code of specified Category and Type from external code set 77, X12 Directories: Data Element/Segment Dictionary. Type is the number of version. Now we have external code list instances with next versions (SubCategory of instance): ‘003050’, ‘003051’, ‘003060’, ‘003070’, ‘004010’, ‘004020’, ‘004040’, ‘004050’, ‘004060’, ‘005010’. If value is not valid, error is optionally reported with message provided by ErrorID and Severity.


###### Syntax


ECVerifyCode77 (Value, Category, Type, Date, ErrorID, Severity)


###### Parameters


Value: required; Type - string


Category: required, value - ‘Element’ or ‘Segment’ or ‘Composite’


Type: required; Type - string


Date: required; Type - string in format CCYYMMDD or YYMMDD


ErrorID: optional; Type - string


Severity: optional; Type - string


###### Returns


1: value is valid code


0: value is not valid


-1: value cannot be verified  





 ECVerifyCodeBM


This function verifies that the value of the node specified by the bookmark is valid from the specified external code list with ID of CodeListID. If the value is not valid, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with message specified by case ID equal to ErrorID and Severity. 


###### Syntax


  function ECVerifyCodeBM (Bookmark, CodeListID, Date, ErrorKey, ErrorID, Severity)


###### Parameters


Bookmark - required, type - string


CodeListID - Required; Type - string


Date- Required; Type - string (CCYYMMDD or YYMMDD)


ErrorKey - Optional; Type - string


ErrorID - Optional; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


1: value is a valid code


0: value is not valid


-1: code list is not found for specified date


-2/-3/-4: specified date is invalid





 ECVerifyCodeBM2CatGrace


This function verifies that the value of the currently active Bookmark object is a valid code from the external code set specified by code listID within a grace period.


If the value is valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an informational message is reported on the ErrorKey node with the provided Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the informational message is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECVerifyCodeBM2CatGrace (CodeListID, LDate, CName1, CValue1, CName2, CValue2, Indicator, ErrorKey, Severity)


###### Parameters


CodeListID - Required; Type - string


LDate - Required; Type - string in format CCYYMMDD or YYMMDD


CNamen, CValuen - Required; Type - string


Indicator - Optional, Value - ‘SE’ or ‘E’


ErrorKey - Optional; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


true: value is valid code


false: value is not valid or cannot be verified





 ECVerifyCodeBMGrace


This function verifies that the value of the currently active Bookmark object is a valid code from the external code set specified by code listID within a grace period.


If the value is valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an informational message is reported on the ErrorKey node with the provided Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the informational message is reported on the current location/node.

###### Returns

false: value is not valid or cannot be verified





 ECVerifyCodeBMCatGrace


This functions verifies that the value of the currently active Bookmark object is a valid code from the external code set specified by code listID within a grace period.


If the value is valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an informational message is reported on the ErrorKey node with the provided Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the informational message is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty

   function ECVerifyCodeBMCatGrace (CodeListID, LDate, CName1, CValue1, Indicator, ErrorKey, Severity)


###### Parameters


CodeListID - Required; Type - string


LDate - Required; Type - string in format CCYYMMDD or YYMMDD


CName1, CValue1 - Required; Type - string

ErrorKey - Optional; Type - string

 ECVerifyCodeByCriteria()


This function verifies that code exists in an external code file based on criteria. In the example shown later in the topic, the file the lookup is performed on is based on Transaction Date, and then the fact that lookup is for Currency.


###### Syntax


    function ECVerifyCodeByCriteria (Value, CodeListID, Date, CName, CValue, ErrorMsg)


###### Parameters


Value - The code value to lookup.


CodeListID - code list to be searched. If multiple files for code lists are specified and they are based on date, then Dateshould be provided. In addition to the date, additional criteria can be specified by providing the name of the criteria, and then the value for the criteria.


Date - required, type - string in format CCYYMMDD or YYMMDD.


CName, CValue - required, type - string.


ErrorMsg - Contains the string that is displayed on the error report in case the lookup is not successful.


Example


    var ErrorMsg = "Value of element CUR02 is incorrect. Expected value is Currency Code." + ec_strErrorElemMsg; ECVerifyCodeByCriteria (ec_DataLink.m_sData, "5", ec_TrDate, "Part", "Currency", ErrorMsg);





 ECVerifyCodeByCriteria2


This function verifies that the value of the current node is valid from the specified external code list with ID of CodeListID and with the criteria of CName and CValue. If the value is not valid, an error is reported with message specified by case ID equal to ErrorID and Severity.


###### Syntax


  function ECVerifyCodeByCriteria2 (CodeListID, Date, CName, CValue, ErrorID, Severity)


###### Parameters


CodeListID - Required; Type - string


Date - Required; Type - string (CCYYMMDD or YYMMDD)


CName - Required; Type - string


CValue - Required; Type - string


ErrorID - Optional; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


1: value is a valid code


0: value is not valid


-1: code list is not found for specified date


-2/-3/-4: specified date is invalid

ECVerifyCodeByCriteriaCC (Node, CodeListID, Date, CName, CValue, ErrorMsg, Severity)

CName,CValue - required, type - string

Severity - optional, type - string  


###### Return Values


true: value is a valid code


false: value is not valid or cannot be verified

<a id="eczip"></a>

##### ECZip

> 📄 Source: [ECZip.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECZip.htm)

###### ECZip

 ECZipCodeCheck


This function verifies that the m_strValue parameter of Node is a valid ZIP code based on Qualifier value representing country:

- ‘CA’ - Canada
- Format check
- ‘US’ or empty - USA
- Format check
- First 5 digits should be from external code list 51, ZIP Code


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with the provided Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECZipCodeCheck (Node, Qualifier, ErrorKey, Severity)


###### Parameters


Node - Required; Type - [EC_ConditionChecker](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EC_ConditionChecker_Object.htm)


Qualifier - Required; Type - string


ErrorKey - Optional; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


true: value is valid code


false: value is not valid or cannot be verified





 ECZipCodeCheck2


This function verifies that the value of the bookmark NodeRef is a valid ZIP code based on the value of the current element representing country:

-  ‘CA’ - Canada

1. Format check

- ‘US’ or empty - USA

1. Format check
2. First 5 digits should be from external code list 51, ZIP Code


If the value is not valid, and ErrorKey was specified, an error is reported on the ErrorKey node with a message provided by ErrorID and Severity. If the ErrorKey parameter is not provided, the error is reported on the current location/node.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function ECZipCodeCheck2 (NodeRef, ErrorID, Severity)


###### Parameters


NodeRef - Required; Type - string


ErrorID - Optional; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


N/A





 ECZipUse


Use this method to verify if value of current element is one of: - ‘CA’ - Canada - ‘US’ or empty - USA and Bookmark is not used, error is reported with provided Severity.  


###### Syntax


ECZipUse (Bookmark, Severity)


###### Parameters


Bookmark: required; Type - string


Severity: optional; Type - string

<a id="verifyhlparent"></a>

##### VerifyHLParent

> 📄 Source: [VerifyHLParent.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/VerifyHLParent.htm)

###### VerifyHLParent

 VerifyHLParent


This function verifies that HL segments are in correct parent-child relationships.


###### Syntax


    function VerifyHLParent (TransactionID, HLLevel, HLParentCode)


###### Parameters


TransactionID - Transaction ID of the message, since this function is only used in EDI X12 the ID is 856, 837, and the rest.


HLLevel - Level of the HL segment.


HLParentCode - The parent code for the HL segment.


Example


    ec_lHLLevel.VerifyHLParent ("837", ec_DataLink.m_sData, ec_strHL02);





 VerifyHLParent270


This function is used to verify an HL loop sequence defined by HL03 value : ‘20’-‘21’- ‘22’-‘23’.


If the sequence is not valid, an error is reported with the provided Severity.


This function requires the HIPAA database to be installed and the Standard property of the guideline must be set to HIPAA or one of our X12N standards:

- X12N - Health Care
- X12N - Life & Annuity
- X12N - Property & Casualty


###### Syntax


   function VerifyHLParent270 (HLLevel, HLParentID, Severity)


###### Parameters


HLLevel - Required; Type - string


HLParentID - Required; Type - string


Severity - Optional; Type - string


###### Returns


true: value complies with requirements


false: value does not comply with requirements





 VerifyHLParent271X040


Use this method to verify HL loops sequence defined by HL03 value: ‘20’-‘21’- ‘GC’- ‘22’-‘23’. If sequence is not valid, error is reported with provided Severity.

false: value does not comply with requirements

 VerifyHLParent856


This function checks the parent-child HL loop structure in an X12 856 document.


###### Syntax


    function VerifyParent856 (IHID, IPID, IHlevel)  


###### Parameters


lHID  - (HL01) Hierarchical ID Number


lPID - (HL02) Hierarchical Parent ID Number


lHlevel - (HL03) Hierarchical Level Code


  The following methods and settings are used for the function:


ec_lPatterns.Add() - This method should be used in the User Global Declaration rule to set up allowed sequences of HL levels. Each level ID code (HL03) has to be separated by "-". Format is "ID-ID….". (no space is allowed.) For example:


ec_lPatterns.Add ("S-E-O-T-P-I");


     ec_lPatterns.Add ("S-E-O-I-T-P");


     ec_lPatterns.Add ("S-O-I");


     ec_lHLevelDescription.Add()  


This method should be used in User Global Declaration rule to set up descriptions for used HL level ID codes. Description is used for creation of error messages. Format is "ID-‘description’". For example:


     ec_lHLevelDescription.Add("S-'Shipment'");


     ec_lHLevelDescription.Add("E-'Equipment'");


     ec_lHLevelDescription.Add("O-'Order'");


     ec_lHLevelDescription.Add("T-'Tare'");


     ec_lHLevelDescription.Add("P-'Pack'");


     ec_lHLevelDescription.Add("I-'Item'");


     ec_bAllowToChangePattern


This variable sets up a mode to use different HL level sequences in transaction if the value is TRUE. If value is FALSE then only one type of sequence from the predefined list can be used. Default value is FALSE.


ec_bStopIfErrorOccurs - This variable sets up a mode to stop HL level sequence validation if an error occurs if the value is TRUE (only first HL level sequence error is reported). If FALSE, all found errors are reported. Default value is FALSE.


ec_bMultipleUse - This variable sets up a mode to allow multiple subordinations within HL level sequence. One HL level can have one or more subordinate levels if the value is TRUE. If value is FALSE then only one child per level is allowed. Default value is TRUE.


ec_bPatternIntegrity - This variable sets up a mode to manage HL level sequence completion. If value is TRUE then function checks if HL level sequence is completed and matches with one in the predefined list . If it is FALSE, then it does not check for the completion of the sequence but just the hierarchy within the sequence. Default value is TRUE.


Note All changes to the default values should be performed in the User Global Declaration Script.


To set up your guideline with the variables used in this function:

1. Add the following script on HL segment of each loop (OnUserNode rule) to set up variables with empty values:  
2. Add the following script on HL01 element (OnUserNode rule) to set up lHID variable:
3. Add the following script on HL02 element (OnUserNode rule) to set up lPID variable:
4. Add the following script on HL03 element (OnUserNode rule)  to set up lHlevel variable and execute function:
5. Add the following script to the OnUserNode of the SE segment:


Depending on your requirements, you may set the variables based on other element values. In the following example, the function is used to check for pattern integrity based on the value of the BSN01 element.


An (OnUserNode rule) is added to element BSN01.


          var ec_lBSN01 = new EC_List ('00', '04', '05'); if (ec_lBSN01.Find (ec_DataLink.m_sData) > -1) { ec_bPatternIntegrity = true; } else ec_bPatternIntegrity = false;

---

<a id="java-rules-reference"></a>

#### Java Rules Reference

> 📄 Source: [Book_Java_Rules_Reference.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Java_Rules_Reference.htm)

##### Java Rules Reference


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="java-rules-overview"></a>

##### Java Rules Overview

> 📄 Source: [Java_Rules_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Java_Rules_Overview.htm)

###### Java Rules Overview


Users can now use external Java classes to implement their own business rules. While in-line JavaScript rules are comprehensive and easy to implement, sometimes external Java classes are more beneficial. Some of the benefits of using external Java classes are listed later:

- Performance: Java class execution is faster than the same rule executed through JavaScript.
- Re-Use: Java classes are external to any guideline, so if the same rule needs to be executed in several guidelines, then Java rules can be written once and re-used multiple times. Additionally, the same Java class can be re-used in multiple applications.
- Database Connectivity and Connection Pooling: In JavaScript, the connection to the database is only provided through an ODBC connection that requires, on non-Windows platforms, a third party database driver to be purchased. With Java classes, a JDBC connection is available by default so there is no need to purchase any third-party database drivers. Additionally, with Java classes you can have a static class that can be shared between multiple executions so the database connections can be shared across executions instead of each execution creating a new connection to the database. You can also cache results of queries which are done often, further minimizing the database connection usage.

---

<a id="integrate-custom-java-classes"></a>

##### Integrate Custom Java Classes

> 📄 Source: [Integrate_Custom_Java_Classes.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Integrate_Custom_Java_Classes.htm)

###### Integrate Custom Java Classes


Note This application does not provide an IDE (Integrated Development Environment) for creating Java classes. Java classes must be created and compiled by using an external development tool such as Eclipse or created with a simple text editor and compiled by using the JDK run-time engine.


The purpose of this section is to describe how to integrate your custom Java classes for use with the validation engine. Please refer to external resources for instructions on writing and compiling Java classes.


To integrate your Java classes with the Run-Time Engine:

- Your class must implement a specific interface (XEJABase) to be accessible by the run-time engine.
- To implement this interface, your Java class code should look like this:
- You need to import three classes as shown earlier. These classes are located in two JAR files (jaction.jar and xengine.jar) that can be found in the product installation folder \Java folder.
- Your Java class can have as many private and public methods as you need to meet your business needs and it can also refer to other Java classes and methods.
- All methods that match the following types of signatures are available for use during guideline rules creation:
- If you do not wish a method to be available for use during rule creation, either the method signature must be different than those shown earlier or it should be moved to a different Java class.

---

<a id="declare-a-java-class"></a>

##### Declare a Java Class

> 📄 Source: [Declare_a_Java_Class.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Declare_a_Java_Class.htm)

###### Declare a Java Class


Once you have created your Java class, you need to declare it so that it can be used for validating business rules in the guidelines.


To declare Java class:

1. Configure your system CLASSPATH, Java Extension folder, directly registered class folders, or JAR files in the [Java/.NET Workspace Editor](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Reference_Custom_JAR_and_NET_DLL_Files_or_Java_and_NET_Classes.htm) to reference your Java class.
2. Open the guideline where you use the class and select the top level guideline node.
3. Click the Rules pane, and then click New Rule ().
4. Click Java Classes Declaration.
5. In the Java Classes Declaration pane, click New.
6. Complete the following fields:
7. Click OK.


Note Some JAR files can contain more than one class.

---

<a id="java-action-automatic-mapping"></a>

##### Java Action Automatic Mapping

> 📄 Source: [Java_Action_Automatic_Mapping.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Java_Action_Automatic_Mapping.htm)

###### Java Action Automatic Mapping


When declaring a new Java class, you may want to apply the class methods to the nodes in the guideline. This is useful when you have one class applied to several guidelines or you want to update the standards you have been using. Mapping is performed based on either comments in the source code or handler names.


To apply mapping:

1. In the [New Java Class Declaration](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Declare_a_Java_Class.htm#NewJavaClassDeclaration) dialog box, click Apply mapping automatically.
2. Click OK. The Automatic mapping wizard wizard page is displayed.


Note Auto-mapping is only available if the path to the source of the declared class is provided. If the source file is present, the Source tab is available in the Java Action dialog box after you declare the class.


*[Image: OnUserEndNode Java Action]*


If the source file is not provided, use the Java/.NET Workspace Editor to add a path to the source file. For more information, see [Reference Custom JAR and .NET DLL Files or Java and .NET Classes](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Reference_Custom_JAR_and_NET_DLL_Files_or_Java_and_NET_Classes.htm).

---

<a id="debug-java-classes"></a>

##### Debug Java Classes

> 📄 Source: [Debug_Java_Classes.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Debug_Java_Classes.htm)

###### Debug Java Classes


There are two possible ways of debugging Java classes - Simple and Advanced.


###### Simple debugging


Sometimes, for debugging purposes, it is helpful to be able to put system output in the Java code so that the value of a variable can be confirmed to determine if a section of code is indeed getting executed.


To configure simple debugging:

1. Modify your Java code, adding the necessary System.out.println statements.
2. On the View tab, click Modify View Layout.
3. Click Console.
4. In the Location column, set the option to Output Window.


When the Analyzer is executed, all output from System.out.println statements is displayed on the Console tab.


###### Advanced debugging


The advanced method of debugging Java Action code involves the use of SpecBuilder and the Eclipse IDE.


To configure advanced debugging:

1. Add the name and location of the JAR files containing your Java Action code to the CLASSPATH environment variable.
2. Add the name and location of the jaextensions.jar file to the CLASSPATH environment variable.
3. Ensure that the value JAVA_HOME environment variable contains the location of your Java installation.
4. Open Windows Registry Editor.
5. Navigate to the *HKEY_CURRENT_USER\SOFTWARE\EDIFECS\XESubsystem\Settings* and create the *JavaStartupParameters* multi-string value data with the following key path:
6. Run SpecBuilder and launch the Analyser Wizard.
7. Run the Eclipse IDE and open the source file that contains your Java Action code. Refer to the Eclipse online documentation for instructions.
8. Using Eclipse, set your breakpoints in the Java Action code.
9. Configure Eclipse to create a new Remote Java Application and set the port number in the Connection Properties dialog box to the value of the "address" parameter in your *JavaStartupParameters* value. In the case where the code example used in Step 5 has been added to the *JavaStartupParameters* value, this parameter would be "8007".
10. Click Debug, to start debugging process.
11. Run a validation on the guideline file. SpecBuilder stops during the data analysis process. Verify that SpecBuilder has stopped by running the Windows Task Manager and checking that the CPU load of the SpecBuilder.exe process is at "00".
12. Proceed to debug the application in Eclipse. If you need to re-run the Java Action code, re-run the validation in SpecBuilder.


Note If the suspend property in the *JavaStartupParameters* value is set to "y", then after SpecBuilder starts, it hangs until the Eclipse remote Java application debugger is started.

---

<a id="java-class-methods"></a>

##### Java Class Methods

> 📄 Source: [Java_Class_Methods.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Java_Class_Methods.htm)

###### Java Class Methods


Once the Java class has been registered in the guideline, the Java class methods can be used for business rules validation.

1. Select the node (such as Transaction, Loop, Segment, Element, and the rest) on which you wish to execute the Java class method.
2. Click the Rules pane.
3. Click New Rule () to insert a new rule.
4. Select either OnUserNode Java or OnUserEndNode Java rules. An OnUserNode Java rule is used when a method should be executed when a node is encountered in the data. An OnUserEndNode Java rule is used after all child nodes have been processed. For those nodes without child nodes, the OnUserEndNode Java rule is not available.
5. Click OK.
6. In the Java Action dialog box, select a Java class from the Java Class Name list or click Add to declare a new Java class. See [Declare a Java Class](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Declare_a_Java_Class.htm) for instructions.
7. In the Java Action dialog box, enter the following information:
8. Click Apply.

<a id="pre-defined-objects"></a>

##### Pre-Defined Objects

> 📄 Source: [Book_PreDefined_Objects_Java.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_PreDefined_Objects_Java.htm)

###### Pre-Defined Objects


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="pre-defined-java-objects-overview"></a>

##### Pre-Defined Java Objects Overview

> 📄 Source: [Pre_Defined_Java_Objects_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Pre_Defined_Java_Objects_Overview.htm)

###### Pre-Defined Java Objects Overview


When writing your Java code and implementing the XEJABase class, certain information is passed directly to the Java object when the method is invoked. Other objects are provided as part of the XEJABase class implementation and may be useful in your Java class creation. This section documents the pre-defined objects available in the XEJABase class.


###### Direct Parameters


Three parameters are passed when XEngine invokes your Java class method:


Parameter 1 (String): Data value from the data file.


Parameter 2 (Long): Instance ID of the guideline node.


Parameter 3 (String): Custom parameter as a text string - any additional values you may need during the method invocation.


###### Pre-Defined Method Groups


Following is a list of pre-defined method groups that are available for use within your XEJABase-derived Java class:


| Method Group | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [Global](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Global_method_group.htm) | Provides access to data node properties. |
| [Bookmark](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Bookmark_Method_Group.htm) | Allows you to bookmark a node so that it can be accessed later. |
| [Data Access](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Access_Method_Group.htm) | Provides access to data node properties. |
| [getDataNode](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/GetDataNode_Method.htm) | Provides access to the XEDataNode Java interface. |
| [External Codelist](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_External_Code_List_Method_Group.htm) | Allows you to check data against External Codelists, which are installed as a standalone package. |



###### Pre-Defined Objects


Following is a list of pre-defined objects that are available for use within your Java code:


| Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [ECError](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECError.htm) | Allows Java class to create an error to be reported during validation. |
| [XEUserException](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/XEUserException.htm) | Allows Java class to raise an exception. |

---

 getId()

Returns -1.





 getName()

 getCacheType()

 getType()
