<a id="translator-java-object-ixecrosswalkresult"></a>

##### Translator Java Object IXECrosswalkResult

> 📄 Source: [Translator_Java_IXECrosswalkResult.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Java_IXECrosswalkResult.htm)

###### Translator Java IXECrosswalkResult


The IXECrosswalkResult object holds the record set of values returned by the IXECrosswalkEngine matching methods. Use the IXECrosswalkResult methods to work with the results.

 getMatchedKeysCount()


This method is used to get the count of the matching keys.


###### Syntax


int getMatchedKeysCount()


###### Return Value


Returns the count of matching keys.





 getAll()


This method is used to get all results in a form of a List<String[]>.


###### Syntax


List<String[]> getAll()


###### Return Value


All result in form of List<String[]>.





 getFirst()


This method is used to get the first element of the first row.


###### Syntax


String getFirst()


###### Return Value


Returns the first element of the first row.





 getLast()


This method is used to get the last element of the first row.


###### Syntax


String getLast()


###### Return Value


Returns the last element of the first row.





 select(String[])


This method is used clarify result by applying provided values.


###### Syntax


IXECrosswalkResult select(String[] values)


###### Parameters


values: Values to compare with the result values.


###### Return Value


Returns clarified result by its first value.


Example


// This code snippet demonstrates how to use IXECrosswalkResult in your code.


// get all results


List<String[]> result = res.getAll();


System.out.println("first result:");


 


// output result


for(int i = 0; i < result.size(); i++)


{


System.out.println(result.get(i)[0] + " " + result.get(i)[1] + " " + result.get(i)[2]);


}


System.out.println("Matched keys count = " + res.getMatchedKeysCount());

---

<a id="translator-java-object-ixecrosswalkengine"></a>

##### Translator Java Object IXECrosswalkEngine

> 📄 Source: [Translator_Java_IXECrosswalkEngine.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Java_IXECrosswalkEngine.htm)

###### Translator Java IXECrosswalkEngine


Note This interface and the methods contained are also applicable for Validator actions.


Use the IXECrosswalkEngine object for Crosswalk lookups. The object represents one or a collection of crosswalk files that match the specified parameters (name, TP name, and criteria).

 match(String[], int)


This method does a lookup for an array of keys. The result is a record set of values from a crosswalk file that best matches the specified parameters. Returns the nearest result, even if not all keys are a match. The result should be valid for the specified date. The format of the date is yyyymmdd. For example, 19990512 is 05/12/1999.


The index of an element in the keys array corresponds to the index of a column in the source table. The value of the element in the keys array corresponds to the value in a table cell. For example, the call keys[1]=’abc’ returns a target record that matches the source record which contains ‘abc’ in the second column.


###### Syntax


IXECrosswalkResult match(String[] keys, int date)


###### Parameters

- keys: The source values.
- date: The date in the format yyyymmdd.


###### Return Value


Returns one IXECrosswalkResult object that represents a record set of values from a crosswalk file that best matches the specified parameters.


Example


// This code snippet demonstrates how to use IXECrosswalkEngine in your code.


// get crosswalk engine by the crosswalk name, Trading Partner name, and criteria


Map<String, String> criteria = new HashMap<>();


criteria.put("criterionName1", "criterionValue1");


IXECrosswalkEngine engine = registry.getCrosswalkEngine("crosswalkName", "TPName", criteria);


if (engine != null)


{


// print debug out information about crosswalk


engine.debugOut();


 


// prepare keys for lookup


String[] vtrKeys = {"0001", "9-to-10", "pcs"};


 


// do match with keys and date 02/08/2001


IXECrosswalkResult res = engine.match(vtrKeys, 20010208);


}


else


{


System.out.println("Crosswalk engine was not found for specified parameters");


}





 match(String[], String[], int)


**Syntax**


IXECrosswalkResult match(String[] keyNames, String[] keyValues, int date)


###### Parameters

- KeyNames: The names of the required source crosswalk columns.
- KeyValues: The source values for columns, which are defined by the KeyNames parameter.
- date: The date in the format yyyymmdd.


###### Return Value


Returns one IXECrosswalkResult object that represents a record set of values from a crosswalk file that best matches the specified parameters.





 fullMatch(String[], int)


This method does a lookup for an array of keys. Returns a result that matches all of the keys. The result should be valid for the specified date. The format of the date is yyyymmdd. For example, 19990512 is 05/12/1999.


The index of an element in the keys array corresponds to the index of a column in the source table. The value of the element in the keys array corresponds to the value in a table cell. For example, the call keys[1]=’abc’ returns a target record that matches the source record which contains ‘abc’ in the second column.


###### Syntax


IXECrosswalkResult fullMatch(String[] keys, int date)


###### Parameters

- keys: The source values.
- date: The date in the format yyyymmdd.


###### Return Value


Returns one IXECrosswalkResult object that represents a record set of values from a crosswalk file that best matches the specified parameters.


Example


IXECrosswalkResult result = engine.fullMatch(new String[] { "source code 1" }, -1);





 fullMatch(String[], String[], int)


This method does a lookup for an array of keys. Returns result matching all of the keys. The result should be valid for the specified date. The format of the date is yyyymmdd. For example, 19990512 is 05/12/1999.


The index of an element in the keys array corresponds to the index of a column in the source table. The value of the element in the keys array corresponds to the value in a table cell. For example, the call keys[1]=’abc’ returns a target record that matches the source record which contains ‘abc’ in the second column.


###### Syntax


IXECrosswalkResult fullMatch(String[] keyNames, String[] keyValues, int date)


###### Parameters

- KeyNames: The names of the required source crosswalk columns.
- KeyValues: The source values for columns, which are defined by the KeyNames parameter.
- date: The date in the format yyyymmdd.


###### Return Value


Returns one IXECrosswalkResult object that represents a record set of values from a crosswalk file that best matches the specified parameters.





 getTargets()


###### Syntax


List<String> getTargets()


###### Return Value


Returns a collection of names of target columns.





 debugOut()


Print all info about crosswalk into java standard output stream.


###### Syntax


void debugOut()


###### Return Value


This method does not return a value.


Example


// print debug out information about crosswalk


engine.debugOut();

---

<a id="translator-java-object-ixecrosswalkregistry"></a>

##### Translator Java Object IXECrosswalkRegistry

> 📄 Source: [Translator_Java_IXECrosswalkRegistry.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Java_IXECrosswalkRegistry.htm)

###### Translator Java IXECrosswalkRegistry


Note This interface and the methods contained are also applicable for Validator actions.


This class has the following methods:

 getCrosswalkEngine(String)


Use this method to get the Crosswalk engine by the name parameter. The method looks for user Crosswalks first, and then for system Crosswalks.


###### Syntax


IXECrosswalkEngine getCrosswalkEngine(String name)


###### Parameters

- name: the Crosswalk name


###### Return Value


Returns null or an IXECrosswalkEngine object that represents one or a collection of crosswalk files that match the specified parameters.





 getCrosswalkEngine(String, String)


Use this method to get the Crosswalk engine by the name and TPName parameters. The method looks for user Crosswalks first, and then for system Crosswalks.


###### Syntax


IXECrosswalkEngine getCrosswalkEngine(String name, String TPName)


###### Parameters

- name: the Crosswalk name
- TPName: the Trading Partner name


###### Return Value


Returns null or an IXECrosswalkEngine object that represents one or a collection of crosswalk files that match the specified parameters.





 getCrosswalkEngine(String, String, Map)


Use this method to get the Crosswalk engine by the name, TP name, and criteria parameters. The method looks for user Crosswalks first, and then for system Crosswalks.


###### Syntax


IXECrosswalkEngine getCrosswalkEngine(String name, String TPName, Map<String, String> criteria)


###### Parameters

- name: the Crosswalk name
- TPName: the Trading Partner name
- criteria: the Crosswalk criteria


###### Return Value


Returns null or an IXECrosswalkEngine object that represents one or a collection of crosswalk files that match the specified parameters.


Example


IXECrosswalkRegistry xwalkRegistry = ctx.getCrosswalkRegistry();


Map<String, String> criteria = new HashMap<String, String>();


criteria.put("purpose", "test");


IXECrosswalkEngine engine = xwalkRegistry.getCrosswalkEngine("test", "test tp", criteria);





 getCrosswalkEngine(XECrosswalkRef)


Use this method to get the Crosswalk engine by the name, TPName, and criteria from Crosswalk reference. The method looks for user Crosswalks first, and then for system Crosswalks.


Note The method is not supported in future releases. We recommend that you use the method getCrosswalkEngine(String, String, Map) instead.


###### Syntax


IXECrosswalkEngine getCrosswalkEngine(XECrosswalkRef crosswalkRef)


###### Parameters


crosswalkRef: the Crosswalk reference


###### Return Value


Returns IXECrosswalkEngine by name, TPName, and criteria from XECrosswalkRef object.


Example


// This code snippet demonstrates how to get crosswalk engine with crosswalk ref


IXECrosswalkEngine engine = registry.getCrosswalkEngine(ref);





 createRef()


Use this method to create mutable Crosswalk reference.


NoteThe method is not supported in future releases.


###### Syntax


XECrosswalkRefMutable createRef()


###### Return Value


Returns a new clean XECrosswalkRefMutable.





 getCrosswalkRefs(String, String, Map)


Use this method to get a collection of XECrosswalkRef objects by the name, TPname, and criteria parameters. When the method does not find a Crosswalk, returns an empty collection.


NoteThe method is not supported in future releases.


###### Syntax


List<XECrosswalkRef> getCrosswalkRefs(String name, String TPName, Map<String, String> criteria)


###### Parameters

- name: the Crosswalk name
- TPName: the Trading Partner name
- criteria: the Crosswalk criteria


###### Return Value


Returns a collection of XECrosswalkRef objects that match the specified parameters.





 getCrosswalks(String, String, Map)


Use this method to get a collection with one IXECrosswalk object that represents one or more crosswalk files that match the specified name, TPName, and criteria parameters. When the method does not find a Crosswalk, returns an empty collection.


Note The method is not supported in future releases. We recommend that you use the method getCrosswalkEngine(String, String, Map) instead.


###### Syntax


List<IXECrosswalk> getCrosswalks(String name, String TPName, Map<String, String> criteria)


###### Parameters

- name: the Crosswalk name
- TPName: the Trading Partner name
- criteria: the Crosswalk criteria


###### Return Value


The method returns an empty collection or a collection with one IXECrosswalk object that represents one or more crosswalk files that match the specified parameters.


Example


// This code snippet demonstrates how to get a crosswalk by the crosswalk name, Trading Partner name, and criteria


Map<String, String> criteria = new HashMap<>();


criteria.put("criterionName1", "criterionValue1");


criteria.put("criterionName2", "criterionValue2");


List<IXECrosswalk> crosswalks = registry.getCrosswalks("crosswalkName", "TPName", criteria);





 flush()


This method flushes out the changes for all IXECrosswalk objects.


NoteThe method is not supported in future releases.


###### Syntax


void flush()


###### Return Value


This method does not return a value.






Note The following methods are no longer supported: getCrosswalk(int), getCrosswalk(String), getCrosswalkRef(int), getCrosswalkRef(String), registerCrosswalk(XECrosswalkRefMutable), registerCrosswalk(XECrosswalkRefMutable, boolean), registerCrosswalk(XECrosswalkRefMutable, String, String), unregisterCrosswalk(int), updateCrosswalk(XECrosswalkRef).

---

<a id="global-method-group"></a>

##### Global Method Group

> 📄 Source: [Global_method_group.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Global_method_group.htm)

###### Global Method Group


This group provides access to various global properties.


###### Methods:


###### public String getGlobalPropEx(String sPropName) throws XEException


Use this method to search through all parent data nodes starting from the current and return a property value. Returns null if the property is not found. You can use this method to determine data file sender, receiver ids, and the rest.


###### public String getGlobalLinkEx(String sLinkName) throws XEException


Use this method to search through all parent data nodes starting from the current and return a link value. Returns null if link is not found. You can use this method to determine data file sender, receiver ids, and the rest.


###### public String setGlobalProp(String sName, String sValue) throws XEException


Use this method to add a user property to the current data node.


###### public void setStopExec() throws XEException


Use this method to raise the stop validation flag. Validation stops from the next link event.


###### public boolean isStopExec() throws XEException


Use this method to check whether the stop validation flag is set. It returns true if the stop validation stop flag is set; otherwise, it returns false.


###### public long getGlobalErrorCount() throws XEException


Use this method to return the total number of errors that have occurred so far in the processing.


###### public String getEngineType() throws XEException


Use this method to return the "validator" string.


###### public String getGlobalLink(String sLinkName) throws XEException


Use this method to return the value of the current data node's link.


###### public long getGlobalRErrorCount() throws XEException


Use this method to return the total number of rejectable errors that have occurred so far in the processing.


###### public String getBookmarkIID() throws XEException


Use this method to return the IID of the guideline node from the current bookmark.


###### public int getDataOffset() throws XEException


Use this method to return the offset of the current data token.


###### public int getDataSize() throws XEException


Use this method to return the size of the current data token.


###### public int getFindBMMaxUse(String sBMName) throws XEException


Use this method to return the "max use" value from the requested bookmark.


###### public String getFindBMData(String sBMName) throws XEException


Use this method to return data from the requested bookmark.

---

<a id="bookmark-method-group"></a>

##### Bookmark Method Group

> 📄 Source: [Bookmark_Method_Group.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Bookmark_Method_Group.htm)

###### Bookmark Method Group


This group operates on bookmarks on a node.


###### Methods:


###### public void addBookmark(String sBMName) throws XEException


Use this method to add a bookmark named sBMName into the container.  The name should be unique.


###### public void removeBookmark(String sBMName)  throws XEException


Use this method to remove the bookmark named sBMName from the container.


###### public boolean findBookmark(String sBMName)  throws XEException


Use this method to search for a bookmark named sBMName in the container. If the bookmark is found, it is marked as selected. Further requests for bookmark information are addressed to the selected bookmark.


###### public String getBookmarkData()  throws XEException


Use this method to return a data token from the current bookmark.


###### public String getBookmarkID()  throws XEException


Use this method to return the node ID from the current bookmark.


###### public String getBookmarkName()  throws XEException


Use this method to return the name of the selected bookmark.


###### public String getBookmarkPos() throws XEException


Use this method to return the guideline node position associated with the current bookmark.


###### public boolean isBookmarkData() throws XEException


Use this method to check for the presence of data on the current bookmark. Returns true if present; otherwise, it returns false.


###### public Map getBookmarkParams(String sBMName, int nOperation, String sSubBMName) throws XEException


Use this method to return bookmark parameters for the specified operation. The real name of the bookmark can be substituted in the parameters with sSubBMName value.


###### Method Parameters:


###### sBMName -


mandatory bookmark name


###### nOperation -


mandatory parameter that specifies the required error parameters; possible values include:

- data value 0x00001 ec_[bookmark_name/substitute]_DVal

- data counter 0x00002 ec_[bookmark_name/substitute]_Counter_[level]

- guideline ID 0x00004 ec_[bookmark_name/substitute]_G_ID_[level]

- guideline Position 0x00008 ec_[bookmark_name/substitute]_G_POS_[level]

- guideline Name 0x00010 ec_[bookmark_name/substitute]_G_Name_[level]

- guideline(counter) parent 1 0x00020

- guideline(counter) parent 2 0x00040


###### sSubBMName -


optional substitute parameter. The specified parameter name is generated with the substitute value instead of the bookmark name.

##### Data Access Method Group

> 📄 Source: [Data_Access_Method_Group.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Access_Method_Group.htm)

###### Data Access Method Group


This method provides access to the current data node and other attributes associated with the current data node.


###### Methods:


###### public String getData() throws XEException


Use this method to return the data token linked with the current guideline node.


###### public long getNodeIID() throws XEException


Use this method to return the current guideline node IID.


###### public boolean isDataPresent() throws XEException


Use this method to return true if current data token is not empty; otherwise, returns false.

<a id="getdatanode-method"></a>

##### getDataNode Method

> 📄 Source: [GetDataNode_Method.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/GetDataNode_Method.htm)

###### GetDataNode Method


Users can access data nodes by using their custom Java classes. Data nodes are items such as Interchanges, Groups, Transactions, and the rest. If there is a need to get a property such as InterchangeSenderID, this can now be accessed with the custom Java class by using the getDataNode method. It returns the XEDataNode Java interface.


For more information on XEDataNode methods and properties please refer to XEngine Java Docs or the XEngine Help File.

---

<a id="external-code-list-method-group"></a>

##### External Code List Method Group

> 📄 Source: [ECL_External_Code_List_Method_Group.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_External_Code_List_Method_Group.htm)

###### External Code List Method Group


With this group, you can check data against external code lists that are installed as a standalone package.


###### Methods:


###### public int checkCodeByName(String sCodeID, String scode listName, String sDate) throws XEException


Use this method to validate a code ID in an external code list identified by name and date.


###### Parameters: 

- sCodeID -code ID;
- scode listName - code list name;
- sDate - code list date; The code list with the closest date is selected, if date is empty then the latest code list is chosen.


Returns -1 if no code list was found, 0 if no matching code in the code list was found, or 1 if a matching code was found in the code list.


###### public int checkCodeByCriteria(String sCodeID, String scode listName, String sDate, String[] vsCriteriaName, String[] vsCriteriaValue) throws XEException


Use this method to validate a code ID in an external code list identified by name, date, and criteria.


###### Parameters:

- sCodeID - code ID;
- scode listName - code list name;
- sDate - code list date; The code list with the closest date is selected, if date is empty then the latest code list is chosen;
- vsCriteriaName - an array of criteria names;
- vsCriteriaValue- an array of criteria values.


Returns -1 if no code list was found, 0 if no matching code in the code list was found, or 1 if a matching code was found in the code list.

<a id="ecerror"></a>

##### ECError

> 📄 Source: [ECError.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECError.htm)

###### ECError


This object provides access to the error object.


###### Methods:


###### public ECError(String sDescription)


Use this method to construct an error object with custom description specified.


###### public ECError(int nEID, String sDescription)


Use this method to construct an error object with event ID and custom error description specified.


###### public void addParam(String sName, String sValue)


Use this method to add an error parameter.


###### public void addParams(Map mapParams)


Use this method to put all parameters from mapParams into an error parameter container.


###### public String getParam(String sName)


Use this method to return a parameter value by name.


###### public void removeParam(String sName)


Use this method to remove a parameter by name.


###### public void clearAllParams()


Use this method to delete all error parameters.


###### public String getDescription()


Use this method to return a custom description. The return value is null if a description is not specified.


###### public void setDescription(String sDescription)


Use this method to set a custom error description.


###### public Object[] getParamNames()


Use this method to return an array filled with parameter names.


###### public Object[] getParamValues()


Use this method to return an array filled with parameter values.


###### public void setEID(int IID)


Use this method to set an event ID for reporting this error.


###### public int getEID()


Use this method to return an event ID if it was specified; otherwise, it returns zero.


###### public void setBookmark(String sBMName)


Use this method to set the bookmark name. The error is registered with the data path that the bookmark refers to. If the bookmark is not found, then an error is registered with the current data path element.


###### public String getBookmark()


Use this method to return the bookmark name.


###### public void setDefaultSeverity(int severityID)


Use this method to set a new severity for the last error event.


severityID - valid severity ID - for example, 1100, 2100, 3100


###### public void setDefaultSeverity(String severityName)


Use this method to set a new severity for the last error event.


severityName - valid severity name - for example, MyInformational1

<a id="additional-base-object-event-interfaces"></a>

##### Additional Base Object Event Interfaces

> 📄 Source: [Additional_Base_Object_Interfaces.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Additional_Base_Object_Interfaces.htm)

###### Additional Base Object Event Interfaces


These additional interfaces are provided as part of the base class implementation.


###### Methods:


###### protected void onStart() throws Exception


Use this method after the Java class object is created. It can be overridden by you in order to execute proper initialization of the custom Java class. This method has an empty default implementation.


###### protected void onFinish() throws Exception


Use this method when the Java class is about to be destroyed and can be overridden by you in order to execute proper finalization routines. This method has an empty default implementation.


###### protected boolean onError(ECError error)


Use this method when the Java class is about to report an ECError object to the validation engine. If you want to filter errors, override this method. If this method returns true, an error is reported; otherwise it is skipped. This method returns true by default (all errors are allowed to be registered with the validation engine).


###### public void reportError(ECError error) throws XEException


Use this method to report an error only if the onError() method (see earlier) returned true. The error is reported immediately.


By default, the ECError object has an event ID equal to zero. Reporting such an error results in creating an event with event ID = 0x810047. The current description of this event is:


Brief: Guideline script generated error.


Description: Can't produce detailed description. Error ID is not specified.


###### public void addEvent(int nEID) throws XEException


###### public void addEvent(int nEID, String sDescription) throws XEException


###### public void addEvent(int nEID, String sDescription, Map mapParams) throws XEException


These three variants of the addEvent method report an error with the specified event ID. The default event description is substituted with the user’s if the sDescription parameter is specified (not equal to null and not empty). If the specified event ID is zero, then an event with ID = 0x810047 is created. The event can be supplied with additional parameters by specifying parameter mapParams.


###### public void addEventByBM(String sBookmarkName, int nEID)  throws XEException


###### public void addEventByBM(String sBookmarkName, int nEID, String sDescription)  throws XEException


###### public void addEventByBM(String sBookmarkName, int nEID, String sDescription, Map mapParams)  throws XEException


These three variants of the addEventByBM method report an error with the specified event ID and bookmark name. If the bookmark is found, then the event is registered by using the data path that the bookmark refers to. Otherwise, the event is registered by using the current data path. The default event description is substituted with the user’s if the sDescription parameter is specified (not equal to null and not empty). If the specified event ID is zero, then an event with ID = 0x810047 is created (see reportError for more details). The event can be supplied with additional parameters by specifying parameter mapParams.


###### public void addEvent(ECError error) throws XEException


Use this method to report an error from a previously-created error object. Its action is similar to reportEvent(ECError) method.


###### public int getErrorCount() throws XEException


Use this method to return a total count of errors registered on the current Data Node.


###### public int getRErrorCount() throws XEException


Use this method to return a count of rejectable errors registered on the current data node.

<a id="net-rules-reference"></a>

#### .NET Rules Reference

> 📄 Source: [Book_NET_Rules_Reference.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_NET_Rules_Reference.htm)

##### .NET Rules Reference


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="declare-a-net-class"></a>

##### Declare a .NET Class

> 📄 Source: [Declare_a_NET_Class.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Declare_a_NET_Class.htm)

###### Declare a .NET Class


You must declare your .NET class before using it for programming business rules in the guidelines.


###### Prerequisites:


Prior to using a .NET class in a guideline, reference your .NET class by registering your .NET assembly. To do this, use one of the following methods:


Note The methods are listed in order of priority.

- Register your .NET assembly in the Global assembly Cache (GAC).
- Put your .NET assembly in the folder with installed SpecBuilder, where SpecBuilder.exe is located.
- Register your .NET assembly in the [Java/.NET Workspace Editor](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Reference_Custom_JAR_and_NET_DLL_Files_or_Java_and_NET_Classes.htm).
- Put your .NET assembly in the %ECRootPath%/Common/NETExtension folder.


Note To use .NET assembly classes, indicate the assembly-qualified name. It should contain the class name defined as '<full class name>, <assembly name>', and also can contain version number and culture type. 


Example: TopNamespace.SubNameSpace.MyClass, Myassembly, Version=1.3.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b17a5c561934e089


To declare a .NET class:

1. Open the guideline where you use the class and select the top level guideline node.
2. Click the Rules pane, and then click New Rule ().
3. Click .NET Classes Declaration, and then click OK.
4. In the .NET Classes Declaration pane, click New.
5. Complete the following fields:
6. Click OK.

---

<a id="pre-defined-objects"></a>

##### Pre-Defined Objects

> 📄 Source: [Book_PreDefined_Objects_NET.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_PreDefined_Objects_NET.htm)

###### Pre-Defined Objects


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="translator-net-object-xecrosswalkengine"></a>

##### Translator .NET Object XECrosswalkEngine

> 📄 Source: [Translator_NET_XECrosswalkEngine.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_NET_XECrosswalkEngine.htm)

###### Translator .NET XECrosswalkEngine


The CrosswalkEngine object is used for Crosswalk lookups.

 FullMatch(String[] keys, int date)


**Note** This method is not new but it was absent in XE 8.4 Help.


Does a lookup for an array of keys. The result is an XECrosswalkResult object. Returns the nearest result, even if not all keys were matched. Result should be valid for the passed date. The format of the date is yyyymmdd. For example, 19990512 is 05/12/1999.


###### Syntax


XECrosswalkResult FullMatch(String[] keys, int date)





 Match(String[] keys, int date)


###### Syntax


XECrosswalkResult Match(String[] keys, int date)






This code snippet demonstrates how to use CrosswalkEngine in your code.


Example



XECrosswalkEngine engine = context.GetCrosswalkRegistry().GetCrosswalkEngine(ref1);


// prepare keys for looup


String[] keys = {"0001", "9-to-10", "pcs"};


// do lookup with keys and date 02/08/2001


XECrosswalkResult result = engine.Match(keys, 20010208);

---

<a id="translator-net-object-xecrosswalkref"></a>

##### Translator .NET Object XECrosswalkRef

> 📄 Source: [Translator_NET_XECrosswalkRef.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_NET_XECrosswalkRef.htm)

###### Translator .NET XECrosswalkRef


Note The XECrosswalkRef class will not be supported in future releases.


Note This interface and the methods contained are also applicable for Validator actions.


The following table lists the members of the XECrosswalkRef object, which holds all the information for the Crosswalk.


| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| int GetId() | Returns -1. NoteThe method is not supported in future releases. |
| String GetName() | Retrieves the Crosswalk name. |
| String GetCrosswalkLink() | Retrieves a path to the Crosswalk database file. Custom crosswalks (Java classes) are no longer supported. NoteThe method is not supported in future releases. |
| uint GetType() | Retrieves the Crosswalk type. Possible value: 0 – Database. Custom crosswalks (Java classes) are no longer supported. NoteThe method is not supported in future releases. |
| uint GetCacheType() | Gets the Crosswalk type of caching. NoteThe method is not supported in future releases. |
| String[] GetTPKeys() | Gets Crosswalk TPKeys (TPNames). |
| String[][] GetCriteria() | Gets the Crosswalk criteria in the form of an array of name-value pairs. |



This code snippet demonstrates how to retrieve the XECrosswalkRef object in your code.


Example



// get crosswalk reference by the crosswalk name, Trading Partner name, and criteria


XECrosswalkRegistry xwalkRegistry = ctx.GetCrosswalkRegistry();


string[][] criteria = new string[][] { new string[] { "purpose", "test" } };


XECrosswalkRef[] refs = xwalkRegistry.GetCrosswalkRefs("crosswalkName", "TPName", criteria);


if (refs != null && refs.Length > 0)


{


XECrosswalkEngine engine = xwalkRegistry.GetCrosswalkEngine(refs[0]);


useCrosswalkEngine(engine, ctx);


}

---

<a id="translator-net-object-xecrosswalkrefcreator"></a>

> 📄 Source: [Translator_NET_XECrosswalkRefCreator.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_NET_XECrosswalkRefCreator.htm)

 Create()

XECrosswalkRefMutable Create()


###### Return Value


Returns a new clean XECrosswalkRefMutable.






Example


This code snippet demonstrates how to create a XECrosswalkRefMutable object in your code.


// create new crosswalk ref object ref1 = XECrosswalkRefCreator.Create();

 Create(XECrosswalkRef)


###### Syntax


XECrosswalkRefMutable Create(XECrosswalkRef prototype)


###### Return Value


Returns a new XECrosswalkRefMutable with the information from a prototype.

<a id="translator-net-object-xecrosswalkrefmutable"></a>

##### Translator .NET Object XECrosswalkRefMutable

> 📄 Source: [Translator_NET_XECrosswalkRefMutable.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_NET_XECrosswalkRefMutable.htm)

###### Translator .NET XECrosswalkRefMutable


Note The XECrosswalkRefMutable class will not be supported in future releases.


Note This interface and the methods contained are also applicable for Validator actions.


You can set or get details by using the *set-* methods that are available in the *XECrosswalkRefMutable* class, and *get-* methods that are available in the *XECrosswalkRef* and *XECrosswalkRefMutable* classes.


| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| void SetId(int id) | Note The method is not supported in future releases. In this release, XEngine does not use the values you provide for this method, but does not throw an exception. |
| void SetName(String name) | Sets the Crosswalk name. |
| void SetCrosswalkLink(String crosswalkLink) | Note The method is not supported in future releases. In this release, XEngine does not use the values you provide for this method, but does not throw an exception. Custom crosswalks (Java classes) are no longer supported. |
| void SetType(uint type) | Note The method is not supported in future releases. In this release, XEngine does not use the values you provide for this method, but does not throw an exception. Custom crosswalks (Java classes) are no longer supported. |
| void SetCacheType(uint cacheType) | Note The method is not supported in future releases. In this release, XEngine does not use the values you provide for this method, but does not throw an exception. |
| void SetTPKeys(String[] TPKeys) | Sets Crosswalk TPKeys (TPNames). |
| void SetCriteria(String[][] criteria) | Sets Crosswalk criteria in the form of an array of name-value pairs. |

<a id="translator-net-object-xecrosswalkregistry"></a>

##### Translator .NET Object XECrosswalkRegistry

> 📄 Source: [Translator_NET_XECrosswalkRegistry.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_NET_XECrosswalkRegistry.htm)

###### Translator .NET XECrosswalkRegistry


Note This interface and the methods contained are also applicable for Validator actions.

 GetCrosswalkEngine(String)


###### Syntax


XECrosswalkEngine GetCrosswalkEngine(String sName)


###### Parameters

- sName: the Crosswalk name


###### Return Value


Returns null or a CrosswalkEngine by Crosswalk name.





 GetCrosswalkEngine(String, String)


###### Syntax


XECrosswalkEngine GetCrosswalkEngine(String sName, String sTPName)


###### Parameters

- sName: the Crosswalk name
- sTPName: the Trading Partner name


###### Return Value


Returns null or a CrosswalkEngine object that matches the specified parameters.





 GetCrosswalkEngine(String, String, String[][])


###### Syntax


XECrosswalkEngine GetCrosswalkEngine(String sName, String sTPName, String [] [] aCriteria)


###### Parameters

- sName: the Crosswalk name
- sTPName: the Trading Partner name
- aCriteria: the Crosswalk criteria


###### Return Value


Returns null or a CrosswalkEngine object that matches the specified parameters.


Example


string[][] criteria = new string[][] { new string[] { "purpose", "test" } };


XECrosswalkEngine engine = xwalkRegistry.GetCrosswalkEngine("test", "test tp", criteria);





 GetCrosswalkRefs(String, String, String[][])


NoteThe method is not supported in future releases.


###### Syntax


XECrosswalkRef [] GetCrosswalkRefs(string name, string TPName, string [] [] criteria)


###### Return Value


Returns an array of XECrosswalkRef objects by Crosswalk parameters.





 GetCrosswalkEngine(XECrosswalkRef ref)


NoteThe method is not supported in future releases.


###### Syntax


XECrosswalkEngine GetCrosswalkEngine(XECrosswalkRef ref)


###### Return Value


Returns an XECrosswalkEngine by name, TPname, and criteria parameters from XECrosswalkRef object.






Note The following methods are no longer supported: GetCrosswalkRef(int id), UpdateCrosswalk(XECrosswalkRef), RegisterCrosswalk(XECrosswalkRefMutable, String, String), RegisterCrosswalk(XECrosswalkRefMutable), UnregisterCrosswalk(int id).

<a id="translator-net-object-xecrosswalkresult"></a>

##### Translator .NET Object XECrosswalkResult

> 📄 Source: [Translator_NET_XECrosswalkResult.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_NET_XECrosswalkResult.htm)

###### Translator .NET XECrosswalkResult


The XECrosswalkResult object holds the record set of values returned by the XECrosswalkEngine match methods. Use the XECrosswalkResult methods to work with the results.

 GetMatchedKeysCount()


###### Syntax


uint GetMatchedKeysCount()


###### Return Value


Returns the count of matched keys.





 GetAll()


###### Syntax


String [][] GetAll()


###### Return Value


Returns all results in form of String[][].





 GetFirst()


###### Syntax


String GetFirst()


###### Return Value


Returns the first element of the first row – [0][0].





 getLast()


###### Syntax


String getLast()


###### Return Value


Returns the last element of the first row – [n][0].





 Select(String[])


###### Syntax


XECrosswalkResult Select(String[])


###### Return Value


Returns a clarified result by first values of it.






Example


This code snippet demonstrates how to use CrosswalkResult in your code.



// do lookup with keys and date 02/08/2001


XECrosswalkResult result = engine.Match(keys, 20010208);


// get all results as array of array


String[][] arrRes = result.GetAll();

<a id="event-data-net-object"></a>

##### Event Data .NET Object

> 📄 Source: [Event_Data_NET_Object.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Event_Data_NET_Object.htm)

###### Event Data .NET Object


The following table shows all methods with descriptions:


| Fields | Description |
| --- | --- |
| unsigned int nEID | Event ID |
| String sBrief | Brief description |
| String sDescription | Detailed description |
| unsigned int nSeverityID | Severity ID |
| String sSeverityName | Severity name |
| System::Collections::Generic::IEnumerator <System::Collections::Generic::KeyValuePair<String, String> parameters | Enumerator for event parameters |

<a id="processor-context-net-object"></a>

##### Processor Context .NET Object

> 📄 Source: [Processor_Context_.NET_Object.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Processor_Context_.NET_Object.htm)

###### Processor Context .NET Object


This parameter provides the following functionality:

- requesting Total Event Count – counter for “Entire Document”
- requesting Total Rejecting Event Count – counter for “Entire Document”
- registering new event – parameters are Event ID, Event Description, Event Severity ID, Event Severity Name and parameter collection


The following table shows all methods with description:


| Methods | Description |
| --- | --- |
| unsigned int GetTotalEventCount(); | Returns total Event Counter for “EntireDocument” Data Node. All Events are retuned, not only rejecting ones. Registers Event with specified data. Optional parameter sKey can specify existing bookmark. |
| unsigned int GetTotalRejectingEventCount(); | Returns total Rejecting Event Counter for “EntireDocument” Data Node. |
| void RegisterEvent(XEEventData eventData, String sKey); | Registers Event with specified data. Optional parameter sKey can specify existing bookmark. |

---

<a id="current-data-node-net-object"></a>

##### Current Data Node .NET Object

> 📄 Source: [Current_Data_Node_NET_Object.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Current_Data_Node_NET_Object.htm)

###### Current Data Node .NET Object


This parameter provides access to current Data Node being processed. It provides all XEngine DataNode interface functionality except requesting Event Viewer. Event traversing is not allowed during validation.


| Methods | Description |
| --- | --- |
| String^ GetName() | Returns data node name which corresponds to Parser Schema item. |
| String^ GetID() | Returns data node ID as GUID. |
| DWORD GetOffset() | Returns data node offset in the inbound data specified in characters, not bytes. |
| DWORD GetSize() | Returns data node size in the inbound data specified in characters, not bytes. |
| String^ GetAttribute(String^ sName) | Get data node item specified in parser schema as property or lookup by name. |
| String^ GetAttributeGlobal(String^ sName) | Get data node item specified in parser schema as property or lookup by name. The search scope of specified item is from current data node up to root. |
| void SetAttribute(String^ sName, String^ sValue) | Set attribute sName with value sValue on current data node. |
| XEDataNode^ GetParent() | Provides access to the parent node. |
| String^ GetValue(String^ sName) | Returns resolved macro with regard to default locale. |
| String^ GetValue(String^ sName, size_t nLocale) | Returns resolved macro with regard to specified locale. |
| String^ FormatString(String^ sTemplate) | Processes macros in template string parameter and returns template with all macros resolved with regard to default locale. |
| String^ FormatString(String^ sTemplate, size_t nLocale) | Processes macros in template string parameter and returns string with all macros resolved with regard to specified locale. |
| DWORD GetEventCount() | Returns total count of the events registered on current data node. |
| DWORD GetEventCount(bool bDeep) | Returns total count of the events. If bDeep parameter equals to true then method returns total count of the vents registered on both current data node and all its child nodes. Otherwise method returns count of the events registered on current data node. |
| DWORD GetREventCount() | Returns rejected events count registered on current data node. |
| DWORD GetREventCount(bool bDeep) | Returns rejected events count. If bDeep parameter equals to true then method returns total count of the rejected events registered on both current data node and all its child nodes. Otherwise method returns count of the rejected events registered on current data node. |
| XENET::XEEventViewer^ GetEventViewer() | Returns Event Viewer configured for traversing through the events registered on current data node. |
| XENET::XEEventViewer^ GetEventViewer(bool bDeep) | Returns Event Viewer configured for traversing through the events. If bDeep parameter equals to true, then events registered on current data node and events registered on all child data nodes are accessible. Otherwise, only events registered on current data node are accessible. |
| System::Collections::Generic::IEnumerable<System::Collections::Generic::KeyValuePair<String^, String^> >^ GetLookups(); | Returns enumerator for Data Node lookups. |
| System::Collections::Generic::IEnumerable<System::Collections::Generic::KeyValuePair<String^, String^> >^ GetProperties(); | Returns enumerator for Data Node properties. |

---

<a id="processed-data-net-object"></a>

##### Processed Data .NET Object

> 📄 Source: [Processed_Data_.NET_Object.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Processed_Data_.NET_Object.htm)

###### Processed Data .NET Object


This parameter provides access to current token. The following operations are supported:

- requesting data content
- requesting Guideline Node ID
- requesting data offset
- requesting data size
- requesting Actions configuration which was previously set by ec_Config parameter


Also this parameter can be asked about presence of data in current token.


The following table shows these methods with descriptions:


| Methods | Description |
| --- | --- |
| String^ GetDataContent(); | Returns content of current token. |
| String^ GetDataContent(String^ sKey); | Returns content of data token specified by Bookmark Key. |
| unsigned int GetGdlNodeIID(); | Returns Guideline Node ID of current token. |
| unsigned int GetGdlNodeIID(String^ sKey); | Returns Guideline Node ID of token specified by Bookmark Key. |
| bool IsDataPresent(); | Returns ‘true’ if current token is not empty, otherwise returns ‘false’ . |
| bool IsDataPresent(String^ sKey); | Returns ‘true’ if token specified by Bookmark Key is not empty, otherwise returns ‘false’ . |
| unsigned int GetDataOffset(); | Returns offset for current token. |
| unsigned int GetDataOffset(String^ sKey); | Returns offset for token specified by Bookmark Key. |
| unsigned int GetDataSize(); | Returns size of data for current token. |
| unsigned int GetDataSize(String^ sKey); | Returns size of data for token specified by Bookmark Key. |
| void AddBookmark(String^ sKey); | Remembers current token with specified Bookmark Key. Can be used later by this key. |
| void RemoveBookmark(String^ sKey); | Removes bookmark with specified key. |
| bool FindBookmark(String^ sKey); | Returns ‘true’ if bookmark specified by ‘key’ exists. Token is available. |
| array<String^>^ Lookahead(String^ sKey, long nOpCode) | Returns collection of data associated with sKey specified as UPath. |
| String^ GetTextParameters(); | Returns custom string specified in ec_Config parameter. |

---

<a id="debug-net-classes"></a>

##### Debug .NET Classes

> 📄 Source: [Debug_NET_Classes.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Debug_NET_Classes.htm)

###### Debug .NET Classes


You can create your own .NET Action code, that is used in the guideline validation. The code can be debugged and corrected in Visual Studio, that allows to find errors easily in the algorithm of the .NET Action work in each certain case.


To debug the .NET Action with Visual Studio:


Note To use sample files and project with .NET Action, go to the product installation folder and navigate to Help/Samples/Source_Debugging/Debugging_NET_Action folder.

1. In SpecBuilder, on the File tab, in the Backstage view, click Open, and select the guideline containing .NET Action.
2. Start Visual Studio.
3. In Visual Studio, on the File menu, select Open, and then click Project/Solution.
4. Select your project with .NET Action, and then click Open.
5. In Visual Studio, on the Debug menu, select Attach to Process.
6. In the Available Processes section, select SpecBuilder.exe, and then click Attach.
7. In Solution Explorer pane, select .NET Action, and set the breakpoint on the first line of the class method you want to debug.
8. In SpecBuilder, on the Tools tab, select Data Analyzer.
9. On the Welcome to the Analyzer Wizard wizard page, select the data file. The Analyzer is started.
10. In the Watch pane, monitor the variables and expressions.
11. In the Call Stack pane, view the names of functions on the call stack, parameter types, and parameter values.

---

<a id="net-class-methods"></a>

##### .NET Class Methods

> 📄 Source: [NET_Class_Methods.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/NET_Class_Methods.htm)

###### .NET Class Methods


Once the .NET class has been registered in the guideline, the .NET class methods can be used for writing validation business rules.


To use a .NET class method:

1. Select the node (such as Transaction, Loop, Segment, Element, and the rest) on which you wish to execute the .NET class method.
2. Click the Rules pane.
3. Click New Rule () to insert a new rule.
4. Select either an OnUserNode .NET or an OnUserEndNode .NET rule. An OnUserNode .NET rule is used when a method should be executed when a node is encountered in the data. An OnUserEndNode .NET rule is used after all child nodes have been processed. For those nodes without child nodes, the OnUserEndNode .NET rule is not available.
5. Click OK.
6. In the .NET Action dialog box, select a .NET class from the .NET Class list or click Add to declare a new .NET class. See [Declare a .NET Class](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Declare_a_NET_Class.htm) for instructions.
7. In the .NET Action dialog box, enter the following information:
8. Click Apply.

---

<a id="lookahead-methods-for-javascriptjavanet-actions"></a>

#### Lookahead Methods for JavaScript/Java/.NET Actions

> 📄 Source: [LookAhead_Methods_for_JavaScript_Java_NET_Actions.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/LookAhead_Methods_for_JavaScript_Java_NET_Actions.htm)

##### Lookahead Methods for JavaScript/Java/.NET Actions


When working with Java/JavaScript/.NET actions, you can use the following Lookahead methods:


Note UPath is the path to the node; the second parameter (0) specifies the action for the node.


The second parameter has the following possible values:

0 - no action; returns collection as it is.

1 - returns sum of all found targets.

2 - returns minimal number or string from found collection of targets.

3 - returns maximum number or string from found collection of targets.


###### ec_DataLink.Lookahead("UPath", 0);


Use this method to specify a lookup path in a JavaScript action.


Example


var vtrResult = ec_DataLink.Lookahead("/Transaction(ID = '101')/Loop(ID = 'DTM')/Loop(ID = 'LX')/Segment(ID = 'IN2')/Element(ID = '1104')", 2);


###### lookahead("UPath", 0);


Use this method to specify a lookup path in a Java action.


You should inherit your class from XEJABase, and then obtain ability to use method lookahead(). Public class SampleAction extends XEJABase.


Example


{


<location in class method>


String[] vtrResult = lookahead("/Transaction(ID = '101')/Loop(ID = 'DTM')/Loop(ID = 'LX')/Segment(ID = 'IN2')/Element(ID = '1104')", 1);


}


###### XEDataContext.Lookahead("UPath", 0);


Use this method to specify a lookup path in a .NET action.


Example


String[] vtrResult = XEDataContext.Lookahead("/Transaction(ID = '101')/Loop(ID = 'DTM')/Loop(ID = 'LX')/Segment(ID = 'IN2')/Element(ID = '1104')", 0);

---

<a id="reference-custom-jar-and-net-dll-files-or-java-and-net-classes"></a>

#### Reference Custom JAR and .NET DLL Files or Java and .NET Classes

> 📄 Source: [Reference_Custom_JAR_and_NET_DLL_Files_or_Java_and_NET_Classes.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Reference_Custom_JAR_and_NET_DLL_Files_or_Java_and_NET_Classes.htm)

##### Reference Custom JAR and .NET DLL Files or Java and .NET Classes


With the Java/.NET Workspace Editor you can reference custom .NET DLL files, JAR files and classes. Once the reference is created, the classes can then be registered and used with the .ecs files.


To reference custom JAR and .NET DLL files or classes:

1. Do one of the following:
2. Select from the following tasks:


With this dialog box, you can change the location of the DLL/ JAR/class files and specify both .NET Documentation/Javadoc and Source files location.

- Remove: Removes the reference to the custom DLL/JAR/class file.
- Export: Exports the reference of the DLL/JAR/class file to an XML file. The XML file can then be referenced by the run-time XEngine application.

---

<a id="javanet-rules-editor"></a>

#### Java/.NET Rules Editor

> 📄 Source: [Java_Net_Rules_Editor.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Java_Net_Rules_Editor.htm)

##### Java/.NET Rules Editor


To add or edit a Java or .NET Action, you normally use the Rules tab. The Java/.NET Rules Editor is an alternative way to view and edit Java or .NET actions.


To launch the Java/.NET Rules Editor:

- On the Home tab, in the Rules group, click Java/.NET Rules.


The Java/.NET Rules Editor shows a list of all Java/.NET actions associated with the currently selected guideline node. If you want to view all the Java/.NET actions used in the guideline, click Show All Handlers.


The Java/.NET Rules Editor toolbar provides the following functions:

- Click Go to Previous Handler () or Go to Next Handler () to move up or down among applied Java/.NET actions.
- Click Copy Selected Text () to copy text from the source.
- Click Find Text () to find text in the source.
- Click Declare New Java Class () to declare a new class. After you click this button, the New Java Class Declaration window is displayed. See [Declaring Java Classes](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Declare_a_Java_Class.htm) for instructions on declaring a new Java class.
- Click Add New Java Handler to Selected Node () to add a new OnUserNode or OnUserEndNode Java rule to the guideline node.
- Click Declare New .NET Class () to declare a new class. After you click this button, the New .NET Class Declaration window is displayed. See [Declaring a .NET Class](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Declare_a_NET_Class.htm) for instructions on declaring a new .NET class.
- Click Add New .NET Handler to Selected Node () to add a new OnUserNode or OnUserEndNode .NET rule to the guideline node.
- Click Remove Selected Node Handler () to delete selected Java/.NET action.


To view information about the currently selected Java/.NET Class:

1. Click View, and then click Declaration, Source, Javadoc, or .NET.

---

<a id="data-splitting-rules"></a>

#### Data Splitting Rules

> 📄 Source: [Book_Data_Splitting_Rules.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Data_Splitting_Rules.htm)

##### Data Splitting Rules


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="data-splitting-rules-overview"></a>

##### Data Splitting Rules Overview

> 📄 Source: [Data_Splitting_Rules_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Splitting_Rules_Overview.htm)

###### Data Splitting Rules Overview


With data splitting rules, you can apply user-defined splitting criteria when processing data files. This makes it possible to not only split the good data from the data containing errors, but also to filter data according to a specific set of rules and retrieve it based on the rules you applied to the data handling. Data splitting rules are applied at the logical data node level (provider, subscriber, and the rest), which greatly increases the precision with which you can filter data and retrieve the results.


For more information about data splitting, see the following topics:

- [Create Custom Rules](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Create_Custom_Rules.htm)
- [Limit the Number of Nodes in the Split File](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Limit_the_Number_of_Nodes_in_a_Split_File.htm)
- [Split Files According to Stream Scope Specification](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Split_Files_According_to_Stream_Scope_Specification.htm)
- Data Splitting Selector Configuration
- [Data Splitting Based on Trading Partner Level](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Split_Rules_Trading_Partner_Level_Interaction.htm)
- Using Data Splitting Rules with XEServer


###### Registry Settings File Configuration


The data splitting rules file can be passed to XEngine either through a Selector object (see Data Splitting Selector Configuration) or explicitly named in the registry file.


###### Sample Data Splitting Rules File


A sample data splitting rules file is provided in ${XERoot}/config/ldns/DataSplitRules.xml.

---

<a id="create-custom-rules"></a>

##### Create Custom Rules

> 📄 Source: [Create_Custom_Rules.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Create_Custom_Rules.htm)

###### Create Custom Rules


Data splitting rules are defined by using a special syntax. There are two versions of the data splitting syntax - version 1.0 and version 2.0 (introduced in XEngine 6.2). There are three different rule element types:

1. Standard name/value pair
2. Virtual name/value pair
3. Unique name/value pair


To create your own rules for standard name/value pairs and unique name value pairs, you build statements by using the following expressions:


| Expression | Description |
| --- | --- |
| <node name>[@property=x] | Use the @ symbol in conjunction with any property name that exists in the data node structure together with the equal sign (=) to define a value to search for, preceded by the node name. For example, the following expression specifies that the name of this rule identifies all transactions of type 837: Transaction[@TransactionID='837'] The possible list of data node names and properties are found in the parser schema file for the type of data file that you are processing. |
| ##unique## | During data splitting, when ##unique## is encountered, it is replaced with a unique value for each property matched. When the data splitting results are available, you can use the GetResults method in the LogicalDataHelper class (Java) or the ILogicalDataHelper interface to retrieve all the data that was matched. |
| and\|or | The And operator indicates that both rules must be matched in order for the rule to be applied. The Or operator indicates that at least one of the rules must match. |
| * | The asterisk (*) matches to all data nodes in the file. When you use this rule, you must take great care where you place it in the data splitting rule settings file. Because the rules are applied to a data file in the order they are listed, each rule takes precedence over the one that follows it. If you place the asterisk ahead of a rule that provides more precision, the rule after it is ignored. |
| .='n' | Limits the number of nodes in the result file. For more information, see [Limit the Number of Nodes in a Split File](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Limit_the_Number_of_Nodes_in_a_Split_File.htm). |
| scope=%scope specification% | Limits the size of the output files and the amount of time that the output stream stays open. For more information, see [Split Files According to Stream Scope Specification](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Split_Files_According_to_Stream_Scope_Specification.htm). |



Below is the sample set of rules in the default data splitting rules settings file and their meanings:

- <Rule name="BadData">*[@AckCode=’R’]</Rule>
- <Rule name="BadClaim">Claim[@AckCode='R'] and Claim[@ClaimID='##unique##']</Rule>
- <Rule name="270">Transaction[@TransactionID=’270’]</Rule>
- <Rule name="PayerID-A">Transaction[@TransactionID=’837’] and Subscriber[@PayerID=’1’]</Rule>
- <Rule name="UniqueClaimID">Transaction[@TransactionID=’837’] and Claim[@ID=’##unique##’]</Rule>
- <Rule name="RestOfData">*</Rule>
- <Rule name="ByGroup">Group[@GroupID='##unique##' and batch(.='1')]</Rule>
- <Rule name="bad">Claim[@ClaimID='##unique##'] and Claim[@AckCode='R' and batch(.='1')]</Rule>
- <Rule name="good">Claim[@ClaimID='##unique##'] and Claim[@AckCode='A' and batch(.='1')]</Rule>


Rules are handled in the order they are specified. The order that you list rules is very important because each rule takes precedence over the following one. For example, if the rule "<Rule name="RestOfData">*</Rule>" precedes the rule "<Rule name="270">Transaction[@TransactionID=’270’]</Rule>", the rule named "270" does not provide the desired results because the * wildcard character has already flagged all data nodes and properties.


All rules except ##unique## generate no more than one stream that corresponds to the rule, unless '.=n' is set.


The logic for rule splitting is straightforward: when a match is found in the data node structure, all leaf nodes of the node that matches the rule and the node itself are marked with the rule name. Rules are applied in the order they are specified and all unmarked nodes correspond to the * rule. All results that match a unique name rule are marked with the rule name and extension that corresponds to the value. For example,  "UniqueClaimID:234". For the following example, assume the following set of rules are specified:


Example


    <Rule name="R1">*[@AckCode=’R’]</Rule>


    <Rule name="R2">Transaction[@TransactionID=’270’]</Rule>


    <Rule name="R3">Transaction[@TransactionID=’837’] and Subscriber[@PID=’A’]</Rule>


    <Rule name="R4">Transaction[@TransactionID=’837’] and Subscriber[@PID=’B’]</Rule>


    <Rule name="R5">Claim [@ClaimID=’##unique##’]</Rule>


    <Rule name="R6’>*</Rule>


When the data file is processed, the resulting data structure is marked accordingly:


| Data Node Structure | Properties | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Interchange |   |   |
|       Group |   |   |
|              Transaction 1 | TransactionID=837 |   |
|                    Provider 1 |   |   |
|                          Subscriber 1.1 | PID=A |   |
|                                Claim 1.1.1 |   | R3 |
|                                Claim 1.1.2 | AckCode=R | R1 |
|                                Claim 1.1.3 |   | R3 |
|                          Subscriber 1.2      | PID=B |   |
|                                Claim 1.2.1 |   | R4 |
|                                Claim 1.2.2 |   | R4 |
|                                Claim 1.2.3 |   | R4 |
|                    Provider 2 |   |   |
|                          Subscriber 2.1 | PID=A |   |
|                                Claim 2.1.1 |   | R3 |
|                                Claim 2.1.2 | AckCode=R | R1 |
|                                Dependent 2.1.3 |   |   |
|                                      Claim 2.1.3.1 | AckCode=R | R1 |
|                                      Claim 2.1.3.2 |   | R3 |
|   |   |   |
|              Transaction 2 | Transaction ID=270 and AckCode=R | R1 |
|              Transaction 3 | TransactionID=270 | R2 |
|              Transaction 4 | TransactionID=837 |   |
|                    Provider 3 |   |   |
|                          Subscriber 3.1 |   |   |
|                                Claim 3.1.1 | ID=1 | R5:1 |
|                                Claim 3.1.2 | ID=2 | R5:2 |
|              Transaction 5 | TransactionID=270 | R2 |
|              ... |   |   |



Note In the preceding example, Subscriber 1.2 is not marked with the 'R4' rule but all of its children nodes are. The reason for this is that, when a data splitting rule is matched, XEngine marks each leaf node of the one matched with the rule and not the parent node. In this case, Subscriber 1.2 matched the properties Transaction="837" and "PID=B", so its children nodes were marked as matching rule "R4".


When data is split based on multiple rules, keep in mind that the results depend both on all children leaf nodes being marked as well as the order that the rules are assigned. In the preceding example, notice that the first rule is designed to match to all rejected data nodes. As the data node structure is traversed, DN Claim 1.1.2 is rejected and marked as a node matching rule R1 (this node itself is a leaf node). Continuing traversal, Claim 2.1.2 is identified as matching and also marked with the R1 rule. The same process continues until traversal is completed.


The following data splitting rule identifies matches on the transaction level with ID ‘270’. The same process described earlier is performed and all matching nodes are marked with the R2 rule.


The third splitting rule identifies two criteria to match: Transaction data nodes with ID ‘837’ and subscriber data nodes with the ID ‘A’. The first match for this rule is found on the Subscriber 1.1 data node - two of the three leaf nodes are then marked with the R3 rule. Claim 1.1.2 is not marked because it has already been marked with the R1 rule. The process continues with all of the rules, marking the leaf nodes appropriately.

---

<a id="limit-the-number-of-nodes-in-a-split-file"></a>

##### Limit the Number of Nodes in a Split File

> 📄 Source: [Limit_the_Number_of_Nodes_in_a_Split_File.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Limit_the_Number_of_Nodes_in_a_Split_File.htm)

###### Limit the Number of Nodes in a Split File


The number of nodes that can be reported in each result file can be limited by using the following rule template:


<Rule name=%*Name of the rule%*>Transaction[@%*Data node property*%=%*Data node property value%* #=%*Total number of transactions per result file%*]</Rule>


This specification should always follow the declaration of the data node or logical data node it identifies. Limiting can be done on the basis of any data node property.


For example, the following rule named "R2" picks up only transactions with an Transaction ID equal to "270" from the inbound data and re-packages them with a maximum of two transactions per file:


<Rule name="R2">Transaction[@TransactionID='270' #='2']</Rule>


The following rule named "R3" picks up only transactions with a Transaction ID equal to "837" and the subscriber PID value of 'A' from the inbound data and re-packages them with one transaction and five subscribers per file:


<Rule name="R3">Transaction[@TransactionID='837' #='1'] and Subscriber[@PID='A' #='5']</Rule>


In the case of a stream factory request for multiple streams for the same rule, all parameters are the same.

---

<a id="split-files-according-to-stream-scope-specification"></a>

##### Split Files According to Stream Scope Specification

> 📄 Source: [Split_Files_According_to_Stream_Scope_Specification.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Split_Files_According_to_Stream_Scope_Specification.htm)

###### Split Files According to Stream Scope Specification


When using IXEOStreamFact methods to process data files containing a very large number of sibling data nodes by using a unique item rule, a separate output stream is created for each potential result. An example of this is a situation where a unique rule is used to generate a separate output file for every Claim data node in the data structure, and the data file contains a Subscriber node that has thousands of child Claim nodes. In this case, an output stream is created for each unique claim and the output streams stay open until the entire Subscriber node is processed. Having multiple streams open uses large amounts of memory.


Specifying the scope of a stream is a way of limiting the amount of data that is written to a specific file and, consequently, when the stream closes. Output streams can be closed sooner, reducing memory usage.


The scope of a stream is specified by the "scope" parameter within a rule, as in the following template:


<Rule name=%*Name of the rule%* scope=%*Scope specification%*>%*Data node name%*[@%*Node properties%*='##unique##]</Rule>


Note The *Data node name* and *Node properties* parameters in the previous rule example are described in [Create Custom Rules](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Create_Custom_Rules.htm).


The value of *Scope specification* can be the name of any data node in the data structure that matches the unique data node or an ancestor of that data node. This value is used to define the number of data nodes that are described in a single stream and when the output stream is closed. Output streams are opened based on the data node specified by the ##unique## node property and closed after processing the Scope specification node.


An example of this is the following rule, which specifies a data node named "Claim" as the Scope specification.  


<Rule name="R5" scope="Claim">Claim [@ClaimID='##unique##']</Rule>


When this rule is applied to the report processing of the following data node structure -


*[Image: Data node structure]*

- the scope of each output stream created is set to each data node named "Claim". When each Claim data node is processed, an output stream is created, the file for that Claim is generated, then that output stream is closed. The result is three valid output files and the output streams are closed after processing each claim.


Specifying "Provider" as the scope specification, as in the following rule -


<Rule name="R5" scope="Provider">Claim [@ClaimID='##unique##']</Rule>

- sets the scope of each output stream to be each data node named "Provider". If both claims in the first transaction have different IDs, three output streams are generated and three files are created. If these claims have the same ID, then two streams are generated and two files are created. Each output stream is not closed until the entire provider is processed.


Note In order to gain the most benefits from the scope specification, the scope should be the same as the unique data node name.

---

<a id="split-rulestrading-partner-level-interaction"></a>

##### Split Rules/Trading Partner Level Interaction

> 📄 Source: [Split_Rules_Trading_Partner_Level_Interaction.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Split_Rules_Trading_Partner_Level_Interaction.htm)

###### Split Rules/Trading Partner Level Interaction


It is important to understand the interaction between splitting data by using both data splitting rules and based on trading partner level. When "splitlevel" is specified, it is expected that multiple splitting rules do not traverse below the specified level because the level specified is the designated atomic unit to be split.


The following examples show splitlevel specified at the Transaction level. The following set of rules are specified:


    <Rule name="R1">*[@AckCode=’R’]</Rule> 


    <Rule name="R2">Transaction[@TransactionID=’270’]</Rule>


    <Rule name="R6’>*</Rule>


Note Setting the splitlevel at Transaction, the whole transaction is rejected if at least one child node of this transaction is rejected even though some claims within the transaction are accepted.


| Data Node Structure | Properties | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Interchange |   |   |
|       Group |   |   |
|              Transaction 1 | TransactionID=837 | R1 |
|                    Provider 1 |   |   |
|                          Subscriber 1.1 | PID=A |   |
|                                Claim 1.1.1 |   |   |
|                                Claim 1.1.2 | AckCode=R |   |
|                                Claim 1.1.3 |   |   |
|                          Subscriber 1.2      | PID=B |   |
|                                Claim 1.2.1 |   |   |
|                                Claim 1.2.2 |   |   |
|                                Claim 1.2.3 |   |   |
|                    Provider 2 |   |   |
|                          Subscriber 2.1 | PID=A |   |
|                                Claim 2.1.1 |   |   |
|                                Claim 2.1.2 | AckCode=R |   |
|                                Dependent 2.1.3 |   |   |
|                                      Claim 2.1.3.1 | AckCode=R |   |
|                                      Claim 2.1.3.2 |   |   |
|   |   |   |
|              Transaction 2 | TransactionID=270 and AckCode=R | R1 |
|              Transaction 3 | TransactionID=270 | R2 |
|              Transaction 4 | TransactionID=837 | R6 |
|                    Provider 3 |   |   |
|                          Subscriber 3.1 |   |   |
|                                Claim 3.1.1 | ID=1 |   |
|                                Claim 3.1.2 | ID=2 |   |
|              Transaction 5 | TransactionID=270 | R2 |
|              ... |   |   |



If the following data splitting rules are specified -


    <Rule name="R1">*[@AckCode=’R’]</Rule>


    <Rule name="R6">*</Rule>


and splitlevel is set to Transaction, the expected result is as follows:


| Data Node Structure | Properties | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Interchange |   |   |
|       Group |   |   |
|              Transaction 1 | TransactionID=837 | R1 |
|                    Provider 1 |   |   |
|                          Subscriber 1.1 | PID=A |   |
|                                Claim 1.1.1 |   |   |
|                                Claim 1.1.2 | AckCode=R |   |
|                                Claim 1.1.3 |   |   |
|                          Subscriber 1.2      | PID=B |   |
|                                Claim 1.2.1 |   |   |
|                                Claim 1.2.2 |   |   |
|                                Claim 1.2.3 |   |   |
|                    Provider 2 |   |   |
|                          Subscriber 2.1 | PID=A |   |
|                                Claim 2.1.1 |   |   |
|                                Claim 2.1.2 | AckCode=R |   |
|                                Dependent 2.1.3 |   |   |
|                                      Claim 2.1.3.1 | AckCode=R |   |
|                                      Claim 2.1.3.2 |   |   |
|   |   |   |
|              Transaction 2 | TransactionID=270 and AckCode=R | R1 |
|              Transaction 3 | TransactionID=270 | R6 |
|              Transaction 4 | TransactionID=837 | R6 |
|                    Provider 3 |   |   |
|                          Subscriber 3.1 |   |   |
|                                Claim 3.1.1 | ID=1 |   |
|                                Claim 3.1.2 | ID=2 |   |
|              Transaction 5 | TransactionID=270 | R6 |
|              ... |   |   |



If the following data splitting rule is specified -


    <Rule name="R2">Transaction[@TransactionID=’270’]</Rule>


and splitlevel is set to Transaction level, the expected result is as follows:


| Data Node Structure | Properties | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Interchange |   |   |
|       Group |   |   |
|              Transaction 1 | TransactionID=837 |   |
|                    Provider 1 |   |   |
|                          Subscriber 1.1 | PID=A |   |
|                                Claim 1.1.1 |   |   |
|                                Claim 1.1.2 | AckCode=R |   |
|                                Claim 1.1.3 |   |   |
|                          Subscriber 1.2      | PID=B |   |
|                                Claim 1.2.1 |   |   |
|                                Claim 1.2.2 |   |   |
|                                Claim 1.2.3 |   |   |
|                    Provider 2 |   |   |
|                          Subscriber 2.1 | PID=A |   |
|                                Claim 2.1.1 |   |   |
|                                Claim 2.1.2 | AckCode=R |   |
|                                Dependent 2.1.3 |   |   |
|                                      Claim 2.1.3.1 | AckCode=R |   |
|                                      Claim 2.1.3.2 |   |   |
|   |   |   |
|              Transaction 2 | TransactionID=270 and AckCode=R | R2 |
|              Transaction 3 | TransactionID=270 | R2 |
|              Transaction 4 | TransactionID=837 |   |
|                    Provider 3 |   |   |
|                          Subscriber 3.1 |   |   |
|                                Claim 3.1.1 | ID=1 |   |
|                                Claim 3.1.2 | ID=2 |   |
|              Transaction 5 | TransactionID=270 | R2 |
|              ... |   |   |
