<a id="work-with-external-rules"></a>

### Work with External Rules

> 📄 Source: [Book_Work_with_External_Rules.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Work_with_External_Rules.htm)

#### Work with External Rules


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="external-rules-overview"></a>

#### External Rules Overview

> 📄 Source: [External_Rules_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/External_Rules_Overview.htm)

##### External Rules Overview


With external rules, you can take out the business logic that you want to add to certain guideline nodes and store the business logic separately outside the guideline. This means that in addition to the internal [rules](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Rules_Overview.htm) that you can add to a specific guideline node to handle special business requirements, you can also use the external rules that are stored in external .js files in your file system and link them to the required guideline.


Placing rules code in external .js files has the following advantages over the rules embedded in a guideline:

- Allows you to re-use the same code for different nodes on different guidelines without having to copy and paste them to each required node individually.
- Simplifies the migration of your business edits to a new version of the guideline standard.
- Makes it easier to update and support external rules. You do not have to update the rules for each individual guideline. All external rules are stored in the same directory to make support convenient.
- You can use modern source code version control systems to work with external rules.


With external rules, you do not have to duplicate the rule to run the rule on several guideline nodes; you just have to [create a rule](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Add_External_Rules.htm) as an external .js file where each rule implements a separate function. Each function refers to a UPath that identifies the node to execute the rule on. A function also contains the onStart/onEnd specifiers that define whether the rule is executed before or after the referred node is processed by XEngine. You can also create global scripts (.global.js files) to define global variables, constants, and helper functions, which can be used by external rules.


There are two types of external rules:

- User: Rules that you create and modify based on your requirements. Following the deprecation of the **Repository Root** option in SpecBuilder 9.2.4, user external rules must be stored at:{ECRootPath}\repository\User\ExternalRules.
- System: Rules provided by Edifecs that you cannot modify. System external rules are stored at the following location: {ECRootPath}\STANDARDS


To create external rules and include them in the guidelines, you can use the External Rules Editor which is available on the External Rules tab of SpecBuilder.The ribbon options Add Files and Add Folder allow you to add existing rules to guidelines.


The External Rules Editor also allows you to add and remove files and folders at the location where you store external rules directly from SpecBuilder.

---

<a id="add-external-rules"></a>

#### Add External Rules

> 📄 Source: [Add_External_Rules.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Add_External_Rules.htm)

##### Add External Rules


With External Rules Editor, you can create a JavaScript and add an internal reference to the script in the required guideline to run the rules on the required guideline nodes specified in the JavaScript file.


To create an external rule:

1. Open the guideline you want to add the external rules to.
2. Click the External Rules tab. To enable the tab, on the View tab of the ribbon, click External Rules.
3. On the Editor tab of the ribbon, in Files, click New Folder.
4. Change the default folder name by using the context menu.
5. On the External Rules tab, click Create Rule in New File and enter the JavaScript. To add a rule for the required guideline node, select the rule in the guideline tree.
6. Click Save to save the updated guideline.


You can create/add as many external JavaScripts as you require. All the files in the folder you create are automatically included in the guideline. To exclude a file with external rules, right-click the required file and then click Exclude from the guideline. Or right-click the required folder and then click Exclude all files in folder to exclude all the scripts this folder contains.


You can also include existing JavaScripts to a guideline.


To add existing external rules:

1. Open the guideline you want to add external rules to.
2. Click the External Rules tab. To enable the tab, on the View tab of the ribbon, click External Rules. On the Editor tab of the ribbon, in Files, click Add Files, and navigate to the files you want to add. The files you select are displayed in the Files pane of the editor and automatically included in the guideline.


By using the New Global option of the ribbon, you can create a global script (.global.js) to define global variables, constants, and helper functions to use their definitions in your external rules. You also have to add global scripts to the Files pane.

---

<a id="export-user-rules"></a>

#### Export User Rules

> 📄 Source: [Export_User_Rules.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_User_Rules.htm)

##### Export User Rules


With the [External Rules Editor](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Editor_Tab_for_External_Rules.htm) tab, you can export user rules you use in the currently open guideline and save them as a separate .js file at the specified location. The newly created .js file is automatically included to the currently open guideline as the external rules file.


To export rules:

1. Open the guideline that contains rules you want to export.
2. Click the External Rules tab. To enable the tab, on the View tab of the ribbon, click External Rules.
3. On the Editor tab of the ribbon, in Tools, click Export User Rules. The Export User Rules dialog appears.
4. Make sure that the path specified in the Location field leads inside the *{ECRoot}\repository* folder where all the external rules are stored, otherwise SpecBuilder will not be able to use the exported rules.
5. If you want to use these rules as external rules, select the Switch the guideline to using external user rules check box. SpecBuilder removes the user rules from the guideline and creates a .js file with link references to the required guideline to which the rules must be applied.
6. Click OK.

---

<a id="migrate-user-rules"></a>

#### Migrate User Rules

> 📄 Source: [Migrate_User_Rules.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Migrate_User_Rules.htm)

##### Migrate User Rules


With the [External Rules Editor](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Editor_Tab_for_External_Rules.htm) tab, you can import external user rules from a source guideline to the currently open guideline.


To migrate user rules:

1. Open the guideline that you want to import rules to.
2. Click the External Rules tab. To enable the tab, on the View tab of the ribbon, click External Rules.
3. On the Editor tab of the ribbon, in Tools, click Migrate User Rules.
4. Navigate to the .ecs file that contains external user rules you want to apply to the currently open guideline, and then click Open.


SpecBuilder copies external rules' references from the source guideline to the currently open guideline. The rule set of the currently open guideline becomes absolutely identical to the rule set of the source guideline.


Note If the currently open guideline already contains external rules, they are removed during the migration.

---

<a id="sample-use-case"></a>

#### Sample Use Case

> 📄 Source: [External_Rules_Use_Case.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/External_Rules_Use_Case.htm)

##### Sample Use Case


The following scenario demonstrates how you can use external rules to perform the following actions in the HIPAA X12 837 Health Care Claim guideline document:

- Raise an error if the claim amount is greater than $1,000
- Raise an error if the postal code does not belong to the list of permitted postal codes


To execute the scenario:

1. On Start Page, on the File tab, in the Backstage view, click Open, and then click Open from Disk or Open from Cloud if you store your guidelines in the Smart Trading Cloud Repository.
2. Navigate to the HIPAA X12 837 Health Care Claim guideline document, and then click Open.
3. Make sure that the External Rules option is enabled on the View tab.
4. On the Editor tab, click New Global, and then enter the following variables:
5. On the External Rules tab, in the Files section, right-click the User folder, click Add, and then select New Folder.
6. When the new folder is added, right-click and rename the folder to Claim.
7. On the External Rules tab, click Create Rule in New File, and then paste the following code:
8. On the External Rules tab, in the Files section, right-click the User folder, click Add, and then select New Folder.
9. When the new folder is added, right-click and rename the folder to PostCode.
10. On the External Rules tab, click Create Rule in New File, and then paste the following code:
11. Click the Analyzer / Data tab and make sure that the Analyzer / Data option is enabled on the View tab. The Data Wizard is displayed.
12. In the Data Wizard, in Load an existing Data File, click , and in the Data File to Analyze dialog.
13. Select a data file to analyze, click Open, and then click Next.
14. Click the Data Errors tab to view the results. You may find error or info events with messages specified in the rules depending on the validation results.

---

<a id="data-generator-pre-defined-macros"></a>

### Data Generator Pre-Defined Macros

> 📄 Source: [Book_Data_Generator_Pre_Defined_Macros.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Data_Generator_Pre_Defined_Macros.htm)

#### Data Generator Pre-Defined Macros


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="pre-defined-macros-overview"></a>

#### Pre-Defined Macros Overview

> 📄 Source: [Pre_Defined_Macros_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Pre_Defined_Macros_Overview.htm)

##### Pre-Defined Macros Overview


The ability to generate good test files is key when dealing with both large and small enterprise application integration projects. The ability to generate meaningful test files is not only important for system testing but also for unit testing to ensure that applications such as translators are functioning as expected.


 With the macros described in this section, you can generate meaningful test files when [running the Data Generator to generate test data files](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Scenario_External_Data_Sources.htm).

---

<a id="accumulate"></a>

#### Accumulate

> 📄 Source: [Accumulate.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Accumulate.htm)

##### Accumulate


Use this macro to add a value to a new or pre-defined variable.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters


Accumulator - Name of the variable that holds the results and to which the value is added.


Value - Value to add to the accumulator variable.


Example


Accumulator: {VAR1}


Value: 15


 


This example demonstrates adding the value 15 to accumulator with name VAR1. If variable VAR1 does not exist, this variable is created.


 


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post -macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="dateadd"></a>

#### DateAdd

> 📄 Source: [DateAdd.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DateAdd.htm)

##### DateAdd


Use this macro to add a specified number of days to a date.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters


Date - Date to which to add days. System date can also be used.


Format - Format for the date; needs to be selected to generate the proper resulting date.


Days - Number of days to add.


Result - Where the results from Date Add should be stored; this should be the name of a variable.


 


Example


Date: 20030512


Format: CCYYMMDD


Days: 5


Result: {$Data}


 


This example demonstrates adding 5 days to date 20030512 and storing the result 20030517 into the current data element.


 


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post -macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="datesubtract"></a>

#### DateSubtract

> 📄 Source: [DateSubtract.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DateSubtract.htm)

##### DateSubtract


Use this macro to subtract a specified number of days from a given date.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters


Date - Date from which to subtract the number of days. System date can also be used.


Format - Date format for the resulting date.


Days - Number of days to subtract.


Result - Where the results are stored; this should be the name of a variable.


Example


Date: 030512


Format: YYMMDD


Days: 5


Result: {$Data}


 


This example demonstrates subtracting 5 days from date 030512 and storing the result 030507 into the current data element.


 


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post -macros when the data item has already been


generated.

---

<a id="convertdatejscripttoedi"></a>

#### ConvertDateJScriptToEDI

> 📄 Source: [ConvertDateJScriptToEDI.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ConvertDateJScriptToEDI.htm)

##### ConvertDateJScriptToEDI


Use this macro to convert date string in JScript format to a date string in EDI format.


Usage 


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters:


Input Date - Value, which would represent input date.


Output Format - Format of the output value.


Result - Where the results should be stored; this should be the name of a variable.


Example


Input Date: Mon Aug 20 14:41:01 2001


Output Format: CCYYMMDD


Result: {$Data}


 


This example demonstrates converting the date in JScript format to EDI format and storing the result into


the current data element.


 


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post -macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="generate"></a>

#### Generate

> 📄 Source: [Generate.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Generate.htm)

##### Generate


This macro indicates to the Data Generator engine whether a data element should be generated. If the condition is true, the data element is generated; otherwise it is not generated. Generate macro doesn't force generation of a data node in case it is prohibited by Standard options (for example, Max Use = 0, or maxOccurs = 0). However, it forces data node generation in case node is either excluded or prohibited by User options.


Note Even if the macro has the Generate condition set to true, the data value may not generate if the parent node was not generated based on the [optional settings in the Data Generator](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Specify_Optional_Data_Generation.htm).


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters


Generate - This value can either be a literal such as True or False or a variable that contains a value indicating true or false.


Example


Generate: {VAR1}


 


In this example the current data element is generated if the value of variable VAR1 is true.


Note This macro does not force the generation of the parent items such as Composites, Segments, Loops. If the segment is being skipped this macro does not cause the creation of the segment, even if generate condition for element is true.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post -macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="getdata"></a>

#### GetData

> 📄 Source: [GetData.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/GetData.htm)

##### GetData


Use this macro to return a data value from a data node that has already been generated. GetData cannot return a value for a node that the Data Generator has not processed.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters


Data Path - This tells the Data Generator engine which node value to return. See [Understand Paths](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Understand_Paths.htm) for more information.


Save To - Name of a variable that stores the result of the macro.


Example 1


Data Path: /Interchange/Group/Transaction/Segment[1]/Element[1]


Result: {$Data}


 


This example demonstrates retrieving the value of the first element of the first segment and storing it into the current data element. In case of X12 with ST segment, this saves the value of ST01 to current data element.


Example 2


Data Path: /Interchange/Group/Transaction/Segment(ID="ST")/Element(ID="143")


Result: {$Data}


 


This example demonstrates retrieving the value of the element ID 143 from segment ID ST and storing it into the current data element. In case of X12 with ST segment, this saves the value of ST01 to current data element.


Note The data path must refer to data node that has already been generated.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="getdateinrange"></a>

#### GetDateInRange

> 📄 Source: [GetDateInRange.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/GetDateInRange.htm)

##### GetDateInRange


Use this macro to return a random date value between the provided date ranges.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node types: All nodes


###### Parameters


Start Range - Starting date


End Range - Ending date


Format- Format for the date; needs to be selected to generate the proper resulting date.


Result- Variable where the result is stored.


Example


Start Range: 20030501


End Range: 20030505


Format: CCYYMMDD


Result: {$Data}


 


This example demonstrates randomly selecting a date between 20030501 and 20030505. The randomly selected date is in format of CCYYMMDD and stored to the current data element.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="getinvalidcodevalue"></a>

#### GetInvalidCodeValue

> 📄 Source: [GetInvalidCodeValue.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/GetInvalidCodeValue.htm)

##### GetInvalidCodeValue


Use this macro for data elements with a code list to return a value that is not in the code list.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters:


Result - Variable name in which to store the invalid code value.


Example


Result: {$Data}


 


This example demonstrates creating an invalid code value and storing the invalid code value to the current data element.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="getinvaliddatatypevalue"></a>

#### GetInvalidDataTypeValue

> 📄 Source: [GetInvalidDataTypeValue.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/GetInvalidDataTypeValue.htm)

##### GetInvalidDataTypeValue


Use this macro to return an element value that does not match the data type defined in the guideline.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: Element


###### Parameters:


Result - Variable name in which to store the invalid data type value.


Example


Result: {$Data}


 


This example demonstrates creating an invalid data type value and storing the invalid value to the current data element.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="getnegativesignednumber"></a>

#### GetNegativeSignedNumber

> 📄 Source: [GetNegativeSignedNumber.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/GetNegativeSignedNumber.htm)

##### GetNegativeSignedNumber


Use this macro to return a numeric value with a negative sign. The size of the value depends on the Length and Precision parameters.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters


Length - The total number of digits to generate.


Precision - The total number of digits after the decimal.


Result - Variable name in which to store the negative value.


Example


Length: 5


Precision: 2


Result: {$Data}


 


This example generates a negative numeric value which is 5 digit long. Three digits prior to decimal and two digits following the decimal. Generated number is stored in the current data element.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="getnonexistentdate"></a>

#### GetNonExistentDate

> 📄 Source: [GetNonExistentDate.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/GetNonExistentDate.htm)

##### GetNonExistentDate


Use this macro to return an invalid date. The part of the date that is made invalid is based on the parameter settings.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters


Format - Format for the date; needs to be selected to generate the proper resulting date.


Make Year (CCYY) Invalid - Indicates that the century and/or year part should be made invalid.


Make Month Invalid - Indicates that the month should be made invalid.


Make Day Invalid - Indicates that the day should be made invalid.


Result - Variable in which to store the invalid date.


Example


Format: CCYYMMDD


Make Year (CCYY) Invalid: False


Make Month Invalid: True


Make Day Invalid: False


Result: {VAR1}


 


This example generates a date with invalid month. Generated date is stored in the variable VAR1.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="getnonexistenttime"></a>

#### GetNonExistentTime

> 📄 Source: [GetNonExistentTime.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/GetNonExistentTime.htm)

##### GetNonExistentTime


Use this macro to return an invalid time. The part of the time that is made invalid is controlled by the parameter settings.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters


Format - Format for the date; needs to be selected to generate the proper resulting date.


Make Hour Invalid - Indicates that the hour should be made invalid.


Make Minutes Invalid - Indicates that the minutes should be made invalid.


Make Seconds Invalid - Indicates that the seconds should be made invalid.


Make Decimals Invalid - Indicates that the decimals should be made invalid.


Result - Variable in which to store the invalid time.


Example


Format: HHMMSSD


Make Hours Invalid: False


Make Minutes Invalid: True


Make Seconds Invalid: False


Make Decimals Invalid: False


Result: {VAR1}


 


This example generates a time with invalid minutes. Generated time is stored in the variable VAR1.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="getpositivesignednumber"></a>

#### GetPositiveSignedNumber

> 📄 Source: [GetPositiveSignedNumber.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/GetPositiveSignedNumber.htm)

##### GetPositiveSignedNumber


Use this macro to return a numeric value with a positive sign. The size of the value is controlled by the Length and Precision parameters.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters


Length - The total number of digits to generate.


Precision - The total number of digits after the decimal.


Result - The variable name in which to store the positive value.


Example


Length: 5


Precision: 2


Result: {$Data}


 


This example generates a positive signed numeric value which is 5 digit long. Three digits prior to decimal and two digits following the decimal. Generated number is stored in the current data element.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="getrandomnumber"></a>

#### GetRandomNumber

> 📄 Source: [GetRandomNumber.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/GetRandomNumber.htm)

##### GetRandomNumber


Use this macro to return a numeric value between starting number and ending number. Value returned can include decimals based on the Precision parameter.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters


Start Range - Starting value


End Range - Ending value


Precision - Number of digits after the decimal


Result - Variable name in which to stores the returned value.


Example


Start Range: 10


End Range: 20


Precision: 0


Result: {$Data}


 


This example demonstrates randomly selecting a number between 10 and 20. The randomly selected number is a whole number since the precision was set to 0. Number is stored to the current data element.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="getrandomdigitstring"></a>

#### GetRandomDigitString

> 📄 Source: [GetRandomDigitString.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/GetRandomDigitString.htm)

##### GetRandomDigitString


Use this macro to generate a string of digits of any length.


Usage 


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: All nodes


Parameters


Length - the length of the string to generate.


Result - the name of the variable where to store the generated string.


Example


Length: 10


Result: {$Data}


 


This example demonstrates 10 char length string which consist of random digits. Number is stored to the current data element.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="getsystemdate"></a>

#### GetSystemDate

> 📄 Source: [GetSystemDate.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/GetSystemDate.htm)

##### GetSystemDate


Use this macro to return the system date in the selected format.  


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters


Format - Format in which the date should be returned.


Result - Variable name in which to stores the system date.


Example


Format: CCYYMMDD


Result: {$Data}


 


This example demonstrates generating the current date in format of CCYYMMDD and storing the value into the current data element.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="getsystemtime"></a>

#### GetSystemTime

> 📄 Source: [GetSystemTime.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/GetSystemTime.htm)

##### GetSystemTime


Use this macro to return the system time in the selected format.  


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters


Format - Format in which the time should be returned.


Results - Variable name in which to stores the system time.


Example


Format: HHMMSS


Result: {$Data}


 


This example demonstrates generating the current time in format of HHMMSS and storing the value into the current data element.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="getvariable"></a>

#### GetVariable

> 📄 Source: [GetVariable.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/GetVariable.htm)

##### GetVariable


Use this macro to return the value which is stored in a given variable name.  


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters:


Variable - Name of the variable from which to retrieve the value.


Result - Variable name in which to store the retrieved value.


Example


Variable: {VAR1}


Result: {$Data}


 


This example demonstrates retrieving the value of variable VAR1 and storing this value into the current data element.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post -macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="repeatbasedoncount"></a>

#### RepeatBasedOnCount

> 📄 Source: [RepeatBasedOnCount.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/RepeatBasedOnCount.htm)

##### RepeatBasedOnCount


Use this macro to generate multiple items in the data file based on the specified count.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: No


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters


Count Value - Indicates number of time the data item should be generated.


Example


Count Value: 3


 


Setting the count value to 3 generates the item (such as Segment, Loop, and the rest) 3 times in the data file.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post -macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="repeatbasedonvalues"></a>

#### RepeatBasedOnValues

> 📄 Source: [RepeatBasedOnValues.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/RepeatBasedOnValues.htm)

##### RepeatBasedOnValues


Use this macro to generate multiple items in the data file based on number of entries that have been specified for a specific element.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: No


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters


Dictionary Path - Points to an item in the data dictionary whose "Data" or "Repeat" Data Set should be used to indicate how many times the item should be generated. See [Understand Paths](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Understand_Paths.htm) for more information on specifying the dictionary path.


Example


Dictionary Path: 270/Element/1037/1037 - Name Middle/Data


 


This example repeats this item based on number of values provided in a "Data" Data Set of a dictionary item for element 1037.


 


Dictionary Path: 270/Element/1037/1037 - Name Middle/Repeat


 


This example repeats this item based on number of values provided in a "Repeat" Data Set of a dictionary item for element 1037.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="return"></a>

#### Return

> 📄 Source: [Return.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Return.htm)

##### Return


Use this macro to skip generating the data value and stop macro script execution if the condition is set to true. 


This macro is very similar to the [Skip](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Skip.htm) macro except that this macro stops macros execution.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: No


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters


Return - This value can either be a literal such as True or False or a variable that contains a value indicating true or false.


Example


Return: {VAR1}


 


In this example Data Generator stops processing the current guideline node and return to the outer node if the value of variable VAR1 is true.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="runjscript"></a>

#### RunJScript

> 📄 Source: [RunJScript.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/RunJScript.htm)

##### RunJScript


Use this macro if the Pre-Defined macros do not meet your needs. Using this macro, you can add your own custom JavaScript code to manipulate data generation.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters


Script - JavaScript text to further manipulate data generation for custom needs. Following objects are provided as JavaScript extension which allow you to integrate your JavaScript with the data generation engine.


###### EC Objects

 ecDGEContext


Use this object to output messages to the Data Generator log.


###### Syntax


ecDGEContext.Log(recordType, message)


###### Parameters

- recordType: The type of the log record. Possible values:
- message: The log text. Type – string.





 ecDateTimeFormatter


Provides conversion between numeric and EDI string date/time representation.


###### Methods


String2DateTime(date : string, format : string) : double;


DateTime2String(date : double, format : string) : string;


###### Parameters


date:double - the number of milliseconds since 1970


date:string - EDI string date/time notation


format:string - EDI date/time format


###### Notes


Formats currently supported: [CC]YYMMDD, HHMM[SS[D[D]]]


Example


    var date1 = new Date(); var strDate = ecDateTimeFormatter.DateTime2String(date.getTime(), "YYMMDD"); var milliseconds = ecDateTimeFormatter.String2DateTime ("151039", "HHMMSS");





 ecGuidelineNode


Provides access to the guideline node currently being processed by the Data Generator engine


###### Attributes for EDI node types:


| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Transaction | ID, Name, Purpose, GroupID, Standard, StdVersion |
| Loop | LoopKey, ID, Name, Requirement, UsrRequirement, Area, Sequence, Count, Excluded, Purpose |
| Segment | ID, Name, Requirement, UsrRequirement, Area, Sequence, MaxUse, Excluded, Purpose |
| Composite | ID, Name, Requirement, UsrRequirement, Sequence, MaxUse, Excluded, Purpose |
| Element | ID, Name, Requirement, UsrRequirement, Sequence, DataType, MinSize, MaxSize, MaxUse, Excluded, Purpose |
| Compositecode list | Compositecode listKey |
| code list | code listKey, Name, Codes, SkipIncludedCodesLookup |
| Externalcode list | Name, Description, Comment |



###### Attributes for DFF node types:


| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Message | ID, Purpose, Standard, Version |
| GroupRecord | ID, Name, MaxUse, MinUse, Excluded, Purpose |
| Record | ID, Name, MaxUse, MinUse, Excluded, Purpose |
| Field | ID, Name, Requirement, UsrRequirement, DataType, Sequence, MaxUse, MinUse, Excluded, Purpose |
| code list | code listKey, Name, Codes |



###### Attributes for HL7 node types:


| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Message | ID, MessageID, Name, Purpose, Event, EventName, Group ID, Standard, StdVersion |
| Group | LoopKey, ID, Name, Requirement, UsrRequirement, Sequence, MaxUse, Excluded, Purpose |
| Segment | ID, Name, Requirement, UsrRequirement, Sequence, MaxUse, Excluded, Purpose |
| Field | ID, Name, Requirement, UsrRequirement, Sequence, MaxSize, DataType,MaxUse, Excluded, Purpose |
| code list | code listKey, Name, Codes |
| DataType | Name, ID, MaxSize, Purpose |
| SimpleDataType | BasicDataType |
| Externalcode list | Name, Description, Comment |



###### Attributes for NCPDP node types:


| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Transmission | ID, Name, Type, Purpose, Standard, StdVersion |
| Segment | ID, Name, Requirement, UsrRequirement, Sequence, Excluded, Purpose |
| Transaction | ID, Name, Purpose, Requirement, UsrRequirement, Sequence, Excluded, Count |
| Field | ID, Name, Purpose, Requirement, UsrRequirement, Sequence, MinSize, DataType,MaxSize, Excluded, Format |
| Externalcode list | Name, Description, Comment |
| code list | code listKey, Name, Codes |
| Group | ID, Name, Requirement, UsrRequirement, Sequence, Excluded, Purpose |
| CounterGroup | MaxUse |
| RepeatSet | MaxUse |



###### Attributes for PFF node types:


| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Message | ID, Name, Purpose, Standard, Version |
| GroupRecord | ID, Name, Requirement, UsrRequirement, MinUse, MaxUse, Excluded, Purpose |
| Record | ID, Requirement, UsrRequirement, MinUse, MaxUse, Excluded, Purpose |
| Composite | ID, Name, Requirement, UsrRequirement, Sequence, Excluded, Purpose, Occurs, OccursTo, OccursFrom, OccursDependingOn |
| Field | ID, Name, Requirement, UsrRequirement, DataType, Sequence, Justification, MaxSize, MinSize, Excluded, PaddingCharacter, Purpose, Occurs, OccursTo, OccursFrom, OccursDependingOn |
| code list | code listKey, Name, Codes |



Note These attributes are read-only, Guideline attributes cannot be updated.


Example 1


    //Example 1: This example sets the data value based on the data type and length if (ecGuidelineNode.DataType == "DT") { if (ecGuidelineNode.MinSize == 8) { ecVariables.$Data = "20030101"; } else { ecVariables.$Data = "030101"; } }


Example 2


This example set the Current Area based Segment guideline node if (ecGuidelineNode.Area == 0) { ecVariables.CurrentArea = "Envelopes"; } if (ecGuidelineNode.Area == 1) { ecVariables.CurrentArea = "Header"; } else if (ecGuidelineNode.Area == 2) { ecVariables.CurrentArea = "Detail"; } else if (ecGuidelineNode.Area == 3) { ecVariables.CurrentArea = "Summary"; } else { ecVariables.CurrentArea = "Unknown area"; }





 ecVariables


Allows macros written in JavaScript to have a common variables pool.


###### Notes


Any text following the ecVariables is treated as a variable name. If using system defined variable then name must prefixed with a dollar sign, followed by the name of system variable. Pre-defined system variables are listed later. Values can only be changed in the $Data system variable.


| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| $CurrentHLIndex | Current HL Index value. |
| $Example | From the current guideline node. Example application field value. |
| $GroupID | From the transaction guideline node, value of Functional Group ID. |
| $GroupVersion | From the transaction guideline node, value of Version. |
| $MessageStatus | From the transaction guideline node, value of Message status. |
| $MessageVersion | From the transaction guideline node, value of Message version. |
| $TransactionID | From the transaction guideline node, value of Transaction ID. |
| $UserNote1 | From the current guideline node, value of User Note 1. |
| $UserNote2 | From the current guideline node, value of User Note 2. |
| $UserNote3 | From the current guideline node, value of User Note 3. |
| $UserNote4 | From the current guideline node, value of User Note 4. |
| $UserNote5 | From the current guideline node, value of User Note 5. |
| $UserNote6 | From the current guideline node, value of User Note 6. |
| $Data | Data element currently being generated. |
| $DataIndex | Index of the data looked up from the "Data" dictionary DataSet for the current guideline node. |
| $RepeatIndex | Index of the data looked up from the "Repeat" dictionary DataSet for the current guideline node. |
| $ParentIteration | 1 based index of the parent node iteration. |
| $RepeatCount | From the current guideline node, value of the Repetition property. |



Example


    // sets a value to system variable if (ToString(ecVariables.$Data) == "null") { ecVariables.$DATA = ecGuidelineNode.$Example; }


    // sets a value to user variable ecVariables.MyIndex = ecVariables.$DataIndex; … if (ecVariables.MyIndex == 0) { ecVariables.$Data = "the first value"; } else { ecVariables.$Data = "not the first value"; } … ecVariables.SomeNodeData = ecVariables.$Data;

---

<a id="selectcodefromcodelist"></a>

#### SelectCodeFromCodeList

> 📄 Source: [SelectCodeFromCodeList.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/SelectCodeFromCodeList.htm)

##### SelectCodeFromCodeList


Use this macro to select a value from a code list of the current data element. If this macro is used on an element without a code list, no value is generated for the data element.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: Element


###### Parameters


Type - Code can be selected based on an Index, Random or Sequential values. Index value must be specified if Index type is selected.


Index Value - Selects the code from code list based on this value. The value is matched to the Row Index column in the code list.


Result - Variable name in which to store the selected code value.


Example


Type: Indexed


Index Value: 5


Result: {$Data}


 


This example demonstrates selecting the 5th code from the codelist and putting the value into the current data element.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="selectcodefromexternalcodelist"></a>

#### SelectCodeFromExternalCodeList

> 📄 Source: [SelectCodeFromExternalCodeList.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/SelectCodeFromExternalCodeList.htm)

##### SelectCodeFromExternalCodeList


Use this macro to select a value from an external code list. This macro can be used on any data element since the external code lists are not associated to any one specific data element.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters:


Code list Name - This is the name of the registered code list. Click on the browse external code list to determine the name of the code list.


Date - Many of the external code lists are published on regular basis, such as quarterly, yearly, and the rest. Specifying the date indicates to the Data Generator which code list file should be used to select the code.


Criteria List - Some code lists consists of several different categories of codes. For example, code list 5 has entries for both country codes and currency codes.  Specifying the criteria indicates which category code list to use.


Type - Code can be selected based on an Index, Random or Sequential values. Index value must be specified if Index type is selected.


Index Value - Selects the code from code list based on this value. The value is matched to the Row Index column in the code list.


TP Key - Identifier for trading partner.


Result - Variable name in which to store the selected code value.


Example


Codelist Name: 130


Date: 20010101


Criteria List: Category="LevelI", SubCategory="Modifier"


Type: Indexed


Index Value: 3


TP Key: Company1


Result: {$Data}


 


This example demonstrates selecting a code from external codelist 130 (Health Care Financing Administration Common Procedural Coding System), where the codelist date is Jan. 01, 2001. Criteria list indicates to select LevelI modifier codes. Selection type of Indexed indicates to select the 3rd code from the codelist. Results are stored into the current data element.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post -macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="setdata"></a>

#### SetData

> 📄 Source: [SetData.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/SetData.htm)

##### SetData


Use this macro to set or override a data value to a data node that has already been generated. SetData cannot be used for a node which the Data Generator has not processed.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters


Data Path - This tells the Data Generator engine which node value to set the data for, see [Understand Paths](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Understand_Paths.htm) for more information.


Data - Value which should be set in the given data path.


Example


Data Path: /Interchange/Group/Transaction/Segment[1]/Element[1]


Data: 850


 


This example demonstrates storing the value 850 into the first element of the first segment. In case of X12 with ST segment, this saves the value of 850 into ST01.


Note The data path must refer to data node that has already been generated.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="setdatawithpadding"></a>

#### SetDataWithPadding

> 📄 Source: [SetDataWithPadding.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/SetDataWithPadding.htm)

##### SetDataWithPadding


This macro is similar to [SetData](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/SetData.htm) and applies to Positional Flat Files only. If the data value is shorter that the field length it pads the data with the padding character up to the length of the field.


*[Image: SetDataWithPadding macro]*


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters


Data Path - This tells the Data Generator engine which node value to set the data for, see [Understand Path](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Understand_Paths.htm) for more information.


Data - Value which should be set in the given data path.

---

<a id="sethlparent"></a>

#### SetHLParent

> 📄 Source: [SetHLParent.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/SetHLParent.htm)

##### SetHLParent


Use this macro to override the HL parent value in HL element 02. The value selected must be for a previously defined HL segment.


*[Image: SetHLParent macro]*


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: HL Segment


###### Parameters


HL Parent Value - Value to use for HL element 02. Value must belong to a previously declared HL segment.


Example


Variable: {VAR1}


Value: My Value


 


In this example literal value "My Value" is stored into variable VAR1 so that it can be later re -used.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="setvariable"></a>

#### SetVariable

> 📄 Source: [SetVariable.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/SetVariable.htm)

##### SetVariable


Use this macro to set the data value for a given variable name.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters


Variable - Name of the variable to which set the value.


Value - Value to save into the variable.


Example


Variable: {VAR1}


Value: My Value


 


In this example literal value "My Value" is stored into variable VAR1 so that it can be later re -used.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="skip"></a>

#### Skip

> 📄 Source: [Skip.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Skip.htm)

##### Skip


This macro tells the Data Generator to skip generating the data value if the condition is set to true.


This macro is very similar to the [Return](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Return.htm) macro except that this macro does not stop macros execution.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: No


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters


Skip - This value can either be a literal such as True or False or Variable which contains a value indicating true or false.


Example


Skip: {VAR1}


In this example the current data element is skipped from generation if the value of variable VAR1 is true.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="data-generator-database-macros"></a>

#### Data Generator Database Macros

> 📄 Source: [Book_Data_Generator_Database_Macros.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Data_Generator_Database_Macros.htm)

##### Data Generator Database Macros


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="database-macros-overview"></a>

##### Database Macros Overview

> 📄 Source: [Database_Macros_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Database_Macros_Overview.htm)

###### Database Macros Overview


In most companies, there are large amounts of data in databases that provide real-life data. With the macros described in this section, you can access that data when [running the Data Generator to generate test data files](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Scenario_External_Data_Sources.htm).

---

<a id="database-macro-usage-example"></a>

##### Database Macro Usage Example

> 📄 Source: [Database_Macro_Usage_Example.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Database_Macro_Usage_Example.htm)

###### Database Macro Usage Example


The following example demonstrates how to establish a connection to a database, and then get the value from the database record and use this value when generating data.

1. Use the ADOCreateConnection macro to establish the database connection.
2. Once the connection is created, select records from a specified table.
3. Once the record is selected, you can use values from this record to populate the generated data.

---

<a id="makedatagreaterthanmaxsize"></a>

##### MakeDataGreaterThanMaxSize

> 📄 Source: [MakeDataGreaterThanMaxSize.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/MakeDataGreaterThanMaxSize.htm)

###### MakeDataGreaterThanMaxSize


Use this macro to return a value that is greater than the max size defined for the data element.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: Element


###### Parameters


Increase By - Indicates how much larger than max size to make the value.


Result - Variable name in which to store the returned value.


Example


Increase By: 3


 


This example demonstrates generating a data value which is 3 characters over the allowed max length. Value is automatically stored into the current data element.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="makedatalessthanminsize"></a>

##### MakeDataLessThanMinSize

> 📄 Source: [MakeDataLessThanMinSize.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/MakeDataLessThanMinSize.htm)

###### MakeDataLessThanMinSize


Use this macro to return a value that is less than the minimum size defined for the data element.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: Element


###### Parameters


Reduce By - Indicates how much to reduce the size of the value by.


Result - Variable name in which to store the returned value.


Example


Reduce By: 3


 


This example demonstrates generating a data value which is 3 characters less than the allowed minimum length. If the reduction exceeds length of zero then data is not generated otherwise value is automatically stored into the current data element.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post -macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="makedatamaxsize"></a>

##### MakeDataMaxSize

> 📄 Source: [MakeDataMaxSize.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/MakeDataMaxSize.htm)

###### MakeDataMaxSize


Use this macro to return a value whose length is equal to the max size defined for the data element.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: Element


###### Parameters:


None

---

<a id="makedataminsize"></a>

##### MakeDataMinSize

> 📄 Source: [MakeDataMinSize.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/MakeDataMinSize.htm)

###### MakeDataMinSize


Use this macro to return a value whose length is equal to the minimum size defined for the data element.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: Element


###### Parameters:


None

---

<a id="adoaddnew"></a>

##### ADOAddNew

> 📄 Source: [ADOAddNew.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADOAddNew.htm)

###### ADOAddNew


Use this macro to add a new record into the recordset which is named in the Recordset parameter of the macro. The new record replaces the current record and you can add content to the columns in the record by using macros such [ADOSetColumnValue](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADOSetColumnValue.htm).  The new record is not added to the database until [ADOUpdate](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADOUpdate.htm) is performed or if the record selection is changed by using [ADONext](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADONext.htm), [ADOPrevious](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADOPrevious.htm), [ADOFirst](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADOFirst.htm), [ADOLast](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADOLast.htm), or [ADOMove](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADOMove.htm) macros.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes 


Post-Macro: Yes


Node types: All


###### Parameters


Recordset - Recordset where the new record is inserted.


Example


Recordset: {MyRecordSet}


 


This example adds a new record into MyRecordSet and makes the added record a current record. To post addition to the recordset you must either explicitly call ADOUpdate, or move to the other record with `ADONext/ADOPrevious/ADOFirst/ADOLast/ADOMove` macro.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="adobegintrans"></a>

##### ADOBeginTrans

> 📄 Source: [ADOBeginTrans.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADOBeginTrans.htm)

###### ADOBeginTrans


Use this macro to start a series of changes made to the source data as a single unit. For example, to transfer money between accounts, you subtract an amount from one and add the same amount to the other. If either update fails, the accounts no longer balance. Making these changes within an open transaction ensures that either all or none of the changes go through. ADOBeginTrans starts the transaction.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node types: All


###### Parameters


Connection - Name of the connection on which to begin the transaction.


Example


Connection: {MyConn}


 


This example demonstrates beginning a transaction by using connection to DB server stored in the MyConn variable.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="adocloseconnection"></a>

##### ADOCloseConnection

> 📄 Source: [ADOCloseConnection.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADOCloseConnection.htm)

###### ADOCloseConnection


Use this macro to close a previously opened connection. This also closes any active Recordset objects associated with the connection.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node types: All


###### Parameters


Connection - Name of the connection to close.


Example


Connection: {MyConn}


 


This example demonstrates closing a connection to DB server stored in the MyConn variable.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="adocloserecordset"></a>

##### ADOCloseRecordSet

> 📄 Source: [ADOCloseRecordSet.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADOCloseRecordSet.htm)

###### ADOCloseRecordSet


Use this macro to close a previously opened Recordset.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node types: All


###### Parameters


RecordSet - Name of the RecordSet to close.


Example


RecordSet: {MyRecordSet}


 


This example demonstrates closing a RecordSet stored in the MyRecordSet variable.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="adocommittrans"></a>

##### ADOCommitTrans

> 📄 Source: [ADOCommitTrans.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADOCommitTrans.htm)

###### ADOCommitTrans


Use this macro to save changes made during a transaction on the connection and end the transaction.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node types: All


###### Parameters


Connection - Name of the connection on which to commit the transaction.


Example


Connection: {MyConn}


 


This example demonstrates committing a current transaction by using connection to DB server stored in the MyConn variable.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="adocreateconnection"></a>

##### ADOCreateConnection

> 📄 Source: [ADOCreateConnection.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADOCreateConnection.htm)

###### ADOCreateConnection


Use this macro to establish a physical connection to a data source. After this macro successfully completes, the connection is live and you can issue other database macros against it and process the results.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node types: All


###### Parameters


Connection Name - Name of the connection to create


UserID - Value that contains a user name to use when establishing the connection


Password - Value that contains a password to use when establishing the connection


Example


Connection: {MyConn}


Connection string: driver=\{SQL Server\};server=MySvr


User Id:


Password:


 


This example demonstrates creating a connection to SQL server on the host MySvr by using Windows authentication and storing it in the MyConn variable. Note The braces are escaped, to avoid treating {SQL Server} as a variable name.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="adodelete"></a>

##### ADODelete

> 📄 Source: [ADODelete.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADODelete.htm)

###### ADODelete


Use this macro to mark the current record or a group of records in a Recordset for deletion. If the Recordset doesn't allow record deletion, an error occurs. If you are in immediate update mode, deletions occur in the database immediately. If a record cannot be successfully deleted (due to database integrity violations, for example), the record remains in edit mode after the call to Update.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node types: All


###### Parameters


Recordset - Represents the entire set of records from a base table or the results of an executed command.


Example


Recordset: {MyRecordSet}


 


This example demonstrates marking a current record of the MyRecordSet for deletion. Note You must use ADOUpdate macro to actually post deletion to the recordset.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="adoexecuteselect"></a>

##### ADOExecuteSelect

> 📄 Source: [ADOExecuteSelect.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADOExecuteSelect.htm)

###### ADOExecuteSelect


Use this macro to execute the query, SQL statement, or stored procedure specified in the SQL select statement box.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node types: All


###### Parameters


Connection - Connection on which to execute the SQL statement.


SQL select statement - A string value that contains the SQL statement, stored procedure, or provider-specific text to execute.


Result recordset - Returns a Recordset object reference.


Example


Connection name: {MyConn}


SQL select statement: SELECT * FROM MyTable


Result Recordset: {MyRecordSet}


 


This example demonstrates executing the SQL select statement and storing the result recordset in the MyRecordSet variable.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="adoexecuteupdate"></a>

##### ADOExecuteUpdate

> 📄 Source: [ADOExecuteUpdate.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADOExecuteUpdate.htm)

###### ADOExecuteUpdate


Use this macro to save any changes you make to the current row of a Recordset object, or the Fields collection of a Record object.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node types: All


###### Parameters


Connection - Connection on which to execute the SQL statement.


SQL select statement - A string value that contains the SQL statement, stored procedure, or provider-specific text to execute.


Records affected - Returns the number of records affected.


Example


Connection name: {MyConn}


SQL update/insert/delete UPDATE MyTable SET SALARY = SALARY * 1.05 WHERE NAME='John


 


statement: Andersen'


Records affected: {RecordsAffected}


 


This example demonstrates executing the SQL update statement and storing the number of affected records in the RecordsAffected variable


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="adofirst"></a>

##### ADOFirst

> 📄 Source: [ADOFirst.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADOFirst.htm)

###### ADOFirst


Use this macro to move the current record to the first position in the Recordset.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node types: All


###### Parameters


Recordset - Represents the entire set of records from a base table or the results of an executed command.


Example


Recordset: {MyRecordSet}


 


This example makes the first record of the MyRecordSet the current record.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="adogetcolumnvalue"></a>

##### ADOGetColumnValue

> 📄 Source: [ADOGetColumnValue.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADOGetColumnValue.htm)

###### ADOGetColumnValue


Use this macro to get the value of a specified column from the current record in the specified Recordset.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node types: All


###### Parameters


Recordset - Represents the entire set of records from a base table or the results of an executed command. Value is returned from the current record.


Name of the column - Column whose value should be returned.  


Result - Name of the variable where to store the returned column value.


Example


Recordset: {MyRecordSet}


Name of the column: FIRST_NAME


Result: {$Data}


 


This example takes the value of the FIRST_NAME column of the current record of the MyRecordSet and stores it to the current data item.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="adolast"></a>

##### ADOLast

> 📄 Source: [ADOLast.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADOLast.htm)

###### ADOLast


Use this macro to move the current record to the last position in the Recordset.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node types: All


###### Parameters


Recordset - Represents the entire set of records from a base table or the results of an executed command.


Example


Recordset: {MyRecordSet}


 


This example makes the last record of the MyRecordset the current record.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

<a id="adomove"></a>

##### ADOMove

> 📄 Source: [ADOMove.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADOMove.htm)

###### ADOMove


Use this macro to move the current record forward by the specified distance.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node types: All


###### Parameters


Recordset - Represents the entire set of records from a base table or the results of an executed command.


Distance - Number of records to move forward.


Example


Recordset: {MyRecordSet}


Distance: 5


 


This example moves the current record position in MyRecordset by number of records specified in the Distance.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

<a id="adonext"></a>

##### ADONext

> 📄 Source: [ADONext.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADONext.htm)

###### ADONext


Use this macro to move the current record to the next position in the Recordset.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node types: All


###### Parameters


Recordset - Represents the entire set of records from a base table or the results of an executed command.


Example


Recordset: {MyRecordSet}


 


This example makes the next record of the MyRecordSet the current record.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.
