<a id="adoopenconnection"></a>

##### ADOOpenConnection

> 📄 Source: [ADOOpenConnection.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADOOpenConnection.htm)

###### ADOOpenConnection


Use this macro to either re-open a previously-closed connection or to establish a connection to a different data source. After this macro successfully completes, the connection is live and you can issue other database macros against it and process the results.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node types: All


###### Parameters


Connection Name - Name of the previously-created connection.


Connection String -The information used to establish a connection to a data source.


UserID - The user name to use when establishing the connection.


Password - Password to use when establishing the connection.


Example


EConnection name: {MyConn}


Connection string: driver=\{SQL Server\};server=MySvr


User Id:


Password:


 


This example demonstrates opening a connection MyConn to SQL server on the host MySvr by using Windows authentication.


Note The braces are escaped to avoid treating {SQL Server} as a variable name.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="adoprevious"></a>

##### ADOPrevious

> 📄 Source: [ADOPrevious.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADOPrevious.htm)

###### ADOPrevious


Use this macro to move the current record to the previous position in the Recordset.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node types: All


###### Parameters


Recordset - Represents the entire set of records from a base table or the results of an executed command.


Example


Recordset: {MyRecordSet}


 


This example makes the previous record of the MyRecordSet the current record


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="adorollbacktrans"></a>

##### ADORollbackTrans

> 📄 Source: [ADORollbackTrans.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADORollbackTrans.htm)

###### ADORollbackTrans


Use this macro to cancel any changes made during the current transaction and end the transaction.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node types: All


###### Parameters


Connection - Name of the connection on which to rollback the transaction.


Example


Connection: {MyConn}


This example rollbacks a transaction by using connection stored in the MyConn variable.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="adosetcolumnvalue"></a>

##### ADOSetColumnValue

> 📄 Source: [ADOSetColumnValue.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADOSetColumnValue.htm)

###### ADOSetColumnValue


Use this macro to set the value of a specified column in the current record.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node types: All


###### Parameters


Recordset - Represents the entire set of records from a base table or the results of an executed command. Value is set on the current record.


Name of the column - Column whose value should be set.  


Value - Value to put into the database record.


Example


Recordset: {MyRecordSet}


Name of the column: FIRST_NAME


Value: John


 


This example sets the FIRST_NAME column of the current record of the MyRecordSet to "John".


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="adoupdate"></a>

##### ADOUpdate

> 📄 Source: [ADOUpdate.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ADOUpdate.htm)

###### ADOUpdate


Use this macro to save any changes you made to a Recordset since calling the AddNew method or since changing any field values in an existing record. The Recordset must support updates.


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node types: All


###### Parameters


Recordset - Represents the entire set of records from a base table or the results of an executed command.


Example


Recordset: {MyRecordSet}


 


This example saves any changes you make to the current row of the MyRecordSet.


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post - macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="getupathfordatanode"></a>

##### GetUPathForDataNode

> 📄 Source: [GetUPathForDataNode.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/GetUPathForDataNode.htm)

###### GetUPathForDataNode


Use this macro to return the UPath for the current data node. Path can be returned in two formats: Forward or Backwards. Forward format starts the path from the top most node and moves to the bottom most node. Backward format starts from the bottom most node and moves to the top most node.  


###### Usage


Pre-Macro: Yes


Post-Macro: Yes


Node Type: All nodes


###### Parameters:


Result - Name of the variable in which to store the path value.


Use Forward Format - Indicates if the path should be top-down or bottom-up.


Example


RecordSet: {MyRecordSet} {CurrentDataUPath}


Use Forward Format: True


 


This example demonstrates retrieving the upath of the current data item and storing it into the variable CurrentDataUPath}.


By setting Use Forward Format True the path returned by the macro starts from the top most node and move to bottom most node, such as: Transaction/Segment(ID="ST")/Element(ID="143").


By setting the Use Forward Forward to False the path returned by the macro starts from bottom most node and move to top most node, such as: Element(ID="143").Segment(ID="ST").Transaction.


 


Note Some variables, for example, {$Data}, are only valid in post -macros when the data item has already been generated.

---

<a id="work-with-configuration-files"></a>

### Work with Configuration Files

> 📄 Source: [Book_Work_with_Configuration_Files.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Work_with_Configuration_Files.htm)

#### Work with Configuration Files


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="ldns-configuration-files"></a>

#### LDNS Configuration Files

> 📄 Source: [Book_LDNS_Configuration_Files.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_LDNS_Configuration_Files.htm)

##### LDNS Configuration Files


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="ldns-configuration-overview"></a>

##### LDNS Configuration Overview

> 📄 Source: [LDNS_Configuration_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/LDNS_Configuration_Overview.htm)

###### LDNS Configuration Overview


Logical Data Node Structure (LDNS) configuration files are used by the data parser to control what data is extracted and shown on the LDNS reports and data audit reports. The configuration files attach a name to a location in the guideline.


Examples of the available LDNS reports include:

- Eligibility Request Report
- Eligibility Response Report
- Payer Claim Report
- Provider Payment Report
- Provider Claim Report
- Acknowledgment Report


Note Configuration files are provided for EDI guidelines, but you can create your own configurations for other non-EDI guidelines.


This functionality can be used for multiple business purposes:

- Generating business reports - You may want to generate business reports from the EDI data where the report only needs certain pieces of data from the data file. You can use LDNS configuration files to indicate the data to extract from the data file and how the various data values should be grouped. The output is provided in a XML file. After the XML is produced, either a generic style sheet (XSLT) or a business-specific style sheet can be applied to convert the XML into an HTML report. These reports can then be used to:
- Loading relational databases for business intelligence and business monitoring - By using XEngine (run-time engine) in conjunction with the LDNS configuration file generated here, you can extract and group key values from production data files into user-defined logical nodes. You can load the resulting XML files into a relational database by using the XML load utilities available with most relational databases. You can then aggregate the information for business intelligence, auditing, tracking, and monitoring of process activity.
- Data Splitting - You can separate good data from bad data, or re-package data based on payer id in the case of HIPAA transactions where state prompt-pay laws require payers to process as much of the good data as possible. The LDNS configuration file specifies where the logical breaks occur for any transaction types and actions on each node type can be used to recalculate appropriate EDI control values, such as HL loop indexes, SE segment count, and the rest.

---

<a id="ldns-configuration-editor"></a>

##### LDNS Configuration Editor

> 📄 Source: [LDNS_Configuration_Editor.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/LDNS_Configuration_Editor.htm)

###### LDNS Configuration Editor


LDNS Configuration Editor displays a tree view of logical data nodes in the left pane and details of the selected node in the right pane.


*[Image: LDNS configuration editor]*


You can perform the following actions by using LDNS Configuration Editor toolbar:

- Click Select LDNS Configuration (), to launch [LDNS Configuration Registry](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/LDNS_Configuration_Registry.htm) and select LDNS Configuration.
- Click Import LDNS Configuration (), to import LDNS Configuration from the external file.
- Click Export LDNS Configuration (), to export the configuration to a file.
- Click New LDNS Configuration (), to create new LDNS Configuration. The LDNS Configuration dialog box is displayed:
- Name: A name of LDNS Configuration.
- Description: A description of LDNS Configuration.
- Document Type: The current document type.
- Comment: Your comment in a free form.
- Guideline Type: A type of the guideline.
- Standard: A standard of configuration.
- Version: A version of configuration.
- Transaction Set/ID: Transaction Set or ID of the currently open guideline.
- Click Delete LDNS Configuration (), to remove existing LDNS Configuration. You are asked if you want to delete configuration associated with the selected configuration.

---

<a id="ldns-configuration-registry"></a>

##### LDNS Configuration Registry

> 📄 Source: [LDNS_Configuration_Registry.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/LDNS_Configuration_Registry.htm)

###### LDNS Configuration Registry


The list of all the LDNS configurations is stored in the LDNS Configuration Registry. The sequence of the files in the registry determines which file is used during data analysis.


To view the registry:

- On the LDNS Configuration tab, click Select LDNS Configuration ().
- Click Import LDNS configuration () to import and register an LDNS configuration file stored on your system.
- Click Export LDNS configuration () to export existing LDNS configuration files so that they can be shared with other users or with the run-time engine.
- Click New LDNS configuration () to create a new LDNS configuration file.
- Click Edit LDNS configuration () to modify the description and properties of a registered LDNS configuration file.
- Click Delete LDNS configuration () to remove the file from the registry and/or delete the LDNS configuration file.

---

<a id="add-nodes-and-fields"></a>

##### Add Nodes and Fields

> 📄 Source: [Add_Nodes_and_Fields.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Add_Nodes_and_Fields.htm)

###### Add Nodes and Fields


You can add group nodes, property nodes, and field elements to LDNS configuration files using the LDNS Configuration Editor in the Logical Structure pane. Group nodes can be added by using the mouse or by drag-and-drop operation.


To add a group node by using the mouse:

1. Right-click the tree.
2. Select Insert to add a sibling node or select Insert Child Node, and then select GroupNode. The following dialog box is displayed:
3. To select the node from the guideline in the UPath entry, click Browse.


Browse button displays the guideline tree and the current node on the guideline is selected by default, you can navigate this tree to select a different node.


To add a group node by using drag-and-drop operation:

1. Click a node in the [Guideline Pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EDI_Guideline_Pane.htm).
2. Drag it to LDNS configuration tree.
3. Release the mouse.


Note When dropping on the LDNS configuration tree, you must position the mouse to the parent node where the node belongs otherwise you are not able to drop the item onto the tree.


To add a property node:

1. In the logical structure tree, right-click a node, select Insert Child Node or Insert (to add a property node as a sibling), and then select PropertyNode.
2. In the Property Node dialog box, specify a name and a UPath for the new node, and then click OK.


To add field elements:

1. In the logical structure tree, right-click a node properties where you want to add a field to.
2. Select Insert Child Node, and then select Field.
3. In the Field dialog box, enter a name for the field and specify either a UPath or a value for this field, and then click OK.

---

<a id="add-actions-to-ldns-configuration"></a>

##### Add Actions to LDNS Configuration

> 📄 Source: [Add_Actions_to_LDNS_Configuration.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Add_Actions_to_LDNS_Configuration.htm)

###### Add Actions to LDNS Configuration


An LDNS configuration file can also be used to indicate how the run-time engine (XEngine) should split data files. When data files are split, certain actions need to be performed in order to create valid data files - for example, updating segment counts or HL segment counters.


To insert an action:

1. Right-click the field on which the action should be performed.
2. Choose Insert Child Node, and then click Action.
3. Select the action to associate with the field from the provided list.


Once the action is associated, parameters for that action should be set.


To add parameters:

1. Right-click the action on the tree.
2. Choose Insert Child Node, and then click Parameter.


The following table describes actions and associated parameters that should be set:


| Action name | Description | Parameters |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AddVal | Ads specified value. | None |
| CBC | Change based on Counter. Changes the data value in the output data file for the specified field. | CounterName: Name of the counter which must be previously defined. (LessThan \| EqualTo \| MoreThan): Indicates the how the counter value should be compared and the value to compare it against. Value: If new value should be put into the data element. Remove: If the data element value should be removed. |
| ChildDNCounter | Child Data Node Counter. Counts the number of children data nodes. | Name: Indicates the data node name which should be counted. Update: If the field on which this macro is set should be updated with the counter value. |
| ChildNodePresent | Changes the value of the field when child data nodes are present. | Value: By default, sets the value to 1, but other value can be specified. Child: Name of the child node which should be checked. |
| Counter | Simple counter to count how many times something has occurred. | Name: Name of the counter. StartVal: Initial value, default is set to 1. IncrementBy: How much to increment the counter value by, default is set to 1. ResetAt: When should the counter value be reset. Should be name of the data node. |
| DNDictionary | Obtains the information from the specified Data Node and places it in data. | Value: Mandatory, provides a dictionary macro (for example, "dns.fnm.Interchange.prp.PropName") that should be processed. Leaf: Optional. If specified then dictionary macro would be applied to the leaf DN that initialized streaming (DN to which the rule is applied). Otherwise dictionary macro is applied to DN token (DS macro) belongs to. |
| ParentProperty | Replaces the value with that of the parent property. | Name: Name of the property in the parent data node whose value should be used as the replacement value. DNName: Parent data node name. |
| RecordCounter | Provides segment count value. | None |
| Remove | Removes the value. | None |
| SetVal | Sets the value to specified value. | Value: Sets the value of in the output file to the value specified in this parameter. |
| Variable | Adds a variable to the value. | None |



Once the LDNS configuration file has been created and registered it is ready to be used during data analysis. Please see the Validating Data Files section of Help for additional information on generating the [XML and HTML Logical Data Reports](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/View_XML_and_HTML_Logical_Data_Reports.htm).

---

<a id="character-set-registry"></a>

#### Character Set Registry

> 📄 Source: [Book_Character_Set_Registry.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Character_Set_Registry.htm)

##### Character Set Registry


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="character-set-registry-overview"></a>

##### Character Set Registry Overview

> 📄 Source: [Character_Set_Registry_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Character_Set_Registry_Overview.htm)

###### Character Set Registry Overview


Many standards restrict the symbols that can be included in data files processed by the guidelines for that standard. The list of acceptable symbols is called a Character Set. Those character sets are stored in the Character Set Registry. Some character sets are defined by standards and cannot be changed. Those are known as "system" character sets.


System character sets are stored by default in the product installation folder config\charsets.


The user can create his own character set to override or to extend the system character set. Those are "user" character sets and they can be changed and deleted.


Note User character sets are stored in a single custom character set file called user.cs and is stored in the %ECRootPath%/common folder.


Each character set has a key. It defines standards for which this character set applies. The key consists of five fields:

- Guideline type
- Standard
- Version
- ID
- Name - required for some standards - for example, EDIFACT


Key components are used to match the character set to a guideline. You can either enter a specific value or leave an asterisk in each field. An asterisk indicates that it matches any value of the corresponding guideline attribute. For example, if ID is set to *, that key matches any transaction set.

---

<a id="character-set-uses"></a>

##### Character Set Uses

> 📄 Source: [Character_Set_Uses.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Character_Set_Uses.htm)

###### Character Set Uses


When you use the [Data Generator](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Overview.htm) to create test data, the Data Generator engine picks up a matching character set and uses only the defined symbols to create data.


When validating the data, the [Analyzer](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Analyzer_Overview.htm) engine picks up the matching character set and flags an error if it encounters a symbol that is not present in the character set.


*[Image: Character Set error]*


Note The Character Set is only applied to alpha-numeric fields.

---

<a id="work-with-the-character-set-registry"></a>

##### Work with the Character Set Registry

> 📄 Source: [Work_with_the_Character_Set_Registry.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Work_with_the_Character_Set_Registry.htm)

###### Work with the Character Set Registry


To launch the Character Set Registry:

- On the Advanced tab, in the Environment group, click Character Sets.
- [View character set properties](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/View_Character_Set_Properties.htm). Using this option you can view properties of the system character sets or edit properties of the user character sets.
- Create new user character sets.
- Delete user character sets.
- [Duplicate an existing character set](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Duplicate_Character_Sets.htm). This can be useful if you want to create a user character set based on the system character set.
- [Export and import character sets](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_and_Import_Character_Sets.htm).

---

<a id="view-character-set-properties"></a>

##### View Character Set Properties

> 📄 Source: [View_Character_Set_Properties.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/View_Character_Set_Properties.htm)

###### View Character Set Properties


Using character set properties dialog box you can view properties of the system character sets and edit properties of the user character sets.


To view or edit character set properties:

- In the [Character Set Registry](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Work_with_the_Character_Set_Registry.htm), click one of the character sets.
- On the toolbar, click Properties ().

- General information: Specify the description of the character set.
- Key: Enter the key components. For more information about key components, see [Character Set Definitions](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Character_Set_Registry_Overview.htm).
- Charset: You can enter symbols by either typing letters or their Hex codes if the symbol is not printable.

---

<a id="add-a-character-set"></a>

##### Add a Character Set

> 📄 Source: [Add_a_Character_Set.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Add_a_Character_Set.htm)

###### Add a Character Set


You can create your own character set to restrict symbols allowed for certain data files.


Note User character sets are stored in a single custom character set file called user.cs and is stored in the %ECRootPath%/common folder.


To add a character set:

1. On the Advanced tab, in the Environment group, click Character Set Registry.
2. On the Character Set Registry toolbar, click New ().
3. Provide information for any of the following properties:

- General information: Specify the description of the character set.
- Key: Enter the key components. For more information about key components, see [Character Set Definitions](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Character_Set_Registry_Overview.htm).
- Charset: You can enter symbols by either typing letters or their Hex codes if the symbol is not printable.


Rather than creating an entirely new character set, you can modify an existing character set by [duplicating an existing character set](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Duplicate_Character_Sets.htm) and making the required modifications.

---

<a id="duplicate-character-sets"></a>

##### Duplicate Character Sets

> 📄 Source: [Duplicate_Character_Sets.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Duplicate_Character_Sets.htm)

###### Duplicate Character Sets


While working with character sets you may want to customize properties of one of the existing character sets and save it with another name. In this case, you can duplicate properties of one of the character sets in the Character Registry.


Note User character sets are stored in a single custom character set file called user.cs and is stored in the %ECRootPath%/common folder.


To duplicate character set properties:

1. In the [Character set Registry](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Work_with_the_Character_Set_Registry.htm), click the character set you want to duplicate.
2. On the toolbar, click Duplicate ().
3. Edit any of the following properties:

---

<a id="export-and-import-character-sets"></a>

##### Export and Import Character Sets

> 📄 Source: [Export_and_Import_Character_Sets.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_and_Import_Character_Sets.htm)

###### Export and Import Character Sets


You can export a system or user character set to a file for the further use.


To export a character set:

1. On the Character Set Registry toolbar, click Export ().
2. Navigate to the place where you want to store your character set. For use with XEngine, this should be in the ${XERoot}\XEngine\config\charsets folder.
3. In the File name box, type a file name, and then click Save.


To import a character set:

1. On the Character Set Registry toolbar, click Import ().
2. Navigate to the location of the character set, and then click Open.
3. The set is displayed in the Registry.

---

<a id="command-prompt-execution"></a>

### Command Prompt Execution

> 📄 Source: [Book_Command_Prompt_Execution.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Command_Prompt_Execution.htm)

#### Command Prompt Execution


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="overview-of-command-prompt-execution"></a>

#### Overview of Command Prompt Execution

> 📄 Source: [Overview_of_Command_Prompt_Execution.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Overview_of_Command_Prompt_Execution.htm)

##### Overview of Command Prompt Execution


You can now launch many of the individual product components from a command prompt. This is especially useful when you need to more closely integrate your applications with the product's various components.   


Command prompt launch is especially useful in case of the Analyzer since it can be used to integrate the Analyzer with your help desk or operations support system. This allows end users who are troubleshooting a production problem to easily launch the Analyzer with a specific data file and guideline, making it easier to determine the cause of the problem.

---

<a id="command-prompt-options"></a>

#### Command Prompt Options

> 📄 Source: [Command_Prompt_Options.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Command_Prompt_Options.htm)

##### Command Prompt Options


Available command prompt options can be viewed by executing SpecBuilder.exe followed by /?.


To view command prompt options:

1. Open a command prompt window.
2. Navigate to the folder where the program is installed.
3. Type SpecBuilder.exe -?.
4. Click OK to continue with command prompt execution.


When using the switches, the following rules apply:

- <folder> - Opens each guideline from the specified folder in SpecBuilder.
- <data> - if not specified, the default data file location is used.
- <guideline> - Guideline file path or standards database path. If not specified, the default guideline location is used.
- <envelope> - File path for envelope database. All specified envelopes are merged into one envelope in specified order - for example, first /e occurrence specifies the Interchange envelope, the second /e occurrence specifies the Group envelope. If not specified, the default guideline location is used.


These additional factors apply to the use of envelopes:

- If you do not specify an envelope and the guideline does not have envelopes, you are prompted for envelopes.
- If you do not specify an envelope and the guideline has envelopes, the existing envelope is used.
- If you specify an envelope and the guideline has envelopes, the existing envelope is removed and external links are created to the specified envelope.
- If you specify an envelope and the guideline does not have envelopes, external links are created to the specified envelope.

---

<a id="execute-analyzer-from-a-command-prompt"></a>

#### Execute Analyzer from a Command Prompt

> 📄 Source: [Execute_Analyzer_From_Command_Prompt.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Execute_Analyzer_From_Command_Prompt.htm)

##### Execute Analyzer From a Command Prompt


To execute Analyzer from a command prompt:

1. Open a command prompt window and change the current directory to where the application is installed.
2. To execute the Analyzer, you can pick from the following options:
3. Once you press Enter, the Analyzer wizard is started and the Analyzer results window is displayed.

---

<a id="data-comparator-command-line-operation"></a>

#### Data Comparator Command Line Operation

> 📄 Source: [Data_Comparator_Command_Line_Operation.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparator_Command_Line_Operation.htm)

##### Data Comparator Command Line Operation


There is an ability to work with Data Comparator by using the command line.


To execute the Data Comparator from a command prompt: 

1. Open a command prompt window and change the current directory to where the application is installed.
2. To execute the Data Comparator, you can pick from the following parameters:


| Parameter | Description | Required |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Comparing multiple pairs of data files** | | |
| -i <InfoFilename> | Configuration file name | Yes |
| -l <logFileName> | The log file name. No log file is created if missing. | No |
| -t <tmpFolder> | The folder to swap data to when working with large data files. User’s temp folder is used if not specified. | No |
| Comparing a single pair of data files | | |
| -s <srcDataFile> | Source data file name | Yes |
| -d <desDataFile> | Destination data file name | Yes |
| -sg <srcRefGuidelineFile> | Source reference guideline file name | Yes |
| -dg <desRefGuidelineFile> | Destination reference guideline name. If not specified, the source reference guideline will be used. | No |
| -o <dcOptionsFile> | Data comparison options file. If missing, last used of default options are used. | No |
| -r <reportFileName> | The difference report output file name. No difference report is created if missing. | No |
| -f <RTF, PDF, HTML,WXML> | The format of the difference report. One of the four values must be specified. It is used only if the -r parameter exists. If missing or wrong value is specified, PDF will be used. If the report file name has the proper extension, this option is not required and the extension takes precedence. | No |
| -l <logFileName> | The log file name. No log file is created if missing. | No |
| -t <tmpFolder> | The folder to swap data to when working with large data files. User’s temp folder is used if not specified. | No |



Note These two usages are allowed to be used in the same command. See Execution Example 3. User can save the options from Data Comparator user interface and pass that file to the command with the -o option. The command options can be entered in any order. <tmpFolder> is only used when comparing large data.


###### Execution Examples:


Example 1


DataComparatorUtil -i <InfoFileName> [-l <logFileName>] [-t <tmpFolder>]


Example 2


DataComparatorUtil -s <srcDataFile> -d <desDataFile> -sg <srcGuidelineFile> [-dg <desGuidelineFileName>] [-o <dcOptionsFile>] [-r <report> [-f <RTF, PDF, HTML or WXML>]] [-l <logFileName>] [-t <tmpFolder>]


Example 3 (Combining example 1 and 2)


DataComparatorUtil -i <InfoFileName> -s <srcDataFile> -d <desDataFile> -sg <srcGuidelineFile> [-dg <desGuidelineFile>] [-o <dcOptionsFile>] [-r <report> [-f <RTF, PDF, HTML or WXML>]] [-l <logFileName>] [-t <tmpFolder>]


###### Configuration file <InfoFilename>


The configuration file is an XML file that stores the properties used by the data comparator when comparing data files. It is mainly used when comparing multiple pairs of data files but it can also be configured for comparing a single set of data files. To use the configuration file, append its location path as value for the -i parameter in the command line.


To create a configuration file:

1. Place the source and destination data files in two different folders. Make sure the names of the data files to be compared against each other match. For more options, see Configuration file variations.
2. Place the source and destination guideline files in two different folders. Make sure the names of the guideline files match the names of the data files that will them. For more options, see Configuration file variations.
3. Create an XML file with the following content:[Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)Configuration File ``` ```
4. Append the location path of the configuration file as value for the -i parameter in the command line.


###### Configuration file variations


The configuration file can be customized to meet the user's needs.

 *Click to view variations of the configuration file When the source data files use one guideline and each destination data file uses its own guideline file (files must match by name*):Copy

```

  
      
      
      
  

```

**Note** If a destination guideline is missing, the source guideline is used.

 When source data files and destination data files use their own guidelines:[Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)
Configuration File

```

  
      
      
      
  

```

**Note** If a destination guideline is missing, the source guideline is used.

 When source data files and destination data files use the same guideline:[Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)


 When each source data file and destination data file use their own guideline from a single folder (*files must match by name*):[Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)

You can also compare single pairs of data files using the configuration file:

<a id="launch-other-applications-from-a-command-prompt"></a>

#### Launch Other Applications from a Command Prompt

> 📄 Source: [Launch_Other_Applications_from_Command_Prompt.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Launch_Other_Applications_from_Command_Prompt.htm)

##### Launch Other Applications from a Command Prompt


To launch other applications from a command prompt:

1. Open a command prompt window and navigate to the folder where SpecBuilder.exe is installed.
2. Use the following format to execute a command: SpecBuilder.exe -t tool.


| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| DataAnalyzer | Use to launch Analyzer. |
| DataGenerator | Use to launch Data Generator. |
| SpecGenerator | Use to launch SpecGenerator. |
| DataDictionaryGenerator | Use to launch Data Dictionary Generator. |



Example 1


Launch Data Generator Wizard SpecBuilder.exe -t DataGenerator


Example 2


 Launch Data Dictionary Generator SpecBuilder.exe -t DataDictionaryGenerator


Note The preceding commands are case-sensitive, so type the names exactly as shown earlier.

<a id="run-setup-in-silent-mode"></a>

#### Run Setup in Silent Mode

> 📄 Source: [Run_Setup_in_Silent_Mode.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Run_Setup_in_Silent_Mode.htm)

##### Run Setup in Silent Mode


You can use command line to run SpecBuilder in silent mode. SpecBuilder is installed with all the default settings.


To get the list of all the available setup command line parameters please type: `"setup.exe /?"`. For the silent installation use the following command line parameter: "`qb`".


In this mode only progress window and error messages are displayed.

<a id="data-dictionaries"></a>

## Data Dictionaries

> 📄 Source: [Book_Data_Dictionaries.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Data_Dictionaries.htm)

### Data Dictionaries


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="data-dictionary-overview"></a>

### Data Dictionary Overview

> 📄 Source: [Data_Dictionary_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Dictionary_Overview.htm)

#### Data Dictionary Overview


With Data Dictionaries, you can specify values for data being generated and macros to be run against the data. The [Data Generator](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Overview.htm) engine follows the options selected in the wizard and selects the elements from the guideline for which to generate data. The engine then checks each node in the guideline tree and checks the dictionaries in the order that they are selected to see if there is a match for that element in the data dictionary. If a match is found, the value or macro in the dictionary is applied to the generated data node. If there is more than one match within a dictionary, the match with the most specific UPath is selected. If no match is found in any of the selected dictionaries, the Data Generator creates the data value based on the data type and length of the element.


There are several ways of creating a data dictionary:

- [Data Dictionary Generator](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Data_Dictionary_Generator_Wizard.htm) - use when you are going to use the Data Generator to generate a new data file; creates a data dictionary from a data file and a guideline.
- [Data Dictionary Editor](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Dictionary_Editor_Overview.htm) - use when you are going to use the Data Generator to update an existing data file with new nodes.


For more information, see [Data Dictionary Examples](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Dictionary_Examples.htm).

---

<a id="data-dictionary-examples"></a>

### Data Dictionary Examples

> 📄 Source: [Data_Dictionary_Examples.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Dictionary_Examples.htm)

#### Data Dictionary Examples


###### Scenario 1


The guideline node for which data is generated is Element 127 in Segment REF. Two data dictionaries have been selected: Dictionary A and Dictionary B in that order. Dictionary A has no value specified for Element (ID="127"). Dictionary B has an entry for Element (ID="127") which contains two values - "Value 1" and "Value 2".


###### Data Generation Action


The Data Generator first looks for a match in Dictionary A. In this scenario, Dictionary A does not have any match. The Data Generator next looks in Dictionary B. It finds a match with Element (ID="127") in dictionary B. Even though the REF segment is not specified in the Dictionary, this is still considered to be a wildcard match. The Data Generator then picks a value from the dictionary entry DataSet.


###### Scenario 2


Same as previous with one addition. In Dictionary B, there is an additional entry for Element (ID="127") with a UPath of Element(ID="127").Segment(ID="REF").


###### Data Generation Action


As before, the Data Generator does not find a match in Dictionary A. However, in Dictionary B it finds two matches - one with a UPath of Element (ID=127) and another with a UPath of Element (ID="127"). Segment (ID="REF"). Since the latter match is more specific, the Data Generator uses the values for this entry to generate data values.


Once a match is selected, the Data Generator executes any Pre-Macros defined for that dictionary item before generating the data. One of the available pre-macros can change the Repeat Count of the current node. If the Repeat Count is zero, the Data Generator does not create the data value for the node.


After generating the data node's value, the Data Generator executes any Post-Macro defined for the selected item.

---

<a id="understand-paths"></a>

### Understand Paths

> 📄 Source: [Understand_Paths.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Understand_Paths.htm)

#### Understand Paths


Paths identify the location of items in the guideline. While working with data dictionary items, there are three different types of paths that can be used:

- Guideline Path: This path is used for dictionary items. This path links items in the Data Dictionary with the guideline nodes, enabling the Data Generator engine to find matches for the guideline node. When new guideline items are created on the Data Dictionary tab, this path is automatically generated. If the Data Dictionary Editor is used to create items, this path must be entered manually or created by using the [Path Builder](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Path_Builder.htm).


Note You can quickly see the path for a certain node by using UPath Editor. For more information, see [Select a Guideline Node](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Select_a_Guideline_Node.htm).


The following types of paths are only used by advanced users who are using macros to control the operation of the Data Generator.

- Data Path: This path is used in some of the macros (GetData and SetData) that are provided for use in the Data Dictionary. With these paths, you can refer to data values that were previously generated by the Data Generator engine. These paths have the following format:
- Paths start from top to bottom. The first entry is the interchange and the last entry is the element.
- Nodes are separated by "/".
- When the same node occurs more than once, an index or property must be specified to uniquely identify the node.


Example


Element[1] - This points to the first element of the current segment.


/Interchange/Group/Transaction/Loop/Loop/Segment(ID="LX")/Element[1] - This points to the first element of Segment with an ID of LX.

- Data Dictionary Path: With this path, you can refer to other entries in the data dictionary. This is useful for macros such as RepeatBasedOnValue. These paths have the following format:
- Paths start with the dictionary name, followed by the node type, node ID, and node name.
- Path items are separated by "/".
- Paths ends with either "Data" or "Repeat" keywords.


Examples


`270/Element/1037/1037-NM1/Data` - This points to the Data Dictionary named 270, Node Type of Element, Node ID of 1037 and with item name of 1037-NM1 and to the Data Data Set.


`270/Element/1037/1037-NM1/Repeat` - This points to the Data Dictionary named 270, Node Type of Element, Node ID of 1037 and with item name of 1037-NM1 and to the Repeat Data Set.

<a id="data-dictionary-editor"></a>

### Data Dictionary Editor

> 📄 Source: [Book_Data_Dictionary_Editor.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Data_Dictionary_Editor.htm)

#### Data Dictionary Editor


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="data-dictionary-editor-overview"></a>

#### Data Dictionary Editor Overview

> 📄 Source: [Data_Dictionary_Editor_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Dictionary_Editor_Overview.htm)

| # Data Dictionary Editor Overview | |
| --- | --- |



You can create and edit data dictionaries by using the Data Dictionary Editor.


To launch the Data Dictionary Editor, click Data Dictionary Editor, on the Tools tab.


The following dialog box is displayed:


*[Image: Data Dictionary Editor]*


You can perform the following operations by using the icons on the toolbar:

- - Create a new data dictionary.
- - Modify the dictionary name and description.
- - Delete the selected data dictionary.
- - Import a data dictionary generated in a previous version of SpecBuilder or generated by an external application.
- - Export the data dictionary to an XML file so that it can be shared with other SpecBuilder users or used in an different application.
- - Start the Data Dictionary Generation Wizard to generate a data dictionary from an existing EDI data file.


To work with specific dictionary items, right-click the item and use the context menu.

---

<a id="add-modify-and-delete-dictionaries"></a>

#### Add, Modify, and Delete Dictionaries

> 📄 Source: [Add_Modify_and_Delete_Dictionaries.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Add_Modify_and_Delete_Dictionaries.htm)

##### Add, Modify, and Delete Dictionaries


To add a dictionary:

1. On the Tools tab, in the Data Dictionary group, click Data Dictionary Editor.
2. On the toolbar, click New Dictionary ().
3. Type a Name for the dictionary. The dictionary name must be unique.
4. In the Type list, select the dictionary type.
5. Type a Description for the dictionary.
6. Click OK.


Note You can also add a dictionary from the Data Dictionaries. On the Data Dictionary tab toolbar, click New Dictionary () and follow the preceding instructions.


*[Image: New Dictionary Properties]*


To modify a dictionary (can only be done in the Data Dictionary Editor):

1. Click the dictionary to modify and, on the Data Dictionary Editor toolbar, click Modify Dictionary.
2. Make any changes to the Name, Description, or Type that are required.
3. Click OK. 


To delete a dictionary (can only be done in the Data Dictionary Editor):

1. Click the dictionary to be removed and, on the toolbar, click Delete Dictionary ().
2. With a confirmation dialog box, you can delete the entire dictionary file, or remove the dictionary from the list of registered dictionaries. When unregistered only, you may re-use it by importing the dictionary file again.


Note Once the dictionary file is deleted, it cannot be recovered and needs to be recreated.

---

<a id="add-dictionary-items"></a>

#### Add Dictionary Items

> 📄 Source: [Add_Dictionary_Items.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Add_Dictionary_Items.htm)

##### Add Dictionary Items


To add items to the dictionary:

1. In the [Data Dictionary Editor](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Dictionary_Editor_Overview.htm), right-click a node, and then click New Node.
2. In the Name box, type a name for the node.
3. In the Loop Type box, if this node is part of a logical loop that you are using to modify the normal sequential processing of loops, select a loop type. Otherwise, leave the default. For information about logical loops, see [Logical Loops](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Logical_Loops.htm).
4. In the Logical Loops group, if this node is part of a logical loop, click New (). See [Logical Loops](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Logical_Loops.htm) for information about creating a logical loop.
5. In the Path group, type the path for the node or click Build () to use the [Path Builder](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Path_Builder.htm).
6. Click Next.
7. On the Data Sets tab, click Data and set the Selection method and possible Values for the element. See [Set DataSets Data](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Set_DataSets_Data.htm) for instructions.
8. On the Data Sets tab, click Repeat and set the Selection method and possible Values for the element. See [Set DataSets Repeat](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Set_DataSets_Repeat.htm) for instructions.
9. Click Next to [Specify Pre and Post Macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Specify_Pre_and_Post_Macros.htm).
10. Click Finish.

---

<a id="path-builder"></a>

#### Path Builder

> 📄 Source: [Path_Builder.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Path_Builder.htm)

##### Path Builder


When you use the Data Dictionary Editor to add a new node, you must create a path for the new node. You can either type a path in the Path box or use the Path Builder by clicking Build ().


*[Image: Dictionary node wizard]*


To build a path by using the Path Builder:


On the Path Nodes toolbar, click New Step () and the following dialog box is displayed:


*[Image: New Step Name]*


Choose the step you want to insert in your path. Choose ? to insert any step. Choose * to insert any number of steps.


You can perform the following operations with the steps:

- Click Delete Step (), to remove the step from the path.
- Click Move Up/Down (), to change the location of the step in the path.


You can also add attributes to any of the steps.

- Click New Attribute (), to choose the name or enter a value for the step.
- Click Delete Attribute (), to remove the selected attribute and its value.


Whether the paths are built automatically or by using the preceding dialog box or manually entered, they all follow the same format:

- Paths are listed from lowest node first to the highest node. So, Element node is listed first and Transaction node is listed last.
- Syntax for the path is node type and in parenthesis property name and value, optionally followed by "." and the parent node type and parent node type properties.


Example


Element(ID="127") - This triggers a match for any element which has an ID of 127.


Element(ID="127",Name="Reference Identification").Segment(ID="REF") - This triggers a match for Element 127 which has a name of "Reference Identification" and is in Segment with ID of REF.

- Asterisk "*" can be used as a wildcard character.


Example


Element(ID="127").*.Loop(ID="2000A") - This triggers a match for Element 127, for all segments which are in Loop with ID of 2000A.

---

<a id="modify-and-delete-dictionary-items"></a>

#### Modify and Delete Dictionary Items

> 📄 Source: [Modify_and_Delete_Dictionary_Items.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Modify_and_Delete_Dictionary_Items.htm)

##### Modify and Delete Dictionary Items


To modify items in the dictionary:


There are several ways to modify items in the dictionary:

- Select an item in the Data Dictionaries pane, and then click Modify Item ().  
- In the [Data Dictionary Editor](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Dictionary_Editor_Overview.htm), navigate to the item in the tree and modify information in the right pane.


To delete items from the dictionary:


- In the Data Dictionaries pane, click the item and, on the Data Dictionaries toolbar, click Delete ().  
- In the Data Dictionary Editor, right-click a node and select Delete Node.

---

<a id="logical-loops"></a>

#### Logical Loops

> 📄 Source: [Logical_Loops.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Logical_Loops.htm)

##### Logical Loops


Logical Loop Repeat Types


With logical loops in the data dictionary, you can group sibling loops in a way that is different from the normal sequential looping. For example, you might have the following structure:


Loop 2000A .... Loop 2000B .... Loop 2000C Without using logical loops in data dictionary, if you would like to repeat the looping structure two times, the outcome of the data is:


Loop 2000A Loop 2000A Loop 2000B Loop 2000B Loop 2000C Loop 2000C


However, the desired structure is:


Loop 2000A Loop 2000B Loop 2000C Loop 2000A Loop 2000B Loop 2000C


To create a data dictionary to support the preceding guideline structure:

1. Right-click the starting loop (Loop 2000A), and then click New Node.
2. In the Loop Type box, click Begin.
3. In the **Logical Loops** group, click New.
4. In the Name box, type a name for the loop.
5. In the Repeat group, click Count.
6. In the Value box, type the number of times the loop should repeat.
7. Right-click the ending loop (Loop 2000C), and then click New Node.
8. In the Loop Type box, choose End.
9. In the **Logical Loops** group, click the New.
10. In the Name box, type the same name as in step 4.
11. In the Repeat group, click Count.
12. In the Value box, type the same number as in step 6.


Note Multiple logical loops can begin and end on any nodes. Additionally, logical loops can be nested inside each other as long as the inside loop ends prior to the outside loop ending or at the same level.


###### Logical Loop Repeat Types


Loops can be repeated based on the following:

- Count - sets the loop to repeat a specific number of times.
- Condition - sets the loop to repeat based on the value of a variable - when the variable is true, the loop repeats; when the variable is false, the loop is not repeated.


To set logical loop generation based on condition:

1. In the Begin loop type, in the **Logical Loops** group, click New.
2. In the Name box, type a name for the loop.
3. In the Repeat group, click Condition.
4. In the Value box, type the name of the variable to check, enclosed in curly brackets ({}).


Note The variable needs to be defined in the User Variables section of the dictionary (see [Define User Variables](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Define_User_Variables.htm)) and the value assigned by using the SetVariable macro.


The Data Generator checks the value of the variable at the start of the loop and if the value is false, the logical loop is not generated.

---

<a id="set-datasets-repeat"></a>

##### Set DataSets Repeat


On the Data Sets tab, click Repeat to indicate how many times an item should be repeated and how the repeat values should be chosen by the Data Generator. Multiple repeat values can be specified as shown on the following image:


*[Image: Specify multiple repeat values]*


The preceding image indicates that this item can either be repeated two times or three times. Which repeat value is chosen depends on the value in the Selection box, which in this case is Random. Therefore, the data generation engine randomly picks between two times or three times.


To set repeat options:

1. In the Selection box, choose one of the following:
2. On the Values toolbar, click New and type a repeat value or choose from one of the following:
3. Continue adding values until they are all entered.

<a id="specify-pre-and-post-macros"></a>

#### Specify Pre and Post Macros

> 📄 Source: [Specify_Pre_and_Post_Macros.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Specify_Pre_and_Post_Macros.htm)

##### Specify Pre and Post Macros


Pre and post macros are specified in the Data Dictionary and allow you to further control what data is generated and what values to generate for each data element. This section describes the general use of pre and post macros. See [Database Macros Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Database_Macros_Overview.htm) for more information about each macro. For each node, data generation has three steps: Pre-data generation, data generation processing, and post-data generation. Macros can only be added to pre- and post-processing steps.   


###### Pre Macros


Pre macros are executed prior to the data generation engine processing the node. These are useful for situations like initializing variables, resetting counters, setting indexes, capturing data from pre-existing segments, elements, and the rest.


Caution: You should not use the $Data property in a Pre macro. Getting the value of $Data in a Pre macro returns an empty value as this value has not been generated, and setting the $Data property gets overwritten by the data generation engine.


###### Post Macros


Post macros are executed after the data generation engine processes the node. These are useful to further manipulate the data that has just been generated, such as overriding the generated data, or for adding values to accumulators and counters.


To specify a Pre or Post macro:

1. In the pre or post macro dialog box, click New.
2. Click the macro to use, and then click **OK**.
3. Specify any parameters for the macro.
4. Click Apply.

---

<a id="define-user-variables"></a>

#### Define User Variables

> 📄 Source: [Define_User_Variables.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Define_User_Variables.htm)

##### Define User Variables


With User variables, you can store values that can be referenced in the Data Dictionary macros.


To declare a user variable:

1. On the navigation pane, click Data Dictionary Editor.
2. In the tree view, right-click UserVariables, and then click New Variable.
3. In the Variable box, type the name of the variable. Variable names must be enclosed in curly {} brackets.
4. In the optional Description box, type a description.
5. Click OK.
6. The right pane displays the User Variable information. You can edit the variable name and description in that pane. The Usages section displays any instances of the variable's usage.

---

> 📄 Source: [Import_Export_Dictionary.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_Export_Dictionary.htm)

##### Import/Export a Dictionary


[Data Dictionary Editor](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Dictionary_Editor_Overview.htm) allows dictionaries to be imported from an XML file or a previous dictionary file. Dictionaries can also be exported to an XML format.


To import a dictionary:

1. Start the [Data Dictionary Editor](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Dictionary_Editor_Overview.htm), and then click the Import Dictionary ().
2. In the File name box, type the file name to import.
3. Click Next.
4. In the Dictionary name box, type a name for the dictionary.
5. Click Next.


To export a dictionary:

1. Start the [Data Dictionary Editor](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Dictionary_Editor_Overview.htm) and select a dictionary node.
2. Click Export Dictionary ().
3. In the File name box, type the name of the dictionary to be saved
4. Click Finish.

##### Preprocessing Options


If you want to clean up the data file, you can use the Preprocessor. With the Preprocessor, you can eliminate any terminator string or block characters in the data.

1. In the Clean Up Data File dialog box, select the Use Preprocessor check box.
2. In the Parameters box, select a saved parameter file to use, if applicable.
3. Do one of the following:
4. To save these settings, click Save As and, in the Preprocessor Parameters Name server, enter a name or select an existing parameter name to modify its definition.
5. Enter a path to save the modified data file or click Browse to locate a folder in which to save the modified data file.
6. Click Next. For standards such as PFF, DFF, and NCPDP, go to step 8.
7. In the Parsing result dialog box, do one of the following:
8. For standards such as PFF, DFF, and NCPDP Batch, in the Record Parsing Information dialog box, select the record delimiter used in your data file or click Next to accept the default delimiter.

##### Data Structure


To select a node to validate:

1. In the Data Structure dialog box, select a node to validate. The node's properties are defined in the right pane. By default, the entire document is validated. To limit the validation to specific levels, highlight the appropriate folder.

<a id="guideline-selection"></a>

#### Guideline Selection

> 📄 Source: [Data_Dictionary_Guideline_Selection.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Dictionary_Guideline_Selection.htm)

##### Guideline Selection

1. In the Guideline selection dialog box, specify the guideline to use for validation. This can be either from the Standards database or from a specific guideline file.

1. Click Next and go to Envelope Segments.


For more information on Envelope Segments, see [Select Envelope Segments](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Dictionary_Select_Envelope_Segments.htm).

<a id="select-envelope-segments"></a>

#### Select Envelope Segments

> 📄 Source: [Data_Dictionary_Select_Envelope_Segments.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Dictionary_Select_Envelope_Segments.htm)

##### Select Envelope Segments


Note Envelopes are not defined for the XML Schema documents, so this option is not available for XML Schema guidelines.


###### Guidelines with envelope segments:


Select one of the following options:

- Use the existing Envelope Segments: Select this option to use the envelope specified in your guideline. Click Next.
- Remove existing and select new Envelope Segments from the database: Select this option to replace envelope segments with ones from the Envelopes database. Click Next, select the envelope segment from the database, click Next, and go to Using Envelope Segments.
- Remove existing and use Envelope Segments from the external guideline file: Select this option to use envelopes stored in an external file. Click Next, select the guideline file to use, and then click Next.
- Remove existing and use Envelope Segments from multiple guideline files: Select this option to use envelopes stored in multiple external files. This option is useful to match the structure of envelope guidelines to that used by the Run-Time engine where Interchange guideline and Functional Group guidelines are stored in separate .ecs files. See "Selecting envelope segments from multiple guideline files" below.


###### Guidelines without envelope segments:

- Select Envelope Segments from the Standards Database: Select this option to add envelope segments from the Standards database. Click Next, select the envelope segment from the database, click Next, and go to Using Envelope Segments.
- Process without Envelope Segments: Select this option to go ahead and analyze the file without envelope segments.
- Use Envelope Segments from the external guideline file: Select this option to add envelope segments stored in an external file.
- Use Envelope Segments from multiple guideline files: Select this option to use envelope segments stored in multiple external files. This option is useful to match the structure of envelope guidelines to that used by the Run-Time engine where Interchange guideline and Functional Group guidelines are stored in separate .ecs files. See "Selecting envelope segments from multiple guideline files" below.


###### Selecting envelope segments from multiple guideline files

1. Specify a guideline file for the Interchange level and one for the Functional Group and then click Next.

1. Click Next to go to the next wizard page.

---

<a id="optimization-options-page"></a>

#### Optimization Options Page

> 📄 Source: [Optimization_Options_Page.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Optimization_Options_Page.htm)

##### Optimization Options Page


On the Optimization Options wizard page, click one of the following optimization methods to use when creating the data dictionary nodes:


*[Image: Optimization Options page]*

- Create one dictionary item with full path for each data element occurrence in the data file: Creates a fully-qualified path for each element and allows data values to be duplicated for the node. For example, if Element-127 is used in two different segments then it creates two different entries in the dictionary, one for each segment.
- Create one dictionary item with short path for all occurrences of a data element (that is the same node ID within the data file: Creates a non-qualified path for each element and allows data values to be duplicated for the node. For example, if Element-127 was used in two different segments with the same value =" REF ID", one dictionary element (Element-127) having the same data value (REF ID) is created.
- Create one dictionary item with short path for all occurrences of a data element with the same ID and same value within the data file: Creates a non-qualified path for each element, but does not allow data values to be duplicated for the node.
- Create dictionary items for empty data elements: Creates an entry even if a data value was not present in the data file.
- Create dictionary items for excluded guideline nodes: Creates an entry even if the guideline nodes are excluded in the SpecBuilder guideline.

- On the Dictionary Information wizard page, in the Name, type a unique dictionary name.
- In the Description, type an optional description for the dictionary.


Click Next to start the generation process.
