<a id="title-setup"></a>

##### Title Setup

> 📄 Source: [Title_Setup.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Title_Setup.htm)

###### Title Setup


Use this page to define a title for the exported document. The title is displayed at the top of your Excel document. See the following example of the sheet title:


*[Image: Title Setup]*


To define the title:

1. In the Title Setup page of the wizard, click Browse () to display the Title designer.
2. Type the text.
3. Click OK. A preview of the title is displayed in the Sheet Title Preview box.
4. Click Next to go to [Setup Source and Target Sections](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Setup_Source_and_Target_Sections.htm).

<a id="setup-source-and-target-sections"></a>

##### Setup Source and Target Sections

> 📄 Source: [Setup_Source_and_Target_Sections.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Setup_Source_and_Target_Sections.htm)

###### Setup Source and Target Sections


Use the Setup Source Section and Setup Target Section pages of the wizard to develop the content of your document. You need to define section headers for the source and target guidelines and specify the columns to be displayed in each section.


Note If you have chosen to publish your document from source to target, then the source section header (Map from SAP IDoc) is displayed in the left part of your document and the target section header (X12 850) in the right as shown in the following picture:


*[Image: Map from SAP IDoc to X12 850]*


For more information on how to change publishing order, refer to [Publishing Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publishing_Options.htm).


To create a section header:

1. On the Setup Source Section page, in the Section Header section, or click Browse to navigate to the location of the file to launch the Header designer.
2. Type text for the header.
3. Click OK. A preview of the header is displayed in the Section Header section.


After you have provided a section header, you need to define columns for your document and add content to the columns.


To add a section column:

1. Under the Section Columns section, click Insert new column ().
2. In the dialog box, type a column name, and then click OK. This is displayed in the following document:
3. Continue adding column names for all the information that you want displayed.


To define column content:


*[Image: Section Columns]*

1. In the Section Columns section, or click Browse to navigate to the location of the file next to the column cell.
2. Type the content or choose a variable from the drop-down list of all available attributes.
3. Click OK to go back to the Setup Source Section wizard.
4. Continue adding content for each cell.
5. After you have set the options for the source guideline section, click Next and repeat the same procedure for the target guideline section.


To edit column content:

- Right-click the cell and do one of the following:
- Click Edit to launch Column Content Designer.
- Click Clear to remove the contents of the cell.
- Click Repeat to use the same content as the parent node has in the same column. This works for derived guidelines as well. For example, if you choose Repeat for Segment it repeats the content of its parent node. In the following example, the contents of all Transaction Segments display the Transaction Name, and all Loop Segments display ID.
- Click Copy to copy the content of the cell.
- Click Paste to paste the copied content in another cell.

### Work with Map Objects

> 📄 Source: [Book_Work_with_Map_Objects.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Work_with_Map_Objects.htm)

<a id="select-or-remove-map-objects"></a>

#### Select or Remove Map Objects

> 📄 Source: [Select_or_Remove_Map_Objects.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Select_or_Remove_Map_Objects.htm)

##### Select or Remove Map Objects


Selecting Map Objects Removing Map Objects


Map objects are the items you use in the map document creation. They are:

- Guideline nodes
- [Functions](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Functions_Overview.htm)
- [Connectors](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Connectors_Overview.htm)
- [Hot points](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Hot_Points.htm)


Objects in the map document can be selected by using a mouse or the keyboard. Selected objects are blue.


###### Selecting Map Objects


To select an object by using the mouse:

- Click on the object.


To select an object by using the keyboard:

1. Move to the correct view by using one of the following:
2. In the Map Area, use the Tab or Shift + Tab keys to move between functions. Then use the left or right arrow keys to move to a hot point, and then to a connector.
3. In the Guideline views, use the up and down arrow keys to move up and down through the guideline. Use the right arrow key to expand the selected level and move down through the levels. Use the left arrow key to collapse the current level and move up through the levels.


For more information about keyboard shortcuts, see [Keyboard Shortcuts](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/MB_Keyboard_Shortcuts.htm).


###### Removing Map Objects


To remove an object by using the mouse:


Right-click the object you want to remove and do one of the following:

- Click Delete to remove a function
- Click Disconnect to remove a connector.


To remove an object by using the keyboard:

- Select the object by using the previous instructions.
- Click Delete.

<a id="hot-points"></a>

#### Hot Points

> 📄 Source: [Hot_Points.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Hot_Points.htm)

##### Hot Points


Points where [connectors](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Connectors_Overview.htm) link to a guideline node or [function](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Functions_Overview.htm) are called hot points. There can only be one hot point for every guideline node, but there can be several hot points for functions.


*[Image: Connection points]*


Hot points that represent incoming data are called input hot points. A function can have several input hot points while a target guideline node can have only one.


The following screen shows examples of input hot points:


*[Image: Input connection points]*


Hot points that represent data results are called output hot points. Functions can have several hot points while guideline nodes can have only one output hot point.


The following screen shows examples of output connection points:


*[Image: Output connection points]*


To add a hot point to the function:

1. Right-click value or result of the function, and then click Parameter.
2. Do one of the following:


You can change names for both input and output hot points. For more information, see [Function Properties General Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Function_Properties_General_Tab.htm).

<a id="guidelines"></a>

> 📄 Source: [Book_Guidelines.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Guidelines.htm)

<a id="multiple-source-guidelines"></a>

##### Multiple Source Guidelines

> 📄 Source: [Multiple_Source_Guidelines.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Multiple_Source_Guidelines.htm)

###### Multiple Source Guidelines


In a new map, only one source guideline is designated, but you can add multiple source guidelines in the document according to your needs.


When running Translator, you can specify multiple data files (one for each guideline source).


All source guidelines are stored in the Sources folder in the source guideline area. Internal Properties and Mismatched Nodes are stored under each guideline node.


*When working with multiple guidelines, the following functions are available: To Do this Add a new source guideline Right-click the Sources node, and then click Add Source. The New Document Wizard is displayed. Add Internal Properties to a Guideline Tree Right-click the source guideline node, and then click Internal Properties. For more information, see Add Internal Properties to a Guideline Tree*.






 Change the current source guideline

 Right-click the source guideline node, and then click Change Source. For more information, see *Change Guidelines*.




 Rename a source guideline

 Right-click the source guideline node, and then click Rename. Enter a new guideline name.




 Delete a source guideline

 Right-click the source guideline node, and then click Delete.




 Move a source guideline up

 Right-click the source guideline node, and then click Move Up.




 Move a source guideline down

 Right-click the source guideline node, and then click Move Down.




 Copy the node path to the clipboard

 Right-click the source guideline node, and then click Copy Business Path.

<a id="buffers"></a>

##### Buffers

> 📄 Source: [Book_Buffers.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Buffers.htm)

###### Buffers


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="buffers-overview"></a>

##### Buffers Overview

> 📄 Source: [Buffers_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Buffers_Overview.htm)

###### Buffers Overview


Edifecs Translator uses the "push mechanism" to process data in the reverse from the very last record up to the very first, element by element. This allows the Translator to reach high performance standards and be able to process gigabytes of data across multiple formats. The drawback of this mechanism is that you cannot revert to the original data that has already been processed. Certain business scenarios require re-processing of the same data several times to fill in information into different sections in the target structure. Buffers and Data Views help you meet this requirement.


Buffers cache the data being processed to make it available for later use through the run-time (visual) mechanism - Data View. Besides being able to re-process data, you also have the flexibility of modifying the data stored in the buffer as many times as you need. You can create a buffer with a unique name and associate a guideline node that corresponds to the data in your file.


When a buffer stores the original data in a cache to let you use it later, a full structured physical copy (with the categorization by segments/records and elements) of the original data is made that enables you to perform advanced data manipulations like editing buffer contents, re-shuffling, and so on.


There are different ways of using buffers, that you can select from when you create a buffer. The default buffer type is Cached. It caches the data in a memory and uses additional disk space for the cache storage (if it exceeds the permitted memory), and in some cases, this may have an impact on the performance due to complex structures stored in memory or in cache. This buffer type is particularly useful if you plan to make programming modifications to the data being cached.


Note The Cached buffer type is used by default and specified in the XERegistry.xml file.


If you do not have to make any further changes to the data kept in a buffer, you can use the buffer "lite" that does not copy the original data physically, but only references to the original source. This improves the performance significantly and reduces memory and disk space consumption. You can specify a buffer to be of the type Lite when you create the buffer. You can also apply a buffer type in the XERegistry.xml file to change the "type" of all the buffers in use across the whole system.


Note You cannot create a buffer of the type "cached" or "lite" for a node that is already stored in another buffer.


You can access buffer structures stored by means of objects of the following classes: XEBuffer for Java and Buffer for JavaScript. These objects are stored in Variable container and you can retrieve them from there using the following methods: [Translator Java XEBufferHelper](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Java_XEBufferHelper.htm), [Translator Java XEBuffer](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Java_XEBuffer.htm), [Translator Java XEBufferNode](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Java_XEBufferNode.htm), [Translator Java XEBufferContainer](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Java_XEBufferContainer.htm) for Java and [Translator JavaScript ec_BufferHelpe](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_JavaScript_ec_BufferHelper.htm)r for JavaScript.

---

<a id="work-with-buffers-and-data-views"></a>

##### Work with Buffers and Data Views

> 📄 Source: [Work_with_Buffers_and_Data_Views.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Work_with_Buffers_and_Data_Views.htm)

###### Work with Buffers and Data Views


You can create a buffer in a map document to collect occurrences of source data and store them in a buffer for later use. Data is collected for every occurrence of the selected node during the map execution. The buffer maintains the tree structure of the node and its children.


To add Buffers and Data Views nodes for a source guideline:

- On the Home tab of the ribbon, click Show Data Views.


Buffers and Data Views nodes are displayed in the Source [Guideline view](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Guideline_Views.htm).


To create a buffer for source data:

1. Right-click the Buffers node, and select Add Buffer.
2. In the Assign Buffer dialog box, click the node to be stored in the buffer.
3. Select the buffer type. The following types of buffers are available:


For more information, see [Buffers Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Buffers_Overview.htm).


Then you need to create a data view to define a [logical structure for the data](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Work_with_Logical_Nodes.htm) stored in the buffer.


To view the data stored in the buffer:

- In the Source Guideline view, right-click the Data View node, and then click Add Data View.
- Right-click New Data View, and select Add Logical Node.
- Right-click the created logical node, and then click Properties.
- In the Logical Node Properties dialog box, on the General tab, select the buffer to associate with the data view.


The logical nodes now refer to the existing buffers:


*[Image: Buffers and Data Views]*

---

<a id="work-with-logical-nodes"></a>

##### Work with Logical Nodes

> 📄 Source: [Work_with_Logical_Nodes.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Work_with_Logical_Nodes.htm)

###### Work with Logical Nodes


You can build a logical structure basing on existing buffers assigned to some source.


When working with logical nodes, you can:

- Sort logical nodes
- Define custom rules between logical nodes


By default, logical nodes that represent buffers, are displayed in the order of appearance.


To sort the elements of the logical node:

- Right-click the node, and select Properties.
- In the Logical Node Properties dialog box, click the Sorting tab.
- To sort basing on the key field, click Browse (), and select the node to be the sorting criteria.
- Select the type of the sorting order, and then click OK.


To set custom rules for the logical node:


Note Custom rules can only be set for the logical nodes of at least second level.

1. Right-click the node, and select Properties.
2. In the Logical Node Properties dialog box, click the Custom Rules tab.
3. To set the rules for the nodes to become children for a higher-level nodes, type constants for the conditions, or select the nodes.
4. Click OK.


For more information about custom rules, see [Define Custom Rules](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Define_Custom_Rules.htm).

---

<a id="define-custom-rules"></a>

##### Define Custom Rules

> 📄 Source: [Define_Custom_Rules.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Define_Custom_Rules.htm)

###### Define Custom Rules


If custom rules are not defined, children nodes should work for the nearest parent node stored before the current logical node. If no parent nodes are defined, the nearest parent node coming after the current child should be taken.


Example


Data View


Provider (Loop 2000B)


Subscriber (Loop 2000C)


Data: 2000C (1), 2000B (2), 2000B (3), 2000C (4), 2000C (5), 2000B (6)


Result:


2000B (2)


2000C (1)


2000B (3)


2000C (4)


2000C (5)


2000B (6)


If custom rules are defined, the logic for the nodes is the same as with rules not defined, but only parent node coming before or after the current child node, and matching the rule, is taken.


Example


Data View


Provider (Loop 2000A)


Subscriber (Loop 2000B)


Rule Subscriber becomes a child of Provider only in case:


Subscriber(2000B)\HL\02 equals to Provider(2000A)\HL\01


OR


Subscriber(2000B)\HL\02 equals to Provider(2000A)\HL\04


Data: 2000A (HL1=1, HL4=0), 2000A (HL1=2, HL4=1), 2000B (HL1=3, HL2=2), 2000B (HL1=4, HL2=1), 2000B (HL1=5, HL2=6), 2000A (HL1=6, HL4=0)


Result:


2000A (1)


2000A (2)


2000B (3) (HL02 = Parent\HL01)


2000B (4) (HL02 = Parent\HL04)


2000A (6)


2000B (4) (HL02 = Parent\HL01)

---

<a id="change-guidelines"></a>

##### Change Guidelines

> 📄 Source: [Change_Guidelines.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Change_Guidelines.htm)

###### Change Guidelines


While working with your map document you may want to change either source or target guideline.


To change a guideline:

1. In the [Guideline View](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Guideline_Views.htm), right-click the root source/target guideline node, and then click Change Source/Change Target.
2. Choose the guideline you want to be displayed. For more information on how to do it, see [Create a Map Document](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Create_a_Map_Document.htm).
3. Click Next.


If the selected guideline doesn't have all the nodes to which the connectors were linked in your map document, corresponding connectors are displayed under Mismatched Nodes section for the referenced guideline.

---

<a id="work-with-mismatched-nodes"></a>

##### Work with Mismatched Nodes

> 📄 Source: [Work_with_Mismatched_Nodes.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Work_with_Mismatched_Nodes.htm)

###### Work with Mismatched Nodes


If the Source or Target guideline or node structure is changed during the work with a map, Mismatched Nodes folder containing mismatched nodes is created and displayed at the top of the guideline tree.


*[Image: Mismatched Nodes]*


Mismatched nodes connectors can be reconnected to the nodes of a new guideline. As soon as the connector is reallocated, the corresponding node disappears from the Mismatched Nodes folder.


Both selected node and the whole branch can be reassigned.


To reassign a node or a branch:


Choose a certain node or branch in the Mismatched Nodes folder, then drag and drop it to the corresponding place within a new guideline.


-or-

1. Right-click a node or a branch in the Mismatched Nodes, and then click Pick.
2. Right-click a node or branch on the guideline tree and choose one of the following:

- Reassign with Children: Click to reassign a node with all its children.
- Reassign Node Only: Click to reassign the selected node without its children.
- Reassign by Order Match: Click to reassign the children nodes as they go in order.


The map with mismatched connectors can be saved so that they can be reassigned later. When you open such guideline, error saying that map has problems appears.

---

<a id="view-items-related-to-node"></a>

##### View Items Related to Node

> 📄 Source: [View_Items_Related_to_Node.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/View_Items_Related_to_Node.htm)

###### View Items Related to Node


Guideline node can have several connectors and functions connected to it. You can view all the items connected to the selected node.


To view functions and connectors related to the selected node:

- Select a node in the Guideline view.
- On the Home tab of the ribbon, click Show for Selection ().


All connectors and functions related to this node along with all selected functions are displayed in the [Map Area View](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Map_Area_View.htm).


You can also view node's connections by selecting this node and pressing Enter on the keyboard. All node's connections are shown alternately.

---

<a id="virtual-node-instances-overview"></a>

##### Virtual Node Instances Overview

> 📄 Source: [Virtual_Node_Instances_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Virtual_Node_Instances_Overview.htm)

###### Virtual Node Instances Overview


When working with guideline nodes you can create virtual instances for nodes with **Max Use** greater than one (1). This makes it easier for you to map nodes where the content varies rather than defining complicated programming statements for the map.


Note You can view the Max Use property on the [Guideline Properties Pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Guideline_Properties_Pane.htm).


Two types of instances can be added - Simple Instances or Key-Based Instances. Simple instances handle data based on the occurrence of the data - for example, the first two records are processed in one way and the next three in a different way. You have to set cardinality for the simple instances to indicate how to distribute data among instances. For more information, see [Specify Simple Guideline Instances](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Specify_Simple_Instances.htm).


Key-based instances handle data based on the value of a particular key field - for example, if you want to map a node differently depending on the type of Identifier received, you can create an instance for each possible Identifier type. Any comma-separated values taken from an available code list or entered manually can serve as keys. For more information, see [Specify Key Based Instances](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Specify_Key_Based_Guideline_Instances.htm).

---

<a id="specify-simple-guideline-instances"></a>

##### Specify Simple Guideline Instances

> 📄 Source: [Specify_Simple_Instances.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Specify_Simple_Instances.htm)

###### Specify Simple Guideline Instances


You can specify a simple instance for leaf nodes with **Max Use** more than 1. After you have specified a simple instance, you have to set cardinality for the simple instances to indicate how to distribute data among instances.


To add a simple instance:

1. Right-click a node for which you want to specify an instance.
2. On the context menu, click Simple Instances.
3. On the toolbar, click New ().
4. Double-click in the Custom Name field and type your instance name.
5. Click in the Cardinality field and type the number of occurrences for this instance.


*[Image: Simple Instances]*


In this example if there are six incoming records, the first and second records are handled by Instance 1-2, the third by Instance 3, and the remainder by Instance 4-5-6.

---

<a id="specify-key-based-guideline-instances"></a>

##### Specify Key Based Guideline Instances

> 📄 Source: [Specify_Key_Based_Guideline_Instances.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Specify_Key_Based_Guideline_Instances.htm)

###### Specify Key Based Guideline Instances


You can specify key-based instances to handle data based on the value of a particular key field. Any comma-separated values taken from the available code list or entered manually can serve as keys.


Key-based instances can be specified for two types of nodes:

- leaf nodes (elements)
- complex nodes (loops, segments, composites)


To specify a key-based instance for a leaf node:

1. Right-click a node for which you want to specify an instance.
2. On the context menu, click Key Based Instances.
3. On the toolbar, click New ().
4. Double-click in the Custom Name field and type an instance name if you want to specify a custom name for an instance. If you don't specify a custom name, the original node name is used.
5. Click the Keys field and provide a key by using one of the following:


When working with complex nodes, you have to specify keys for items of this node. For example if you specify a key instance for a segment node, you set keys for necessary elements in this segment.


To specify a key-based instance for a complex node:

1. Right-click a complex node for which you want to specify an instance.
2. On the context menu, click Key Based Instances.
3. On the toolbar, click New Instance ().
4. On the toolbar, click New Key Field ().
5. Click the element you want to provide keys for, and then click OK.
6. To change the key field, click Browse next to the field under the instance field:
7. In the Manage Instances dialog box, click the key field next to the element and provide the key value.


In this example, translation for Identification TH works if data for the Composite(ID = 'C040')[1]/Element(ID = '128')[2] contains 04 or 06 and Element(ID = '127')[1] contains TH.


Mapping for Identification KH works if data for Element(ID = '127')[1] contains RR or WS and Composite(ID = 'C040')[1]/Element(ID = '128')[1] contains KH.

---

<a id="specify-mapping-options"></a>

##### Specify Mapping Options

> 📄 Source: [Specify_Mapping_Options.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Specify_Mapping_Options.htm)

###### Specify Mapping Options


With mapping options, you can specify different mapping behavior for one and the same node. This is useful when several source node relate to the same node on the target side.


To specify mapping options:

1. On the target guideline, right-click the node where you want to specify mapping options, and then click Mapping Options.
2. In the Manage Mapping Options dialog box, click New ().
3. Provide a name of the new mapping option.
4. Click OK.


The original node gets <mapping options> mark. All the options are listed under this node.


*[Image: Mapping Options]*

---

<a id="add-internal-properties-to-a-guideline-tree"></a>

##### Add Internal Properties to a Guideline Tree

> 📄 Source: [Add_Internal_Properties_to_a_Guideline_Tree.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Add_Internal_Properties_to_a_Guideline_Tree.htm)

###### Add Internal Properties to a Guideline Tree


Using the internal properties, you can add the required custom data to a guideline without affecting its business structure. For example, you may want to add envelope segments data that is not included to the guideline as guidelines provide transaction-level information only. For this, you can use internal properties that you can add to the source and target guideline trees and map them using connectors the way you require.


To add custom internal properties:

1. In the [Guideline View](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Guideline_Views.htm), right-click the guideline.
2. On the context menu, click Internal Properties, and then click Create Empty. The empty Internal Properties folder appears.
3. Right-click the Internal Properties folder, and then click **Add Property**. The new property is added.
4. To set a test value to the new property in the source guideline, right-click the property, click **Set Test Value**, and then provide a value.


The predefined properties can be used to display information from envelope segments, which are ignored as the interchange and group data node levels are not displayed in the guideline. As your guideline displays transaction-level data only, to use envelopes segments data in mapping, you can add predefined internal properties to the source and target guideline trees and establish connections between them.


To add predefined internal properties:

1. In the [Guideline View](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Guideline_Views.htm), right-click the guideline.
2. On the context menu, click Internal Properties, and then click Populate Predefined.


The set of predefined properties is formed according to the fields of a corresponding standard that are provided in the Agreements.xml configuration file. The predefined properties created according to the fields of the interchange and group levels contain information from envelope segments. For more information, see the Agreements File Structure topic in XEngine Help.


The following table lists internal properties that SpecBuilder prepopulates based on the Agreements.xml configuration file (for EDI standard).


| Internal Property | Data Node UPath |
| --- | --- |
| InterchangeAuthorizationInfoQual | Element(ID='I01').Segment(ID='ISA').Transaction |
| InterchangeAuthorizationInfo | Element(ID='I02').Segment(ID='ISA').Transaction |
| InterchangeSecurityInfoQual | Element(ID='I03').Segment(ID='ISA').Transaction |
| InterchangeSecurityInfo | Element(ID='I04').Segment(ID='ISA').Transaction |
| InterchangeSenderQual | Element(ID='I05',Sequence='05').Segment(ID='ISA').Transaction |
| InterchangeSenderID | Element(ID='I06').Segment(ID='ISA').Transaction |
| InterchangeReceiverQual | Element(ID='I05',Sequence='07').Segment(ID='ISA').Transaction |
| InterchangeReceiverID | Element(ID='I07').Segment(ID='ISA').Transaction |
| InterchangeDate | Element(ID='I08').Segment(ID='ISA').Transaction |
| InterchangeTime | Element(ID='I09').Segment(ID='ISA').Transaction |
| InterchangeControlStandard_RepeatingSeparator | Element(ID='I10').Segment(ID='ISA').Transaction Element(ID='I65').Segment(ID='ISA').Transaction |
| InterchangeControlVersion | Element(ID='I11').Segment(ID='ISA').Transaction |
| InterchangeControlNumber | Element(ID='I12').Segment(ID='ISA').Transaction Element(ID='I12').Segment(ID='IEA').Transaction |
| InterchangeAckRequested | Element(ID='I13').Segment(ID='ISA').Transaction |
| InterchangeUsageIndicator | Element(ID='I14').Segment(ID='ISA').Transaction |
| SubelementDelimiter | Element(ID='I15').Segment(ID='ISA').Transaction |
| InterchangeChildCount | Element(ID='I16').Segment(ID='IEA').Transaction |
| GroupID | Element(ID='479').Segment(ID='GS').Transaction |
| GroupSenderID | Element(ID='142').Segment(ID='GS').Transaction |
| GroupReceiverID | Element(ID='124').Segment(ID='GS').Transaction |
| GroupDate | Element(ID='373').Segment(ID='GS').Transaction Element(ID='29').Segment(ID='GS').Transaction |
| GroupTime | Element(ID='337').Segment(ID='GS').Transaction Element(ID='30').Segment(ID='GS').Transaction |
| GroupControlNumber | Element(ID='28').Segment(ID='GS').Transaction Element(ID='28').Segment(ID='GE').Transaction |
| GroupAgencyCode | Element(ID='455').Segment(ID='GS').Transaction |
| GroupVersionNumber | Element(ID='480').Segment(ID='GS').Transaction |
| GroupChildCount | Element(ID='97').Segment(ID='GE').Transaction |
| TransactionID | Element(ID='143').Segment(ID='ST').Transaction |



If the source data file does not contain values for the required internal properties or you need custom values, you can set them manually. Right-click the property, click **Set Test Value**, and then provide a value. The data related to the properties you added is displayed in the translation result data.


The internal properties you add are applicable for the current guideline mapping only and are not saved when you close a map. To be able to reuse internal properties in further guideline mapping, you can define the node properties in an external [properties file](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Properties_File_Translator.htm) that you can use when you [execute Translator](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Execute_Translator.htm).

---

<a id="connectors"></a>

#### Connectors

> 📄 Source: [Book_Connectors.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Connectors.htm)

##### Connectors


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="connectors-overview"></a>

##### Connectors Overview

> 📄 Source: [Connectors_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Connectors_Overview.htm)

###### Connectors Overview


Connectors link two [hot points](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Hot_Points.htm). See the following example of connectors:


*[Image: Connectors]*


When a node or a function gets out of the visible pane, connectors from/to guideline node shall become hidden and only indicator on the source or target guideline node highlights the presence of the corresponding connector. See the following example of such indicator:


*[Image: Indicators of corresponding connectors]*


Double-click the indicator to show the node linked to this connector on the target or source guideline tree.

---

<a id="create-or-remove-a-connector"></a>

##### Create or Remove a Connector

> 📄 Source: [Create_or_Remove_a_Connector.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Create_or_Remove_a_Connector.htm)

###### Create or Remove a Connector


Connectors create a link between two [hot points](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Hot_Points.htm). See the following example of connectors:


*[Image: Connectors]*


You can create a connector in the following ways:

- Dragging the mouse from one hot point to another
- Using keyboard shortcuts


Note You cannot create connectors for the excluded nodes.


To create a connector by using a drag-and-drop operation:

1. Click a connection point on a node of the guideline or function.
2. Drag the mouse to another hot point.
3. Release the mouse.


To create a connector by using the keyboard:

1. Select the first connection point you would like to link.
2. Press Ctrl+Enter to pin the connection.
3. Select the second connection point for the connector.
4. Press Ctrl+Enter to create the connection.


Note After you have linked hot points they revert to their normal state.


To remove a connector:

1. Select the connector you want to remove.
2. To remove the connector, do one of the following:

---

<a id="connector-properties"></a>

##### Connector Properties

> 📄 Source: [Connector_Properties.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Connector_Properties.htm)

###### Connector Properties


Basics Guideline filters   Data filters Target-side manipulation Limitations


###### Basics


Guideline mapping has three types of connector properties. These properties affect the behavior of a connector at run-time. The three types of connector properties are:

- Guideline filters can be used on regular connectors originating from the source guideline nodes and activators. They cannot be used for connectors originating from the target guideline nodes.
- Data filters can be used on any connector including those passing from one function to another.
- Target-side manipulator properties can be used only on connectors connecting to target nodes.


These properties are set on the [Connector Properties pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Connector_Properties_Pane.htm) in the [Details pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Details_Pane.htm) or in the Connector Properties window.


###### Guideline filters  


Guideline filters have three settings. The settings are mutually exclusive, so only one can be active at a time.


The on begin node filter throws an event notification when node processing starts. This filter is applied as the default, if no explicit filter value is set.


The on end node filter throws an event notification, when node processing ends.


The on last instance end filter throws an event notification, when processing of the last sequential data instance of the given guideline node is complete.


###### Data filters


The Data filters filter based on the existence/non-existence of data. Data filters have three settings. The settings are mutually exclusive, so only one can be active at a time. Data filters are available on connectors originating from the result of a function or a guideline node.


The exists filter creates an event only when data is present at the source of the connector activation. This filter is applied as the default, if no explicit filter value is set.


The not exists filter creates an event only when data is not present at the source of the connector activation.


The always filter creates an event regardless of data presence. The connector is triggered regardless of the connector source’s result availability once a function has been executed. It passes an empty string if a result is not available.


###### Target-side manipulation


The Target-side manipulation properties are only relevant to connectors that have an item on the target structure as a destination hot point.


The Retain for use property allows you to save source data and use it for every occurrence of the same target structure. For example, your source structure has a Claim ID header segment, and you have a repeated Service Line loop structure. In your target, each service line is a DFF record, which has Claim ID as a field. By using the Retain for use property, you can copy Claim ID with every Service Line loop that is mapped to the target. Note that only the last source value is cached, so if there is a new claim, a new Claim ID is used for all subsequent Service Lines. Thereby, the Retain for use property is applied to all the nodes of the same structure to ensure data reuse in multiple repetitions of the source loop automatically. This property has the following possible values: true, false. The following example demonstrates the source and the corresponding target when the Retain for use property is set to true.


Example:


**Source**:


...


IN2~01~Name1~Name11


IN2~01~~Name12


IN2~01~Name3


IN2~01~~Name14


IN2~01~~Name15


...


**Target**:


...


IN2~01~Name1~Name11


IN2~01~Name1~Name12


IN2~01~Name3


IN2~01~Name3~Name14


IN2~01~Name3~Name15


...



The Target-side behavior property has the following possible values:

- Set value is the default setting.
- The Force creation property forces the creation of a new target instance (record) along with the regular operation regardless of the current cardinality of target. For every subscriber a new record is created. In this case, to avoid the last empty record, you need to make sure that no data is going to be received. Or you can use the force completion connector property.
- The Force completion property forces the completion of the current target instance (record). If new data is received, it is added to a new record. The following is an example of when the force completion property can be used:


Example


The Provider 1 (P1) has two Subscribers (S1,S2). The Provider 2 (P2) has the Subscriber 3 (S3). The source is the following:


P1

- S1

- S2


P2

- S3


When P1 is being mapped, it is added to the current record, S1 is added to the same record, and this record is currently active (P1S1). Since the currently active record is full, a new record is created for S2. If the data about the Provider is received, it is added to the newly created record, if not - the retain for use property adds P1 there. In this example, P2 is received and added to the record (P2S2). It also rewrites the retain for use property with the P2 value. S3 is not added to the same record since it is full and creates a new record instance. The retain for use property adds P2 to this record (P2S3). The following incorrect target is received:


P1S1


P2S2


P2S3


The problem is that S2 created a new record and adds the subscriber-related information only. The provider data remains empty and, if no data is received, the retain for use property adds the last provider data there. If new provider data is received, it is added to the record, and rewrites the retain for use property. To avoid that, you can use the force completion property to create a new record for every subscriber. In this example, after S2 is mapped, you can close the record. The retain for use property adds the Provider data (P1) there, and a new record is created for P2. The following correct target is received:


P1S1


P1S2


P2S3

- Default value property is available for a target item that can contain a value and is ignored if no value can be stored on the target. It sets up a default value that is used if a target object instance is created and no other data connection is present.
- Force order property forces the creation of a new target instance based on the guideline structure.
- Weak value property is used when you have a map with several repeated values in the source and only one value in the target. In this case, repeated values are ignored and only the first value is used in a target. The following example demonstrates the source with repeated values and the corresponding target when the target-side behavior is set to Weak value.


**Example**


**Source**:


...


N1~01~Name1~1~Identification Code~01~01


N1~01~Name2~1~Identification Code~01~01


N1~01~Name3~1~Identification Code~01~01


...


**Target**:


...


N1~01~Name1~1~Identification Code~01~01


GE~1~1005


...


###### Limitations


Due to the business rules of translation, certain connectors are invalid from a logical standpoint and are prohibited. The following comments apply only to executable functions. Documentation functions can connect any node to any node.

- Connectors cannot connect from a group node (such as Segment, Loop) to a leaf node (such as Element, Field).
- You cannot create two identical connectors.
- Each target attribute (leaf node) can have no more than one data connector with the set value target-side manipulation connector property associated with it and no more than one default connector with the default value target-side manipulation connector property. Changing the set value property to default value on one connector changes default value to set value on another connector, and vice versa.
- If a function has an activation connector to any child node on the target side, a connector cannot connect from the function to its parent node.


*[Image: Connector limitation]*

---

<a id="set-connector-properties"></a>

##### Set Connector Properties

> 📄 Source: [Set_Connector_Properties.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Set_Connector_Properties.htm)

###### Set Connector Properties


There are two ways to access connector properties once a connector is in place. In both instances, to change a property’s settings, click on the setting. A drop-down box appears, showing the options for that property. 


To access the Connector Properties dialog box:

1. Place a connector.
2. Right-click the connector.
3. Click **Properties**.


The Connector Properties dialog box appears and gives access to the properties.


To access the [Connector Properties Pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Connector_Properties_Pane.htm):

1. Place a connector.
2. Click the Connector Properties pane in the Details pane.


The Connector Properties pane displays the filters.


If a property is set, a small circle appears on the connector. Guideline and Data filter circles are displayed close to the source side. Target-side manipulation property circles are displayed close to the target side. Hovering the mouse cursor over the connector displays the connector properties assigned. If a connector is reassigned, Translator applies the new rules and return all properties to default settings. A reassignment can be undone which returns the connector and all properties to their previous settings.

---

<a id="create-implicit-connectors"></a>

##### Create Implicit Connectors

> 📄 Source: [Create_Implicit_Connectors.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Create_Implicit_Connectors.htm)

###### Create Implicit Connectors


Using this option you can implicitly connect two guidelines without having to really build connectors. This option saves time in case you have to auto-link two identical guidelines.


Note The option is only available if you click From the existing source option in the New Document Wizard.


To connect two guidelines implicitly:

1. Right-click any source node that have children, and then click Pick.
2. Right-click appropriate target node, click Auto Link Children and choose Implicitly.


Note If you have instanced nodes below the node that you pick for Implicit connection, connectors for them are not created. You have to connect those nodes manually according to your business needs.


*[Image: Connect children implicitly]*

---

<a id="functions"></a>

#### Functions

> 📄 Source: [Book_Functions.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Functions.htm)

##### Functions


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="functions-overview"></a>

##### Functions Overview

> 📄 Source: [Functions_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Functions_Overview.htm)

###### Functions Overview


Input data may not map directly to output data. Some operations may need to be performed on the input data to produce the correct output format. For instance, two input fields might need to be concatenated to produce a single output field or two input values might need to be added together to produce an output value. Functions are used to display that operation graphically.


The following screen shows an example of a function where two input fields are concatenated to form a single output field:


*[Image: Function]*


Note Most functions can have several input hot points and one output hot point.


You can view all connection which a particular function has.


To view function's connections:

- In the [Map Area view](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Map_Area_View.htm), right-click a function.
- On the context menu, click Expand all connections.


All connected nodes are shown in the guideline views. You can perform the same operation by using Functions pane in the Details Pane. For more information on how to do it, see [Functions Pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Functions_Pane.htm).

---

<a id="create-or-remove-a-function"></a>

##### Create or Remove a Function

> 📄 Source: [Create_or_Remove_a_Function.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Create_or_Remove_a_Function.htm)

###### Create or Remove a Function


Function templates are provided for your use in documenting the translation process. These templates have pre-defined rules for cardinality and appearance. You can also create your own functions.


Once the function is created, you can use Function Properties dialog box to document its use.


To create a function:

1. Do one of the following:
2. Click Select Template.
3. In the Select Function Template dialog box, click the template to use, and then click OK.


You can also insert a function directly into a connector. 


To insert a function into a connector:

- Right-click a connector, and then click Replace with function.
- Repeat steps 2 and 3.


Note You can only insert functions, which have both input and output [hot points](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Hot_Points.htm).


All function templates are separated by categories. You can click the drop-down menu and choose the category you need. All function templates relating to this category are displayed. For more information about function templates, see [Function Templates Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Function_Templates_Overview.htm).


To remove a function, do one of the following:

- Click the function and press Delete.
- Right-click the function, and then click Delete.
- Click the function and, on the Home tab of the ribbon, click Delete.

---

<a id="functions-activation-option"></a>

##### Functions Activation Option

> 📄 Source: [Functions_Activation_Option.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Functions_Activation_Option.htm)

###### Functions Activation Option


Some functions can have activation connections which serve as triggers for function execution. You can provide an activation connection from either source or target guidelines.


The activation connection option is available for the following functions: 

- .NET
- Activate Data View
- Change Variable
- Conditional Copy
- Create Variable
- Decrement
- Error
- Exception
- Increment
- Java
- JScript
- Lookahead
- Set Value
- Simple Copy
- System Date/Time


To switch to the activation model:

- Right-click a function, and then click Activation. Activation box is added to the function:


Note If you had input data connections for the function and switched to the activation model, all the previously-created input connections are dropped. At the same time, if you go back from activation to regular model, then all activation connections are dropped.


However, you may still provide constant values for the input connections.


To provide constant values for the connections:

1. Do one of the following:
2. In the Parameters box, click in the Value field and provide a constant value.
3. Click OK.


You can also use an implicit activation model which always triggers function execution using a target guideline. For implicit function activation, make sure the required function fulfills the following conditions:

- The activation option is not used.
- The input values are constants or variables.
- The function has only one result output.


In this case, the function is triggered when the required segment is formed in the corresponding parent node. If the function's output is connected to several nodes of different parent nodes, the function is triggered by each parent node separately.



If the function does not meet the required conditions and cannot be executed, you will receive a notification to prevent unexpected data mapping.

---

<a id="function-templates-overview"></a>

##### Function Templates Overview

> 📄 Source: [Function_Templates_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Function_Templates_Overview.htm)

###### Function Templates Overview


Guideline mapping provides functions that can be used for documenting the translation between your source and target guidelines.


Guideline mapping provides you with a predefined Function Template Library that can be used as a base for creating new functions. Function templates define how data is transformed and therefore determine the appearance and the number of inputs and results for the function.


There are several categories of function templates provided in guideline mapping:


| Categories | Function Templates | Function Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Copy | Conditional Copy | With this function, you can perform a condition-based copy of one or more source fields to a different target field. The condition can be a value of a qualifier field or any other parameter. This function can have any number of input and result values. This function can be activated. |
|   Conversional Copy | With this function, you can copy any source field to a target field with a conversion being performed during the copy operation. This function requires one input value and returns one result value. | |
| Crosswalk | With this function, you can convert a group of input values for an external Crosswalk map. This function may accept as many inputs as the number of source columns in the selected Crosswalk map, and returns a result of lookup in the form of a Data Set. | |
| Simple Copy | With this function, you can simply copy one source field to a target field. This function requires one input value and returns one result. | |
| Core | Activate Data View | With this function, you can specify when to start retrieving the information from Data View, storing the data in buffers. One or more data views can be created. This function does not have any inputs or outputs and can be activated only. |
| Change Variable | With this function, you can change the value of the referenced existing variable. This function requires an input value to store and a variable name. This function can be activated. | |
| Clone | With this function, you can provide explicit multiplexing abilities with a controlled order of execution for output connections. It can have a single source connection and provides a direct copy of the source to all output connections. Output connections are executed in the order they are provided in the function. | |
| Decrement | With this function, you can decrement a value of the referenced existing variable. This function does not have any inputs or outputs and can be activated only. | |
| Increment | With this function, you can increment a value of the referenced existing variable. This function does not have any inputs or outputs and can be activated only. | |
| Set Value | Use this function to set a variable value or a constant to the target. This function can be activated. | |
| Error | With this function, you can report an error during map execution. This function can have one or more inputs and no results. This function can be activated. | |
| Exception | With this function, you can throw the User Exception with the specified message. This function can have one or more inputs and no results. This function can be activated. | |
| Lookahead | With this function, you can gather information from a node that occurs later in the data file. Specify the Lookup path and the Group operation. The activation should be defined for the source. | |
| Clear Buffer | With this function, you can select a buffer to be cleared. | |
| Date/Time | Convert Date/Time | With this function, you can change the source guideline date format. This function requires one input value and returns one result. |
| System Date/Time | With this function, you can return the system date/time. This function only requires one input value to indicate the format to return the date/time. | |
| Documentation | Free Form | With this function, you can describe any translation for which there is no suitable existing function. This function can have any number of input and result values. |
|          Math | Absolute | With this function, you can return the absolute value of a numeric value. This function requires one input value and returns one result value. |
| Add | With this function, you can add numeric values. This function requires one or more input values and returns one result value. | |
| Divide | With this function, you can divide two numeric values. This function requires two input values and returns one result value. | |
| Modulus | With this function, you can divide two numeric values and get the remainder. This function requires two input values and returns one result value. | |
| Multiply | With this function, you can multiply numeric values. This function requires one or more input values and returns one result value. | |
| Round | With this function, you can round a numeric value to a certain number of decimals. This function requires one value to perform the operation, one value to specify the number of decimals, and returns one result value. | |
| Subtract | With this function, you can subtract numeric values. This function requires one or more input values and returns one result value. Second and later input values are subtracted from the first. | |
|                   String | Concatenate   | With this function, you can concatenate series of string values. This function requires one or more input values and returns one result value. |
| Lower Case | With this function, you can convert text to lowercase. This function requires one input value and returns one result value. | |
| Numeric Conversion | With this function, you can convert the text value to the specified numeric format. This function requires one input value and returns one result value. | |
| Size | With this function, you can return the length of a string. This function requires one input value and returns one result value. | |
| Substring | With this function, you can extract a part of a string value. This function requires three input values: string value to perform the function, starting position and the length to extract. This function returns one result value. | |
| Trim | With this function, you can trim the selected Padding Characters by the method specified by the Trim Type. This function requires one input value and returns one result value. | |
| Name Normalization | With this function, you can implement Operating Rules\CAQH CORE Rule 258. It normalizes the name in the way described in the rule allowing certain customization defined in the function settings. | |
| Upper Case | With this function, you can convert text to uppercase. This function requires one input value and returns one result value. | |
| Append | With this function, you can append source data to the Variable after the Separator. | |
| Split | With this function, you can split data by using the specified delimiter. | |
| Numeric Conversion | With this function, you can set numeric format of the result data. | |
|   Programming | .NET | With this function, you can execute custom .NET code. It can have any number of input and result values, which can be used in code. This function can be activated. |
| Java | With this function, you can execute custom Java code. It can have any number of input and result values, which can be used in code. This function can be activated. | |
| JScript | With this function, you can execute custom JavaScript code. It can have any number of input and result values, which can be used in code. This function can be activated. You can also use this template to execute external JavaScript rules that are stored in external .js files in your file system and use them in maps through the JavaScript function. For more information, see [External Rules Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/External_Rules_Overview.htm). | |

---

<a id="edit-function-properties"></a>

##### Edit Function Properties

> 📄 Source: [Book_Edit_Function_Properties.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Edit_Function_Properties.htm)

###### Edit Function Properties


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="function-properties-overview"></a>

##### Function Properties Overview

> 📄 Source: [Function_Properties_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Function_Properties_Overview.htm)

###### Function Properties Overview


You can enter information about the use of the function in the Function Properties dialog box. This provides documentation for the translating process.


To edit function properties:


In the Map Area, do one of the following:

- Double-click a function.
- Right-click a function, and then click Properties.


For most of the functions, there are two tabs:

- [General](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Function_Properties_General_Tab.htm)
- [User Notes](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Function_Properties_User_Notes_Tab.htm)


Some functions have the Settings tab. These are functions that need additional settings used in the transformation process.

---

<a id="function-properties-general-tab"></a>

##### Function Properties General Tab

> 📄 Source: [Function_Properties_General_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Function_Properties_General_Tab.htm)

###### Function Properties General Tab


Use the General tab to view and edit a function's name, status or parameters.


The following options are available on the General tab: 

- Name: Displays the name of the currently selected function. You can change or edit the name in this field.

- Inputs:
- Name field: Displays the name for the value.
- Value field: Shows the node assigned to this parameter or the value of the parameter.
- Reset field: Check to clean up the parameter before the next Translator run.
- Description field: Displays the description for the value. You can type information in this field or edit it.
- Outputs:
- Name field: Displays the name for the value.
- Target field: Shows the target node assigned to this parameter or the value of the parameter.
- Description field: Displays the description for the value. You can type information in this field or edit it.


Click Move Up () or Move Down () on the toolbar, to change the position of inputs or outputs in the list. In some functions you can't change the position of some parameters because of their specification. For example, you can't move down first input value of the Divide function.


Note You can also view and edit function properties in the [Function Parameters Pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Function_Parameters_Pane.htm) in the Details pane.

---

<a id="function-properties-user-notes-tab"></a>

##### Function Properties User Notes Tab

> 📄 Source: [Function_Properties_User_Notes_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Function_Properties_User_Notes_Tab.htm)

###### Function Properties User Notes Tab


Use the User Notes pane to edit global user notes content or to edit and add local user notes. 


To clear the content of a global user note:

- Right-click the note, and then click Clear.
- On the toolbar, click Clear ().


To add a new local user note:

- Right-click any user note, and then click New Local User Note.
- On the toolbar, click New Local User Note ().


To rename a local user note:

- Right-click a local user note, and then click Rename.
- On the toolbar, click Rename ().


To change the position of the local user note:

- Right-click a local user note, and then click Move up or Move down.
- On the toolbar, click Move up or Move down ().


To delete a local user note:

- Right-click a local user note, and then click Delete on the context menu.
- On the toolbar, click Delete ().


When typing your notes, you can use Word-wrap option to format the text of the note. To use this option, click Word-wrap () on the toolbar.


Note You can also edit user notes in the [User Notes Pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/User_Notes_Pane.htm) in the Details pane.

---

<a id="function-properties-settings-tab"></a>

##### Function Properties Settings Tab

> 📄 Source: [Book_Function_Properties_Settings_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Function_Properties_Settings_Tab.htm)

###### Function Properties Settings Tab


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="append-function-settings"></a>

##### Append Function Settings

> 📄 Source: [Append_Function_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Append_Function_Settings.htm)

###### Append Function Settings


The Append function is used to append source data to the Variable after the Separator. Use this tab to set separator and variable to be used while appending data.


On the Separator drop-down menu, click the character to be used as a separator while appending data.


On the Variable drop-down menu, click the variable you want to use.

---

<a id="clear-buffer-function-settings"></a>

##### Clear Buffer Function Settings

> 📄 Source: [ClearBuffer_Function_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ClearBuffer_Function_Settings.htm)

###### Clear Buffer Function Settings


Buffers are used to collect occurrences of source data and store them for later use. Data is collected for every occurrence of the selected node during map execution and the buffer maintains the tree structure of the node and its children. The Clear Buffer function cleans up a specified buffer. Use the **Clear Buffer** function Settings tab to provide a name of the buffer to clean-up.


On the drop-down menu, select a buffer to be cleared.


For more information about how to work with buffers, see [Work with Buffers and Data Views](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Work_with_Buffers_and_Data_Views.htm).

---

<a id="crosswalk-function-settings"></a>

##### Crosswalk Function Settings

> 📄 Source: [Crosswalk_Function_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Crosswalk_Function_Settings.htm)

###### Crosswalk Function Settings


Use the **Crosswalk** function to convert a group of input values for an external Crosswalk map. This function may accept as many inputs as the number of source columns in the selected Crosswalk map, and returns a result of lookup in the form of a Data Set.


Note The explicit crosswalk selection is deprecated since 9.2. To migrate the crosswalks created before 9.2 and to provide crosswalk parameters, you must specify Crosswalk Name.


In the Function Properties (Crosswalk) dialog box, from the drop-down list, select the Сrosswalk Name that is going to be used to determine a proper crosswalk table(s) at runtime. The Crosswalk is not predefined and depends on the incoming data. Inputs of the function are pre-populated with all possible parameters of Criteria and Trading Partner.


The Crosswalk function also has the Completion output. If a Crosswalk returns multiple rows, the Completion output indicates that all Crosswalk rows are retrieved and gets the true value.


If there are several crosswalk tables with the specified name, all their parameters are pre-populated as inputs and outputs of the function.


Note The input date parameter should be provided in the CCYYMMDD format. Use the [ConvertDate/Time function](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ConvertDateTime_Function_Settings.htm) for the different date format.

---

<a id="net-function-settings"></a>

##### .NET Function Settings

> 📄 Source: [NET_Function_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/NET_Function_Settings.htm)

###### .NET Function Settings


Use this tab to create and declare new .NET class for .NET function.


*[Image: Function Properties (.NET) Settings tab]*


To declare a new .NET class:

1. On the Settings tab, click Add.
2. Complete the following fields:
3. Click OK.


Note Some .dll files can contain more than one class.


For more information about .NET classes, see [Reference Custom JAR and .NET DLL Files or Java and .NET Classes](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Reference_Custom_JAR_and_NET_DLL_Files_or_Java_and_NET_Classes.htm).


To integrate your .NET classes with the Run-Time Engine:


Your class must implement a specific interface (XEMapFunction) to be accessible by the run-time engine.


To declare your .NET class, use the following pattern:


using XENET;


public void <method_name>(XENET.XEMapperContext context)


Example


using System;


using System.Collections.Generic;


using System.Text;


using XENET;


namespace SampleNETFunction


{


public class ElementFunction


{


public ElementFunction()


{


// constructor


}


public void run(XENET.XEMapperContext context)


{


// user method implementation


}


}


}

---

<a id="activate-data-view-settings"></a>

##### Activate Data View Settings

> 📄 Source: [Activate_Data_View_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Activate_Data_View_Settings.htm)

###### Activate Data View Settings


Use this pane to specify the source data to be retrieved and translated into the target. The activation should be defined for the current source so the data node to view can be selected.

---

<a id="change-variable-function-settings"></a>

##### Change Variable Function Settings

> 📄 Source: [Change_Variable_Function_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Change_Variable_Function_Settings.htm)

###### Change Variable Function Settings


Use this tab to change the value of an existing variable.


*[Image: Change Variable function Settings tab]*


Variable name: On the drop-down menu, click the variable you want to change.


New value: Provide a new value for the variable.

---

<a id="concatenate-function-settings"></a>

##### Concatenate Function Settings

> 📄 Source: [Concatenate_Function_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Concatenate_Function_Settings.htm)

###### Concatenate Function Settings


Use this tab to specify the separator for the input values. Type the character you want to be used as a separator or choose one from the drop-down list.


*[Image: Function Properties (Concatenate) Settings tab]*

---

<a id="conditional-copy-function-settings"></a>

##### Conditional Copy Function Settings

> 📄 Source: [ConditionalCopy_Function_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ConditionalCopy_Function_Settings.htm)

###### Conditional Copy Function Settings


Use this tab to set conditions that are used during the transformation in this function.


To set conditions:

1. In the if fields, type values for the arguments or choose them from the drop-down lists and choose the condition .
2. Click Insert expression (), to provide additional conditions that are linked to the prior condition by and, or, and not, or not.
3. In the then field, provide the results that are produced under the conditions set earlier. 
4. Click Add "else" block () to enter an else result.
5. Provide the results that are produced if your data doesn't meet the condition(s) provided. See the example with the following provided settings:
6. Click Remove Block () to delete the previous block.
7. Click Move Block Up () or Move Block Down () to reposition condition blocks.


Note You can set as many conditions as you wish for one function.


By enabling the Force String Comparison option, you set data for this comparison to be treated as a string. By default, the comparison type is set to be 'smart'.


Example


Expected value to come is 4. The value comes as 04. For the 'string' comparison 04 is not the same as 4. Whereas for 'smart' comparison 04 equals to 4.


Click Case Insensitive Comparison to ignore letter case in the condition string.


With the Compare Against Value List option, you can provide the list of values on the right separated by commas.


Note The Compare Against Value List option is not applicable for the following comparison actions: less than, greater than, less than or equal, greater than or equal.

---

<a id="convert-datetime-function-settings"></a>

##### Convert Date/Time Function Settings

> 📄 Source: [ConvertDateTime_Function_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ConvertDateTime_Function_Settings.htm)

###### Convert Date/Time Function Settings


Use this tab to change the format of the date.


You can define source and target date/time format by selecting the format from the list of predefined formats for different standards or by providing the format you want to use. For example, you have the date XX070220 in the X12 Date format, where XX is the century, 07 is the year, 02 is the month, and 20 is the day, and you want to change it to display 070220. In this case, in the In date/time format box, choose CCYYMMDD from the drop-down list. In the Out date/time format box, choose YYMMDD. Then click OK.


*[Image: Function Properties (Convert Date/Time) Settings tab]*


The Convert Date/Time Function supports all date/time patterns from the SimpleDateTime Java class. The following examples show how date and time patterns are interpreted in the U.S. locale. The given date and time are 2001-07-04 12:08:56 local time in the U.S. Pacific Time time zone.


| Date and Time Pattern | Result |
| --- | --- |
| "yyyy.MM.dd G 'at' HH:mm:ss z" | 2001.07.04 AD at 12:08:56 PDT |
| "EEE, MMM d, ''yy" | Wed, Jul 4, '01 |
| "h:mm a" | 12:08 PM |
| "hh 'o''clock' a, zzzz" | 12 o'clock PM, Pacific Daylight Time |
| "K:mm a, z" | 0:08 PM, PDT |
| "yyyyy.MMMMM.dd GGG hh:mm aaa" | 02001.July.04 AD 12:08 PM |
| "EEE, d MMM yyyy HH:mm:ss Z" | Wed, 4 Jul 2001 12:08:56 -0700 |
| "yyMMddHHmmssZ" | 010704120856-0700 |



For more information about SimpleDateFormat Java class, see [Oracle documentation](http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/text/SimpleDateFormat.html).

---

<a id="conversional-copy-function-settings"></a>

##### Conversional Copy Function Settings

> 📄 Source: [ConversionalCopy_Function_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ConversionalCopy_Function_Settings.htm)

###### Conversional Copy Function Settings


Use this tab to convert an input value to a new output result. Provide an input value and what you want to get as a result. Results can vary depending on the input value.


Use the following options to edit the rows in the conversion table:

- Click Import Settings (), to import settings from a .csv file.


If default value is empty, no errors should be displayed.

- Click Export Settings (), to export settings to a.csv file for reuse with another function.


When no Look up is found, or qualified characters are optional, the value is defined as Default.

- Click Insert Row (), to add another row to the table.
- Click Delete Row (), to remove the selected row from the table.
- Click Move Row Up (), or Move Row Down (), to change the location of the selected row in the table.


When no Look up is found, or qualified characters are optional, the value is defined as Default.


To edit the content of the cell, double-click the cell and use the keyboard or context menu.


If you are working with the function linked to an element containing a code lists, you can use the code list set to insert input or output values. Code lists of the source guideline are used for the input values and target guideline code lists are used for the output values.


To insert values from code list:

1. Make sure that the function is linked to an element node that contains a code list.
2. Double-click the function.
3. Click the Settings tab and do one of the following:
4. Click the code you want to use, and then click OK.


Note Insert Values from Code List option is enabled only if your function is linked to the element node that contains a code list.


You can also choose to insert values from any code list available on the guideline tree.


To insert values from any guideline code list:

1. Double-click the function.
2. Click the Settings tab and do one of the following:
3. Select a code list set you want to use, and then click OK.
4. Click the code you want to use, and then click OK.

---

<a id="decrement-function-settings"></a>

##### Decrement Function Settings

> 📄 Source: [Decrement_Function_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Decrement_Function_Settings.htm)

###### Decrement Function Settings


Use this tab to specify variable name to be decremented.


Variable name: Type in the variable name to be used or choose it from the drop-down list. Variable name must be defined in braces {}. Name must start with a letter and cannot contain spaces or non-alpha numeric characters. For example: {Var123}.

---

<a id="error-function-settings"></a>

##### Error Function Settings

> 📄 Source: [Error_Function_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Error_Function_Settings.htm)

###### Error Function Settings


Use this function to report an error during map execution. Error function can have many inputs and optional activation.

- Error ID: If not defined, new Error ID should be used. If Error ID is defined, Message, Severity, Brief and Description are taken from the resources. If specified as a parameter of function, they appear instead of corresponding values from the resources.
- Severity: Select Normal, Warning, Information, Ignore, or Critical Severity Value.
- Brief: Error summary of the event.
- Description: Short description of the event. In description you can refer to the existing inputs by using the [input] format, and variables by using the {Var1} format.

---

<a id="exception-function-settings"></a>

##### Exception Function Settings

> 📄 Source: [Exception_Function_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Exception_Function_Settings.htm)

###### Exception Function Settings


This function is used to throw User Exception with the specified message. If User Exception is thrown, runtime execution is interrupted and no result is created.


In message you can refer to the existing inputs by using the [input] format, and variables by using the {Var1} format.

---

<a id="increment-function-settings"></a>

##### Increment Function Settings

> 📄 Source: [Increment_Function_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Increment_Function_Settings.htm)

###### Increment Function Settings


Use this tab to specify variable name to be incremented.


Variable name: Specify the name of the variable. Variable name must be defined in braces {}. Name must start with a letter and cannot contain spaces or non-alpha numeric characters. For example: {Var123}.

<a id="java-function-settings"></a>

##### Java Function Settings

> 📄 Source: [Java_Function_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Java_Function_Settings.htm)

###### Java Function Settings


Use this tab to create and declare new java class for java function.


To declare a new Java class:

1. On the Settings tab, click Add.
2. Complete the following fields:
3. Click OK.


Note Some JAR files can contain more than one class.


For more information about Java classes, see [Reference Custom JAR and .NET DLL Files or Java and .NET Classes](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Reference_Custom_JAR_and_NET_DLL_Files_or_Java_and_NET_Classes.htm).


To integrate your Java classes with the Run-Time Engine:

1. Your class must implement a specific interface (XEMapFunction) to be accessible by the run-time engine. To declare your Java class, use the following pattern:
2. The function you create serves as a wrapper for your custom java code and must have the following structure:
