<a id="javascript-function-settings"></a>

##### JavaScript Function Settings

> 📄 Source: [JavaScript_Function_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/JavaScript_Function_Settings.htm)

###### JavaScript Function Settings


Provide user JavaScript code on this tab. The code is executed upon function activation.


*[Image: Function Properties JScript ]*


You can click the External Script option to navigate to the required external .js file and open it in the Code Editor for further editing. For more information, see [External Rules Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/External_Rules_Overview.htm).

---

<a id="lookahead-function-settings"></a>

##### Lookahead Function Settings

> 📄 Source: [Lookahead_Function_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Lookahead_Function_Settings.htm)

###### Lookahead Function Settings


Use this tab to specify the Lookup node UPath and the Group operation.


*[Image: Lookahead function settings]*


The activation should be defined for the current source so the Lookup node can be selected.


To specify the Lookup node and the Group operation:

1. Click Browse () to open the Lookup node selector.
2. Click the node or type a value in the UPath field.
3. Click OK.
4. Click a Group operation from the drop-down list.

---

<a id="split-function-settings"></a>

##### Split Function Settings

> 📄 Source: [Split_Function_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Split_Function_Settings.htm)

###### Split Function Settings


The Split function is used for splitting the source data and mapping target node instances. Use the Settings tab in the Function Properties (Split) dialog box to set a Split delimiter. Type the character you want to be used as a delimiter or choose one from the drop-down list. The strings are split after the delimiter provided in this field. After a string is split, linked target node instances are created according to the number of splits.

---

<a id="numeric-conversion-settings"></a>

##### Numeric Conversion Settings

> 📄 Source: [Numeric_Conversion_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Numeric_Conversion_Settings.htm)

###### Numeric Conversion Settings


Use this tab to set numeric format of the result data.


*[Image: Numeriс Conversion function settings tab]*


In the Source and Target format boxes, choose one of the following:

- Plain text: Numeric is the string format as `[-]{<digit>}[.{<digit>}]`. This format is used by default.
- X12 Numeric: (N, N1-N9) in the format `[-]{<digits>}`.
- X12 Decimal Number: (R, R1-R9) in the format `[-]{<digit>}[.{<digit>}][E[-]{<digit>}]` or `[-].{<digit>}[E[-]{<digit>}]`. Where incoming is recognized automatically and outgoing is selected.
- NCPDP Telecom signed numeric: In the format of `[{<digit>}][<letter or curly bracket>]`.

---

<a id="substring-function-settings"></a>

##### Substring Function Settings

> 📄 Source: [Substring_Function_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Substring_Function_Settings.htm)

###### Substring Function Settings


Use this tab to define the position and length of the substring to extract.

- Extract value starting from: provide a starting position in the string.
- Length of value: provide the length of the value to be extracted.

---

<a id="system-datetime-function-settings"></a>

##### System Date/Time Function Settings

> 📄 Source: [SystemDateTime_Function_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/SystemDateTime_Function_Settings.htm)

###### System Date/Time Function Settings


Use this tab to choose the date format for the output result.


*[Image: System Date Time Function Settings]*

- Date/time format: Provide the date/time format you want to use for the output result or from the list, click the predefined format you want to use.

- Convert time to UTC format: Click to convert time to Universal Coordinated Time (GMT).


Note If the check box is cleared then time format from the XERegistry file is used.


However, you might want to use different time formats for XEngine and map document. For example you have XERegistry file set to use GMT format for all XEngine files but you want to use your local time format in map document. In this case, you have to use JScript function to output in your local time format. See the following sample JSript Function settings:


*[Image: Sample JSript Function Settings]*

---

<a id="trim-function-settings"></a>

##### Trim Function Settings

> 📄 Source: [Trim_Function_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Trim_Function_Settings.htm)

###### Trim Function Settings


Use this tab to specify the Padding Characters and Trim Type. Choose a selection from the drop-down list.


*[Image: Function Properties (Trim)Settings tab]*

---

<a id="name-normalization-function-settings"></a>

##### Name Normalization Function Settings

> 📄 Source: [Name_Normalization_Function_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Name_Normalization_Function_Settings.htm)

###### Name Normalization Function Settings


With the Name Normalization Function, you can implement Operating Rules\CAQH CORE Rule 258. It normalizes the name in the way described in the rule allowing certain customization defined in the function settings.


*[Image: Name Normalization Function Settings]*


The following options are available:

- Basic X12 Charset: The following characters are removed from the name.


: " & ' ( ) * + , - . / : ; ? =

- Extended Charset: All characters are removed except for space, capitalized Latin letters, and numbers.

---

<a id="translator"></a>

### Translator

> 📄 Source: [Book_Translator.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Translator.htm)

<a id="execute-translator"></a>

#### Execute Translator

> 📄 Source: [Execute_Translator.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Execute_Translator.htm)

##### Execute Translator


Translator converts a source data file to the target format by executing the instructions defined in a map document.


To run Translator:

- On the Home tab of the ribbon, click Translator ().


After you have your data translated you may want to perform some changes and translate the same data once again. In this case, you do not have to go through all the wizard steps again. You can simply retranslate your data.


To retranslate data:

1. In the [Details Pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Details_Pane.htm) , click the [Data Translation Pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Translation_Pane.htm) .
2. On the Source Data toolbar, click Retranslate ().

---

<a id="the-translator-wizard"></a>

#### The Translator Wizard

> 📄 Source: [Book_The_Translator_Wizard.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_The_Translator_Wizard.htm)

##### The Translator Wizard

<a id="translator-wizard-overview"></a>

##### Translator Wizard Overview

> 📄 Source: [Translator_Wizard_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Wizard_Overview.htm)

###### Translator Wizard Overview


Translator helps you to convert a source data file into target one. It guides you through the following steps:


[Welcome to the Translator Wizard](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Welcome_to_the_Translator_Wizard.htm)


[Source Data Structure](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Structure.htm)


[Source Envelope Segments](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Select_Envelope_Segments.htm)


In addition, [Record Parsing Information](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Record_Parsing_Information_Translator.htm) step is available for PFF, DFF, and NCPDP Batch standards, and [Preprocessing Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Preprocessing_Options.htm) step is available for EDI, HL7 and NCPDP Telecom standards.


###### Translator Architecture


The following diagram shows the process of data translation.


*[Image: SpecBuilder Translator]*

---

<a id="welcome-to-the-translator-wizard"></a>

##### Welcome to the Translator Wizard

> 📄 Source: [Welcome_to_the_Translator_Wizard.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Welcome_to_the_Translator_Wizard.htm)

###### Welcome to the Translator Wizard


Use this wizard to translate a source data file into the target one by using translation rules created when mapping guidelines.

- Select the source data files: Navigate to the location of the data file you want to use. You can also navigate to the Smart Trading Cloud Repository directories and select the data file you want to use.
- Show Advanced Options: Click the box if you want to specify any of the following options:


Note The following steps assume that you selected this option.

- Select nodes to process from the data structure tree.
- Select envelope segments.
- Click Next, to start translation or go to [Data Structure](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Structure.htm) page if you use advanced mode.

---

<a id="preprocessing-options"></a>

##### Preprocessing Options

> 📄 Source: [Translator_Preprocessing_Options.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Preprocessing_Options.htm)

###### Preprocessing Options


Note This wizard step is not available for XML Schema standard.


In Clean Up Data File wizard step, Preprocessor options are displayed. With the Preprocessor, you can eliminate any terminator string or block characters in the data.


*[Image: Select how to use Preprocessor]*


To specify Preprocessor settings:

1. In the Clean Up Data File dialog box, check the Use Preprocessor.
2. In the Parameters box, choose a saved parameter file to use, if applicable.
3. Do one of the following:
4. To save these settings, click Save As and, in the Preprocessor Parameters Name box, type a name or select an existing parameter name to modify its definition.
5. Type in a path to save the modified data file or click Browse to locate a folder in which to save the modified data file.
6. Click Next to go to the next Translator Wizard step.

---

<a id="record-parsing-information"></a>

##### Record Parsing Information

> 📄 Source: [Record_Parsing_Information_Translator.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Record_Parsing_Information_Translator.htm)

###### Record Parsing Information


This wizard step is available for PFF and DFF standards.


Select one of the following options:

- Create a new message if a data record matches the first record in a guideline: Select to create a new message if a data record of a DFF/PFF file matches the first record in a guideline. In the following example, three messages are created if the option is enabled. If disabled - one message is created.
- File is EBCDIC encoded: Select this check box if a file is EBCDIC encoded.
- In the Open Existing Parser Schema field, to navigate to the file location, provide the file with parser schema or click ().
- Generate schema for delimited records: Select this check box to generate parser schema for delimited records. Clear this option for fixed length records, and auto-schema generation mode is used. In the Edit Delimiters field, specify the delimiters which separate records in the data file.

---

<a id="properties-file"></a>

##### Properties File

> 📄 Source: [Properties_File_Translator.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Properties_File_Translator.htm)

###### Properties File


You can select a file with the list of all data node properties needed to be set for the input data file.


A properties file is a configuration .properties file that is used to add custom properties to the specified data nodes. The parameters are stored as key-value pairs. You can use these properties for guideline mapping.


A property file contains lines of the following format that add properties to the required data nodes:


dns.fnm.<data node name>.prp.<name>=<value>

- *dns* - a root value that indicates a data node structure
- *fnm.<data node name>* - moves selection to the data node with the matching name. For example, dns.fnm.Interchange moves the selection to the interchange node.
- *prp.<name>* - creates a new property with the name <name> and the value <value>. If the specified property already exists then its value is overwritten.


Example


dns.fnm.EntireDocument.prp.TransmissionFormat=HIPAA5010A


Note If a data node used in the instruction does not exist, the instruction is ignored.


To select a data node properties file:

1. In the Select properties file dialog box, click to navigate to the required properties file.
2. Click Next to go to the next wizard page.

---

<a id="data-structure"></a>

##### Data Structure

> 📄 Source: [Data_Structure_Translator.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Structure_Translator.htm)

###### Data Structure


To select a node to validate:

1. In the Data Structure dialog box, select a node to validate. The node's properties are defined in the right pane. By default, the entire document is validated. To limit the validation to specific levels, highlight the appropriate folder.

1. Click Next to go to the next wizard page.

---

<a id="select-envelope-segments"></a>

##### Select Envelope Segments

> 📄 Source: [Select_Envelope_Segments_Translator.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Select_Envelope_Segments_Translator.htm)

###### Select Envelope Segments


Note Envelopes are not defined for the XML Schema documents, so this option is not available for XML Schema guidelines.


###### Guidelines with envelope segments:


Select one of the following options:

- Use the existing Envelope Segments: Select this option to use the envelope specified in your guideline. Click Next.
- Remove existing and select new Envelope Segments from the database: Select this option to replace envelope segments with ones from the Envelopes database. Click Next, select the envelope segment from the database, click Next, and go to Using Envelope Segments.
- Remove existing and use Envelope Segments from the external guideline file: Select this option to use envelopes stored in an external file. Click Next, select the guideline file to use, and then click Next.
- Remove existing and use Envelope Segments from multiple guideline files: Select this option to use envelopes stored in multiple external files. This option is useful to match the structure of envelope guidelines to that used by the Run-Time engine where Interchange guideline and Functional Group guidelines are stored in separate .ecs files. See "Selecting envelope segments from multiple guideline files" below.


###### Guidelines without envelope segments:

- Select Envelope Segments from the Standards Database: Select this option to add envelope segments from the Standards database. Click Next, select the envelope segment from the database, click Next, and go to Using Envelope Segments.
- Process without Envelope Segments: Select this option to go ahead and analyze the file without envelope segments.
- Use Envelope Segments from the external guideline file: Select this option to add envelope segments stored in an external file.
- Use Envelope Segments from multiple guideline files: Select this option to use envelope segments stored in multiple external files. This option is useful to match the structure of envelope guidelines to that used by the Run-Time engine where Interchange guideline and Functional Group guidelines are stored in separate .ecs files. See "Selecting envelope segments from multiple guideline files" below.


###### Selecting envelope segments from multiple guideline files

1. Specify a guideline file for the Interchange level and one for the Functional Group and then click Next.

1. Click Next to go to the next wizard page.

---

<a id="translator-options"></a>

##### Translator Options

> 📄 Source: [Translator_Options.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Options.htm)

###### Translator Options


Use this wizard step to specify if you want to suppress empty trailing nodes on the target side. Translator Options wizard page is only available when running the wizard with advanced options.


Note This option is available for all guidelines except XML Schema. By default, this option is checked for all standards except HL7.

---

<a id="translator-macros"></a>

#### Translator Macros

> 📄 Source: [Translator_Macros.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Macros.htm)

##### Translator Macros


The following Translator macros can be mapped to the target as data:

- #ControlNumber(<Data Node Name>)# is substituted with a special value provided by Selector. The way the returned value is formed and its content depend on Selector. The return value is always considered to be formatted and goes to the output untouched. You can obtain a Control Number from Selector by using a data node name. For this, use the parameter of the ControlNumber macro. For example, #ControlNumber(Transaction)#. Note that the macro even without a parameter can get a value from Selector. Thatis why we do not recommend this option.
- #SequenceNumber(<Data Node Name>)# is required in addition to the standard ControlNumber macro. The SequenceNumber macro is defined the same way as the ControlNumber macro, but without parameters. The SequenceNumber macro is supported for the HL7 standard only.
- #RecordCount# - is substituted with the sequential number of a data record the macro is mapped to. Note that a data record is a record outputted to target data.

---

<a id="map-debugger"></a>

### Map Debugger

> 📄 Source: [Book_Map_Debugger.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Map_Debugger.htm)

#### Map Debugger


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="map-debugger-overview"></a>

#### Map Debugger Overview

> 📄 Source: [Map_Debugger_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Map_Debugger_Overview.htm)

##### Map Debugger Overview


Map Debugger is a function, which shows Debugger Interface and performs necessary operations for the debugging process.


With Map Debugger, you can perform the following tasks:

- Give user ability to execute translation step by step.
- [Collect Map Trace History](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Collect_Map_Trace_History.htm) to identify the pre-history and reasons which leaded to the current state of the particular objects.
- [Monitor values of objects in the system](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Watch_Pane.htm), including the data which comes into the map and locates on the target side.
- [View the context of selected object](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Variables_Pane.htm).
- [View Functions](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Function_States_Pane.htm).
- Stop execution by [setting breakpoints](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Set_Breakpoints.htm) to view the current state of a certain object.
- [View Variables](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Variables_Pane.htm) registered in the system.

---

<a id="debug-process-overview"></a>

#### Debug Process Overview

> 📄 Source: [Debug_Process_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Debug_Process_Overview.htm)

##### Debug Process Overview


Debugging is processed with the help of the following elements:

- Debug position () - moves around the map during the execution.
- Previous debug position () - moves back in history to show the previous state of mapper objects.
- Toggle Breakpoint () - locates a breakpoint in a chosen place on the map/removes the breakpoint.
- Debugging operations:
- Run () - starts/Resumes debugging.

- Pause () - pauses debugging.
- Stop () - stops debugging.

- Step into () - moves debugging to the next child node. Disabled when running.

- Step over () - processes child nodes not stopping execution. Disabled when running.

- Step out () - executes only sibling objects and stops on the next parent object.

---

<a id="run-map-debugger"></a>

##### Run Map Debugger

> 📄 Source: [Run_Map_Debugger.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Run_Map_Debugger.htm)

###### Run Map Debugger 


Note Make sure Data Mapping is selected on the View tab of the ribbon.


If you clear the Show Advanced Option check box, proceed from the step 4.


To run a mapper with debugging:

1. On the Debug menu, click Run.
2. On the Translator Wizard, select the source data file you use, and then click Next.
3. In the Source Data Structure dialog box, select the node to process and then click Next.
4. To use Source Envelope Segments, select one of the listed options, and then click Next. For more information about selecting envelope segments, see [Select Envelope Segments](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Select_Envelope_Segments.htm).
5. For the Target Envelope Segments, repeat step 4.

---

<a id="set-breakpoints"></a>

##### Set Breakpoints

> 📄 Source: [Set_Breakpoints.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Set_Breakpoints.htm)

###### Set Breakpoints


On the Map, choose a place where you want to set a breakpoint ().


To set a breakpoint on a function:

1. Right-click to choose Breakpoint.
2. Click Before/After Execution.
3. Click Insert Breakpoint.


To set a breakpoint on a connector/hot point:

1. Right-click to choose Breakpoint.
2. Click Insert Breakpoint.


Set Breakpoints are displayed on the [Breakpoints Pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Breakpoints_Pane.htm).

---

<a id="collect-map-trace-history"></a>

##### Collect Map Trace History

> 📄 Source: [Collect_Map_Trace_History.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Collect_Map_Trace_History.htm)

###### Collect Map Trace History


Map Trace History is collected and maintained by Map Debugger.


You can return to certain events to see a condition of the following event or reason for a function result.


Debugger collects history with each event, but user can access this information only when breakpoint is activated. This is a point from which Map Trace History can be viewed.


To view Map Trace History:

1. On the Map, choose a place from where you want to record Map Trace History and put there a Breakpoint.
2. Click Start/Resume Debugging ().
3. In the Translator wizard, select the source data file.
4. Click Step into till the Debug position reaches the place you need.
5. To see the process details, click Map Trace History pane:

---

<a id="work-with-debugger-views"></a>

##### Work with Debugger Views

> 📄 Source: [Work_with_Debugger_Views.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Work_with_Debugger_Views.htm)

###### Work with Debugger Views


To monitor values of objects in the System, click [Watch Pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Watch_Pane.htm).


To view values according to the currently selected History element, click [Locals Pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Locals_Pane.htm).


To view available characteristics of functions, click [Function States Pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Function_States_Pane.htm).


To view variables registered in the system, click [Variables Pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Variables_Pane.htm).

---

<a id="common-tasks"></a>

#### Common Tasks

> 📄 Source: [Book_Common_Tasks.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Common_Tasks.htm)

##### Common Tasks


This section contains the following information:

<a id="debug-a-function-with-map-debugger"></a>

##### Debug a Function with Map Debugger

> 📄 Source: [Debug_a_Function_with_Map_Debugger.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Debug_a_Function_with_Map_Debugger.htm)

###### Debug a Function with Map Debugger


To debug a certain function:

1. Select the function that you want to debug and set a breakpoint.

---

<a id="trace-changes-of-variables"></a>

##### Trace Changes of Variables

> 📄 Source: [Trace_Changes_of_Variables.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Trace_Changes_of_Variables.htm)

###### Trace Changes of Variables


Map Trace History view filters events related to the selected variable.


To trace changes of a certain variable:

1. Open a pre-defined map that has a created variable and multiple Change Variable functions setting different values for this variable.
2. Insert an Increment function to make changes in the variable, right-click the Increment function, and then click Breakpoint.
3. Click After Execution, and then click Insert Breakpoint.
4. On the Home tab, click Start.
5. Click the Map Trace History pane to view changes in variable values:
6. To monitor changes of variables' values, select the corresponding filter:
7. To see why the specific value is now assigned to a certain variable, use a filter selection:

---

<a id="translator-java-xedatahelper"></a>

##### Translator Java XEDataHelper

> 📄 Source: [Translator_Java_XEDataHelper.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Java_XEDataHelper.htm)

###### Translator Java XEDataHelper


Use this class to access incoming data.


This class has the following methods:

 getConnectorCount()


This method is used to retrieve a number of inputs.


###### Syntax


int getConnectorCount()


###### Return Value


A positive, or zero numeric value that is a number of inputs.


Example


// The following example shows how to retrieve number of inputs:


XEDataHelper dh = ctx.getDataHelper();


int nInputCount = dh.getConnectorCount();





 getFloat(int)


This method is used to get incoming data by an input index.


###### Syntax


float getFloat(int index)


###### Parameters


index: An input index.


###### Return Value


Returns number as a float.


Example


// The following example shows how to access incoming float data:


XEDataHelper dh = ctx.getDataHelper();


float fInput3 = dh.getFloat(0);





 getFloat(string)


This method is used to get incoming data by an input name.


###### Syntax


float getFloat(java.lang.String name)


###### Parameters


name: An input name.


###### Return Value


Returns number as a float.


Example


// The following example shows how to access incoming float data:


XEDataHelper dh = ctx.getDataHelper();


float fInput3 = dh.getFloat("Value3");





 getInt(int)


This method is used to get integer incoming data.


###### Syntax


int getInt(int index)


###### Parameters


index: An input index.


###### Return Value


A positive, or zero numeric value that is a whole number.


Example


// The following example shows how to access incoming integer data:


XEDataHelper dh = ctx.getDataHelper();


int nInput2 = dh.getInt(1);





 getInt(String)


This method is used to get incoming data by an input name.


###### Syntax


int getInt(java.lang.String name)


###### Parameters


name: An input name.


###### Return Value


A positive, or zero numeric value that is a whole number.


Example


// The following example shows how to access incoming integer data:


XEDataHelper dh = ctx.getDataHelper();


int nInput2 = dh.getInt("Value2");





 getString(int)


This method is used to get string data by input index.


###### Syntax


java.lang.String getString(int index)


###### Parameters


index: Index for insertion.


###### Return Value


Returns the value as a string.


Example


// The following example shows how to access incoming string data:


XEDataHelper dh = ctx.getDataHelper();


String sInput1 = dh.getString(0);





 getString(String)


This method is used to get string data by an input name.


###### Syntax


java.lang.String getString(java.lang.String name)


###### Parameters


name: An input name.


###### Return Value


Returns the value as a string.


Example


// The following example shows how to access incoming string data:


XEDataHelper dh = ctx.getDataHelper();


String sInput1 = dh.getString("Value1");





 lookahead(String, int)


This method is used to get data from a node that occurs later in the tree by specifying its UPath. You can specify an action using an action parameter, which is performed on the result.


###### Syntax


java.lang.String[] lookahead(java.lang.String lookupUPath, int action)


###### Parameters

- lookupUPath: Use this parameter to specify a lookup path for the node.
- action: Use this parameter to specify an action for the node. 0 - no action; returns collection as it is. 1 - returns the sum of all found targets. 2 - returns a minimal number or string from the retrieved collection of targets. 3 - returns the maximum number or string from the retrieved collection of targets.


###### Return Value


Targeted number or string.


Example


// The following example shows how to retrieve all data repetitions for a specified node:


String[] arrData = ctx.getDataHelper().lookahead(


"/Transaction(ID = '101')


/Loop(ID = 'DTM')


/Loop(ID = 'LX')


/Segment(ID = 'LX')


/Element(ID = '554')"


, 0


)

---

<a id="translator-java-xemacrohelper"></a>

##### Translator Java XEMacroHelper

> 📄 Source: [Translator_Java_XEMacroHelper.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Java_XEMacroHelper.htm)

###### Translator Java XEMacroHelper


Use this class to resolve macros in your code.


This class has the following method:

 resolveMacro(String)  


This method is used to resolve XEngine macros.


###### Syntax


java.lang.String resolveMacro(java.lang.String name)


###### Parameters


name: A macro name.


###### Return Value


Returns a resolved macro as a string.


Example


// The following example shows how to use a macro to retrieve results:


XEMacroHelper mh = ctx.getMacroHelper();


String macro1 = "inp.value1.icnt";


String macro2 = "inp.value2.icnt";


String result1 = mh.resolveMacro(macro1);


String result2 = mh.resolveMacro(macro2);


System.out.println("result1=" + result1);


System.out.println("result2=" + result2);

---

<a id="translator-java-xeresulthelper"></a>

##### Translator Java XEResultHelper

> 📄 Source: [Translator_Java_XEResultHelper.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Java_XEResultHelper.htm)

###### Translator Java XEResultHelper


Use this method to report results in a number of ways.


This class has the following methods:  

 getConnectorCount()


This method is used to retrieve number of outputs.


###### Syntax


int getConnectorCount()


###### Return Value


A positive, or zero numeric value that is a whole number.


Example


// The following example shows how to obtain number of outputs for reporting:


XEResultHelper rh = ctx.getResultHelper();


int nOutputCount = rh.getConnectorCount();





 reportResult(Properties)


This method is used to output result as properties.


###### Syntax


void reportResult(java.util.Properties prop)


###### Parameters


prop: Use this parameter to provide property results.


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.


Example


// The following example shows how to obtain a result helper for properties-based reporting:


XEResultHelper rh = ctx.getResultHelper();


Properties prop = new Properties();


prop.setProperty("Result1", "Value1");


prop.setProperty("Result2", "Value2");


rh.reportResult(prop);





 reportResult(String, String)


This method is used to report result by an output name.


###### Syntax


void reportResult(java.lang.String name, java.lang.String value)


###### Parameters

- name: An output name
- value: A string value


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.


Example


// The following example shows how to obtain a result helper by an output name:


XEResultHelper rh = ctx.getResultHelper();


String sResult = "ResultValue";


rh.reportResult("Result1", sResult);





 reportResult(String[])


This method is used to output results as array.


###### Syntax


void reportResult(java.lang.String[] result)


###### Parameter


result: Use this parameter to specify output results array.


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.


Example


// The following example shows how to obtain a result helper for array-based reporting:


XEResultHelper rh = ctx.getResultHelper();


String[] res = new String[2];


res[0] = "Value1";


res[1] = "Value2";


rh.reportResult(res);





 reportResult(int, String)


This method is used to output a string result.


###### Syntax


void reportResult(int index, java.lang.String value)


###### Parameters

- index: An output index
- value: A string value


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.


Example


// The following example shows how to obtain a result helper for index-based reporting:


XEResultHelper rh = ctx.getResultHelper();


String sResult = "ResultValue";


rh.reportResult(0, sResult);





 send()


This method is used to send filled output results through the outbound connectors.


###### Syntax


void send()


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.


Example


// The following example shows how to send prepared results through output connectors:


XEResultHelper rh = ctx.getResultHelper();


rh.send();

---

<a id="translator-java-xevariablehelper"></a>

##### Translator Java XEVariableHelper

> 📄 Source: [Translator_Java_XEVariableHelper.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Java_XEVariableHelper.htm)

###### Translator Java XEVariableHelper


This class is used to obtain results from variables in your code.


This class has the following methods:

 getBool(String)


This method is used to get a boolean result from a variable.


###### Syntax


boolean getBool(java.lang.String sName)


###### Parameters


sName: Name of the variable.


###### Return Value


Boolean value of the variable.


Example


// The following example shows how to get results from variables:


XEVariableHelper vh = ctx.getVariableHelper();


boolean bRes = vh.getBool("BoolVar");





 getFloat(String)


This method is used to get a float value of a variable.


###### Syntax


float getFloat(java.lang.String sName)


###### Parameters


sName: Name of the variable.


###### Return Values


Value of the variable as a float.


Example


// The following example shows how to get results from variables:


XEVariableHelper vh = ctx.getVariableHelper();


float fRes = vh.getFloat("FloatVar");





 getInt(String)


This method is used to get integer incoming data.


###### Syntax


int getInt(java.lang.String sName)


###### Parameters


sName: Name of the variable, required.


###### Return Value


Value of the variable as a whole number.


Example


XEVariableHelper vh = ctx.getVariableHelper();


int nRes = vh.getInt("IntVar");





 getObject(String)


This method is used to get an object.


###### Syntax


Object getObject(java.lang.String sName)


###### Parameters


sName: Name of the variable.


###### Return Value


Value of the variable as an object.





 getRecordSet(String)


This method is used to get a record set. If there is no RecordSet with an XEVariableHelper name, Translator creates it.


###### Syntax


XERecordSet getRecordSet(java.lang.String sName)


###### Parameters


sName: Name of the variable.


###### Return Value


XERecordSet object from a variable.





 getString(String)


This method is used to get string data by a variable name.


###### Syntax


String getString(java.lang.String sName)


###### Parameters


sName: Name of the variable, required.


###### Return Value


Value of the variable as a string.


Example


// XEVariableHelper vh = ctx.getVariableHelper();


String sRes = vh.getString("StringVar");





 setBool(String, boolean)


This method is used to set boolean as a local variable.


###### Syntax


void setBool(java.lang.String sName, boolean bValue)


###### Parameters

- sName: Name of the variable, required.
- bValue: Boolean value, required.


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.


Example


// The following example shows how to set boolean variables:


XEVariableHelper vh = ctx.getVariableHelper();


boolean bVal4 = true;


vh.setBool("BoolVar", bVal4);





 setBool(String, boolean, boolean)


This method is used to set boolean as a global variable if bIsGlobal is set to true or as a local variable in case it is false.


###### Syntax


void setBool(java.lang.String sName, boolean bValue, boolean bIsGlobal)


###### Parameters

- sName: Name of the variable, required.
- bValue: Boolean value, required.
- bIsGlobal: Set this parameter to true if you want the variable to be global, required.


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.





 setFloat(String, float)


This method is used to set float as a local variable.


###### Syntax


void setFloat(java.lang.String sName, float fValue)


###### Parameters

- sName: Name of the variable.
- fValue: Float value.


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.


Example


// The following example shows how to set float variables:


XEVariableHelper vh = ctx.getVariableHelper();


float fVal3 = 456F;


vh.setFloat("FloatVar", fVal3);





 setFloat(String, float, boolean)


This method is used to set float as a global variable if bIsGlobal is set to true.


###### Syntax


void setFloat(java.lang.String sName, float fValue, boolean bIsGlobal)


###### Parameters

- sName: Name of the variable, required.
- fValue: Float value, required.
- bIsGlobal: Set this parameter to true if you want the variable to be global, required.


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.


Example


// The following example shows how to set float variables:


XEVariableHelper vh = ctx.getVariableHelper();


float fVal3 = 456F;


vh.setFloat("FloatVar", fVal3, true/*use global scope*/);





 setInt(String, int)


This method is used to set integer as a local variable.


###### Syntax


void setInt(java.lang.String sName, int nValue)


###### Parameters

- sName: Name of the variable, required.
- nValue: Integer value, required.


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.


Example


// The following example shows how to set integers as a local variable:


XEVariableHelper vh = ctx.getVariableHelper();


int nVal1 = 123;


vh.setInt("IntVar", nVal1);





 setInt(String, int, boolean)


This method is used to set integer as a global variable if bIsGlobal is set to true.


###### Syntax


void setInt(java.lang.String sName, int nValue, boolean bIsGlobal)


###### Parameters

- sName: Name of the variable.
- nValue: Integer value.
- bIsGlobal: Set this parameter to true if you want the variable to be global.


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.


Example


// The following example shows how to set integers as a global variable:


XEVariableHelper vh = ctx.getVariableHelper();


int nVal1 = 123;


vh.setInt("IntVar", nVal1, true/*use global scope*/);





 setObject(String, Object)


This method is used to set object as a local variable.


###### Syntax


void setObject(java.lang.String sName, java.lang.Object objValue)


###### Parameters

- sName: Name of the variable, required.
- objValue: Object value, required.


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.





 setObject(String, Object, boolean)


This method is used to set object as a global variable if bIsGlobal is set to true.


###### Syntax


void setObject(java.lang.String sName, java.lang.Object objValue, boolean bIsGlobal)


###### Parameters

- sName: Name of the variable.
- objValue: Object value.
- bIsGlobal: Set this parameter to true if you want the variable to be global.


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.





 setRecordSet(String, XERecordSet)


This method is used to set RecordSet as a local variable.


###### Syntax


void setRecordSet(java.lang.String sName, XERecordSet rs)


###### Parameters

- sName: Name of the variable, required.
- rs: RecordSet name, required.


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.





 setRecordSet(String, XERecordSet, boolean)


This method is used to set RecordSet as a global variable if bIsGlobal is set to true and local if set to false.


###### Syntax


void setRecordSet(java.lang.String sName, XERecordSet rs, boolean bIsGlobal)


###### Parameters

- sName: Name of the variable, required.
- rs: RecordSet name, required.
- bIsGlobal: Set this parameter to true if you want the variable to be global, required.


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.





 setString(String, String)


This method is used to set string as a local variable.


###### Syntax


void setString(java.lang.String sName, java.lang.String sValue)


###### Parameters

- sName: Name of the variable, required.
- sValue: Value for the parameter, required.


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.


Example


// The following example shows how to set variables:


XEVariableHelper vh = ctx.getVariableHelper();


String sVal2 = "TestData";


vh.setString("StringVar", sVal2);





 setString(String, String, boolean)


This method is used to set string as a global variable if bIsGlobal is set to true.


###### Syntax


void setString(java.lang.String sName, java.lang.String sValue, boolean bIsGlobal)


###### Parameters

- sName: Name of the variable, required.
- sValue: Value for the parameter, required.
- bIsGlobal: Set this parameter to true if you want the variable to be global, required.


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.

---

<a id="translator-java-xebufferhelper"></a>

##### Translator Java XEBufferHelper

> 📄 Source: [Translator_Java_XEBufferHelper.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Java_XEBufferHelper.htm)

###### Translator Java XEBufferHelper


With the XEBufferHelper class, you can work with an existing buffer container or create a new buffer container.


This class has the following methods:

 createBufferContainer(string)


This method is used to create an XEBufferContainer with the given name and stores it in internal cache.


###### Syntax


XEBufferContainer createBufferContainer(java.lang.String sBufferName)


###### Parameters


sBufferName: The name of the buffer, required.


###### Return Value


Returns XEBufferContainer object.


Example


// The following example shows how to create a buffer container:


XEBufferHelper bh = ctx.getBufferHelper();


XEBufferContainer bc1 = bh.createBufferContainer("Buffer1");





 getBufferContainer(string)


This method is used to return the buffer container with the specified name. Returns null if the buffer container does not exist.


###### Syntax


XEBufferContainer getBufferContainer(java.lang.String sBufferName)


###### Parameters


sBufferName: The name of the buffer, required.


###### Return Value


Returns XEBufferContainer object.


Example


// The following example shows get an existing buffer container:


XEBufferHelper bh = ctx.getBufferHelper();


XEBufferContainer bc2 = bh.getBufferContainer("Buffer2");

---

<a id="translator-java-xeerrorhelper"></a>

##### Translator Java XEErrorHelper

> 📄 Source: [Translator_Java_XEErrorHelper.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Java_XEErrorHelper.htm)

###### Translator Java XEErrorHelper


Use this class to add custom events.


This class has the following method:


addEvent(XEEvent) This method is used to add a user error event.


###### Syntax


void addEvent(XEEvent event)


###### Parameters


event: Use this parameter to specify an error event.


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.


Example


// The following example shows how to perform event reporting:


XEErrorHelper eh = ctx.getErrorHelper();


XEEvent event = new XEEvent();


event.addParam("SeverityID", "1000");


event.addParam("SeverityName", "MySeverity1");


event.setDescription("My Description1");


event.setEID(100);


eh.addEvent(event);

---

<a id="translator-java-xerecordset"></a>

##### Translator Java XERecordset

> 📄 Source: [Translator_Java_XERecordset.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Java_XERecordset.htm)

###### Translator Java XERecordSet


Use this class to store two-dimensional array strings.


The following methods are available:

 absolute(int)


This method is used to set the pointer to the specified row.


###### Syntax


boolean absolute(int row)


###### Parameters


row: Use this parameter to specify the row where to put a pointer.


###### Return Value


Returns true in case specified row exists.





 addRow()


This method is used to add a row to a record set after all already existing rows.


###### Syntax


void addRow()


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.





 clear()


This method is used to remove all content from RecordSet.


###### Syntax


void clear()


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.





 create()


This method is used to create a record set.


###### Syntax


static XERecordSet create()


###### Return Value


Returns an XERecordSet object.





 deleteRow()


This method is used to delete the current row from a record set.


###### Syntax


void deleteRow()


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.





 destroy()


This method is used to destroy a record set.


###### Syntax


void destroy()


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.





 first()


This method is used to set a pointer to the first row.


###### Syntax


boolean first()


###### Return Value


Returns true if RecordSet has rows.





 getFields()


This method is used to get a current row content as a string array.


###### Syntax


java.lang.String[] getFields()


###### Return Value


String array of field values.





 getRow()


This method is used to get a current row number.


###### Syntax


int getRow()


###### Return Value


A positive, or zero numeric value that is a whole number.





 getString(int)


This method is used to get string data by index from the current row.


###### Syntax


java.lang.String getString(int columnIndex)


###### Parameters


columnIndex: Index of the column as an integer, required.


###### Return Value


Returns the value as a string.





 insertRow()


This method is used to insert a row after the current one.


###### Syntax


void insertRow()


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.





 isFirst()


This method is used to check if the current row is the first row.


###### Syntax


boolean isFirst()


###### Return Value


Returns true if the current row is the first row.





 isLast()


This method is used to check if the current row is the last row.


###### Syntax


boolean isLast()


###### Return Value


Returns true if the current row is the last row.





 last()


This method is used to set the current row as the last row in RecordSet.


###### Syntax


boolean last()


###### Return Value


Returns true if RecordSet has rows.





 next()


This method is used to set a current pointer to the next row.


###### Syntax


boolean next()


###### Return Value


Returns true in case the next row exists.





 previous()


This method is used to set a current pointer to the previous row.


###### Syntax


boolean previous()


###### Return Value


Returns true in case the previous row exists.





 updateRow(String[])


Use this method to update a current row in the RecordSet.


###### Syntax


void updateRow(java.lang.String[] fields)


###### Parameters


fields: String data to be updated.


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.





 updateString(int, String)


Use this method to update string value in the RecordSet.


###### Syntax


void updateString(int columnIndex, java.lang.String x)


###### Parameters

- columnIndex: Index of the column as an integer.
- x: String data to be updated.


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.

---

<a id="translator-java-xebuffer"></a>

##### Translator Java XEBuffer

> 📄 Source: [Translator_Java_XEBuffer.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Java_XEBuffer.htm)

###### Translator Java XEBuffer


This class is used to work with buffers.


This class has the following methods:

 clear()


This method is used to remove all content from the buffer.


###### Syntax


void clear()


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.


Example


// The following example shows how to clear a buffer:


XEBuffer buffer1 = container.getBuffer(0);


buffer1.clear();





 cloneBuffer()


This method is used to create a complete copy of the buffer, not assigned to any BufferContainer, but having all connections to the guideline.


###### Syntax


XEBuffer cloneBuffer()


###### Return Value


Returns cloned XEBuffer object.


Example


// The following example shows how to clone a buffer:


XEBuffer buffer1 = container.getBuffer(0);


XEBuffer buffer2 = buffer1.cloneBuffer();





 getName()


This method is used to get the name of the buffer as defined upon creation.


###### Syntax


java.lang.String getName()


###### Return Value


Buffer name.


Example


// The following example shows get the name of a buffer:


XEBuffer buffer1 = container.getBuffer(0);


String sBufferName = buffer1.getName();





 getRoot()


This method is used to get the root node of the buffer storage.


###### Syntax


XEBufferNode getRoot()


###### Return Value


Returns root node of the buffer storage.


Example


// The following example shows how to get the root node for a buffer:


XEBuffer buffer1 = container.getBuffer(0);


XEBufferNode root1 = buffer1.getRoot();





 query(String)


This method is used to obtain the node based on the specified UPath.


###### Syntax


XEBufferNode query(java.lang.String sUPath)


###### Parameters


sUPath: A path to the node within the data tree.


###### Return Value


Returns node as a XEBufferNode object. Returns null if the node does not exist.


Example


// The following example shows how to get a node from a buffer by using UPath:


XEBuffer buffer1 = container.getBuffer(0);


XEBufferNode node1 = buffer1.query(


"Segment(ID = 'PR1')


/Field(ID = '00393')


/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);





 setRoot(XEBufferNode)


This method is used to set a new root node – all previously-saved data is discarded.


###### Syntax


void setRoot(XEBufferNode node)


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.


Example


// The following example shows how to get a buffer, and to then set a root node to that buffer:


XEBuffer buffer1 = container.getBuffer(0);


buffer1.setRoot(node1);

---

<a id="translator-java-xemappercontext"></a>

##### Translator Java XEMapperContext

> 📄 Source: [Translator_Java_XEMapperContext.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Java_XEMapperContext.htm)

###### Translator Java XEMapperContext


The inputs and outputs of the function in the map are provided to the java function (that is java code) through MapperContext. You have to declare the MapperContext class through:


import com.edifecs.xengine.mapper.XEMapperContext;


In the declaration of the java function method, you have to declare a variable to store the context:


public void lookupDB(XEMapperContext ctx) throws Exception;


To get the IXECrosswalkRegistry instance from XEngine:


IXECrosswalkRegistry ctx.getCrosswalkRegistry()


To get access to the inputs of the java function in the map, use getDataHelper:


String lookupValue = ctx.getDataHelper().getString(0);


To write data to the outputs of the java function in the map, use getResultHelper:


XEResultHelper resultHelper = ctx.getResultHelper();


resultHelper.reportResult(0, res.getString("FirstName"));

---

<a id="translator-java-xebuffercontainer"></a>

##### Translator Java XEBufferContainer

> 📄 Source: [Translator_Java_XEBufferContainer.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Java_XEBufferContainer.htm)

###### Translator Java XEBufferContainer


This class is used to work with multiple buffer instances.


This class has the following methods:

 addBuffer(XEBuffer)


This method is used to add a new buffer to the buffer container.


###### Syntax


void addBuffer(XEBuffer buffer)


###### Parameters


buffer: The buffer object.


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.


Example


// The following example shows how to create a buffer container, and to then add a buffer to that container:


XEBufferContainer container = bh.createBufferContainer(


"BufferContainer1"


);


container.addBuffer(buffer1);





 clear()


This method is used to remove all buffers from a buffer container.


###### Syntax


void clear()


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.





 createBuffer()


This method is used to create a new unassigned empty buffer.


###### Syntax


XEBuffer createBuffer()


###### Return Value


Returns a new XEBuffer object.


Example


// The following example shows how to create a buffer container, and then create a buffer within that container:


XEBufferContainer container = bh.createBufferContainer(


"BufferContainer1"


);


XEBuffer buffer1 = container.createBuffer();





 getBuffer(int)


This method is used to get a buffer based on the index.


###### Syntax


XEBuffer getBuffer(int nIndex)


###### Parameters


nIndex: The Index value as an integer.


###### Return Value


Returns specified XEBuffer object.


Example


// The following example shows how to create a buffer container, and then get a buffer within that container based on the index:


XEBufferContainer container = bh.createBufferContainer(


"BufferContainer1"


);


XEBuffer buffer2 = container.getBuffer(nBufferCount - 1);





 getBuffersCount()


This method is used to obtain the number of buffers.


###### Syntax


int getBuffersCount()


###### Return Value


Returns the number of buffers.


Example


// The following example shows how to create a buffer container, and to then get a count of buffers contained within:


XEBufferContainer container = bh.createBufferContainer(


"BufferContainer1"


);


int nBufferCount = container.getBuffersCount();





 insertBuffer(XEBuffer, int)


This method is used to insert a buffer before the specified index in the buffer container.


###### Syntax


void insertBuffer(XEBuffer buffer, int nIndex)


###### Parameters

- buffer: The buffer object.
- nindex: The Index value.


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.


Example


// The following example shows how to create a buffer container, and to then insert a buffer in the first position:


XEBufferContainer container = bh.createBufferContainer(


"BufferContainer1"


);


container.insertBuffer(buffer2, 0);





 removeBuffer(int)


This method is used to remove the buffer at the specific index in the buffer container.


###### Syntax


XEBuffer removeBuffer(int nIndex)


###### Parameters


nindex: The Index value.


###### Return Value


Returns just removed XEBuffer object.


Example


// The following example shows how to create a buffer container, and then remove the last buffer:


XEBufferContainer container = bh.createBufferContainer(


"BufferContainer1"


);


container.removeBuffer(nBufferCount - 1);

---

<a id="translator-java-xebuffernode"></a>

##### Translator Java XEBufferNode

> 📄 Source: [Translator_Java_XEBufferNode.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Java_XEBufferNode.htm)

###### Translator Java XEBufferNode


This class is used to work with nodes within a buffer.


This class has the following methods:

 addChild(XEBufferNode)


This method is used to add a child node to the buffer.


###### Syntax


void addChild(XEBufferNode node)


###### Parameters


node: A buffer node.


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.


Example


// The following example shows how to get a node from buffer by using UPath, and then add it as a child node to the buffer:


XEBufferNode node1 = buffer1.query(


"Segment(ID = 'PR1')


/Field(ID = '00393')


/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);


node1.addChild(node1);





 cloneBufferNode()


This method is used to create an exact copy of the current node, not assigned to any parent, but having all guideline information for the node and its children.


###### Syntax


XEBufferNode cloneBufferNode()


###### Return Value


Returns cloned XEBufferNode object.


Example


// The following example shows how to get a node from buffer by using UPath, and then clone that node:


XEBufferNode node1 = buffer1.query(


"Segment(ID = 'PR1')


/Field(ID = '00393')


/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);


XEBufferNode node2 = node1.cloneBufferNode();





 createChild(String)


This method is used to create a child node and add it to the child list. The guideline information is retrieved from the relative forward UPath, starting from the current node. The created node can then be used in data view processing. The UPath may contain only one item – like "Segment(ID = 'NM1')", otherwise an error must be thrown. An empty UPath designates a node without any connection to a guideline.


###### Syntax


XEBufferNode createChild(java.lang.String sUPath)


###### Parameters


sUPath: A path to the node within the data tree.


###### Return Value


Returns new XEBufferNode object.


Example


// The following example shows how to get a node from buffer by using UPath, and then create a child node by using UPath:


XEBufferNode node1 = buffer1.query(


"Segment(ID = 'PR1')


/Field(ID = '00393')


/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);


XEBufferNode node3 = node1.createChild(


"/Message(ID = 'ADT')


/Segment(ID = 'DB1')


/Segment(ID = 'PR1')


/Field(ID = '00393')


/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);





 createNode(String)


This method is used to create an unassigned node by a given UPath (similar to createChild, but without auto-assignment to the parent node).


###### Syntax


XEBufferNode createNode(java.lang.String sUPath)


###### Parameters


sUPath: A path to the node within the data tree.


###### Return Value


Returns new XEBufferNode object.


Example


// The following example shows how to get a node from buffer by using UPath, and then create a node by using UPath:


XEBufferNode node1 = buffer1.query(


"Segment(ID = 'PR1')


/Field(ID = '00393')


/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);


XEBufferNode node4 = node1.createNode(


"/Message(ID = 'ADT')


/Segment(ID = 'DB1')


/Segment(ID = 'PR1')


/Field(ID = '00393')


/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);





 createRepetitionHolder()


This method is used to create a repetition holder node. By nature this node has no UPath and may be inserted to any parent node.


###### Syntax


XEBufferNode createRepetitionHolder()


###### Return Value


Returns a new XEBufferNode object for repetition holder node.


Example


// The following example shows how to get a node from buffer by using UPath, and then create a holder for repetitions:


XEBufferNode node1 = buffer1.query(


"Segment(ID = 'PR1')


/Field(ID = '00393')


/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);


XEBufferNode node5 = node1.createRepetitionHolder();





 getChild(int)


This method is used to get a child node based on the index.


###### Syntax


XEBufferNode getChild(int nChildIndex)


###### Parameters


nChildIndex: Index of the child node.


###### Return Value


Returns specified child node as XEBufferNode object.





 getChildrenCount()


This method is used to get node children count.


###### Syntax


int getChildrenCount()


###### Return Value


Returns node children count.





 getData()


This method is used to return data for leaf nodes or XML mixed content nodes; returns null for group XML nodes.


###### Syntax


java.lang.String getData()


###### Return Value


Returns the value as a string.


Example


// The following example shows how to get a node from buffer by using UPath, and then get associated string data:


XEBufferNode node1 = buffer1.query(


"Segment(ID = 'PR1')


/Field(ID = '00393')


/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);


String sData = node1.getData();





 getParent()


This method is used to return the parent node; returns null if the node is root.


###### Syntax


XEBufferNode getParent()


###### Return Value


Returns parent XEBufferNode object. Returns null if the node is root.





 insertChild(XEBufferNode, int)


This method is used to insert a child node before the specified index.


###### Syntax


void insertChild(XEBufferNode node, int nChildIndex)


###### Parameters

- nChildIndex: Index of the child node.
- node: A buffer node.


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.


Example


// The following example shows how to get a node from buffer by using UPath, and then insert a child node:


XEBufferNode node1 = buffer1.query(


"Segment(ID = 'PR1')


/Field(ID = '00393')


/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);


node1.insertChild(node3, 0);





 isRepetitionHolder()


This method returns true if node is repetition holder; otherwise, returns false.


###### Syntax


Boolean isRepetitionHolder()


###### Return Value


Returns true if node is repetition holder; otherwise, returns false.


Example


// The following example shows how to get a node by using UPath, and then check if that node is a holder for repetitions:


XEBufferNode node1 = buffer1.query(


"Segment(ID = 'PR1')


/Field(ID = '00393')


/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);


boolean bIsRepetitionHolder = node1.isRepetitionHolder();





 queryGuidelineDictionary(String)


This method is used to return the value of the guideline dictionary macro such as "id" applied to the node related to the current XEBufferNode; returns null if there is no guideline node associated with this XEBufferNode.


###### Syntax


java.lang.String queryGuidelineDictionary(java.lang.String sMacro)


###### Parameters


sMacro: Data dictionary macros as a string.


###### Return Value


Returns the value as a string. Returns null if there is no guideline node associated with this XEBufferNode.


Example


// The following example shows how to get a node from a buffer by using UPath:


XEBufferNode node1 = buffer1.query(


"Segment(ID = 'PR1')


/Field(ID = '00393')


/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);


String sGuidelineID = node1.queryGuidelineDictionary("id");





 removeChild(int)


This method is used to remove the child node at the specific index.


###### Syntax


XEBufferNode removeChild(int nChildIndex)


###### Parameters


nChildIndex: Index of the child node.


###### Return Value


Returns just removed node as XEBufferNode object.





 setData(String)


This method is used to allow a manual data change for the node; has no effect for non-XML group nodes.


###### Syntax


void setData(java.lang.String sData)


###### Parameters


sData: Data string that needs to be set to the node.


###### Return Value


Method does not return a value.

---

<a id="translator-java-ixecrosswalk"></a>

##### Translator Java IXECrosswalk

> 📄 Source: [Translator_Java_IXECrosswalk.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Java_IXECrosswalk.htm)

###### Translator Java IXECrosswalk


Note The IXECrosswalk class will not be supported in future releases.


Note This interface and the methods contained are also applicable for Validator actions.


Note The following methods are no longer supported: addRow(String[], DateRange[], boolean), addRow(String[], String[], DateRange[], boolean), addRow(Map, Map, DateRange[], boolean), deleteRow(String[]), deleteRow(Map).


Use the IXECrosswalk object for Crosswalk lookups. The IXECrosswalk interface has been inherited from IXECrosswalkEngine. For more information, see [Translator Java IXECrosswalkEngine](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Java_IXECrosswalkEngine.htm).

---

<a id="translator-java-sample-code"></a>

##### Translator Java Sample Code

> 📄 Source: [Translator_Java_Sample_Code.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_Java_Sample_Code.htm)

###### Translator Java Sample Code


package com.edifecs.xengine.mapper.test;


import com.edifecs.xengine.mapper.*;


import com.edifecs.xengine.XEException;


import java.util.Properties;


import com.edifecs.xengine.mapper.XEMapperContext;


import com.edifecs.xengine.mapper.helpers.XEDataHelper;


import com.edifecs.xengine.mapper.helpers.XEResultHelper;


public class SampleMapFunction extends XEMapFunction


{


protected void onStart()


{


System.out.println("Hello from 'onStart' function");


}


 


protected void onEnd()


{


System.out.println("Hello from 'onEnd' function");


}


 


public void testFunc(XEMapperContext ctx)throws Exception


{


try


{


// ==========================


// 1. Retrieve incoming data


// ==========================


// Obtain data helper for accessing incoming data


XEDataHelper dh = ctx.getDataHelper();


 


// Retrieve data by using appropriate type-based accessor


int nInput2 = dh.getInt("Value2");


 


// ==========================


// 2. Prepare result by using variables (optional)


// ==========================


// Obtain variables helper


XEVariableHelper vh = ctx.getVariableHelper();


 


// Set variables


vh.setInt("IntVar", nInput2, true/*use global scope*/);


 


// Get result from variables


int nRes = vh.getInt("IntVar");


 


// ==========================


// 3. Report result


// ==========================


// Obtain result helper for reporting results


XEResultHelper rh = ctx.getResultHelper();


 


// Position-based result reporting into first output connector


rh.reportResult(0, String.valueOf(nVal1));


 


// Sending of already reported results through output connectors


rh.send();

---

##### Translator JavaScript Methods


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="translator-javascript-methods-overview"></a>

##### Translator JavaScript Methods Overview

> 📄 Source: [Translator_JavaScript_Methods_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_JavaScript_Methods_Overview.htm)

###### Translator JavaScript Methods Overview


ec_VariableHelper Method Summaryec_DataHelper Method SummaryRecordSet Method Summaryec_ResultHelper Method Summaryec_MacroHelper Method Summary


The following are pre-defined (global) JavaScript methods.


###### ec_VariableHelper


###### Method Summary


void SetVariable(string name, Var value, bool isGlobal) Var GetVariable(string name)


RecordSet GetRecordSet(string name)


void SetRecordSet(string name, Var value, bool isGlobal)


###### ec_DataHelper


###### Method Summary


Var GetValueByName(string name)


Var GetValueByIndex(integer index)


###### RecordSet


###### Method Summary


| boolean | absolute(int row) Moves the cursor to the given row number in this recordset object. |
| --- | --- |
| void | clear() Deletes all rows from the recordset object. |
| void | deleteRow() Deletes the current row from this recordset object. |
| boolean | first() Moves the cursor to the first row in this recordset object. |
| String[] | getFields() Retrieves all columns of the current row. |
| int | getRow() Retrieves the current row number. |
| String | getString()int columnIndex Retrieves the value of the designated column in the current row of this recordset object as a String. |
| void | insertRow() Inserts the new row at the current position into this recordset object. |
| boolean | isFirst() Retrieves whether the cursor is on the first row of this recordset object. |
| boolean | isLast() Retrieves whether the cursor is on the last row of this recordset object. |
| boolean | last() Moves the cursor to the last row in this recordset object. |
| boolean | next() Moves the cursor down one row from its current position. |
| boolean | previous() Moves the cursor to the previous row in this recordset object |
| void | updateRow (String[]fields) Updates the current row of this recordset object with the array content. |
| void | updateString(int columnIndex, String x) Updates the designated column with a String value. |
| void | addRow() Adds the new row at the end of this recordset object. |



###### Method Summary ec_ResultHelper


void ReportResultByName(string name, string value)


void ReportResultByIndex(integer index, string value)


void ReportResultArray(string[] values)


void Send()


###### Method Summary ec_MacroHelper


string ResolveMacro(string macro)
