<a id="translator-javascript-ec_datahelper"></a>

##### Translator JavaScript ec_DataHelper

> 📄 Source: [Translator_JavaScript_ec_DataHelper.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_JavaScript_ec_DataHelper.htm)

###### Translator JavaScript ec_DataHelper


With the XEDataHelper class, you can access incoming data.

 GetInputsCount


This method is used to get the count of function inputs.


###### Syntax


Var GetInputsCount()


###### Return Value


Integer: Inputs count.


Example


The following example shows how to access incoming data:


var2 = ec_DataHelper.GetInputsCount();





 GetValueByName


This method is used to get data by its name.


###### Syntax


Var GetValueByName(string name)


###### Parameters


name: A connection name.


###### Return Value


Var: Data value.


Example


The following example shows access incoming data:


var1 = ec_DataHelper.GetValueByName("value1");





 GetValueByIndex


This method is used to get data by its index.


###### Syntax


Var GetValueByIndex(index number)


###### Parameters


index: A connection index. This parameter is zero-based.


###### Return Value


Integer: Index number.


Example


The following example shows access incoming data:


var1 = ec_DataHelper.GetValueByIndex(0);





 Lookahead


This method is used to get data from the node that occurs later in the tree by specifying its UPath. Action parameter is used to specify an action, which is to be performed on the result.


###### Syntax


Var Lookahead(string upath, integer action)


###### Parameters


action: Use this parameter to specify an action for the node.

- 0: No action; returns collection as it is.
- 1: Returns the sum of all found targets.
- 2: Returns minimal number or string from found collection of targets.
- 3: Returns maximum number or string from found collection of targets.


upath: The location of a node within a guideline tree.


###### Return Value


Var: Data value.


Example


The following example shows how to retrieve all data repetitions for a specified node:


var1 = ec_DataHelper.Lookahead(


"/Transaction(ID = '101')


/Loop(ID = 'DTM')/Loop(ID = 'LX')


/Segment(ID = 'IN2')


/Element(ID = '1104')"


, 0


)

---

<a id="translator-javascript-ec_macrohelper"></a>

##### Translator JavaScript ec_MacroHelper

> 📄 Source: [Translator_JavaScript_ec_MacroHelper.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_JavaScript_ec_MacroHelper.htm)

###### Translator JavaScript ec_MacroHelper


With the XEMacroHelper class, you can resolve macros in your code.

 ResolveMacro


This method is used to resolve XEngine macros.


###### Syntax


string ResolveMacro(string macro)


###### Parameters


macro: XEngine Data Dictionary macro.


###### Return Value


String: Result of macro resolution.


Example


The following example shows how to get the instance count of a parent within a guideline node that is connected to the named input, then get the instance count of that node, and then to get the completed target record count:


var myVar = ec_MacroHelper.ResolveMacro("inp.Value1.par.icnt");


var myVar = ec_MacroHelper.ResolveMacro("inp.Value1.icnt");


var myVar = ec_MacroHelper.ResolveMacro("trg.rcnt");

---

<a id="translator-javascript-recordset"></a>

##### Translator JavaScript RecordSet

> 📄 Source: [Translator_JavaScript_RecordSet.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_JavaScript_RecordSet.htm)

###### Translator JavaScript RecordSet


With the XERecordSet class, you can store two-dimensional array strings.

 absolute


This method is used to set the pointer to the specified row.


###### Syntax


bool absolute(integer index)


###### Parameters


index: Row number.


###### Return Value


bool: Returns a true or false value.





 addRow


This method is used to add a row to a record set after all already exit rows.


###### Syntax


void addRow()


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.





 clear


This method is used to remove all content from RecordSet.


###### Syntax


void clear()


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.





 deleteRow


This method is used to delete current row from a record set.


###### Syntax


void deleteRow()


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.





 first


This method is used to set pointer to the first row. Returns true if RecordSet has rows.


###### Syntax


bool first()


###### Return Value


bool: Returns a true or false value.





 getFields


This method is used to get a specified row's content – string array.


###### Syntax


Var getFields()


###### Return Value


Var: Data value.





 getRow


This method is used to get a current row number.


###### Syntax


integer getRow()


###### Return Value


integer: A positive, or zero numeric value that is a whole number.





 getString


This method is used to get string data by index from the current row.


###### Syntax


string getString(integer index)


###### Parameters


index: String index.


###### Return Value


String: A string value.





 insertRow


This method is used to insert row after the current one.


###### Syntax


void insertRow()


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to work with one or more rows within a record set:


myRS.insertRow();


myRS.updateRow(res);


var res2 = myRS.getFields();





 isFirst


This method is used to check if the row is the first row.


###### Syntax


bool isFirst()


###### Return Value


bool: Returns a true or false value.





 isLast


This method is used to check if the row is the last row.


###### Syntax


bool isLast()


###### Return Value


bool: Returns a true or false value.





 last


This method is used to set current row to last row in RecordSet.


###### Syntax


bool last()


###### Return Value


bool: Returns a true or false value.





 next


This method is used to set pointer to the next row.


###### Syntax


bool next()


###### Return Value


bool: Returns a true or false value.





 previous


This method is used to set a pointer to the previous row.


###### Syntax


bool previous()


###### Return Value


bool: Returns a true or false value.





 reset


This method is used to reset a row in the RecordSet.


###### Syntax


void reset()


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.





 updateRow


Use this method to update row in the RecordSet.


###### Syntax


void updateRow(var value)


###### Parameters


value: An array of string values.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to insert and update one or more rows within a record set:


myRS.insertRow();


myRS.updateRow(res);


var res2 = myRS.getFields();





 updateString


Use this method to update strings in the RecordSet.


###### Syntax


void updateString(integer index, string value)


###### Parameters


index: String index.


value: A string value.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.

---

<a id="translator-javascript-ec_resulthelper"></a>

##### Translator JavaScript ec_ResultHelper

> 📄 Source: [Translator_JavaScript_ec_ResultHelper.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_JavaScript_ec_ResultHelper.htm)

###### Translator JavaScript ec_ResultHelper


The XEResultsHelper method allows you to report results in different ways.

 GetOutputCount


This method is used to get a count of function outputs.


###### Syntax


integer GetInputsCount ()


###### Return Value


integer: Outputs count.


Example


The following example shows how to access incoming data:


Var1 = ec_ResultHelper.GetOutputsCount();





 ReportResultArray


Report array of results to function’s outputs.


###### Syntax


void ReportResultArray(string[] values)


###### Parameters


values: An array of string values.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to use an array for reporting results:


var res = new Array(2);


res[0]="res 1";


res[1]="res 2";


ec_ResultHelper.ReportResultArray(res);





 ReportResultByIndex


Report result on function output by output’s index.


###### Syntax


void ReportResultByIndex(integer index, string value)


###### Parameters

- index: Output connection number.
- value: A string value.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to get simple reporting results:


ec_ResultHelper.ReportResultByIndex(1, var2);





 ReportResultByName


Report result on function output by output’s name.


###### Syntax


void ReportResultByName(string name, string value)


###### Parameters

- name: A connection name.
- value: A string value.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to get simple reporting results:


ec_ResultHelper.ReportResultByName("result1", var1);





 Send


This method is used to send filled output results through the outbound connectors.


###### Syntax


void Send()


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to send reported results through output connectors:


ec_ResultHelper.Send();





 PrintIn


This method is used to print the line specified.


###### Syntax


void println(string value)


###### Parameters

- value: A string value.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to print a message:


ec_ResultHelper.println("A sample message");

---

<a id="translator-javascript-ec_variablehelper"></a>

##### Translator JavaScript ec_VariableHelper

> 📄 Source: [Translator_JavaScript_ec_VariableHelper.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_JavaScript_ec_VariableHelper.htm)

###### Translator JavaScript ec_VariableHelper


The XEVariableHelper class allows you to obtain results from variables in your code.

 GetRecordSet


This method is used to get a record set. If there is no RecordSet with such name, Translator creates it.


###### Syntax


RecordSet GetRecordSet(string name)


###### Parameters


name: The name of the record set.


###### Return Value


RecordSet: Returns a RecordSet object.


Example


The following example shows how to create RecordSet:


var myRS = ec_VariableHelper.GetRecordSet("myVar");


myRS.insertRow();


ec_VariableHelper.SetRecordSet("myVar", myRS, false);





 GetVariable


This method is used to extract a variable from a container.


###### Syntax


Var GetVariable(string name)


###### Parameters


name: A variable name.


###### Return Value


Var: Variable value.


Example


The following example shows how to extract a variable from a container, and to then report on the results:


var myVar = "test val";


var resVar = ec_VariableHelper.GetVariable("myVar");


ec_ResultHelper.ReportResultByIndex(0, resVar);





 SetRecordSet


This method is used to set RecordSet as a global variable if bIsGlobal is set to true and local if set to false.


###### Syntax


void SetRecordSet(string name, Var value, bool isGlobal)


###### Parameters


isGlobal: Set this parameter to true if you want to make the variable global.


name: The name of the record set.


value: RecordSet object.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to set a row within a record set and set RecordSet:


var myRS = ec_VariableHelper.GetRecordSet("myVar");


myRS.insertRow();


ec_VariableHelper.SetRecordSet("myVar", myRS, false);





 SetVariable


This method is used to set the variable as a global variable if IsGlobal is set to true and local if false.


###### Syntax


void SetVariable(string name, Var value, bool isGlobal)


###### Parameters


isGlobal: Set this parameter to true if you want to make the variable global.


name: A variable name.


value: A string value.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to set a variable for an internal container, and to then report on the result:


var myVar = "test val";


ec_VariableHelper.SetVariable("myVar", myVar, false);


ec_ResultHelper.ReportResultByIndex(0, resVar);

---

<a id="translator-javascript-ec_errorhelper"></a>

##### Translator JavaScript ec_ErrorHelper

> 📄 Source: [Translator_JavaScript_ec_ErrorHelper.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_JavaScript_ec_ErrorHelper.htm)

###### Translator JavaScript ec_ErrorHelper


With the XEErrorHelper class, you can add custom events. It has an addEvent (XEEvent) function, which is derived from the ECError class defined in com.edifecs.xengine.ja.

 AddEvent


This method is used to add a user error event.


###### Syntax


void AddEvent(integer ID, string brief, string description, string paramName1, string paramValue1, string paramName2, string paramValue2, . . .)


###### Parameters


brief: Brief description as a string.


ID: Event ID.


description: Event description.


paramName1: Event parameter name.


paramName2: Event parameter name.


paramValue1: Event parameter value.


paramValue2: Event parameter value.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to add an error event:


ec_ErrorHelper.AddEvent(100, "MyBrief", "MyDescription", "Param1", "Value1", "Param2", "Value2", "Param3", "Value3");


 





 ThrowException


Use this method to throw an exception from function code.


###### Syntax


void ThrowException(string message)


###### Parameters


message: The description of the exception.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to throw an exception message:


ec_ErrorHelper.ThrowException("MyExceptionMessage");





 ProcessEB


This method is used in HIPAA to merge all EB segments in buffer loops.


###### Syntax


void ProcessEB(string bufferName)


###### Parameters


bufferName: The name of the buffer.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to obtain a buffer helper, and to then merge buffer loops in 271 HIPAA transactions:


ec_HIPAAHelper.ProcessEB("Buffer1");

---

<a id="translator-javascript-ec_bufferhelper"></a>

##### Translator JavaScript ec_BufferHelper

> 📄 Source: [Translator_JavaScript_ec_BufferHelper.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_JavaScript_ec_BufferHelper.htm)

###### Translator JavaScript ec_BufferHelper


With the XEBufferHelper class, you can work with an existing buffer container or create a new buffer container.

 CreateBufferContainer


This method is used to create an XEBufferContainer with the given name and stores it in internal cache.


###### Syntax


BufferContainer CreateBufferContainer(string bufferName)


###### Parameters


bufferName: The name of the buffer.


###### Return Value


BufferContainer: Returns BufferContainer object.


Example


The following example shows how to create a buffer container:


var container1 = ec_BufferHelper.CreateBufferContainer("BufferContainer1");





 GetBufferContainer


This method is used to return the buffer container with the specified name. Returns null if the buffer does not exist.


###### Syntax


BufferContainer GetBufferContainer(string bufferName)


###### Parameters


bufferName: The name of the buffer.


###### Return Value


BufferContainer: Returns BufferContainer object.


Example


The following example shows how to get an existing buffer container:


var container2 = ec_BufferHelper.GetBufferContainer("BufferContainer1");

---

<a id="translator-javascript-buffer"></a>

##### Translator JavaScript Buffer

> 📄 Source: [Translator_JavaScript_Buffer.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_JavaScript_Buffer.htm)

###### Translator JavaScript Buffer


With the XEBuffer class, you can work with buffers.

 clear


This method is used to remove all content from RecordSet.


###### Syntax


void clear()


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.





 cloneBuffer


This method is used to create a complete copy of the buffer, not assigned to any BufferContainer, but having all connections to the guideline.


###### Syntax


Buffer clone()


###### Return Value


buffer: The buffer object.


Example


The following example shows how to get a buffer, and then clone that buffer:


var buffer1 = container.getBuffer(0);


var buffer2 = buffer1.clone();





 getName


This method is used to get the name of the buffer as defined upon creation.


###### Syntax


string getName()


###### Return Value


String: Allows character values.


Example


The following example shows how to get a buffer, and then get the name of that buffer:


var buffer1 = container.getBuffer(0);


var sBuffername = buffer1.getName();





 getRoot


This method is used to get the root node of the buffer storage.


###### Syntax


BufferNode getRoot()


###### Return Value


BufferNode: Returns buffer node.


Example


The following example shows how to get a buffer, and then get a root node from that buffer:


var buffer1 = container.getBuffer(0);


var root1 = buffer1.getRoot();





 query


This method is used to return the XEBufferNode based on the specified UPath. Returns null if the node does not exist.


###### Syntax


BufferNode query(string upath)


###### Parameters


upath: The location of a node within a guideline tree.


###### Return Value


BufferNode: Returns buffer node.


Example


The following example shows get a buffer, and then get a node from that buffer by using UPath:


var buffer1 = container.getBuffer(0);


var node1 = buffer1.query(


"Segment(ID = 'PR1')


/Field(ID = '00393')


/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);





 setRoot


This method is used to set a new root node – all previously-saved data is discarded.


###### Syntax


void setRoot(BufferNode node)


###### Parameters


node: A buffer node.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to get a buffer, and then set that buffer as the root buffer:


var buffer1 = container.getBuffer(0);


buffer2.setRoot(node1);

---

<a id="translator-javascript-buffercontainer"></a>

##### Translator JavaScript BufferContainer

> 📄 Source: [Translator_JavaScript_BufferContainer.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_JavaScript_BufferContainer.htm)

###### Translator JavaScript BufferContainer


With the XEBufferContainer class, you can work with multiple buffer instances.

 addBuffer


This method is used to add a new child node to the buffer.


###### Syntax


void addBuffer(Buffer buffer)


###### Parameters


buffer: The buffer object.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to create a buffer container, and then add a buffer to that buffer container:


var container = ec_BufferHelper.CreateBufferContainer(


"BufferContainer1"


);


container.addBuffer(buffer1);





 clear


This method is used to remove all content from RecordSet.


###### Syntax


void clear()


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.





 createBuffer


This method is used to create a new unassigned empty buffer.


###### Syntax


Buffer createBuffer()


###### Return Value


buffer: The buffer object.


Example


The following example shows how to create a buffer container, and then to create a new buffer within that container:


var container = ec_BufferHelper.CreateBufferContainer(


"BufferContainer1"


);


var buffer1 = container.createBuffer();





 getBuffer


This method is used to get a buffer based on the index.


###### Syntax


Buffer getBuffer(integer index)


###### Parameters


index: Index for insertion.


###### Return Value


buffer: The buffer object.


Example


The following example shows how to create a buffer container, and then get the last buffer based on the index:


var container = ec_BufferHelper.CreateBufferContainer(


"BufferContainer1"


);


var buffer2 = container.getBuffer(nBufferCount - 1);





 getBuffersCount


This method is used to return the number of buffers.


###### Syntax


integer getBuffersCount()


###### Return Value


integer: A positive, or zero numeric value that is a whole number.


Example


The following example shows how to create a buffer container, and then get the count of the buffers in that container:


var container = ec_BufferHelper.CreateBufferContainer(


"BufferContainer1"


);


var nBufferCount = container.getBuffersCount();





 insertBuffer


This method is used to insert a buffer before the specified index in the buffer container.


###### Syntax


void insertBuffer(Buffer buffer, integer index)


###### Parameters


buffer: The buffer object.


index: Index for insertion.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to create a new buffer container, and then add a buffer in the 0 position:


var container = ec_BufferHelper.CreateBufferContainer(


"BufferContainer1"


);


container.insertBuffer(0, buffer2);





 removeBuffer


This method is used to remove the buffer at the specific index in the buffer container.


###### Syntax


Buffer removeBuffer(integer index)


###### Parameters


index: Buffer index.


###### Return Value


buffer: The buffer object.


Example


The following example shows how to create a new buffer container, and then to remove the last buffer:


var container = ec_BufferHelper.CreateBufferContainer(


"BufferContainer1"


);


container.removeBuffer(nBufferCount - 1);

---

<a id="translator-javascript-buffernode"></a>

##### Translator JavaScript BufferNode

> 📄 Source: [Translator_JavaScript_BufferNode.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_JavaScript_BufferNode.htm)

###### Translator JavaScript BufferNode


With the XEBufferNode class, you can work with nodes within a buffer.

 addChild


This method is used to add a new child node.


###### Syntax


void addChild(BufferNode node)


###### Parameters


node: A buffer node.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to add a child to a node:


node1.addChild(node2);





 cloneBufferNode


This method is used to create an exact copy of the current node, not assigned to any parent, but having all guideline information for the node and its children.


###### Syntax


BufferNode clone()


###### Return Value


BufferNode: Returns buffer node.


Example


The following example shows how to clone a buffer node:


var node2 = node1.clone();





 createChild


This method is used to create a child node and add it to the child list. The guideline information is retrieved from the relative forward UPath, relative starting from the current node. The created node can then be used in data view processing. The UPath may contain only one item – like "Segment(ID = 'NM1')", otherwise error must be thrown. An empty UPath designates a node without any connection to a guideline.


###### Syntax


BufferNode createChild(string upath)


###### Parameters


upath: The location of a node within a guideline tree.


###### Return Value


BufferNode: Returns buffer node.


Example


The following example shows how to create a child node by UPath, and then add the new node as a child to the specified node:


var node3 = node1.createChild(


"/Message(ID = 'ADT')


/Segment(ID = 'DB1')


/Segment(ID = 'PR1')


/Field(ID = '00393')


/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);





 createNode


This method is used to create unassigned node by given UPath (similar to createChild, but without auto-assignment to the parent node).


###### Syntax


BufferNode createNode(string upath)


###### Return Value


BufferNode: Returns buffer node.


Example


The following example shows how to create a node by UPath, and then return the new node:


var node4 = node1.createNode(


"/Message(ID = 'ADT')


/Segment(ID = 'DB1')


/Segment(ID = 'PR1')


/Field(ID = '00393')


/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);





 createRepetitionHolder


This method is used to create a repetition holder node. By nature this node has no UPath and may be inserted to any parent node.


###### Syntax


BufferNode createRepetitionHolder()


###### Return Value


BufferNode: Returns buffer node.


Example


The following example shows how to create a repetition holder:


var node5 = node1.createRepetitionHolder();





 getChild


This method is used to get a child based on the index.


###### Syntax


BufferNode getChild(integer childindex)


###### Parameters


childindex: Index of the child node.


###### Return Value


BufferNode: Returns buffer node.





 getChildrenCount


This method is used to get a child based on the index.


###### Syntax


int getChildrenCount()


###### Return Value


int: A positive, or zero numeric value that is a whole number.





 getData


This method is used to return data for leaf nodes or XML mixed content nodes; returns null for group XML nodes.


###### Syntax


string getData()


###### Return Value


String: Data, associated with node.


Example


The following example shows how to get string data associated with a buffer node:


var sData = node1.getData();





 getParent


This method is used to return the parent node; returns null if the node is root.


###### Syntax


BufferNode getParent()


###### Return Value


BufferNode: Returns buffer node.





 insertChild


This method is used to insert a child node before the specified index.


###### Syntax


void insertChild(BufferNode node, integer childindex)


###### Parameters


childindex: Index of the child node.


node: A buffer node.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to insert a child node in a buffer:


node1.insertChild(node3, 0);





 isRepetitionHolder


This method returns true if node is repetition holder; otherwise, returns false.


###### Syntax


bool isRepetitionHolder()


###### Parameters


bool: Returns a true or false value.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to check whether a buffer is a holder for repetitions:


var bIsRepetitionHolder = node1.isRepetitionHolder();





 queryGuidelineDictionary


This method is used to return the value of the guideline dictionary macro such as "id" applied to the node related to the current XEBufferNode; returns null if there is no guideline node associated with this XEBufferNode.


###### Syntax


string queryGuidelineDictionary(string macro)


###### Parameters


macro: XEngine Data Dictionary macro.


###### Return Value


String: Resolved macro value.


Example


The following example shows how to resolve a macro to obtain the ID of a guideline:


var sGuidelineID = node1.queryGuidelineDictionary("id");





 removeChild


This method is used to remove the child node at the specific index.


###### Syntax


BufferNode removeChild(integer childindex)


###### Parameters


childindex: Index of the child node.


###### Return Value


BufferNode: Returns buffer node.





 setData


This method is used to allow a manual data change for the node; has no effect for non-XML group nodes.


###### Syntax


void setData(string data)


###### Parameters


data: Data string that needs to be set to the node.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to set string data for a buffer node:


node1.setData("New Data");

---

<a id="translator-javascript-crosswalk"></a>

##### Translator JavaScript Crosswalk

> 📄 Source: [Translator_JavaScript_Crosswalk.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_JavaScript_Crosswalk.htm)

###### Translator JavaScript Crosswalk


Note This interface and the methods contained are also applicable for Validator actions.


Use the Crosswalk object for Crosswalk lookups. The Crosswalk class has been inherited from CrosswalkEngine. For more information, see [Translator JavaScript CrosswalkEngine](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_JavaScript_CrosswalkEngine.htm).


Note The following methods are no longer supported: addRow(String[], String[]), addRow(String[], String[], DateRange[]), deleteRow(String[]), createDateRange(int, int).

---

<a id="translator-javascript-sample-code"></a>

##### Translator JavaScript Sample Code

> 📄 Source: [Translator_JavaScript_Sample_Code.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_JavaScript_Sample_Code.htm)

###### Translator JavaScript Sample Code


// JScript code in this section is executed upon function activation.


var myRS = ec_VariableHelper.GetRecordSet("{MyVar}"); myRS.insertRow();


ec_VariableHelper.SetRecordSet("{MyVar}", myRS, false);


 


// ==========================================


// Accessing incoming data


// ==========================================


var1 = ec_DataHelper.GetValueByName("value1");


var2 = ec_DataHelper.GetValueByIndex(0);


 


// ==========================================


// Reporting result


// ==========================================


 


// Simple result reporting


ec_ResultHelper.ReportResultByName("result1", var1);


ec_ResultHelper.ReportResultByIndex(1, var2);


 


// Array results reporting


var res = new Array(2);


res[0]="res 1";


res[1]="res 2";


ec_ResultHelper.ReportResultArray(res);


 


// Sending of already reported results through output connectors


ec_ResultHelper.Send();


 


// ==========================================


// Variables usage


// ==========================================


var myVar = "test val";


 


// Set the variable into internal container


ec_VariableHelper.SetVariable("{MyVar}", myVar, false);


 


// Extract variable value from the container


var resVar = ec_VariableHelper.GetVariable("{MyVar}");


 


// Report extracted result


ec_ResultHelper.ReportResultByIndex(0, resVar);


 


// ==========================================


// MacroHelper usage


// ==========================================


var myVar = ec_MacroHelper.ResolveMacro("inp.Value1.par.icnt");


 


// ==========================================


// RecordSet usage


// ==========================================


 


// Obtain empty RecordSet object


var myRS = ec_VariableHelper.GetRecordSet("{MyVar}");


var res = new Array(2);


res[0]="res 1";


res[1]="res 2";


 


// Fulfill RS with data


myRS.insertRow(); myRS.updateRow(res);


var res2 = myRS.getFields();


 


// Set RS into internal container


ec_VariableHelper.SetRecordSet("{MyVar}", myRS, false);


 


// Extract RS from container and report its content into result var resRS = ec_VariableHelper.GetRecordSet("{MyVar}");


ec_ResultHelper.ReportResultArray(resRS.getFields());

---

<a id="translator-net-classes"></a>

#### Translator .NET Classes

> 📄 Source: [Book_Translator_NET_Classes.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Translator_NET_Classes.htm)

##### Translator .NET Classes


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="translator-net-xebuffer"></a>

##### Translator .NET XEBuffer

> 📄 Source: [Translator_NET_XEBuffer.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_NET_XEBuffer.htm)

###### Translator .NET XEBuffer

 Clear


This method is used to remove all content from RecordSet.


###### Syntax


void Clear()


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.





 Clone


This method is used to create unassigned node by given UPath (similar to createChild, but without auto-assignment to the parent node).


###### Syntax


XEBuffer Clone()


###### Return Value


XEBuffer: Returns XEBuffer object.


Example


// The following example shows how to get a buffer, and to then clone that buffer:


XEBuffer buffer3 = container.GetBuffer(0);


XEBuffer buffer4 = buffer1.Clone();





 GetName


This method is used to get the name of the buffer as defined upon creation.


###### Syntax


String GetName()


###### Return Value


String: Allows character values.


Example


// The following example shows how to get a buffer, and to then get the name of that buffer:


XEBuffer buffer3 = container.GetBuffer(0);


String sBuffername = buffer1.GetName();





 GetRoot


This method is used to get the root node of the buffer storage.


###### Syntax


XEBufferNode GetRoot()


###### Return Value


XEBufferNode: Returns XEBufferNode object.


Example


// The following example shows how to get a buffer, and to then get a root node from the buffer:


XEBuffer buffer3 = container.GetBuffer(0);


XEBufferNode root1 = buffer1.GetRoot();





 Query


This method is used to return the XEBufferNode based on the specified UPath. Returns null if the node does not exist.


###### Syntax


XEBufferNode Query(String sUPath)


###### Parameters


sUPath: A path to the node within the data tree.


###### Return Value


XEBufferNode: Returns XEBufferNode object.


Example


// The following example shows how to get a buffer, and to then get a node from that buffer by using UPath:


XEBuffer buffer3 = container.GetBuffer(0);


XEBufferNode node1 = buffer1.Query(


"Segment(ID = 'PR1')


/Field(ID = '00393')


/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);





 SetRoot


This method is used to set a new root node – all previously-saved data is discarded.


###### Syntax


void SetRoot(XEBufferNode node)


###### Parameters


node: A buffer node.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


// The following example shows how to get a buffer, and to then set a root node to that buffer:


XEBuffer buffer3 = container.GetBuffer(0);


buffer2.SetRoot(node1);

---

<a id="translator-net-xebuffercontainer"></a>

##### Translator .NET XEBufferContainer

> 📄 Source: [Translator_NET_XEBufferContainer.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_NET_XEBufferContainer.htm)

###### Translator .NET XEBufferContainer

 AddBuffer


This method is used to add a new child node to the buffer.


###### Syntax


void AddBuffer(XEBuffer buffer)


###### Parameters


buffer: The buffer object.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


// The following example shows how to create a new buffer container, and then add a new buffer to that container:


XEBufferContainer container = bh.CreateBufferContainer(


"BufferContainer1"


);


container.AddBuffer(buffer1);





 Clear


This method is used to remove all content from RecordSet.


###### Syntax


void Clear()


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.





 CreateBuffer


This method is used to create a new unassigned empty buffer.


###### Syntax


XEBuffer CreateBuffer()


###### Return Value


XEBuffer: Returns XEBuffer object.


Example


// The following example shows how to create a new buffer container, and to then create a new buffer:


XEBufferContainer container = bh.CreateBufferContainer(


"BufferContainer1"


);


XEBuffer buffer1 = container.CreateBuffer();





 GetBuffer


This method is used to get a buffer based on the index.


###### Syntax


XEBuffer GetBuffer(uint nIndex)


###### Parameters


nIndex: The Buffer number.


###### Return Value


XEBuffer: Returns XEBuffer object.


Example


// The following example shows how to create a new buffer container, and to then get the most recent buffer in the index:


XEBufferContainer container = bh.CreateBufferContainer(


"BufferContainer1"


);


XEBuffer buffer2 = container.GetBuffer(nBufferCount - 1);





 GetBuffersCount


This method is used to return the number of buffers.


###### Syntax


uint GetBuffersCount()


###### Return Value


uint: Number of connections.


Example


// The following example shows how to create a new buffer container, and then to get a count of the buffers in that container:


XEBufferContainer container = bh.CreateBufferContainer(


"BufferContainer1"


);


uint nBufferCount = container.GetBuffersCount();





 InsertBuffer


This method is used to insert a buffer before the specified index in the buffer container.


###### Syntax


void InsertBuffer(XEBuffer buffer, uint nIndex)


###### Parameters


buffer: The buffer object.


nIndex: Index for insertion.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


// The following example shows how to create a new buffer container, and then inserting a buffer in the 0 position:


XEBufferContainer container = bh.CreateBufferContainer(


"BufferContainer1"


);


container.InsertBuffer(0, buffer2);





 RemoveBuffer


This method is used to remove the buffer at the specific index in the buffer container.


###### Syntax


XEBuffer RemoveBuffer(uint nIndex)


###### Parameters


nIndex: The Index value as an integer.


###### Return Value


XEBuffer: Returns XEBuffer object.


Example


// The following example shows how to create a new buffer container, and then removing the previous buffer:


XEBufferContainer container = bh.CreateBufferContainer(


"BufferContainer1"


);


container.RemoveBuffer(nBufferCount - 1);

---

<a id="translator-net-xebufferhelper"></a>

##### Translator .NET XEBufferHelper

> 📄 Source: [Translator_NET_XEBufferHelper.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_NET_XEBufferHelper.htm)

###### Translator .NET XEBufferHelper

 createBufferContainer


This method is used to create an XEBufferContainer with the given name and stores it in internal cache.


###### Syntax


XEBufferContainer createBufferContainer(String sBufferName)


###### Parameters


sBufferName: The name of the buffer.


###### Return Value


XEBufferContainer: Returns XEBufferContainer object.


Example


// The following example shows how to obtain a buffer helper, and to then create a buffer container:


XEBufferHelper bh = context.GetBufferHelper();


XEBufferContainer bc1 = bh.CreateBufferContainer("Buffer1");





 getBufferContainer


This method is used to return the buffer container with the specified name. Returns null if the buffer does not exist.


###### Syntax


XEBufferContainer getBufferContainer(String sBufferName)


###### Parameters


sBufferName: The name of the buffer.


Return Value


XEBufferContainer: Returns XEBufferContainer object.


Example


// The following example shows how to obtain a buffer helper, and then to get the existing buffer container:


XEBufferHelper bh = context.GetBufferHelper();


XEBufferContainer bc2 = bh.GetBufferContainer("Buffer2");

<a id="translator-net-xebuffernode"></a>

##### Translator .NET XEBufferNode

> 📄 Source: [Translator_NET_XEBufferNode.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_NET_XEBufferNode.htm)

###### Translator .NET XEBufferNode


With the XEBufferNode class, you can work with nodes within a buffer.

 AddChild


This method is used to add a new child node.


###### Syntax


void AddChild(XEBufferNode node)


###### Parameters


node: A buffer node.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to get a node from buffer by using UPath, and then adding a child node:


XEBufferNode node2 = buffer1.Query(


"Segment(ID = 'PR1')/Field(ID = '00393')/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);


node2.AddChild(node3);





 Clone


This method is used to create unassigned node by given UPath (similar to createChild, but without auto-assignment to the parent node).


###### Syntax


XEBufferNode Clone()


###### Return Value


XEBufferNode: Returns XEBufferNode object.


Example


The following example shows how to get a node from buffer by using UPath, and then cloning that node:


XEBufferNode node2 = buffer1.Query(


"Segment(ID = 'PR1')/Field(ID = '00393')/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);


XEBufferNode node3 = node1.Clone();





 CreateChild


This method is used to create a child node and add it to the child list. The guideline information is retrieved from the relative forward UPath, relative starting from the current node. The created node can then be used in data view processing. The UPath may contain only one item – like "Segment(ID = 'NM1')", otherwise error must be thrown. An empty UPath designates a node without any connection to a guideline.


###### Syntax


XEBufferNode CreateChild(String sUPath)


###### Parameters


sUPath: A path to the node within the data tree.


###### Return Value


XEBufferNode: Returns XEBufferNode object.


Example


The following example shows how to get a node from buffer by using UPath, and then creating a child node by using UPath:


XEBufferNode node2 = buffer1.Query(


"Segment(ID = 'PR1')


/Field(ID = '00393')


/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);


XEBufferNode node4 = node1.CreateChild(


"/Message(ID = 'ADT')


/Segment(ID = 'DB1')


/Segment(ID = 'PR1')


/Field(ID = '00393')


/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);





 CreateNode


This method is used to create unassigned node by given UPath (similar to createChild, but without auto-assignment to the parent node).


###### Syntax


XEBufferNode CreateNode(String sUPath)


###### Parameters


sUPath: A path to the node within the data tree.


###### Return Value


XEBufferNode: Returns XEBufferNode object.


Example


The following example shows how to get a node from buffer by using UPath. Once created, the new node is returned:


XEBufferNode node2 = buffer1.Query(


"Segment(ID = 'PR1')


/Field(ID = '00393')


/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);


XEBufferNode node5 = node1.CreateNode(


"/Message(ID = 'ADT')


/Segment(ID = 'DB1')


/Segment(ID = 'PR1')


/Field(ID = '00393')


/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);





 CreateRepetitionHolder


This method is used to create a repetition holder node. By nature this node has no UPath and may be inserted to any parent node.


###### Syntax


XEBufferNode CreateRepetitionHolder()


###### Return Value


XEBufferNode: Returns XEBufferNode object.


Example


The following example shows how to get a node from buffer by using UPath and then creating a repetition holder:


XEBufferNode node2 = buffer1.Query(


"Segment(ID = 'PR1')/Field(ID = '00393')/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);


XEBufferNode node6 = node1.CreateRepetitionHolder();





 GetChild


This method is used to get a child based on the index.


###### Syntax


XEBufferNode GetChild(uint nChildIndex)


###### Parameters


nChildIndex: Index of the child node.


###### Return Value


XEBufferNode: Returns XEBufferNode object.





 GetChildrenCount


This method is used to get a child based on the index.


###### Syntax


int GetChildrenCount()


###### Return Value


int: A positive, or zero numeric value that is a whole number.





 GetData


This method is used to return data for leaf nodes or XML mixed content nodes; returns null for group XML nodes.


###### Syntax


String GetData()


###### Return Value


String: Data, associated with node.


Example


The following example shows how to get a node from buffer by using UPath, and then getting string data that is associated with that node:


XEBufferNode node2 = buffer1.Query(


"Segment(ID = 'PR1')/Field(ID = '00393')/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);


String sData = node1.GetData();





 GetParent


This method is used to return the parent node; returns null if the node is root.


###### Syntax


XEBufferNode GetParent()


###### Return Value


XEBufferNode: Returns XEBufferNode object.





 InsertChild


This method is used to insert a child node before the specified index.


###### Syntax


void InsertChild(XEBufferNode node, uint nChildIndex)


###### Parameters


nChildIndex: Index of the child node.


node: A buffer node.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how get a node from buffer by using UPath, and then inserting a child node:


XEBufferNode node2 = buffer1.Query(


"Segment(ID = 'PR1')/Field(ID = '00393')/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);


node1.InsertChild(0, node3);





 IsRepetitionHolder


This method returns true if node is repetition holder; otherwise, returns false.


###### Syntax


bool IsRepetitionHolder()


###### Return Value


bool: Returns a true or false value.


Example


The following example shows how to get a node from buffer by using UPath and then checking to see if that node contains repetitions:


XEBufferNode node2 = buffer1.Query(


"Segment(ID = 'PR1')/Field(ID = '00393')/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);


bool bIsRepetitionHolder = node1.IsRepetitionHolder();





 QueryGuidelineDictionary


This method is used to return the value of the guideline dictionary macro such as "id" applied to the node related to the current XEBufferNode; returns null if there is no guideline node associated with this XEBufferNode.


###### Syntax


String QueryGuidelineDictionary(String sMacro)


###### Parameters


sMacro: Data dictionary macros as a string.


###### Return Value


String: Resolved macro value.


Example


The following example shows how to get a node from buffer by using UPath, and then by using a macro to obtain the ID of the guideline:


XEBufferNode node2 = buffer1.Query(


"Segment(ID = 'PR1')/Field(ID = '00393')/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);


String sGuidelineID = node1.QueryGuidelineDictionary("id");





 RemoveChild


This method is used to remove the child node at the specific index.


###### Syntax


XEBufferNode RemoveChild(uint nChildIndex)


###### Parameters


nChildIndex: Index of the child node.


###### Return Value


XEBufferNode: Returns XEBufferNode object.





 SetData


This method is used to allow a manual data change for the node; has no effect for non-XML group nodes.


###### Syntax


void SetData(String sData)


###### Parameters


sData: Data string that needs to be set to the node.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to get a node from buffer by using UPath and then set string data for that node:


XEBufferNode node2 = buffer1.Query(


"Segment(ID = 'PR1')/Field(ID = '00393')/Simple Field(ID = '4')"


);


node1.SetData("New Data");

---

<a id="translator-net-xedatahelper"></a>

##### Translator .NET XEDataHelper

> 📄 Source: [Translator_NET_XEDataHelper.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_NET_XEDataHelper.htm)

###### Translator .NET XEDataHelper

 GetInputByIndex


This method is used to get data by its index.


###### Syntax


String GetInputByIndex(uint nInputIndex)


###### Parameters


nInputIndex: Input connection index as an integer.


###### Return Value


String: Allows character values.


Example


// The following example shows how to obtain a data helper to access incoming data, and to then retrieve position-based data:


XEDataHelper dataHelper = context.GetDataHelper();


String sInput1 = dataHelper.GetInputByIndex(0);





 GetInputByName


This method is used to get data by its name.


###### Syntax


String GetInputByName(String sInputName)


###### Parameters


sInputName: Input connection name.


###### Return Value


String: Allows character values.


Example


// The following example shows how to obtain a data helper to access incoming data, and to then retrieve name-based data:


XEDataHelper dataHelper = context.GetDataHelper();


String sInput2 = dataHelper.GetInputByName("input2");





 GetInputsCount


This method is used to retrieve number of inputs or outputs.


###### Syntax


uint GetInputsCount()


###### Return Value


uint: Number of connections.


Example


// The following example shows how to obtain a data helper to access incoming data, and to then retrieve inputs:


XEDataHelper dataHelper = context.GetDataHelper();


uint nInputsCount = dataHelper.GetInputsCount();





 Lookahead


This method is used to get data from the node that occurs later in the tree by specifying its UPath. Action parameter is used to specify an action, which is to be performed on the result.


###### Syntax


String[] Lookahead(String sLookupUPath, uint nAction)


###### Parameters


nAction: Use this parameter to specify an action for the node. 0 - no action; returns collection as it is. 1 - returns sum of all found targets. 2 - returns minimal number or string from found collection of targets. 3 - returns maximum number or string from found collection of targets.


sLookupUPath: Use this parameter to specify a lookup path for the node.


###### Return Value


String[]: Targeted value as a string.


Example


// The following example shows how to retrieve all data repetitions for a specified node (with an ID of 554):


String[] arrData = context.GetDataHelper().Lookahead(


"/Transaction(ID = '101')


/Loop(ID = 'DTM')


/Loop(ID = 'LX')


/Segment(ID = 'LX')


/Element(ID = '554')"


, 0


;

---

<a id="translator-net-xeerrorhelper"></a>

##### Translator .NET XEErrorHelper

> 📄 Source: [Translator_NET_XEErrorHelper.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_NET_XEErrorHelper.htm)

###### Translator .NET XEErrorHelper

 AddEvent


This method is used to add a user error event.


###### Syntax


void AddEvent(uint nID, String sBrief, String sDescription, String[] aParamNames, String[] aParamValues)


###### Parameters


aParamNames: Event parameter name.


aParamValues: Event parameter value.


nID: Event ID.


sBrief: A short description of an error.


sDescription: The string containing the error message. Should be specified in either the PlaceError or the Activate function. Event or error description.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


// The following example shows how to prepare parameters for an event, and to then report on that event:


String[] parNames = {"SeverityID","SeverityName"};


String[] parValues = {"1000", "MySeverity10"};


context.GetErrorHelper().AddEvent(


100500, "MyBrief", "MyDescription", parNames, parValues


);

<a id="translator-net-xemacrohelper"></a>

##### Translator .NET XEMacroHelper

> 📄 Source: [Translator_NET_XEMacroHelper.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_NET_XEMacroHelper.htm)

###### Translator .NET XEMacroHelper

 ResolveMacro


This method is used to resolve XEngine macros.


###### Syntax


String ResolveMacro(String sMacro)


###### Parameters


sMacro: Data dictionary macros as a string.


###### Return Value


String: Result of macro resolution.


Example


The following example shows how to obtain a macro helper, prepare test data, and to then resolve the macro and retrieve results:


XEMacroHelper macroHelper = context.GetMacroHelper();


String macro1 = "inp.value1.icnt";


String macro2 = "inp.value2.icnt";


String result1 = macroHelper.ResolveMacro(macro1);


String result2 = macroHelper.ResolveMacro(macro2);

<a id="translator-net-xerecordset"></a>

##### Translator .NET XERecordSet

> 📄 Source: [Translator_NET_XERecordSet.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_NET_XERecordSet.htm)

###### Translator .NET XERecordSet


With the XERecordSet class, you can store two-dimensional array strings.

 Absolute


This method is used to set the pointer to the specified row.


###### Syntax


void Absolute(uint nRowNumber)


###### Parameters


nRowNumber: Row number as an integer.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.





 AddRow


This method is used to add a row to a record set after all already exit rows.


###### Syntax


void AddRow()


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows create a record set, and then add a row at the end of the record set:


XERecordSet rs1 = variableHelper.CreateRecordSet();


rs1.AddRow();





 Clear


This method is used to remove all content from RecordSet.


###### Syntax


void Clear()


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to create a record set, and then work with various settings available to a record set:


XERecordSet rs1 = variableHelper.CreateRecordSet();


rs1.Reset();


rs1.Clear();


rs1.First();


rs1.InsertRow();


String[] fields1 = { "1", "22", "333" };


String[] fields2 = { "1", "22", "333", "4444", "55555" };


rs1.UpdateRow(fields1);


rs1.InsertRow();


rs1.UpdateRow(fields2);


rs1.Next();


String string1 = rs1.GetString(2);





 DeleteRow


This method is used to delete current row from a record set.


###### Syntax


void DeleteRow()


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.





 First


This method is used to set pointer to the first row. Returns true if RecordSet has rows.


###### Syntax


bool First()


###### Return Value


bool: Returns a true or false value.


Example


The following example shows how to create a record set and to then step through the record set:


XERecordSet rs1 = variableHelper.CreateRecordSet();


rs2.First();


rs2.Next();


String string2 = rs2.GetString(3);





 GetFields


This method is used to get a specified row's content – string array.


###### Syntax


String[] GetFields()


###### Return Value


String[]: Targeted value as a string.





 GetRow


This method is used to get a current row number.


###### Syntax


uint GetRow()


###### Return Value


uint: Number of connections.





 GetString


This method is used to get string data by index from the current row.


###### Syntax


String GetString(uint nColumnNumber)


###### Parameters


nColumnNumber: The number of a column as an integer.


###### Return Value


String: A string value.





 InsertRow


This method is used to insert row after the current one.


###### Syntax


void InsertRow()


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to create a record set, and then work with various settings available to a record set:


XERecordSet rs1 = variableHelper.CreateRecordSet();


rs1.Reset();


rs1.Clear();


rs1.First();


rs1.InsertRow();


String[] fields1 = { "1", "22", "333" };


String[] fields2 = { "1", "22", "333", "4444", "55555" };


rs1.UpdateRow(fields1);


rs1.InsertRow();


rs1.UpdateRow(fields2);


rs1.Next();


String string1 = rs1.GetString(2);





 isFirst


This method is used to check if the row is the first row.


###### Syntax


bool isFirst()


###### Return Value


bool: Returns a true or false value.





 isLast


This method is used to check if the row is the last row.


###### Syntax


bool isLast()


###### Return Value


bool: Returns a true or false value.





 Last


This method is used to set current row to last row in RecordSet.


###### Syntax


bool Last()


###### Return Value


bool: Returns a true or false value.


Example


The following example shows how to create a record set, and then add a new row at the end of the record set:


XERecordSet rs1 = variableHelper.CreateRecordSet();


rs1.AddRow();


rs1.Last();





 Next


This method is used to set pointer to the next row.


###### Syntax


bool Next()


###### Return Value


bool: Returns a true or false value.


Example


The following example shows how to create a record set and then step through the record set:


XERecordSet rs1 = variableHelper.CreateRecordSet();


rs2.First();


rs2.Next();


String string2 = rs2.GetString(3);





 Previous


This method is used to set a pointer to the previous row.


###### Syntax


bool Previous()


###### Return Value


bool: Returns a true or false value.





 Reset


This method is used to reset a row in the RecordSet.


###### Syntax


void Reset()


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to create a record set and then work with various settings available to a record set:


XERecordSet rs1 = variableHelper.CreateRecordSet();


rs1.Reset();


rs1.Clear();


rs1.First();


rs1.InsertRow();


String[] fields1 = { "1", "22", "333" };


String[] fields2 = { "1", "22", "333", "4444", "55555" };


rs1.UpdateRow(fields1);


rs1.InsertRow();


rs1.UpdateRow(fields2);


rs1.Next();


String string1 = rs1.GetString(2);





 UpdateRow


Use this method to update row in the RecordSet.


###### Syntax


void UpdateRow(String[] aFields)


###### Parameters


aFields: Field value.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to create a record set and then work with various settings available to a record set:


XERecordSet rs1 = variableHelper.CreateRecordSet();


rs1.Reset();


rs1.Clear();


rs1.First();


rs1.InsertRow();


String[] fields1 = { "1", "22", "333" };


String[] fields2 = { "1", "22", "333", "4444", "55555" };


rs1.UpdateRow(fields1);


rs1.InsertRow();


rs1.UpdateRow(fields2);


rs1.Next();


String string1 = rs1.GetString(2);





 UpdateString


Use this method to update strings in the RecordSet.


###### Syntax


void UpdateString(uint nColumnNumber, String sField)


###### Parameters


nColumnNumber: The number of a column as an integer.


sField: A string value.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.

<a id="translator-net-xeresulthelper"></a>

##### Translator .NET XEResultHelper

> 📄 Source: [Translator_NET_XEResultHelper.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_NET_XEResultHelper.htm)

###### Translator .NET XEResultHelper

 GetOutputsCount


This method is used to get the root node of the buffer storage.


###### Syntax


uint GetOutputsCount()


###### Return Value


uint: Number of connections.


Example


// The following example shows how to obtain a result helper, and to then retrieve output counts:


XEResultHelper resultHelper = context.GetResultHelper();


uint nOutputsCount = resultHelper.GetOutputsCount();





 ReportResultArray


Report array of results to function’s outputs.


###### Syntax


void ReportResultArray(String[] aDataArray)


###### Parameters


aDataArray: Array to report.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


// The following example shows how to obtain a result helper, and to then perform array-based reporting:


XEResultHelper resultHelper = context.GetResultHelper();


String[] array = { "First", "Second" };


resultHelper.ReportResultArray(array);





 ReportResultByIndex


Report result on function output by output’s index.


###### Syntax


void ReportResultByIndex(uint nOutputIndex, String sData)


###### Parameters


nOutputIndex: Output connector index.


sData: Data string that needs to be set to the node.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to obtain a results helper for use with position-based reporting:


XEResultHelper resultHelper = context.GetResultHelper();


resultHelper.ReportResultByIndex(0, "Result1");





 ReportResultByName


Report result on function output by output’s name.


###### Syntax


void ReportResultByName(String sOutputName, String sData)


###### Parameters


sOutputName: The output connector name.


sData: Data string that needs to be set to the node.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to obtain a result helper for use with name-based reporting:


XEResultHelper resultHelper = context.GetResultHelper();


resultHelper.ReportResultByName("output2", "Result2");





 Send


This method is used to send filled output results through the outbound connectors.


###### Syntax


void Send()


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


The following example shows how to obtain a result helper, and to then send already-reported results through output connectors:


XEResultHelper resultHelper = context.GetResultHelper();


resultHelper.Send();

---

<a id="translator-net-xevariablehelper"></a>

##### Translator .NET XEVariableHelper

> 📄 Source: [Translator_NET_XEVariableHelper.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_NET_XEVariableHelper.htm)

###### Translator .NET XEVariableHelper

 CreateRecordSet


This method is used to create a record set.


###### Syntax


XERecordSet CreateRecordSet()


###### Return Value


XERecordSet: Returns an XERecordSet object.


Example


// The following example shows how to obtain helper variables, and to then set those variables:


XEVariableHelper variableHelper = context.GetVariableHelper();


XERecordSet recordSet1 = variableHelper.CreateRecordSet();


variableHelper.SetRecordSet("MyRecordSet1", recordSet1, true);





 GetRecordSet


This method is used to get a record set. If there is no RecordSet with such name, Translator creates it.


###### Syntax


XERecordSet GetRecordSet(String sName)


###### Parameters


sName: The name of the record set.


###### Return Value


XERecordSet: Returns an XERecordSet object.


Example


//The following example shows how to get a result from one or more variables:


XERecordSet recordSet2 = variableHelper.GetRecordSet("MyRecordSet1");





 GetVariable


This method is used to extract a variable from a container.


###### Syntax


String GetVariable(String sName)


###### Parameters


sName: Name of the variable.


###### Return Value


String: Allows character values.


Example


//The following example shows how to get results from one or more variables:


String var1 = variableHelper.GetVariable("var1");


String var2 = variableHelper.GetVariable("var2");





 SetRecordSet


This method is used to set RecordSet as a global variable if bIsGlobal is set to true and local if set to false.


###### Syntax


Void SetRecordSet(String sName, XERecordSet refRecordSet, bool bGlobal)


###### Parameters


bGlobal: Set this parameter to true if you want the variable to be global.


refRecordSet: The RecordSet name.


sName: Name of the variable.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.





 SetVariable


This method is used to set the variable as a global variable if IsGlobal is set to true and local if false.Syntax


Void SetVariable(String sName, String sValue, bool bGlobal)


###### Parameters


bGlobal: Set this parameter to true if you want the variable to be global.


sName: Name of the variable.


sValue: Value for the parameter.


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


//The following example shows how to obtain helper variables, and to then set those variables:


XEVariableHelper variableHelper = context.GetVariableHelper();


variableHelper.SetVariable("var1", "value1", true);


variableHelper.SetVariable("var2", "value2", false);

<a id="translator-net-xenetsimplebdf"></a>

##### Translator .NET XENETSimpleBDF

> 📄 Source: [Translator_NET_XENETSimpleBDF.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Translator_NET_XENETSimpleBDF.htm)

###### Translator .NET XENETSimpleBDF


XENET.XENETSimpleBDF.XENETSimpleBDF(string sPath, uint nConnectorType) is a constructor of the XENET.XENETSimpleBDF class.


sPath is a path to a folder which is used as a temporary Data Node Structure (DNS) storage.


nConnectorType is a type of a connector. It specifies the way of storing DNS. The following types are allowed:


1: each data node is saved in a separate file.


2: each data node structure level is saved in a separate file.


3: a mode for multi-source processing in Translator, which extends the type 2.


This class has the following methods:

 CreateN2XBDF()


###### Syntax


XEDataProc CreateN2XBDF()


###### Return Value


Returns an XEDataProc object.


Example


This code snippet demonstrates how to create a data processor for Native to XData processing in the BDF mode.


using (XENETSimpleBDF simpleBDF = new XENETSimpleBDF("path", 2))


{


using (XEDataProc dataProc = simpleBDF.CreateN2XBDF())


}





 CreateTranslatorBDF()


###### Syntax


XEDataProc CreateTranslatorBDF()


###### Return Value


Returns an XEDataProc object.


Example


This code snippet demonstrates how to create an engine which controls the translator in the BDF mode.


using (XENETSimpleBDF simpleBDF = new XENETSimpleBDF("path", 2))


{


using (XEDataProc dataProc = simpleBDF.CreateTranslatorBDF())


}





 CreateResultProcBDF()


###### Syntax


XEResultProc CreateResultProcBDF()


###### Return Value


Returns an XEResultProc object.


Example


This code snippet demonstrates how to create an engine for the result processor in the BDF mode.


using (XENETSimpleBDF simpleBDF = new XENETSimpleBDF("path", 2))


{


using (XEResultProc resProc = simpleBDF.CreateResultProcBDF())


}





 Clear()


###### Syntax


void Clear();


###### Return Value


void: Method does not return a value.


Example


This code snippet demonstrates how to remove temporary data stored on a hard disk drive in BDF mode.


using (XENETSimpleBDF simpleBDF = new XENETSimpleBDF("path", 2))


{


simpleBDF.Clear();


}

<a id="debug-net-function"></a>

#### Debug .NET Function

> 📄 Source: [Debug_NET_Function.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Debug_NET_Function.htm)

##### Debug .NET Function


You can create your own .NET function code, that is used in the translation process. The code can be debugged and corrected in Visual Studio, that allows to find errors easily in the algorithm of the .NET function work in each certain case.


To debug the .NET function with Visual Studio:


Note To use sample files and project with .NET Action, go to the product installation folder and navigate to Help/Samples/Source_Debugging/Debugging_NET_Function folder.

1. On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click Open, and then select the map containing .NET function.
2. Start Visual Studio.
3. In Visual Studio, on the File menu, select Open.
4. Click Project/Solution, select your project with .NET function, and then click Open.
5. In Visual Studio, on the Debug menu, select Attach to Process.
6. In the Available Processes section, select MapBuilder.exe, and then click Attach.
7. In Solution Explorer pane, select the .NET function, and set the breakpoint on the first line of the class method you want to debug.
8. On the Home tab of the ribbon, select Translator.
9. On the Welcome to the Mapper Wizard wizard page, select the data file. The Translator is started.

---

<a id="lookahead-methods-for-javascriptjavanet-functions"></a>

#### Lookahead Methods for JavaScript/Java/.NET Functions

> 📄 Source: [LookAhead_Methods_for_JavaScript_Java_NET_Functions.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/LookAhead_Methods_for_JavaScript_Java_NET_Functions.htm)

##### Lookahead Methods for JavaScript/Java/.NET Functions


When working with Java/JavaScript/.NET functions, you can use the following Lookahead methods:


Note UPath is the path to the node; the second parameter (0) specifies the action for the node.


The second parameter has the following possible values:

0 - no action; returns collection as it is.

1 - returns sum of all found targets.

2 - returns minimal number or string from found collection of targets.

3 - returns maximum number or string from found collection of targets.


###### ec_DataHelper.Lookahead("UPath", 0);


Use this method to specify a lookup path in a JavaScript function.


Example


var1 = ec_DataHelper.Lookahead("/Transaction(ID = '101')/Loop(ID = 'DTM')/Loop(ID = 'LX')/Segment(ID = 'IN2')/Element(ID = '1104')", 0);


###### ctx.getDataHelper().lookahead("UPath", 0);


Use this method to specify a lookup path in a Java function.


Example


String[] arrData = ctx.getDataHelper().lookahead("/Transaction(ID = '101')/Loop(ID = 'DTM')/Loop(ID = 'LX')/Segment(ID = 'LX')/Element(ID = '554')", 0);


###### context.GetDataHelper().Lookahead("UPath", 0);


Use this method to specify a lookup path in a .NET function.


Example


String[] arrData = context.GetDataHelper().Lookahead("/Transaction(ID = '101')/Loop(ID = 'DTM')/Loop(ID = 'LX')/Segment(ID = 'LX')/Element(ID = '554')", 0);

---

<a id="create-and-register-java-functions"></a>

#### Create and Register Java Functions

> 📄 Source: [Create_and_Register_Java_Functions.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Create_and_Register_Java_Functions.htm)

##### Create and Register Java Functions


You can use Java callouts in a map but your Java class should be derived from com.edifecs.xengine.mapper.XEMapFunction, and the methods in this class should have specific signatures.


To use Java functions in a map:

1. Create a Java class that extends com.edifecs.xengine.mapper.XEMapFunction. For example, public class MyClass extends XEMapFunction. You can use the XE Java classes or the standard Java classes by importing them into your Java code. For more information, see [Java Function Settings](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/JavaScript_Function_Settings.htm).
2. In your Java class, create methods with the public void <method name>(XEMapperContext ctx) throws XEException signature. For example, public void MyMethod (XEMapperContext ctx) throws XEException.
3. To register your .jar file, navigate to the Edifecs installation folder Common\JAExtension and place your .jar file there. The default path is C:\Edifecs\Common\JAExtension.
4. On the Home tab of the ribbon, click Java Classes Declaration and in the Java Classes Declaration Editor dialog box, declare the variables you want to use. To do this, click New and complete the following fields:
5. Click OK.
6. Select your class and method on the Settings tab of the Java Function in your map.


For more information on Java classes declaration, see [Java Classes Declaration](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Java_Classes_Declaration.htm).

---

<a id="java-classes-declaration"></a>

#### Java Classes Declaration

> 📄 Source: [Java_Classes_Declaration.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Java_Classes_Declaration.htm)

##### Java Classes Declaration


Once you have created your Java class, you need to declare it. 

1. Configure your system CLASSPATH, Java Extension folder, directly registered class folders, or JAR files in the [Java/.NET Workspace Editor](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Reference_Custom_JAR_and_NET_DLL_Files_or_Java_and_NET_Classes.htm) to reference your Java class.
2. On the Home tab of the ribbon, click Java Classes Declaration.
3. In the Java Classes Declaration Editor dialog box, click New.
4. Complete the following fields:
5. Click OK.


Note Some JAR files can contain more than one class.


To integrate your Java classes with the Run-Time Engine:


Your class must implement a specific interface (XEMapFunction) to be accessible by the run-time engine.


To implement this interface, your Java class code should have the following structure:


import com.edifecs.xengine.mapper.*;


import com.edifecs.xengine.XEException;


import java.util.Properties;


import com.edifecs.xengine.mapper.XEMapperContext;


import com.edifecs.xengine.mapper.helpers.XEDataHelper;


import com.edifecs.xengine.mapper.helpers.XEResultHelper;


public class <class name> extends XEMapFunction

---

<a id="net-classes-declaration"></a>

#### .NET Classes Declaration

> 📄 Source: [NET_Classes_Declaration.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/NET_Classes_Declaration.htm)

##### .NET Classes Declaration


To reference your .NET class, register your .NET assembly by using one of the following methods:


Note The methods are listed in order of priority.

- Register .NET assembly in Global Assembly Cache.
- Put your .NET assembly in the folder with installed SpecBuilder, where SpecBuilder.exe is located.
- Register .NET assembly in [Java/.NET Workspace Editor](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Reference_Custom_JAR_and_NET_DLL_Files_or_Java_and_NET_Classes.htm).
- Put your .NET assembly in folder %ECRootPath%/Common/NETExtension.


To declare your .NET class:

1. On the Home tab of the ribbon, click .NET Classes Declaration.
2. In the .NET Classes Declaration Editor dialog box, click New.
3. Complete the following fields:

1. Click OK.


Note Some .dll files can contain more than one class.


To integrate your .NET classes with the Run-Time Engine:


Your class must implement a specific interface (XEMapFunction) to be accessible by the run-time engine.


To declare your .NET class, use the following pattern:


using XENET;


public void <method_name>(XENET.XEMapperContext context)


Example


using System;


using System.Collections.Generic;


using System.Text;


using XENET;


namespace SampleNETFunction


{


public class ElementFunction


{


public ElementFunction()


{


// constructor


}


public void run(XENET.XEMapperContext context)


{


// user method implementation


}


}


}

---

<a id="keyboard-shortcuts"></a>

### Keyboard Shortcuts

> 📄 Source: [MB_Keyboard_Shortcuts.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/MB_Keyboard_Shortcuts.htm)

#### Keyboard Shortcuts


Map Document contains several shortcuts to support mouse-free navigation in addition to the standard Windows keyboard shortcuts.


| Keyboard Command | Used in | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Alt + 1 | Map Editor | Jumps to the Source Guideline view. |
| Alt + 2 | Map Editor | Jumps to the Map Area view. |
| Alt + 3 | Map Editor | Jumps to the Target Guideline view. |
| Ctrl + Tab | Map Editor | Allows to navigate through tabs by Tab (forward) or Shift+Tab (backward) while holding the Ctrl key. |
| Arrow keys | Map Area view | Changes the selection between hot points, connectors and functions. |
| Tab | Map Area view | Moves forward sequentially through all the functions in the map document. |
| Shift + Tab | Map Area view | Moves backward sequentially through all the functions in the map document. |
| Ctrl + Enter | Map Area view | Enters a connector between two selected connection points. |
| Ctrl+F5 | Map Editor | Starts Translator. |
| F5 | Map Editor | Starts/Resumes debugging. |
| F12 | Map Editor | Pauses debugging. |
| Shift + F5 | Map Editor | Stops debugging. |
| F11 | Map Editor | Moves debugging to the next child node. Disabled when running. |
| F10 | Map Editor | Processes child nodes not stopping execution. Disabled when running. |
| Shift + F11 | Map Editor | Executes only sibling objects and stops on the next parent object. |
| F9 | Map Editor | Toggles breakpoint to the selected object. |

---

<a id="map-comparison-tool"></a>

### Map Comparison Tool

> 📄 Source: [Map_Comparison_Tool.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Map_Comparison_Tool.htm)

#### Map Comparison Tool


The Map Comparison Tool's purpose is to create a comprehensive visualization process for review and localization of differences and their types that are obtained after comparing two maps.


The tool offers the possibility to export the differences to a .csv file that can help to update map data or track data modifications performed on a map.


##### Tool Usage

1. Open a map with higher version or a map containing various customizations.
2. Click on **Compare** from the Home ribbon:[*Select the map with lower version or the base map (before customizations) in order to visualize what has been Inserted*, *Modified*, or *Deleted* compared to the higher version map or the customized map, respectively.](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Resources/Images/CompareButtonHomeRibbon.png)Access the **Changes** pane to explore the differences for various elements (Variables, Functions, etc.):*Double click on any item or use the context menu to find its location within the map itself:You can also click on Modified* to see more details about the change:*The **User Notes** tab offers quick access to the item's notes that were modified as part of the change:**Note** All notes related to the item can be viewed by accessing the Properties option from the context menu.The **Settings** tab offers code level differences for the selected item – Current vs Previous:You can also explore the modified **Instances** for Guideline Nodes:**Context Menu options**Below are the options available when performing right-click on an item:OptionDescription**Show**Shows the location of the item within the map structure.**Show Related Items**Show the item's related items.**Clear Filter**Clears any set Filter.**Copy**Copies the item.**Properties**Opens the Function Properties or Connector Properties window.**Note** The Properties option is enabled only for Functions and Connectors. Access the **Changes** (General) tab to use the tool's options and functions:OptionDescription**Compare**Selects another map to compare against the source map.**Refresh**Use this button to refresh the view when adding new changes to the map.**Clear**Clears and resets all the current changes (differences) and tracking. Click on **Compare** to select a map. **Set**Select filters to narrow down the list of changes as per your requirements:**Clear**Clears the selected filters.**Export to CSV**Exports all the differences to a .csv file. The reports can be used to migrate map data from one version to another or track data modifications performed on a map. These can be viewed in the **DifferencesMapReport.xml** file generated by the tool. Go to: {tool_location} This folder should also contain the maps that are being compared, e.g., map1.ems* and *map2.ems*.
3. Open a command prompt window and use the following line with the required parameters: map_comparison_tool.bat <base_map> <modified_map> Example map_comparison_tool.bat map1.ems map2.ems Keep in mind that the second map will always be compared against the first map. In case of the above example, *map2.ems* will be compared against the *map1.ems*.
4. Open the **DifferencesMapReport.xml** file and investigate the differences.


##### Tool CLI Parameters


The following options/parameters are available:


| Usage | Option | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| parameter_1 | -i | example |
|   |   |   |
|   |   |   |
|   |   |   |



##### Differences Map Reports


The tool generates two types of reports: **DifferencesMapReport.emd**, which can be opened directly in the UI, and the **DifferencesMapReport.xml**, which displays the list of modifications in an organized and readable manner.


###### Report Sample Example


The **DifferencesMapReport.xml**'s nodes and structure reflect the modifications made to various elements and entities present in a map. These modifications consist of *Changed*, *Deleted* and *Inserted* data performed on Functions, Variables, Notes, etc.


Below you can find a base and modified map example:


<screen>


In this case, the Functions value was renamed. This update is present in the generated report under the **Functions - Changed** node:


<screen>


 


 
-->
