<a id="create-a-new-guideline-document"></a>

#### Create a New Guideline Document

> 📄 Source: [Create_a_New_Guideline_Document.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Create_a_New_Guideline_Document.htm)

##### Create a New Guideline Document


You can create a new specification guideline document based on one of the many published standards supplied with SpecBuilder.


To create a new specification guideline document:

1. On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click New, and then click Guideline.
2. In the left pane of the Please select a guideline dialog box, click the type of document to create.
3. In the right pane, do one of the following:
4. Click Finish.


The specification guideline is created and the Guideline panes and multiple tabs are displayed.


Note If the format of Standards has changed since the last time a specification guideline was created, a message saying that the specification guideline is being upgraded is shown. This upgrades only the changes in the format and does not change rules or add any content to the guideline. For information about migrating rules and/or content, see [Migrator Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Migrator_Overview.htm).

<a id="create-a-new-xml-schema-document"></a>

#### Create a New XML Schema Document

> 📄 Source: [Create_a_New_XML_Schema_Document.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Create_a_New_XML_Schema_Document.htm)

##### Create a New XML Schema Document


An XML Schema is a guideline that specifies the structure and format of a document.


To create a new XML Schema guideline document:

1. On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click New, and then click Guideline.
2. In the New document creation dialog box, click the XMLSchema folder, and then double-click Blank XML Schema.

---

<a id="work-with-nodes"></a>

#### Work with Nodes

> 📄 Source: [Work_with_Nodes.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Work_with_Nodes.htm)

##### Work with Nodes


Nodes are graphical representations of the structure of a guideline document. The structure is shown in the Guideline pane, and the information about the node is displayed on the [Properties Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Properties_Pane_Overview.htm). Therefore, all the information included in the guideline is represented in the user interface (UI).


*[Image: Working with nodes]*


Right-click any node in the Guideline pane to perform any of the following actions:

- Include or Exclude: Marks a node as included or excluded in the guideline. An excluded node is prevented from being used within a guideline, but can be reincorporated by selecting Include. Other components use this information during operations such as exporting a guideline.
- Move Up or Move Down: Moves the selected node up or down.
- Cut, Copy, Paste: Removes or duplicates a node, or moves a node anywhere in the tree. For some standards, shortcut icons are provided to allow users to move nodes up and down by one selection.
- Paste As Child: Nests the node within another node.
- Delete: Removes the node permanently from the guideline document. The node cannot be reincorporated into the guideline.
- Add to User Node Dictionary: Use to add a selected node to the User Node Dictionary for further use in other guidelines. For more information, see [User Node Dictionary Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/User_Node_Dictionary_Overview.htm).
- Copy Business Path: Copies a path to a node in a user friendly form.
- Print Node: Prints this node only.
- Print Preview: Preview the print format for this node only.
- User Node Dictionary: Inserts a sibling or a child from the user node dictionary and saves a node to a user node dictionary.
- Add Error Severity: Adds the selected note to the error severity.
- Insert Sibling: Inserts an allowed node such as a loop, segment, composite, or element in EDI guidelines. The available node type options may be different depending on what type of node you select. You can also insert an empty node, which you can customize to suit your needs.
- Insert Child: Nests a node within another node. The available options may be different depending on what type of node you select. You can also insert an empty node, which you can customize to suit your needs.


Note Most of these actions are also available on the Home tab.

---

<a id="generate-a-guideline-from-data-file"></a>

### Generate a Guideline from Data File

> 📄 Source: [Book_Generate_a_Guideline_from_Data_File.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Generate_a_Guideline_from_Data_File.htm)

#### Generate a Guideline from Data File

<a id="specgenerator-overview"></a>

#### SpecGenerator Overview

> 📄 Source: [SpecGenerator_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/SpecGenerator_Overview.htm)

| # SpecGenerator Overview | |
| --- | --- |



SpecGenerator enables you to create a guideline document from one or more existing data (.dat) files and the standard or another guideline file. Using multiple data files allows you to fine-tune the specification.


Note The more complete the data is in the data file(s), the more complete the generated specification is.


###### Features and Benefits


The features and benefits of using SpecGenerator are:

- Cost Savings: By regenerating guidelines from data files, you can quickly and easily make changes to your guidelines without having to manually scan through a text file for errors or invalid data.
- Data Viewing/Editing: State of the art, intuitive, easy-to-use interface for creating, viewing, and editing EDI data.
- Node Editing: Easy modification of information at the segment, element, composite, and code level.
- SpecGenerator can now generate XML Schema guidelines from XML files.

---

<a id="xsd-specgenerator"></a>

#### XSD SpecGenerator

> 📄 Source: [XSD_SpecGenerator.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/XSD_SpecGenerator.htm)

##### XSD SpecGenerator


Example of the source XML Schema Example of a generated XSD Example of the source XML data file Example of the main generated XSD External schema generated by SpecGenerator Example of ambiguous occurrences Example of inconsistent element types in XML Example of inconsistent element types in XSD Example of xsi:type Usage Example of character data in XML Example of a complexType in the resulting XSD


SpecGenerator for XSD takes an XML document and creates a W3C XML Schema as defined by [the XML Schema recommendation](http://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema-1/).


Note XSD SpecGenerator processes only XML files with an .xml extension.


###### The generated XSD has the following properties:

- The schema is produced by using the [Salami Slice](http://www.xfront.com/GlobalVersusLocal.html#SecondDesign) design.
- All elements are defined globally but the types are defined locally. This way other schemas can reuse the elements.
- Each declared XML namespace is imported to the main schema.


Example of the source XML Schema



Example of a generated XSD



Example of the source XML data file



Example of the main generated XSD



External schema generated by SpecGenerator



###### SpecGenerator for XSD has the following limitations:

- SpecGenerator for XSD may generate schemas that violate W3C XML Schema's restrictions on ambiguous content models.


Each element in an instance document corresponds unambiguously to exactly one `<xsd:element>` or ` <xsd:any>` particle in its parent's content model.


The most common causes of ambiguity are `<xsd:any>` wildcard characters and particles that have variable occurrence ranges, such as `minOccurs/maxOccurs`. Any schemas that contain a type with a potentially ambiguous content model are rejected.


The following content models are ambiguous:

- XML data files containing xsi attributes, such as `xsi:type` and `xsi:nil` attributes, are ignored by XSD SpecGenerator.
- Any schemas that contain inconsistent element types are rejected.
- Some real XML data files, such as files from a STAR XML standard, have character data within elements.
- SpecGenerator for XSD creates a` complexType` for such elements and doesn't set mixed property value to true. In this case, correspondent XML validation errors are reported.



Example of ambiguous occurrences



Example of inconsistent element types in XML



Example of inconsistent element types in XSD



Example of xsi:type Usage



Example of character data in XML



Example of a complexType in the resulting XSD

<a id="the-specgenerator-wizard"></a>

#### The SpecGenerator Wizard

> 📄 Source: [Book_The_SpecGenerator_Wizard.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_The_SpecGenerator_Wizard.htm)

##### The SpecGenerator Wizard


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="select-a-data-file"></a>

##### Select a Data File

> 📄 Source: [SpecGenerator_Select_Data_File.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/SpecGenerator_Select_Data_File.htm)

###### Select a Data File


With SpecGenerator, you can create a guideline based on the contents of a data file. Then you can view and edit the guideline to meet your business needs.


To generate an EDI, NCPDP Telecom, or HL7 guideline from a data file: 

1. On the Navigation pane, click SpecGenerator.
2. In the Select data file to analyze box, type the data file name or click Browse to select the file.
3. In the Specify data file type box, select the file type.
4. Select Advanced Options if you want to specify any of the following:

1. Select Initial SpecGenerator pass when processing the first of multiple data files to create the guideline.


Note Select Initial SpecGenerator pass function for the first data file only. Unused nodes in the data file are flagged as Excluded in the guideline. When processing additional files, nodes that are present but were initially marked as Excluded are marked as Included now.

1. Click Next to go to set [SpecGenerator Preprocessing Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Preprocessor_Options.htm).


To generate a DFF or PFF guideline from a data file: 


For Positional Flat Files and Delimited Flat Files you can now interactively construct guidelines from the data file only.

1. In the Specify data file type field, choose Delimited Flat File or Positional Flat File.
2. Check Based on Data Only box, to create a guideline from the data file only.
3. Click Next to go to [SpecGenerator Message Definition](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/SpecGenerator_Message_Definition.htm) page.


To generate an XML Schema guideline from an XML file:

1. In the Specify data file type field, select XML.
2. In the Select data file to analyze box, type the data file name or click Browse to select the file.
3. Click Next to generate a guideline.


For more information about XSD generation, see [XSD SpecGenerator](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/XSD_SpecGenerator.htm).


To generate a JSON guideline from a data file:

1. In the Specify data file type field, select JSON.
2. In the Select data file to analyze box, type the data file name or click Browse to select the file.
3. Click Next to generate a guideline.

---

<a id="preprocessor-options"></a>

##### Preprocessor Options

> 📄 Source: [Preprocessor_Options_SpecGenerator.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Preprocessor_Options_SpecGenerator.htm)

###### Preprocessor Options


If you want to clean up the data file, you can use the Preprocessor. With the Preprocessor, you can eliminate any terminator string or block characters in the data.

1. In the Clean Up Data File dialog box, select the Use Preprocessor check box.
2. In the Parameters box, select a saved parameter file to use, if applicable.
3. Do one of the following:
4. To save these settings, click Save As and, in the Preprocessor Parameters Name server, enter a name or select an existing parameter name to modify its definition.
5. Enter a path to save the modified data file or click Browse to locate a folder in which to save the modified data file.
6. Click Next. For standards such as PFF, DFF, and NCPDP, go to step 8.
7. In the Parsing result dialog box, do one of the following:
8. For standards such as PFF, DFF, and NCPDP Batch, in the Record Parsing Information dialog box, select the record delimiter used in your data file or click Next to accept the default delimiter.
9. Click Next to go to the [Select properties file wizard page](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Properties_File_Analyzer.htm).

---

<a id="properties-file"></a>

##### Properties File

> 📄 Source: [Properties_File_SpecGenerator.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Properties_File_SpecGenerator.htm)

###### Properties File


You can select a file with the list of all data node properties needed to be set for the input data file.


A properties file is a configuration .properties file that is used to add custom properties to the specified data nodes. The parameters are stored as key-value pairs. You can use these properties in Java Script, Java, and .NET rules.


A property file contains lines of the following format that add properties to the required data nodes:


dns.fnm.<data node name>.prp.<name>=<value>

- *dns* - a root value that indicates a data node structure
- *fnm.<data node name>* - moves selection to the data node with the matching name. For example, dns.fnm.Interchange moves the selection to the interchange node.
- *prp.<name>* - creates a new property with the name <name> and the value <value>. If the specified property already exists then its value is overwritten.


Example


dns.fnm.EntireDocument.prp.TransmissionFormat=HIPAA5010A


Note If a data node used in the instruction does not exist, the instruction is ignored.


To select a data node properties file:

1. In the Select properties file dialog box, click to navigate to the required properties file.
2. Click Next to go to the next wizard page.

---

<a id="data-structure"></a>

##### Data Structure

> 📄 Source: [Data_Structure_SpecGenerator.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Structure_SpecGenerator.htm)

###### Data Structure


To select a node to validate:

1. In the Data Structure dialog box, select a node to validate. The node's properties are defined in the right pane. By default, the entire document is validated. To limit the validation to specific levels, highlight the appropriate folder.

1. Click Next to go to the next wizard page.

---

<a id="guideline-selection"></a>

##### Guideline Selection

> 📄 Source: [Guideline_Selection_SpecGenerator.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Guideline_Selection_SpecGenerator.htm)

###### Guideline Selection

1. In the Guideline selection dialog box, specify the guideline to use for validation. This can be either from the Standards database or from a specific guideline file.

1. Click Next and go to Envelope Segments.


For more information on Envelope Segments, see [Select Envelope Segments](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Select_Envelope_Segments_Analyzer.htm).

---

<a id="select-envelope-segments"></a>

##### Select Envelope Segments

> 📄 Source: [Select_Envelope_Segments_SpecGenerator.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Select_Envelope_Segments_SpecGenerator.htm)

###### Select Envelope Segments


Note Envelopes are not defined for the XML Schema documents, so this option is not available for XML Schema guidelines.


###### Guidelines with envelope segments:


Select one of the following options:

- Use the existing Envelope Segments: Select this option to use the envelope specified in your guideline. Click Next.
- Remove existing and select new Envelope Segments from the database: Select this option to replace envelope segments with ones from the Envelopes database. Click Next, select the envelope segment from the database, click Next, and go to Using Envelope Segments.
- Remove existing and use Envelope Segments from the external guideline file: Select this option to use envelopes stored in an external file. Click Next, select the guideline file to use, and then click Next.
- Remove existing and use Envelope Segments from multiple guideline files: Select this option to use envelopes stored in multiple external files. This option is useful to match the structure of envelope guidelines to that used by the Run-Time engine where Interchange guideline and Functional Group guidelines are stored in separate .ecs files. See "Selecting envelope segments from multiple guideline files" below.


###### Guidelines without envelope segments:

- Select Envelope Segments from the Standards Database: Select this option to add envelope segments from the Standards database. Click Next, select the envelope segment from the database, click Next, and go to Using Envelope Segments.
- Process without Envelope Segments: Select this option to go ahead and analyze the file without envelope segments.
- Use Envelope Segments from the external guideline file: Select this option to add envelope segments stored in an external file.
- Use Envelope Segments from multiple guideline files: Select this option to use envelope segments stored in multiple external files. This option is useful to match the structure of envelope guidelines to that used by the Run-Time engine where Interchange guideline and Functional Group guidelines are stored in separate .ecs files. See "Selecting envelope segments from multiple guideline files" below.


###### Selecting envelope segments from multiple guideline files

1. Specify a guideline file for the Interchange level and one for the Functional Group and then click Next.

1. Click Next to go to the next wizard page.

---

<a id="select-modeoutputs"></a>

##### Select Mode/Outputs

> 📄 Source: [Select_Mode_Outputs.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Select_Mode_Outputs.htm)

###### Select Mode/Outputs

1. In the Analyzer Mode and Outputs dialog box, specify the following Analyzer options:
2. Click Next. After the file has been processed, the Wizard Result dialog box informs you if your file has any errors or not.

---

<a id="specgenerator-message-definition"></a>

##### SpecGenerator Message Definition

> 📄 Source: [SpecGenerator_Message_Definition.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/SpecGenerator_Message_Definition.htm)

###### SpecGenerator Message Definition


Use this page of the wizard to set the properties for the top-level message node.

- Run Data Analyzer at the end: Select this check box to run Data Analyzer with the generated guideline and the data file. Otherwise only the generated guideline is shown after finishing the wizard.
- ID (Required): Type a message ID.
- Name: Type a message name.
- Standard: Type the guideline standard.
- Version: Type the guideline version.
- Purpose: Type a purpose for the message.


Click Next, to go to [Record Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/SpecGenerator_Record_Options.htm) page.

---

<a id="specgenerator-record-options"></a>

##### SpecGenerator Record Options

> 📄 Source: [SpecGenerator_Record_Options.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/SpecGenerator_Record_Options.htm)

###### SpecGenerator Record Options


Use this step of the wizard to specify record terminators and text identifiers that are used in the data file. This page differs for PFF and DFF data files.


To set record options for DFF:


*[Image: Record Options]*

- Delimiter: Specify a delimiter for parsing the data into records. You can provide a delimiter in three ways:
- Text qualifier: Provide a character to be used as a text qualifier. This can be useful to distinguish text when the text contains delimiter characters. You can also provide a text qualifier by using the preview window. In the preview window, select a character, right-click it and choose Set text qualifier.
- Data Preview Size (KB): You can change the data preview size. The default data preview size is one KB. It is also the minimum size or the data file size if it is less than the size value.
- Data is in table format: Click to turn on a table mode. Only one record is available, Record Definition page is skipped.
- First record defines field names: This option is available if you use table mode. Click to use first record data as a definition for the field names. Two records are created when this option is used with the first record keyed based on the names with max use set to 1 and second record representing data content.
- Wrap line: Clear the box to preview the data in a line without wrapping.


After you have set the record options, click Next to go to the [Record Definition](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/SpecGenerator_Record_Definition.htm) page.


To set record options for PFF:


*[Image: Record Options]*

- Delimited: Click to specify a delimiter for parsing the data into records. You can provide a delimiter in three ways:
- In the Delimiter box, enter a character to be used.
- In the Hex box, provide a hex value for the delimiter.
- In the preview window, select a character to be used as delimiter, right-click it, and then click Set Delimiter.
- Positional: Click to parse the data by position. To enter the position value, do one of the following:
- In the Position field, provide the number of characters to be included in one record.
- In the preview window, set the cursor where you want to set a position, right-click, and then click Set position.
- Data Preview Size (KB): You can change data preview size. The default data preview size is one KB. It is also the minimum size or the data file size if it is less than the size value. 
- Wrap line: Clear the box to view the data without line wrapping.
- Data is in table format: Click to turn on a table mode. Only one record is available, the Record Definition page is skipped.
- First record defines field names: This option is available if you use table mode. Click to use first record data as a definition for the field names. Two records are created when this option is used with the first record keyed based on the names with max use set to 1 and second record representing data content.


After you have set the record options, click Next to go to the [Field Definition](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/SpecGenerator_Field_Definition.htm) or [Record Definition](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/SpecGenerator_Record_Definition.htm) page.

---

<a id="specgenerator-record-definition"></a>

##### SpecGenerator Record Definition

> 📄 Source: [SpecGenerator_Record_Definition.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/SpecGenerator_Record_Definition.htm)

###### SpecGenerator Record Definition


Use this page to define the record structure for the generated guideline.


*[Image: Record Definition]*


You can add or remove group records and move existing records around to define the structure of the message. Use the toolbar to add or remove group nodes.


Note If you remove the group node all its children are moved one level up in the tree.


You can edit records by changing their status, max use, min use, requirement and user options. To edit the record, right-click it and choose one of the following:

- Set 'Status' to:
- Normal: Choose to leave the record without changes.
- Skip: To skip the record while generating a guideline.
- Repeat: To show that this item repeats the previous one and can be skipped while parsing. You can choose to skip it or not in the [Field Definition](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/SpecGenerator_Field_Definition.htm) page.
- Set 'Requirement' to: Choose the appropriate requirement.
- Set 'User Option' to: Choose the user option.
- Click Auto adjust max use (), to automatically set the max use of the repeated record as shown in the following example:


If you do not use the Auto adjust max use function, then you have to change the max use value manually.


After you have defined the record structure, click Next to go to the [Field Definition](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/SpecGenerator_Field_Definition.htm) page.

---

<a id="specgenerator-field-definition"></a>

##### SpecGenerator Field Definition

> 📄 Source: [SpecGenerator_Field_Definition.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/SpecGenerator_Field_Definition.htm)

###### SpecGenerator Field Definition


Use this page of the wizard to create the fields for each identified record or a composite field. Record data that is currently being parsed is highlighted in the Data Preview window.

- Record ID: Enter a valid ID for the record.
- Record Name: You can optionally enter a record name.
- Wrap line: Clear the box to preview the data in a line without wrapping.
- Field separator: Provide a field separator or its hexadecimal value in this field.


Note This field is only available for DFF data files. 


To set options:

1. On the toolbar, click Options ().
2. Choose to perform one of the following:


###### Edit Fields


Modify values in the following fields:

- ID: Edit the name of the field. Spaces are not allowed in the name.
- Type: Select a value from the list of supported data types. By default, the value is set to String.
- Requirement: Select a value from the list of supported requirements. By default, the value is set to Optional.
- User Option: Select a value from the list of supported options. By default, the value is set to Used.


Positional Flat Files additionally have the following fields:

- Offset: This field is available in the read-only mode. It is automatically updated when the value in the size column is changed.
- Size: Provide the length of the field.
- Justification: Choose whether you want to use right or left justification. By default, left justification is used.
- Padding: Choose whether you want to use a zero or a space for a padding character. By default, Space is set.

---

<a id="customize-guidelines"></a>

### Customize Guidelines

> 📄 Source: [Book_Customize_Guidelines.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Customize_Guidelines.htm)

#### Customize Guidelines


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="insert-a-node"></a>

#### Insert a Node

> 📄 Source: [Insert_a_Node.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Insert_a_Node.htm)

##### Insert a Node


You can customize a standard system guideline document to suit your business or transaction needs. Adding a node allows you to add critical or missing information to complete the specification guideline document as necessary. Nodes are individual items on a tree. Nodes summarize detail values or other nodes, and may or may not roll up into other nodes or levels. A node can be a parent and a child at the same time depending on its location within the tree.


*[Image: Insert node]*


To add a new node to your document:

1. In the Guideline pane, click the Guideline tab, and then right-click the node where you want to add the new node.
2. Select Insert Sibling or Insert Child, and then click the type of node you want to insert.
3. Do one of the following:

---

<a id="insert-or-remove-elements"></a>

#### Insert or Remove Elements

> 📄 Source: [Insert_or_Remove_Elements.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Insert_or_Remove_Elements.htm)

##### Insert or Remove Elements


Elements can be deleted from a guideline permanently, excluded temporarily, re-included, or copied from one node and inserted in another.


To delete an element:

1. In the Guideline pane, right-click the element you want to delete.
2. Click Delete. The element is then permanently removed from the guideline.


To exclude an element:

1. In the Guideline pane, right-click the element you want to exclude.
2. Click Exclude. The element is then temporarily excluded from the guideline.


To include an excluded element:

1. In the Guideline pane, right-click the element you want to include.
2. Click Include. The element is added back to the guideline.


To copy and insert an element:

1. In the Guideline pane, right-click the element you want to copy.
2. Click Copy.
3. Navigate to the node or element where you want to insert the element.
4. Right-click the node or element.
5. Click Paste.


The element is inserted after the selected node or element.


Note You can perform these operations for multiple nodes simultaneously. For instructions, see [Modifying Multiple Nodes](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Modify_Multiple_Nodes.htm).

---

<a id="search-nodes"></a>

#### Search Nodes

> 📄 Source: [Search_Nodes.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Search_Nodes.htm)

##### Search Nodes


To modify information in multiple instances of a node or node type (for example, change all comments within a certain node type), you may need to use the search function.


To search all nodes within a guideline for a specific text:

- On the Home tab, click Find/Replace.
- Find what: Type the text for search here.
- Replace with: Type replacement text here.
- Find Next or Find All: Click to locate your search criteria. 
- Replace or Replace All: Click to insert your replacement text in the guideline.
- Advanced: You can restrict your search to affect only specific nodes and properties:


###### Options:

- Match Case: Distinguishes between upper and lower case characters.
- Match Word: Clear the check box to search for the matching parts of the word or phrase. Check the box to view only the total match results.  


###### Direction:


Choose the direction in which you want to search (**Up** or **Down**).


###### View:

- Guideline: Click to apply search to the whole guideline.
- Functions: Click to search only within Functions.
- Variables: Click to search only within Variables.

---

<a id="modify-nodes"></a>

#### Modify Nodes

> 📄 Source: [Modify_Nodes.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Modify_Nodes.htm)

##### Modify Nodes


The right pane shows information about each node (segment, element, composite, code list, and the rest) related to that node's position and function in the guideline. You can modify the following information:

- General: Modify the name, position, standard option, user option, and other important information about the guideline.
- Notes: View the default description and/or add up to six user notes of your own. You can also see the semantics and comments associated with each node in this section.
- Application Field: Record the source or destination of the information contained in your internal database.
- Advanced Settings: Specify the specific elements in a segment that are the key fields the validation engine should use to link the data file to the guideline.
- Syntax Rules: Create, modify or delete the syntax rules that govern how elements are arranged within segments. See [Edit Syntax and Dependency Rules](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Edit_Syntax_and_Dependency_Rules.htm) for more information.


*[Image: Properties tab]*


To modify node's properties:

1. In the Details pane, click the tab that contains the information you want to modify.
2. Make the appropriate changes, and then click Apply to add the information to the node's properties and to the specification guideline document.


Note You may not be able to modify all properties. The ability to modify certain fields is controlled by [Global Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Set_Global_Options.htm).

---

<a id="modify-multiple-nodes"></a>

#### Modify Multiple Nodes

> 📄 Source: [Modify_Multiple_Nodes.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Modify_Multiple_Nodes.htm)

##### Modify Multiple Nodes


There is often a need to perform an identical operation on multiple nodes and it is useful to select multiple nodes and do this operation just once.


When performing some operations on a parent node, the operation extends to all child nodes. However, if you mark a parent node as excluded or included, it does not change the state of the child node - for example, if a child node was excluded and the parent is marked as included, then the child remains excluded.


To modify multiple nodes:

1. In the Guideline pane, click a parent node.
2. Click the Children pane.
3. Select the nodes that you want to change.
4. Right-click anywhere in the window and click the action to perform - Exclude, Copy, Cut, Select All, or Delete.

---

<a id="edit-syntax-and-dependency-rules"></a>

#### Edit Syntax and Dependency Rules

> 📄 Source: [Edit_Syntax_and_Dependency_Rules.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Edit_Syntax_and_Dependency_Rules.htm)

##### Edit Syntax and Dependency Rules


Syntax and dependency rules can be modified to meet your specific requirements.


To change the syntax and dependency rules:

1. Open a guideline, and then click a data segment.
2. Click the Properties tab and go to the Syntax Rules section.
3. Click Edit Rules.
4. Add, modify, or delete the rule as shown later:


To add a new syntax or dependency rule:

1. Click Add.
2. Select the type of rule to be added.
3. Click the elements (or entities in the case of EDIFACT) placeholder to specify the elements in the rule.
4. Select an element or elements.
5. Click OK to confirm the elements.
6. Click OK to confirm the rule type.
7. Click OK to confirm adding the rule.


Note To select multiple elements, click and drag the cursor over adjacent elements, or hold the control key down, and then click on non-adjacent elements.


To modify a syntax or dependency rule:

1. Click the rule to be changed.
2. Click the underlined element to be changed.
3. Select a new element or elements from the list of elements in the segment.
4. Click OK to confirm the elements.
5. Click OK to confirm modifying the rule.


To delete a syntax or dependency rule:

1. Select the rule to be removed.
2. Click Remove.
3. Click OK.

---

<a id="insert-envelope-segments"></a>

#### Insert Envelope Segments

> 📄 Source: [Insert_Envelope_Segments.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Insert_Envelope_Segments.htm)

##### Insert Envelope Segments


Envelope segments are used to encapsulate transaction messages. Like an envelope for sending a letter, envelope segments contain addressing information (routing data). Envelope segments consist of a header, a trailer, and sometimes other control segments for defining the start and the end of an individual transaction message.


To insert an envelope segment into a guideline document:

1. On the Home tab, click Insert Envelope Segments.
2. Select one of the following as the source of the envelope segment:

---

<a id="upath-overview"></a>

#### UPath Overview

> 📄 Source: [UPath_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/UPath_Overview.htm)

##### UPath Overview


A UPath is the selection language that determines the location of a node within a tree. It allows you to specifically identify any node in a tree, starting either from the top or bottom of the tree.


A UPath consists of data node names, attributes, and/or steps, separated by the following special characters:

- Forward: In this case, all the steps in the path are separated by slashes (/). The notation is left to right.
- Backward: In this case, all the steps of the path are separated by dots (.). The notation is right to left.


###### Symbols Used in the Path


| Symbol | Description |
| --- | --- |
| / | A step separator for forward paths. |
| . | A step separator for backward paths. |
| .. | Represents a parent node. This can be only used in a forward path. |
| * | A wildcard representing one or more location steps. |
| ? | A wildcard representing a single location step. |
| , | An attribute separator. |
| () | Contains an attribute or a group. For example (ID = 'A47'). |
| [] | Contains an index entry. |
| next: | Represents a next sibling node. This can be only used in a forward path. |
| previous: | Represents a previous sibling node. This can be only used in a forward path. |



 


UPath Examples


Element(ID="628").Segment(ID="HL").Loop(ID="2000C").Transaction - starting at the bottom of the tree - identifies the element with an ID=628 in the segment with ID=HL in the Loop with ID=2000C in a transaction


Transaction(ID=837)/Loop(ID="2000C")/Loop(ID="2010BA")/Segment(ID="REF")[2] - starting at the top of the tree - identifies the second segment with ID=REF in the Loop with ID=2010BA in the Loop with ID=2000C in Transaction with ID=837


/Transaction(ID='101')/Segment(ID='BGN')/next:Segment/Element(ID='96') – selects the /Transaction(ID='101')/Segment(ID='SE')/Element(ID='96') node


/Transaction(ID='101')/Loop(ID='DTM')/previous:? – selects the /Transaction(ID='101')/Segment(ID='BGN') node


/Transaction(ID='101')/Loop(ID='DTM')/../Segment(ID='BGN') – selects the /Transaction(ID='101')/Segment(ID='BGN')


Element(ID = '373').*.Transaction(ID = '101') – matches both /Transaction(ID = '101')/Segment(ID = 'BGN')/Element(ID = '373') and /Transaction(ID = '101')/Loop(ID = 'DTM')/Segment(ID = 'DTM')/Element(ID = '373')


Element(ID = '373').?.Transaction(ID = '101') – matches the only /Transaction(ID = '101')/Segment(ID = 'BGN')/Element(ID = '373')


You can also use a UPath to refer data instances, for more information, see [Reference Data Instances](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Reference_Data_Instances.htm).

---

<a id="reference-data-instances"></a>

#### Reference Data Instances

> 📄 Source: [Reference_Data_Instances.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Reference_Data_Instances.htm)

##### Reference Data Instances


When there are several instances of the needed node in the incoming data, you can reference instances.



To specify the data instance restriction, change the UPath syntax as follows:

- Add Instance = 'first()' to the node path to process the first instance of the node.
- Add Instance = 'current()' to the node path to process only a current instance of the node in the file.
- Add Instance = 'last()' to the node path to process the last instance of the node.
- Add Instance = 'n’, where n is a positive integer value, to the node path to process the n instance of the node.


Note If there is only one instance of the node present in the initial file, only the current node is processed.


If the needed instance is not indicated in the path, all the existing instances are processed.

---

<a id="select-a-guideline-node"></a>

#### Select a Guideline Node

> 📄 Source: [Select_a_Guideline_Node.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Select_a_Guideline_Node.htm)

##### Select a Guideline Node


With the Guideline Node Selector dialog box, you can see the UPath for the selected node within the guideline tree. UPath is the selection language determining the location of the node within the tree.


To open the Guideline Node Selector window:

1. On the Home tab, click Select.
2. Click UPath for runtime to represent the UPath in a way compatible with runtime.


For detailed information on UPath formats, see [UPath Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/UPath_Overview.htm).


The Business Path field displays a path to a node in a user friendly form. This field is read only.


Note If a UPath is used at run-time (XEngine) configuration files, make sure that UPath for runtime box is checked.

---

<a id="user-node-dictionary"></a>

#### User Node Dictionary

> 📄 Source: [Book_User_Node_Dictionary.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_User_Node_Dictionary.htm)

##### User Node Dictionary


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="user-node-dictionary-overview"></a>

##### User Node Dictionary Overview

> 📄 Source: [User_Node_Dictionary_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/User_Node_Dictionary_Overview.htm)

###### User Node Dictionary Overview


When composing a guideline you have the following options: you can create guideline nodes from scratch or use existing templates stored in the form of a dictionary. With the User Node Dictionary, you can have your own set of node templates, which can be used to create a guideline. You can save and load both node dictionaries and transaction templates. Using the User Node Dictionary you can now share the nodes between two or more guidelines. Therefore, nodes for a particular standard can be pulled from the dictionary and new nodes can be created from different guidelines.


###### Features and Benefits

- All types of nodes available for the standards are stored in the User Node Dictionary.
- You can now add nodes to the dictionary directly from the guideline.
- You can edit and create new nodes within the User Node Dictionary.
- You can store guideline templates, and then create new transactions based on that template.

---

<a id="user-node-dictionary-browser"></a>

##### User Node Dictionary Browser

> 📄 Source: [User_Node_Dictionary_Browser.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/User_Node_Dictionary_Browser.htm)

###### User Node Dictionary Browser


The User Node Dictionary Browser allows you to modify existing nodes or create new user node dictionaries.


To launch User Node Dictionary Browser:


On the Advanced tab, select User Node Dictionary. The following dialog box is displayed:


You can perform the following operations:

- Click New (), to create new user dictionary.
- Click Import (), to import previously created user dictionary from a file.
- Click Export (), to export a dictionary to the file.
- Click Cut (), to cut the node from the dictionary.
- Click Copy (), to copy the selected node.
- Click Paste (), to paste the node to another place in the dictionary.
- Click Delete (), to remove a node from the dictionary.
- Click Apply (), to apply the changes made to current node.
- After applying the changes to the current node, you can click Apply to apply the changes to the all nodes that have been edited.
- Click Reset () to discard any changes made to the current node.


To create a new user dictionary:

1. Choose a standard which you want to create a dictionary for.
2. Do one of the following:
3. Provide the category name and User Node Dictionary description, and then click Apply ().


The new user node dictionary is displayed with all types of nodes available for the selected standard.


You can also edit nodes in your user dictionaries by using User Node Dictionary Browser. For more information on how to do it, see [Work with Nodes in the User Node Dictionary](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Work_with_Nodes_in_the_User_Node_Dictionary.htm).

---

<a id="work-with-nodes-in-the-user-node-dictionary"></a>

##### Work with Nodes in the User Node Dictionary

> 📄 Source: [Work_with_Nodes_in_the_User_Node_Dictionary.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Work_with_Nodes_in_the_User_Node_Dictionary.htm)

###### Work with Nodes in the User Node Dictionary


You can add a separate node or a set of nodes to the User Node Dictionary. When the required nodes are added to the dictionary you can edit or modify them up to your needs.


To add a node to the User Node Dictionary:

1. Launch the guideline you need. For more information on how to do it, see [Create a New Guideline Document](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Create_a_New_Guideline_Document.htm).
2. Right-click the node you want to add to the dictionary, select User Node Dictionary, and then click Save Node to User Node Dictionary.
3. Provide the following information for the node:
4. Click OK and the node is added to the user node dictionary.


To insert Node from User Node Dictionary:

- Right-click the node on a certain level, select Insert Sibling from User Node Dictionary, and then click Next. Choose a node from the user node dictionary, and then click Finish.


To edit a node:

1. On the Advanced tab, in the User Nodes group, click User Node Dictionary. The [User Node Dictionary Browser](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/User_Node_Dictionary_Browser.htm) is displayed.
2. Double-click the standard, choose the dictionary and double-click it to expand.
3. Click the node you want to edit. The properties of this node are displayed.
4. Change the properties of the node according to your needs. All the changes are automatically validated.
5. Click Apply (), to save the changes in the node or click Reset () to go back to the previously saved changes.


If you have a code list included in your node and you want to modify it, see [Modify Code Lists](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Modify_Code_Lists.htm).

---

<a id="standard-code-lists"></a>

#### Standard Code Lists

> 📄 Source: [Book_Standard_Code_Lists.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Standard_Code_Lists.htm)

##### Standard Code Lists


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="standard-code-lists-overview"></a>

##### Standard Code Lists Overview

> 📄 Source: [Standard_Code_Lists_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Standard_Code_Lists_Overview.htm)

###### Standard Code Lists Overview


A standard code list is a set of code values that is included in the guideline.


When creating a code list for a node, you can create a brand new code list or simply customize codes in an existing list. A new code list should be created when you want to run a check against a new type of code values - for example, you want to start checking zip codes or state codes.

---

<a id="insert-or-add-a-standard-code-list"></a>

##### Insert or Add a Standard Code List

> 📄 Source: [Insert_or_Add_a_Standard_Code_List.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Insert_or_Add_a_Standard_Code_List.htm)

###### Insert or Add a Standard Code List


You can add a standard code list or a composite code list into a guideline node. You can also create your own standard code list or composite code list and add it to the guideline.


To insert a standard code list or composite code list:

1. In the Guideline pane, click the element where the new code list is assigned.
2. Right-click the element, point to Insert Child Node, and then click Code List or Composite Code List.
3. On the Welcome to the New Child Node Wizard wizard page, click Select an Existing Node, and then click Next.
4. Click the node to use, and then click Finish.


To add a new standard code list:

1. In the Guideline pane, click the element where the new code list is assigned.
2. Right-click the element, point to Insert Child Node, and then click Code List or Composite Code List.
3. On the Welcome to the New Child Node Wizard wizard page, click Create a New Node, and then click Next.
4. Do one of the following:

 For a new standard code list:

1. In the Properties dialog box, in the Code List Key/Value box, type a unique identifier for the code list.
2. In the Codes Value box, click Table to add the codes and values.
3. Click Add Row to add a new code and type information or click Import Codes to import a list from an XML or CSV file.
4. In the Name box, type a name for the code list.
5. Click Finish.


Note Specifying a code is case-sensitive. For example, CODE and Code are considered as different values.





 For a new composite code list:

1. In the Composite Code List Key/Value box, type a unique identifier for this composite code list, and then click Finish.
2. Add a code list as a child node.

---

<a id="modify-standard-code-properties"></a>

##### Modify Standard Code Properties

> 📄 Source: [Modify_Standard_Code_Properties.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Modify_Standard_Code_Properties.htm)

###### Modify Standard Code Properties


You can change the properties for codes included in standard code lists.


When you select a code list in the Guideline pane, information about the code list is displayed on the Properties tab. The information displayed includes:

- Codelist Properties - a list of code IDs, names, and Row Indexes.
- Code user notes - notes on the properties of the code selected in the Codelist Properties.


To modify code properties:

1. In the Guidelines pane, click the code list to edit.
2. On the Properties tab, in the Codelist Properties window, click the code to edit, and then click Edit () on the Codelist Properties toolbar.
3. Click Edit User Note () to edit the Description or any User Note as necessary in the User Note Editor.


Note Code lists that were changed are marked with blue. Unchanged ones remain gray.


For more information about User Notes, see [Add or Edit a User Note](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Add_or_Edit_a_User_Note.htm).


To insert a new code:

1. On the Properties tab toolbar, click Insert ().
2. In the Code dialog box provide the ID and Name for a new code.
3. Click OK.

---

<a id="modify-code-lists"></a>

##### Modify Code Lists

> 📄 Source: [Modify_Code_Lists.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Modify_Code_Lists.htm)

###### Modify Code Lists


With the [Dictionary pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Dictionary_Pane.htm), you can make global changes to a code list within a guideline from a single location.


To modify a code list:

1. In the [Dictionary pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Dictionary_Pane.htm), open the Code list folder.
2. Select the code list to be modified.
3. On the Quick View tab, in the codesValue field for Codes, double-click Table. A list of codes is displayed:
4. To import new codes from an XML or CSV file, click Import Codes, select From XML File or From CSV File, navigate to the file to import, and then click Open.
5. To add a new entry to the code list, click Add Row, and enter an ID, Name, and Description.
6. To remove a row, select the row, and then click Remove Row.
7. Click OK to save your changes.

---

<a id="exclude-and-include-codes"></a>

##### Exclude and Include Codes

> 📄 Source: [Exclude_and_Include_Codes.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Exclude_and_Include_Codes.htm)

###### Exclude and Include Codes


When you define your guideline, you can choose the codes to use and exclude the ones you do not need.


To exclude a code from a standard code list:

1. In the Guideline pane, click the code list from the appropriate element. The list of codes then appears at the top of the right pane in the Code List Properties window.
2. Right-click the code you want to exclude, and then click Exclude.
3. Repeat as necessary.


To Include a code you have excluded:

1. On the View tab, click View Excluded.
2. Right-click the code you want to include, and then click Include.

---

<a id="user-notes"></a>

#### User Notes

> 📄 Source: [Book_User_Notes.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_User_Notes.htm)

##### User Notes


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="add-or-edit-a-user-note"></a>

##### Add or Edit a User Note

> 📄 Source: [Add_or_Edit_a_User_Note.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Add_or_Edit_a_User_Note.htm)

###### Add or Edit a User Note


User notes are useful for adding descriptive text to guideline nodes.


To add or edit a user note:

1. Click the Guideline pane.
2. Click the node that you want to edit.
3. Click the User Notes pane.
4. Click the user note you want to add or edit.
5. Double-click the user note you want to edit or on the toolbar, click Edit User Note (Enter) (). User Note Editor is displayed.
6. In User Note Editor, enter the text for your note. If required, use the edit options on the toolbar to customize text formatting and structure and to add hyperlinks, tables and so on.
7. Click OK to save your changes.


Note You can add an unlimited number of user notes per node. To add more than 20 notes, go to Document Options.


The formatting, placement, and labels for user notes can be customized to suit your business requirements. To learn how to change the labels, see [Document Options Notes Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Document_Options_User_Notes_Tab.htm).


By default, all notes use the font type, size, italics, bold, and shading that is specified in Document Options settings. When you change any of these settings in document options, they are automatically changed everywhere the default formatting style is used. You still can change formatting in user notes individually.


To view and modify the default notes formatting:

1. On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click Document Options.
2. Choose the Notes tab.
3. Set the default font type, size, italics, bold, and shading.


Note Shading is not displayed in the user interface but is used during publication.


###### Formatting limitations:

- Tabs are not supported due to the nature of HTML formatting.  
- Not all HTML formatting options are supported at this time due to some limitations with publication engine.

---

<a id="add-or-edit-user-notes-in-xml-schemas"></a>

##### Add or Edit User Notes in XML Schemas

> 📄 Source: [Add_or_Edit_User_Notes_in_XML_Schemas.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Add_or_Edit_User_Notes_in_XML_Schemas.htm)

###### Add or Edit User Notes in XML Schemas


Global labels Predefined global labels Local labels


Each node of an XML Schema document can have custom user notes provided by the user. Notes can consist of any text and are associated with labels that can assist with identification. Unlike other standards, XML Schemas can have several types of labels for user notes:

- Global labels - labels that are available for use in all nodes.
- Predefined global labels - labels that are pre-defined for new XML schema documents.
- Local labels - labels that are only available for the node where they are created.


Note While labels may be global, the notes associated with those labels only apply to the node where they are created.


###### Global labels


Unlike local labels, global labels are defined for the whole document and visible on each node of the document. Global labels are created and modified on the Document Options page and they are document-specific. Global labels must be unique.


Global labels cannot be changed in the User Notes pane of the guideline.


To create/modify a global label:

1. On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click Document Options.
2. Click the Notes tab, and then click Edit next to Global User Note Labels.
3. In the Edit value list dialog box do one of the following:
4. Click OK.
5. Return to the guideline to create a note associated with the label.


###### Predefined global labels


These are basically default labels for all newly-created XML schemas. A predefined global label can be changed once a schema guideline is created. This has no effect on previously-created guidelines.


To create a predefined global label:

1. Follow the previous instructions for creating a global label.
2. On the toolbar at the bottom of the window, click Set as default options.
3. Click OK.


###### Local labels


Local labels are available only for the node where they are created. You can add a label, remove it, or change the name of the note. You can have any number of user notes with local labels.


To create a local label:

1. In the [Guideline pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/XML_Schema_Guideline_View_in_Technical_Perspective.htm), click a node where you want to place a label.
2. Click the [User Notes pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/User_Notes_Pane_Overview.htm) and do the following:


To modify/delete a local label:

1. In the [Guideline pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/XML_Schema_Guideline_View_in_Technical_Perspective.htm), click a node where you want to modify/delete a label.
2. Click the [User Notes pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/User_Notes_Pane_Overview.htm), click the label to modify/delete and click Edit User Note (Enter) (), or click Delete User Note (Delete).


To add a user note:

1. In the [Guideline pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/XML_Schema_Guideline_View_in_Technical_Perspective.htm), click a node where you want to place a user note.
2. Click the [User Notes pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/User_Notes_Pane_Overview.htm).
3. Double-click the user note you want to edit or on the toolbar, click Edit User Note (Enter) ().
4. In User Note Editor, enter the text for your note. If required, use the edit options on the toolbar to customize text formatting and structure and to add hyperlinks, tables and so on.
5. Click OK to save your changes.

---

<a id="publish-guidelines"></a>

### Publish Guidelines

> 📄 Source: [Book_Publish_Guidelines.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Publish_Guidelines.htm)

#### Publish Guidelines


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="publish-overview"></a>

#### Publish Overview

> 📄 Source: [Publish_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_Overview.htm)

##### Publish Overview


You can publish your guideline document in the following formats: PDF (.pdf), Rich Text Format (.rtf), HTML (.htm), Excel XML or Word XML. For X12 and HIPAA, you have the option to publish the documents to the X12 Companion Guide. For more information, see [Publish to X12 Companion Guide Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_to_X12_Companion_Guide_Overview.htm).


Publishing your guideline document in a different format allows you to distribute your document to other trading partners that may not have SpecBuilder installed. You can make text formatting changes if your guideline document is published in Rich Text Format. Publishing your guideline document in .htm format allows your document to be viewed in a browser.


To configure the appearance, layout, and structure of the published document, access [Document Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Document_Options.htm) and select the options that suit your business requirements.


To publish a guideline:

1. On the File menu, click Publish.
2. Click HTML, PDF, RTF, Word, Excel, or **X12 Companion Guide**.
3. In the File Name box, type a name for the file.
4. Click OK.


Note The default location for the published file is the Guidelines folder, but you can choose another location.


To view the file published in .pdf file format you must have Adobe Acrobat Reader. Bookmarks in the .pdf documents are supported for all the standards.

---

<a id="set-document-properties-for-cover-page"></a>

#### Set Document Properties for Cover Page

> 📄 Source: [Set_Document_Properties_for_Cover_Page.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Set_Document_Properties_for_Cover_Page.htm)

##### Set Document Properties for Cover Page


The Guideline Document Properties allow to set general parameters for a guideline. These parameters are also used for printing a cover page when publishing a guideline. Whether the cover page is printed or not is controlled on the [Document Options General tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Document_Options_General_Tab.htm). Information printed on the cover page is created in the file properties of the guideline.


To set document properties for cover page:

1. On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click Document Properties.
2. Type information into any of the following optional fields:**General tab**: - Title: The user-defined title of the implementation guideline. - Subject: The user-defined area where a subject can be entered. - Author: The name of the party who created the document. - Version: The version of the guideline. - Publish Date: The publication date for the document. - Company: The company creating the guideline. - Trading Partner: The name of the trading partner. - Keywords: User-specific keywords that describe the guideline. - Status: The status of the guideline - Draft or Final. - File Name: Displays a file name. - Internal ID: The ID generated for the guideline. - Created by: The version and the date of the guideline creation. - Modified by: The version and the date of the guideline modification. - Created from: The Standards Database version. - XData version: The version of the exported XData. - XData namespace: The custom namespace given to the exported XData. - XData static GUID: The static guid that was given to XData. - Notes: Any notes or remarks that should be included on the cover page of the implementation guideline. **Notes tab**:You can use Rich Text to add any notes or remarks that should be included on the cover page of the implementation guideline.**Change Log tab**:You can use Rich Text to include Change Log information on the cover page of the implementation guideline.
3. Click OK.


To print the cover page, see [Document Options General Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Document_Options_General_Tab.htm).

---

<a id="preview-a-guideline"></a>

#### Preview a Guideline

> 📄 Source: [Preview_a_Guideline.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Preview_a_Guideline.htm)

##### Preview a Guideline


Use the **File** – **Print** – Print Preview feature to view your document before printing or publishing. With Print Preview, you can do the following:

- View the document page by page: To go to a specific page, type the page number in the Goto Page box, and then click Go to the specified page (). You can also use the four arrow keys to see the first, previous, next, and last page of the document.
- Zoom in or out: Use the zoom in or zoom out features to read specific sections or see the entire page.
- Print: Print your document.


###### Keyboard Shortcuts


The Print Preview dialog box supports the following keyboard shortcuts:


| Key | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Left arrow | Scrolls the screen contents left. |
| Ctrl+left arrow | Scrolls the screen contents to the left-most edge. |
| Right arrow | Scrolls the screen contents right. |
| Ctrl+right arrow | Scrolls the screen contents to the right-most edge. |
| Up arrow | Scrolls the screen contents up. |
| Home/Ctrl+up arrow | Scrolls to the top of the screen. |
| End/Ctrl+down arrow | Scrolls to the bottom of the screen. |
| Page Up | Moves to the previous page. |
| Page Down | Moves to the next page. |
| Ctrl+Page Down | Moves to the last page. |
| Escape (Esc) | Stops page rendering (same as clicking Stop). If the page is not rendering, Esc closes the Print Preview dialog box. |

---

<a id="print-a-guideline"></a>

#### Print a Guideline

> 📄 Source: [Print_a_Guideline.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Print_a_Guideline.htm)

##### Print a Guideline


To print your guideline document:

1. On the File menu, click Print, and then click Print.
2. In the Page Range dialog box, specify the pages to print.
3. In the Copies section, specify the Number of Copies, and then click Collate if you want the copies collated.
4. Select the Count Only Main Content check box, to print content without Table of Contents.
5. Click OK.

---

<a id="publish-to-excel-xml"></a>

#### Publish to Excel XML

> 📄 Source: [Book_Publish_to_Excel_XML.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Publish_to_Excel_XML.htm)

##### Publish to Excel XML


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="welcome-to-excel-publishing-wizard"></a>

##### Welcome to Excel Publishing Wizard

> 📄 Source: [Welcome_to_Excel_Publishing_ Wizard.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Welcome_to_Excel_Publishing_ Wizard.htm)

###### Welcome to Excel Publishing Wizard


To publish your guideline to MS Excel:

1. On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click Publish, and then click EXCEL.
2. Select the settings to be used for the export:
3. Click Next. If you choose to use default settings or settings from the external file not by using Advanced mode, you are asked if you want to view the exported document:

- Click Yes to launch MS Excel and view the exported document.
- Click No to go back to the SpecBuilder workspace.


If you want to define new settings or to run the wizard in the advanced mode, go to [Global Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_to_Excel_Global_Options.htm).

---

<a id="publish-to-excel-xml-wizard-overview"></a>

##### Publish to Excel XML Wizard Overview

> 📄 Source: [Publishing_to_Excel_XML_Wizard_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publishing_to_Excel_XML_Wizard_Overview.htm)

###### Publish to Excel XML Wizard Overview


You can use the Publish to Excel XML wizard to define the format and content of the exported document. The exported document has the Excel .xml format. The Publishing to Excel XML wizard walks you through the following tasks:


[Define an output file name and choose the settings to use](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Welcome_to_Excel_Publishing_ Wizard.htm)


[Global Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_to_Excel_Global_Options.htm)


[Index Area Section](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_to_Excel_Setting_Document_Sections.htm)


[Guideline Area Section](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_to_Excel_Setting_Document_Sections.htm)


[Table Area Section](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_to_Excel_Setting_Document_Sections.htm)


[Code List Area Section](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_to_Excel_Setting_Document_Sections.htm)


[Save Settings](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_to_Excel_Saving_Settings.htm)

---

<a id="publish-to-excel-global-options"></a>

##### Publish to Excel Global Options

> 📄 Source: [Publish_to_Excel_Global_Options.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_to_Excel_Global_Options.htm)

###### Publish to Excel Global Options


Use this page of the wizard to specify the export options.

- Template file name: In the Template Excel file name box, type a name for the template file or click Browse to navigate to the location of the template file.
- Guideline worksheet section: Click the areas you want to include in the document.
- Additional worksheets section: Click Each code list on a separate worksheet, to include code list information in the exported document and display each code list on a separate worksheet in the workbook.
- Suppression selector section: Define what nodes you want to exclude from the guideline. These nodes are not displayed in the exported document.


Click Next to go to the [Set Document Sections Settings](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_to_Excel_Setting_Document_Sections.htm).

---

<a id="publish-to-excel-setting-document-sections"></a>

##### Publish to Excel Setting Document Sections

> 📄 Source: [Publish_to_Excel_Setting_Document_Sections.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_to_Excel_Setting_Document_Sections.htm)

###### Publish to Excel Setting Document Sections


Use this page to specify section content for the exported document. Depending on what options you have set in the [Global Options page](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_to_Excel_Global_Options.htm), you have to provide settings for one or more of the following sections:

- Index Area Section
- Guideline Tree Area Section
- Table Area Section
- Code List Area Section


*[Image: Index Area Section]*


Note For XSD guidelines, only node types included in the business perspective are displayed.


To add a column: 

- On the toolbar, click Insert new column ().
- Type a name for the new column, and then click OK.


To remove a column: 

- On the toolbar, click Remove selected column ().


To rename a column:  

- On the toolbar, click Rename selected column ().


To define column content:

1. Do one of the following
2. Type the content or choose an attribute from the drop-down list.
3. Click OK to go back to the Publishing wizard.
4. Continue adding content for each cell.
5. Provide columns and content for all areas used in your document, and then click Next to go to the [Saving Settings page of the wizard](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_to_Excel_Saving_Settings.htm).

---

Use this page of the wizard to save the document settings for further use. 


To save your settings:

1. Do one of the following:
2. Click Next.
3. When asked if you want to view the exported document, do one of the following:

<a id="publish-to-word-xml"></a>

#### Publish to Word XML

> 📄 Source: [Book_Publish_to_Word_XML.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Publish_to_Word_XML.htm)

##### Publish to Word XML


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="publish-to-word-xml-wizard-overview"></a>

##### Publish to Word XML Wizard Overview

> 📄 Source: [Publish_to_Word_XML_Wizard_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_to_Word_XML_Wizard_Overview.htm)

###### Publish to Word XML Wizard Overview


You can use the Publish to Word XML wizard to define the format and content of the exported document. The exported document is in the Word XML format. The following custom properties are added to the document when values are not null:


| Guideline Type | Properties |
| --- | --- |
| EDI | ID, Name, Standard, Version |
| X12 | Functional Group |
| EDIFACT | Message Status |
| TRADACOMS | File Version |
| DFF, PFF | ID, Name, Standard, Version |
| HL7 | ID, Name, Event, Event Name, Standard, Version |
| HL7 version 3 | ID, Name, Standard, Version, Domain, Event ID, Event Name, Message ID, Message Name, Transmission ID, Transmission Name, Control Act ID, Control Act Name, Sending Role ID, Sending Role Name, Receiving Role ID, Receiving Role Name |
| NCPDP | ID, Name, Standard, Version, Type |
| Schema | attributeFromDefault, blockDefault, elementFormDefault, finalDefault, targetNamespace, version, xml_lang, id |



The Publishing to Word XML wizard walks you through the following tasks:


[Defining an output file name and choosing the settings to use](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Welcome_to_Publish_to_Word_XML_Wizard.htm)


[Advanced Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_to_Word_XML_Advanced_Options.htm)

---

<a id="welcome-to-publish-to-word-xml-wizard"></a>

##### Welcome to Publish to Word XML Wizard

> 📄 Source: [Welcome_to_Publish_to_Word_XML_Wizard.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Welcome_to_Publish_to_Word_XML_Wizard.htm)

###### Welcome to Publish to Word XML Wizard


To publish a guideline to Word XML: 

1. On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click Publish, and then click WORD.

- Enter Output File Name: Type a name for the Word XML file or click Browse to navigate to the location of the file, and then click the name of the file to use.
- Advanced mode: Click to run the wizard in the advanced mode to use document templates.
- Update page references if possible: Click to update the page references in the published document. This option is only available if you have Word 2003 or later installed at the time of the publishing.
- Table of content only: Click to create page numbers in the table of contents. If this is not selected, all page numbers display as "1". You can update page numbers manually by selecting the table of contents and pressing F9.
- Whole document (takes more time): Click to update page numbers in the summary table of the document. If this is not selected, all page numbers display as "1".


Note This only applies if the Print Page Number of each segment/loop page option is selected on the [Transaction tab of Document Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EDI_Transaction_and_Message_Tabs.htm).


If you want to define file templates, go to [Advanced Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_to_Word_XML_Advanced_Options.htm).


If you choose not to use Advanced mode, once the output file is created, you are asked if you want to view the exported document:

- Click Yes to launch MS Word and view the exported document.
- Click No to go back to the SpecBuilder workspace.

---

##### Publish to Word XML Advanced Options

> 📄 Source: [Publish_to_Word_XML_Advanced_Options.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_to_Word_XML_Advanced_Options.htm)

###### Publish to Word XML Advanced Options


Use this wizard step to select the templates you want to be used for publishing.

1. Set the following options:
2. Click Finish to create the Word XML file.
