<a id="publish-to-x12-companion-guide"></a>

#### Publish to X12 Companion Guide

> 📄 Source: [Book_Publish_to_X12_Companion_Guide.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Publish_to_X12_Companion_Guide.htm)

##### Publish to X12 Companion Guide


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="publish-to-x12-companion-guide-overview"></a>

##### Publish to X12 Companion Guide Overview

> 📄 Source: [Publish_to_X12_Companion_Guide_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_to_X12_Companion_Guide_Overview.htm)

###### Publish to X12 Companion Guide Overview


According to DISA, no structures related to X12 guidelines may be published or saved except for internal purpose. Such guidelines or publications cannot be sent out to the external trading partners.


Due to that, every attempt to save the HIPAA or X12N – Healthcare guideline is associated with the special confirmation dialog. If this information is acknowledged and accepted, user may choose not to show this again, which means automatic acceptance and compliance with DISA Copyright regulations.


SpecBuilder publishing was empowered with the capability of creating the Companion Guides compliant to new WEDI/X12 regulations, mandated specific and very strict template for the published documents. An X12 Companion Guide is a Microsoft Word document that contains information about custom changes for the X12 guideline being published. It does not repeat any standard guideline content published by X12. You can publish an X12 Companion Guide from X12 guidelines and X12 Companion Guide packages.


The document is published by using a template. A template is a predefined document with macros, which are resolved for every guideline of a specified type. The published document contain a plain text of the used template and resolved macros, which were added to the template.


When the document is published to the Companion Guide, information is collected from the user notes with the certain labels. You can modify user note labels by using the Document Options dialog box. For more information about how to customize user notes labels, see [Document Options Notes Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Document_Options_User_Notes_Tab.htm).


For Segments, Loops, and Composite nodes the following user note labels are processed while publishing:

- CG: Notes/Comments


For the Element node the following user note labels are processed while publishing:

- CG: Codes
- CG: Notes/Comments


Note For the Codes nodes user notes are not supported.


User notes with labels other than the ones provided earlier are ignored during X12 Companion Guide publishing. While opening a guideline, CG: Codes and CG: Notes/Comments notes are added automatically if a guideline doesn't have them.


For the customers that are using Operating Rules package, the behavior of the user notes stays the same, however the notes have the following format.


For Segments, Loops, and Composite:

- CORE: Page #
- CORE: Comments/Note


For Element nodes:

- CORE: Codes
- CORE: Length
- CORE: Page #
- CORE: Comments/Note


For Transaction nodes:

- IG: Name


To publish a guideline:

1. Open an X12 or HIPAA guideline that you want to publish.
2. On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click Publish.
3. Click X12 Companion Guide.


Note You can choose an output file type while publishing. The following types are available: PDF (.pdf), Rich Text Format (.rtf), HTML (.htm), Microsoft Word (.doc, .docx). When publishing to a specific file format, make sure that saving to this format is not blocked in Microsoft® Word. To verify that, go to **File** – **Options** – **Trust Center Settings** – **File Block Settings**.


The Companion Guideline Publishing wizard is displayed. This wizard walks you through the following steps:


[Template Selection](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_to_X12_Companion_Guide_Template_Selection.htm)


[Destination Path](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_to_X12_Companion_Guide_Target_Location.htm)


According to DISA, no structures related to X12 guidelines may be published or saved except for internal purpose. Such guidelines or publications cannot be sent out to the external trading partners. For more information, see [Data Interchange Standards Association Copyright](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Interchange_Standards_Association_Copyright.htm).

---

<a id="template-selection"></a>

##### Template Selection

> 📄 Source: [Publish_to_X12_Companion_Guide_Template_Selection.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_to_X12_Companion_Guide_Template_Selection.htm)

###### Template Selection


Use the **Template Selection** page to navigate to the location of the template or use the default template. Publication template is needed to minimize the effort on building printable documents out of multiple Companion Guide guidelines. Since they all have the same template mandated by X12/CORE with only difference in few key properties and custom nodes on the updated fields, the typical way of solving the publication task is use the template having different guidelines applied to it.


In the Companion Guide Template Selection dialog box, choose from the following options:

- Sample Generic Template: Click to use a sample template showing how to create your own template with supported macros. This template is developed for a sample HIPAA 276A1 transaction.
- WEDI Default (Transaction Info): Click to use a predefined WEDI Transaction Information Companion Guide Template.  This is a standard template released by X12/WEDI that represents the baseline and starting point for all publications of Transaction Info.
- WEDI Default (Trading Partner Info): Click to use a predefined WEDI Trading Partner Information Companion Guide Template. This is a standard template for Trading Partner information publication.
- From the file: Navigate to the template file location.

---

<a id="target-location"></a>

##### Target Location

> 📄 Source: [Publish_to_X12_Companion_Guide_Target_Location.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_to_X12_Companion_Guide_Target_Location.htm)

###### Target Location


Use the **Target Location** page to provide the output file path for the published document.


Type a name for the Companion Guide file, or click Browse to navigate to the location of the file, and then click the name of the file to use.


Note You can choose an output file type while publishing. The following types are available: PDF (.pdf), Rich Text Format (.rtf), HTML (.htm), Microsoft Word (.doc, .docx).

---

<a id="data-interchange-standards-association-copyright"></a>

##### Data Interchange Standards Association Copyright

> 📄 Source: [Data_Interchange_Standards_Association_Copyright.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Interchange_Standards_Association_Copyright.htm)

###### Data Interchange Standards Association Copyright


According to DISA, no structures related to X12 guidelines may be published or saved except for internal purpose. Such guidelines or publications cannot be sent out to the external trading partners.


Due to that, every attempt to save the HIPAA or X12N – Healthcare guideline is associated with the special confirmation dialog. If this information is acknowledged and accepted, user may choose not to show this again, which means automatic acceptance and compliance with DISA Copyright regulations.


For more information about publishing options, please refer to [Publish to X12 Companion Guide](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_to_X12_Companion_Guide_Overview.htm).

---

<a id="document-options"></a>

#### Document Options

> 📄 Source: [Book_Document_Options.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Document_Options.htm)

##### Document Options


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="set-document-options"></a>

##### Set Document Options

> 📄 Source: [Set_Document_Options.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Set_Document_Options.htm)

###### Set Document Options


You have a wide range of choices for the appearance of your printed guideline document. Those choices are set by using Document Options. These options can be applied to an individual document or you can save them and reuse them to print other guidelines.


To set document options:

1. Open a guideline document.
2. On the File menu, click Document Options:



Some options are only available for certain file types. For more information on the specific options available for the document type you are working with, refer to the appropriate section of the Help.


To save options:

- Once you are satisfied with your selections, at the bottom of the dialog box, click Save options (). You are prompted to save your options as a document template (.sdt) file.


To retrieve a saved option file:

- At the bottom of the dialog box, click Load options (), navigate to the location of the file, and then click Open.


To set options as default:


Once you have set the options, you can make the options the default for all files.

- At the bottom of the dialog box, click Set as default options (). The settings become the default options for future files.


To restore defaults:


If you have set some options that you don't want to save and/or don't want to use, you can restore the default settings.

- At the bottom of the dialog box, click Restore default options ().

---

<a id="document-options-general-tab"></a>

##### Document Options General Tab

> 📄 Source: [Document_Options_General_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Document_Options_General_Tab.htm)

###### Document Options General Tab


From the General tab, you can choose to print a cover page for the guideline and set its format. This tab appears in all guidelines types.


Note By default, a cover page is not included in the document options.


To create a cover page:

1. Select Print Cover Page.
2. Select which information you want to be included on the cover page by selecting the desired items.

---

<a id="document-options-layout-tab"></a>

##### Document Options Layout Tab

> 📄 Source: [Document_Options_Layout_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Document_Options_Layout_Tab.htm)

###### Document Options Layout Tab


With the Layout tab, you can set page margins as well as the contents of headers and footers. This tab appears in all guideline types.


You can configure the following settings:

- Margins: Sets the margins for all sides of the page.
- Header/Footer position: Sets the position of the headers and footers.
- Header/Footer content: Sets the contents for headers and footers. If Customize even page header/footer is checked, these settings apply only to odd pages and you must create the even page settings separately.
- Image path: Provides a path to the image for any header/footer.


The following fields can be used for headers and footers:


- Company : Inserts your company name.
- Document Version: Inserts the version of the document being created.
- File Date: Inserts the date when a file was created.
- File Name: Inserts the name of the file being created.
- File Path: Inserts the location of the file.
- Current Date: Inserts today's date.
- Page: Inserts page numbers.
- SetName-ID: Inserts the Transaction Set name and ID (for example: Invoice-810).
- Standard-Version: Inserts the standard and version (for example: X12-V4010).
- Version: User-defined field.
- Image: Inserts selected image.

---

<a id="document-options-user-notes-tab"></a>

##### Document Options User Notes Tab

> 📄 Source: [Document_Options_User_Notes_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Document_Options_User_Notes_Tab.htm)

###### Document Options User Notes Tab


With the User Notes tab, you can add new notes, customize the default font style and labels for the user notes in your guideline. An unlimited number of user notes can be created. User note labels can be changed for each node type (see the following Note). This tab appears in all guideline types.



Note For the Delimited Flat Files, RosettaNet, and Positional Flat Files guideline types, you can only set the **Default printing styles for user notes** notes and **User Note Labels** options.


###### Default printing styles for user notes:


To change the default font name and size, click the item to change and select the new font/size. Select other formatting options.


###### Print location for user notes:


You can also specify the following print locations for user notes (for Transaction, Segment, and Loop/Group) :

- **At the beginning** – user notes will be printed at the beginning of the section (before Element Summary description).
- **At the end** – user notes will be printed at the end of the section (after Element Summary description).
- **On separate pages** – user notes will be printed on individual pages after Element Summary description.**Note** Available only for EDI guidelines.


###### User Note Labels:


To modify the label name, or the number of user notes, click Edit next to the User Notes label node.


To add a user note, click New (). The number of notes to be added is unlimited.


To rename a user note, click Edit ().


To delete a user note, click Delete ().


To move the note up or down the list, click Move Up () or Move down ().


Click OK when finished.

---

<a id="document-options-code-tab"></a>

##### Document Options Code Tab

> 📄 Source: [Document_Options_Code_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Document_Options_Code_Tab.htm)

###### Document Options Code Tab


To print the information associated with codes in a guideline, click the Code tab, select the Print Codes check box, and then select the type of code you want to print.

- Print Codes: Select this to print any codes within the guideline, and then select from the following options:
- Only included codes: Prints only codes that are included within the guideline.
- All codes: Prints all default codes.
- Only changed codes: Prints only codes that have been modified within the guideline.
- Print Description: Prints the user-defined description for a code.
- Print alternate text if all codes are included and unchanged: If all codes are included and unchanged in an element, the Alternate text is printed. To change this text, click the underlined text, select it and type new text. If this check box is not selected, all of the codes are printed individually.
- User Notes: Select the user notes to print.

---

<a id="document-options-application-fields-tab"></a>

##### Document Options Application Fields Tab

> 📄 Source: [Document_Options_Application_Fields_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Document_Options_Application_Fields_Tab.htm)

###### Document Options Application Fields Tab


Use the Application Fields tab to customize both the user interface and the printed guideline document.


The Application Fields tab appears in the following guideline types:

- EDI
- HL7
- NCPDP
- Positional Flat Files


###### Selecting Application Fields to Print


In the Print Application Fields section, select which of the application fields to include when the document is printed. Select Print Application Fields, and then the specific fields to be printed.


###### Modifying Selection Lists


In the Mode Selection List and Control Selection List sections, you can modify the lists shown in the Mode and Control drop-down boxes in the Application Field section of the Properties pane.


To modify the selection list:

1. Click Mode Selection List or Control Selection List.
2. Click Edit Selections List.
3. To add a new selection, click New and type the new selection.
4. To delete a selection, click the selection, and then click Delete.
5. To change a selection's position in the list, click Move Up or Move Down.  


###### Specifying Application Field Labels


In the Edit application field labels section, you can customize the field labels shown in the Application Field section of the Properties pane. This label is shown in the user interface and printed in the guideline document.

---

<a id="document-options-usage-tab"></a>

##### Document Options Usage Tab

> 📄 Source: [Document_Options_Usage_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Document_Options_Usage_Tab.htm)

###### Document Options Usage Tab


With the Usage tab, you can customize the usage terms. These terms replace the standard usage labels (Used, Must Use, Not Used, and so on) in the published guideline.  


This tab appears in the following guideline types:

- Delimited Flat Files
- EDI
- HL7
- NCPDP
- Positional Flat Files


To modify a term, click the underlined text, select the current term, and type new text.

---

<a id="document-options-labels-tab"></a>

##### Document Options Labels Tab

> 📄 Source: [Document_Options_Labels_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Document_Options_Labels_Tab.htm)

###### Document Options Labels Tab


Use the Labels tab to change the labels for special user notes within your printed guideline document. User note labels can be changed for each node type. User notes for transactions, segments/loops, element/composites, or codes are set on the [User Notes tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Document_Options_User_Notes_Tab.htm).


The type of user notes set on the Labels tab differ by guideline type.


To modify the label name, click Edit, and then edit the label.

---

<a id="document-options-nodes-tab"></a>

##### Document Options Nodes Tab

> 📄 Source: [Document_Options_Nodes_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Document_Options_Nodes_Tab.htm)

###### Document Options Nodes Tab


The Nodes tab is used by the following guidelines:

- Delimited Flat Files
- Positional Flat Files
- RosettaNet
- XML Schema


Use the Nodes tab to define the nodes that are printed in your document. The types of nodes available differ by guideline type.

---

<a id="document-options-xdata-20jdata-templates-tab"></a>

##### Document Options XData 2.0/JData Templates Tab

> 📄 Source: [Document_Options_XData20_JData_Templates_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Document_Options_XData20_JData_Templates_Tab.htm)

###### Document Options XData 2.0/JData Templates Tab


Click the XData 2.0/JData Templates tab to edit XData 2.0 or JData name templates.


The following options are available:

- Click Reset (), to reset to the default template.
- Click Open (), to navigate to the location of the template file.
- Click Save (), to save a template to the file.
- Click Show type (), to show types in the template.
- Abbreviated element name prefixes: Click to choose first letters of the node type names as prefixes.


To edit templates:

1. Click the node, and then click Browse () next to it.
2. Type the attribute for the node or choose it from the drop-down menu. The assembled template is shown later in the read-only mode.
3. Click OK to return to the XData 2.0/JData templates tab.

---

<a id="edi-document-options"></a>

##### EDI Document Options

> 📄 Source: [Book_EDI_Document_Options.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_EDI_Document_Options.htm)

###### EDI Document Options


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="edi-transaction-and-message-tabs"></a>

##### EDI Transaction and Message Tabs

> 📄 Source: [EDI_Transaction_and_Message_Tabs.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EDI_Transaction_and_Message_Tabs.htm)

###### EDI Transaction and Message Tabs

 Click here for information about the EDI Transaction tab.


From the Transaction tab, you can specify what transaction-level information you want printed in the guideline. 


Choose from the following options:

- Print Functional Group: Select this option to include the functional group header element into the printed guideline.
- Print Purpose: Select this option to add a description of the set included in the standard.
- Print Branching Diagram: Select this option to include a graphical representation of the document structure.  
- Print Notes: Select this option to include remarks defined by the standard in the printed guideline. Selecting this option does not print user notes. To print transaction user notes, select Print Transaction User Notes.
- Print Comments: Select this option to include transaction comments defined by the standard for specific segments. Comments are viewable in Segment Properties.
- Print Rules: Select this option to include rules specified on the transaction node.
- Print excluded segments: Select this option to print segments that have been excluded in the guideline. Two options are available:
- Mark excluded segments/loops with: Enter a character to mark excluded segments.
- Don't print usage for segments/loops within excluded groups/loops: Select this option not to print usage properties for segments or loops that are excluded or are part of an excluded segment or loop.
- Print page number of each Segment/Loop page: Select this option to include a page number for each segment/loop in a column on the right side of the page.
- Print page number column on the left: Select this option to include a page number in a column on the left side of the page.
- Suppress printing by User Option: Select this option to print segments/loops with the listed user options. Check the specific user options you want to print.
- Print Transaction User Notes: Select this option to print the selected transaction-level user notes. You can choose to print all notes or select the specific user notes you want to print.





 Click here for information about the EDIFACT Message tab


With the Message tab, you can determine what information about messages are included in your printed guideline.

- Print Message Status: Select this option to include the message status into the printed guideline.
- Definition: Select this option to include the purpose/definition of the transaction/message in the printed guideline.
- Print Branching Diagram: Select this option to include a branching diagram in the printed guideline.
- Clarification: Select this option to print the set notes that are defined by the standard for specific segments. You can view notes in Segment Properties.
- Print Clarification at the end of summary table: Select this option to print Notes and Comments after Message summary table. Clear the box to print them in front of the table.
- Print Rules: Select this option to print any rules specified on the transaction node.
- Print excluded segments/loops: Select this option to print segments/loops that have been excluded in the guideline.
- Mark excluded segment/loops with: Select a character to be used to mark the excluded segments/loops. Select this option not to print usage property for segments or loops/groups that are excluded or are the part of excluded loops/groups.
- Don't print usage for segments/loops within excluded segments/loops: Select this option not to print usage property for segments or loops/groups which are excluded or a part of excluded loops/groups.
- Print page number of each Segment/Loop page: Select this option to add a column displaying the page number for each segment/loop. By default, this column appears on the right. In print preview either a page number or a place holder <PH> for page number is displayed. Place Holder <PH> is replaced with the actual number during publishing.
- Print page number column on the left: Select this option to print the page number column on the left.
- Suppress printing by User Option: Select this option to print Segment/Loops based on User Option value. Then check the specific User Option values you want to print.
- Print message User Notes: Check this to option to print all or selected transaction user notes.

---

<a id="edi-segmentloop-or-segmentgrouptab"></a>

##### EDI Segment/Loop or Segment/GroupTab

> 📄 Source: [EDI_Segment_Loop_or_Segment_Group_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EDI_Segment_Loop_or_Segment_Group_Tab.htm)

###### EDI Segment/Loop or Segment/Group Tab

 Click here for information about the EDI Segment/Loop tab.


With the Segment/Loop tab, you can determine what information about segments or loops is included in your printed guideline.


Choose from the following options:

- Print Purpose: The description of the segment/loop included in the standard.
- Print Semantics: The standards-defined comment related to the segment's semantics.
- Print Syntax Rules: The standards-defined comment related to the segment's syntax.
- Print User Option: A column showing the usage (for example,: required, situational).
- Print Rules: The business rules defined for the segment/loop.
- Print envelope segments: Envelope segments.
- Print excluded loops/segments: Excluded segments/loops.
- Suppress printing by User option: Segments/loops with the listed user options. Check the specific user options you want to print.  
- Print Comments: Remarks defined by the standard as it applies to the specific segment being referenced.
- Print full loop path: Prints the full loop path on each segment page. The loop path displays the path starting from transaction to the parent loop of the current segment.
- Print separate loop page: Prints each loop on a separate page.
- Print segment/loop User Notes: Prints the selected user notes. Select this option to print all notes or select the specific user notes you want to print.





 Click here for information about the EDIFACT Segment/Group tab.


With the Segment/Group tab, you can determine what information about segments or groups is included in your printed guideline. 

- Function: Select this option to print Purpose notes.
- Notes: Select this option to print comments.
- Print Dependency Notes: Select this option to print Syntax rules/Dependency Notes.
- Print User Option: Select this check box to print User Option (i.e: Required, Situational, an so on).
- Print Rules: Select this option to print Rules specified on a Segment/Loop.
- Print envelope segments: Select this option to print envelope segments. In order for this option to work properly, a proper envelopes database must be installed, otherwise, all envelope segments are treated as another segment node.
- Print excluded Groups/Segments: Select this option to print excluded Loops/Groups and Segments.
- Print full group path: Select this option to print full loop path on Segment/Loop pages.
- Print separate group page: Select this option to add a separate page for loops.
- Print Notes and Dependency Notes at the end of the section: Select this option to print Semantics, Comments, and Syntax Rules of X12 at the end of the section.
- Suppress printing by User Option: Select this option to print Segment/Loops based on User Option value. Then check the specific User Option values you want to print.
- Print segment/group User Notes: Check this to option to print all or selected Segment/Loop user notes.

---

<a id="edi-compositeelement-tab"></a>

##### EDI Composite/Element Tab

> 📄 Source: [EDI_Composite_Element_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/EDI_Composite_Element_Tab.htm)

###### EDI Composite/Element Tab


To print the information associated with the elements in a guideline, click the Composite/Element tab.


Choose from the following options:

- Print Description: The standards-defined description of the element.
- Print User Option: This allows you to include these options for printing in direct relation to the Segment Requirement Designator.
- Print Rules: The business rules defined for the composite/element.
- Print Repetition: The number of times the element can be repeated within the transaction/message.
- Print Syntax: The standards-defined comment related to the segment's syntax.
- Print Semantics: The standards-defined comment made in reference to the segment's semantics.
- Print Comments: Remarks defined by the standard as it applies to the specific segment being referenced.
- Use full width: Enables you to print the composite/element details by using the full table width. This reduces the number of pages required to print the guideline.
- Print excluded element: Composites/elements excluded from the guideline.
- Excluded field/code mark: Allows you to set a character with which to mark excluded segments.
- Print with codes: All codes within excluded elements.
- Suppress printing by User Option: Prints composite/element with the listed user option values. You must check the specific user option values you want to select for printing.
- Appendix of elements: Select this to print an appendix, and then click one of the following options that indicates the elements to include:
- Print list of all included elements which are within the included segments: Prints an appendix of all elements in the included segments.
- Print list of included elements which are within any segments: In the appendix, prints all included elements within all segments (included and excluded).
- Print list of all elements which are within the included segments: In the appendix, prints all elements (included and excluded) within included segments.
- Print list of all elements which are within any segments: In the appendix, prints all elements (included and excluded) within all segments (included and excluded).
- Print composite/element User  Notes: Prints the selected user notes. Select this option to print all notes or select the specific user notes you want to print.

---

<a id="hl7-document-options"></a>

##### HL7 Document Options

> 📄 Source: [Book_HL7_Document_Options.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_HL7_Document_Options.htm)

###### HL7 Document Options


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="hl7-message-tab"></a>

##### HL7 Message Tab

> 📄 Source: [HL7_Message_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/HL7_Message_Tab.htm)

###### HL7 Message Tab


From the Message tab, you can specify what message-level information you want printed in the guideline.


The following options are available:

- Print Purpose: Description of the set included in the standard.
- Print Rules: Prints any rules specified on the transaction node.
- Print excluded segments: Messages that have been excluded in the guideline. Two options are available:
- Mark excluded segments/loops with: Select a character to mark excluded segments.
- Don't print segments within excluded group: Won't print segments that are excluded or are part of an excluded group.
- Message User notes: Prints the selected message user notes. Select this option to print all notes or select the specific user notes you want to print.

---

<a id="hl7-groupsegment-tab"></a>

##### HL7 Group/Segment Tab

> 📄 Source: [HL7_Group_Segment_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/HL7_Group_Segment_Tab.htm)

###### HL7 Group/Segment Tab


Use the Group/Segment tab to specify what group/segment information to include in the printed guideline document.


Choose from the following options:

- Print Purpose: Description of the set included in the standard.
- Print excluded groups/segments: Groups/segments that have been excluded in the guideline.
- Print separate group page: Prints a separate page for groups.
- Print envelope segments: Prints envelope segments.
- Print Rules: Prints any rules specified on the group/segment node.
- Group/Segment User notes: Prints the selected group/segment user notes. Select this option to print all notes or select the specific user notes you want to print.

---

<a id="hl7-field-tab"></a>

##### HL7 Field Tab

> 📄 Source: [HL7_Field_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/HL7_Field_Tab.htm)

###### HL7 Field Tab


Use the Field tab to determine what fields are printed in the guideline document.


Choose from the following options:

- Print Purpose: Description of the fields included in the standard.
- Print Rules: Rules specified on the field node.
- Print excluded fields: Fields that have been excluded in the guideline. Two options are available:
- Excluded field/code mark: Select a character to mark excluded fields.
- Print with codes: Print excluded fields with codes.
- Print appendix of fields: Select this to print an appendix, and then click one of the following options that indicates the fields to include.
- Print list of all included fields which are within the included segments: Prints an appendix of all fields in the included segments.
- Print list of included fields which are within any segments: In the appendix, prints all included fields within all segments (included and excluded).
- Print field User notes: Prints the selected field user notes. Select this option to print all notes or select the specific user notes you want to print.

---

<a id="hl7-data-types-tab"></a>

##### HL7 Data Types Tab

> 📄 Source: [HL7_Data_Types_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/HL7_Data_Types_Tab.htm)

###### HL7 Data Types Tab


Use this tab to determine what data types are printed in the guideline document.

- Print data types: Select this option to print all the data types of the message in the printed guideline.
- Print data types first: Select this option to print data types before the message; otherwise, the data types are printed at the end.
- Print purpose: Select this option to include the purpose of the data type in the printed guideline.
- User notes: Click the notes you want to print.

---

<a id="ncpdp-transmission-tab"></a>

> 📄 Source: [NCPDP_Transmission_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/NCPDP_Transmission_Tab.htm)

###### NCPDP Transmission Tab


With the Transmission tab, you can specify what transmission information you want printed in the guideline.


The following options are available:

- Print Definition: Prints the transmission definition as stated in the standard.
- Print Rules: Prints any rules specified on the transmission node.
- Print excluded transactions/segments: Prints transactions and segments that have been excluded from the guideline.
- Mark excluded segments with: Allows you to set a character with which to mark excluded segments.
- Print segments within excluded transaction: Prints segment that are within excluded transactions.
- Transmission User notes: Prints the selected transmission-level user notes. Select this option to print all notes or select the specific user notes you want to print.

<a id="ncpdp-grouprepeatset-tab"></a>

##### NCPDP Group/RepeatSet Tab

> 📄 Source: [NCPDP_Group_RepeatSet_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/NCPDP_Group_RepeatSet_Tab.htm)

###### NCPDP Group/RepeatSet Tab


From the Group/Repeat Set tab, you can specify what group-level information you want printed in the guideline.


The following options are available:

- Print Definition: Prints the description of the group as stated in the standard.
- Print Rules: Prints any rules specified on the group/repeatset node.
- Print excluded groups/repeat sets: Prints groups/ and repeats sets that have been excluded in the guideline.
- Excluded group/repeatset mark: Allows you to set a character with which to mark excluded segments.
- Group/RepeatSet User Notes: Prints the selected user notes. Select this option to print all notes or select the specific user notes you want to print.

<a id="ncpdp-field-tab"></a>

##### NCPDP Field Tab

> 📄 Source: [NCPDP_Field_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/NCPDP_Field_Tab.htm)

###### NCPDP Field Tab


To print the information associated with fields in a NCPDP guideline, click the Field tab.

- Print Definition: Prints the field description from the standard.
- Print Rules: Prints any rules specified on the field.
- Print excluded fields: Prints those fields that are marked as excluded from the guideline.
- Excluded field/code mark: Allows you to set a character to mark excluded segments.
- Print with codes: Prints all codes within excluded fields.
- Print appendix of fields: Select this to print an appendix, and then click one of the following options to indicate what fields to include:
- Print list of all included fields which are within the included Segments/Groups/Repeatsets: In the appendix, prints all included fields in the included Segments/Groups/Repeatsets.
- Print list of included fields which are within any Segments/Groups/Repeatsets: In the appendix, prints all included fields within all segments (included and excluded).
- Print field User notes: Prints the selected field user notes. Select this option to print all notes or select the specific user notes you want to print.

<a id="ncpdp-transactionsegment-tab"></a>

##### NCPDP Transaction/Segment Tab

> 📄 Source: [NCPDP_Transaction_Segment_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/NCPDP_Transaction_Segment_Tab.htm)

###### NCPDP Transaction/Segment Tab


With the Transaction/Segment tab, you can determine what information related to transactions and segments appear in your printed guideline.


Choose from the following options:

- Print Definition: Prints the description from the standard.
- Print Rules: Prints any rules specified on the transaction/segment.
- Print excluded Transactions/Segments: Prints those transactions/segments that are marked as excluded from the guideline.
- Print separate transaction page: Prints each transaction/segment on a separate page.
- Transaction/Segment User Notes: Prints the selected transaction/segment user notes. Select this option to print all notes or select the specific user notes you want to print.

<a id="importexport-guidelines"></a>

### Import/Export Guidelines

> 📄 Source: [Book_Import_Export_Guidelines.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Import_Export_Guidelines.htm)

#### Import and Export Guidelines


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="import-and-export-files"></a>

#### Import and Export Files

> 📄 Source: [Import_and_Export_Files.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_and_Export_Files.htm)

##### Import and Export Files


SpecBuilder opens and saves guideline documents in .ecs format. This format can only be viewed with SpecBuilder.


However, SpecBuilder provides wizards to import and export the guideline documents. The [Import Wizard](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_Wizard.htm) is available from the main window as well as guideline windows. The [Export Wizard](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_Wizard.htm) is available from the guideline windows only. A guideline must be open in order to export it.

---

<a id="add-a-custom-importexport-module"></a>

#### Add a Custom Import/Export Module

> 📄 Source: [Add_a_Custom_Import_Export_Module.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Add_a_Custom_Import_Export_Module.htm)

##### Add a Custom Import/Export Module


If you have written your own conversion routines in gXML by using XSL or XSLT, you can integrate those conversion routines into SpecBuilder. Integrated routines then appear in the Import and Export wizards.


You no longer have to export to gXML, and then run the XSL conversion as a second step. By selecting the custom conversion module, conversion to gXML is done first, and then the custom XSL or XSLT is executed automatically.


The XSL or XSLT must be compliant with the W3C standard and must also work with the Microsoft XML 3.0 parser (MSXML). The XSL or XSLT can be written to create or import from custom XML files, HTML files or any other proprietary file formats supported by XSL/XSLT.


To add your own conversion module:

1. You find the file **XSLTModuleInfo.xml** at: *${SpecBuilderRootFolder}\Config\XSL*.
2. You can add your own conversion module to this file by adding an `XSLTImportModule` or `XSLTExportModule` xml node. The following information is needed to create a successful entry into the XSLTModuleInfo.xml file:


Example Export Entry


<XSLTExportModule DocType = "EDI" Name="Module GXML 1.0" gXMLType="10" SaveExcluded = "Yes" OutputType = "XML"> <DisplayName>Mendelson Transformation Engine Format</DisplayName> <Description>Convert to XML description format file used by Mendelson Transformation Engine.</Description> <XSLPath RelativePath="Yes">XSLModuleFile.xsl</XSLPath> <OutputFileFilter>XML Files (*.xml)|*.xml|All Files (*.*)|*.*||</OutputFileFilter> <DefaultExt>xml</DefaultExt> </XSLTExportModule>  


The preceding entry appears in the Export Wizard.


Example Import Entry


<XSLTImportModule Name="Module GXML 1.0" gXMLType="10"> <DisplayName>Mendelson Transformation Engine Format for gXML 1.0</DisplayName> <Description>Import from XML description format file used by Mendelson Transformation Engine.</Description> <XSLPath RelativePath="Yes">fd_gxml10.xsl</XSLPath> </XSLTImportModule>


 


Note If you would like your conversion module to be part of SpecBuilder setup and install, please contact Edifecs support.

---

<a id="clean-up-cobol-copybook-files-before-importing"></a>

#### Clean up COBOL Copybook Files Before Importing

> 📄 Source: [Clean_up_COBOL_Copybook_Files_Before_Importing.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Clean_up_COBOL_Copybook_Files_Before_Importing.htm)

##### Clean up COBOL Copybook Files Before Importing


Before importing COBOL copybook files, do the following tasks to make sure that the import goes smoothly:

- Make each record an 01
- Remove notes and other non-statement information
- Convert EBCDIC files to ASCII

---

<a id="import-guidelines"></a>

#### Import Guidelines

> 📄 Source: [Book_Import_Guidelines.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Import_Guidelines.htm)

##### Import Guidelines


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="import-wizard"></a>

##### Import Wizard

> 📄 Source: [Import_Wizard.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_Wizard.htm)

###### Import Wizard


SpecBuilder contains conversion modules that enable you to import guidelines created in different file formats. You can then save the guideline as an .ecs file.


You can import the following file formats:

- [COBOL Copybook](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_from_COBOL_Copybook.htm)
- EDI gXML v0.8, v1.0
- Gentran DDF (for EDI and PFF)
- Gentran UNIX IG
- gXML v 1.0
- HL7MIF to .ecs
- Mendelson Transformation Engine Format for gXML 1.0
- [Pervasive XML](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_from_Pervasive_XML.htm)
- [RosettaNet Modular XSD](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_RosettaNet_from_Modular_XSD.htm)
- [SAP IDoc guideline](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_from_SAP_IDoc.htm)
- [SAP XML IDoc](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_from_SAP_XML_IDoc.htm)
- [Standard Exchange Format (SEF)](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_from_Standard_Exchange_Format_SEF.htm)
- TIE Documentation Manager Format (EDM)
- [XML Schema (XSD)](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_from_XML_Schema.htm)
- [JSON Schema](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_from_JSON_Schema.htm)


To import a file:

1. On the File menu, click Import, and then click Import from a Data Format.
2. Click the file format you want to import, and then click Next.
3. Click Browse and navigate to the file to import or type the path and file name that you want to import, and then click Next.
4. Complete any additional options, if required (see Related Topics for imports requiring special options).
5. Click Finish to close the Import Wizard.


Note While the file imports, you can click Close on Finish to automatically close the wizard when the import finishes.

---

<a id="import-from-cobol-copybook"></a>

##### Import from COBOL Copybook

> 📄 Source: [Import_from_COBOL_Copybook.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_from_COBOL_Copybook.htm)

###### Import from COBOL Copybook


A COBOL copybook is a text-based program file that is usually used to describe the physical layout of data in a output file, input file, or shared between programs.


Before importing a COBOL copybook file, follow the instructions in [Clean up COBOL Copybook Files Before Importing](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Clean_up_COBOL_Copybook_Files_Before_Importing.htm) to remove any issues that might affect processing of the file.


To import from COBOL Copybook format:

1. On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click Import, and then click Import from a Data Format.
2. In the Choose an import to perform section, click Cobol Copybook File.
3. In the File to Import box, type a file name for the COBOL copybook or click Browse to navigate to the file location, and then click Next.
4. In the Cobol Copybook Import Options dialog box, select from the following options:
5. Click Next to go to the next screen, and then click Finish to import the file.


The new Positional Flat File guideline displays for your review.

---

<a id="import-from-standard-exchange-format-sef"></a>

##### Import from Standard Exchange Format (SEF)

> 📄 Source: [Import_from_Standard_Exchange_Format_SEF.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_from_Standard_Exchange_Format_SEF.htm)

###### Import from Standard Exchange Format SEF


To import an SEF file:

1. On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click Import, and then click Import from a Data Format.
2. In Choose an import to perform, click Standard Exchange Format (SEF), and then click Next.
3. Click Browse and navigate to the file to import or type the path and file name that you want to import, and then click Next.
4. In the Import dialog box, choose from the following options:
5. In the Envelope Segments dialog box, click Insert Envelope Segments to include envelope segment, click the envelope segment to include, and then click Next.
6. Click Finish to close the Import Wizard.


Note While the file imports, you can click Close on Finish to automatically close the wizard when the import finishes.

---

<a id="import-from-sap-idoc"></a>

##### Import from SAP IDoc

> 📄 Source: [Import_from_SAP_IDoc.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_from_SAP_IDoc.htm)

###### Import from SAP IDoc


To import a SAP IDoc file:

1. On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click Import, and then click Import from a Data Format.
2. In Choose an import to perform, click SAP IDoc, and then click Next.
3. Click Browse and navigate to the file to importer type the path and file name that you want to import, and then click Next.
4. In the Select Import options dialog box, in the Record section handling group, click Create record section to include records in the export file. Records are comparable to envelopes in EDI guidelines.
5. Select the structure version to use.
6. Click Next.
7. Click Finish to close the Import Wizard.


Note While the file imports, you can click Close on Finish to automatically close the wizard when the import finishes.

2. In Choose an import to perform, click SAP XML IDoc, and then click Next.
3. Click Browse and navigate to the file to import or type the path and file name that you want to import, and then click Next.

<a id="import-oracle-seebeyond-monk-format-ssc"></a>

##### Import Oracle (SeeBeyond) Monk Format (SSC)

> 📄 Source: [Import_Oracle_SeeBeyond_Monk_Format.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_Oracle_SeeBeyond_Monk_Format.htm)

###### Import Oracle (SeeBeyond) Monk Format (SSC) Overview


Monk is a specialized but extensible programming language that was developed by SeeBeyond Technology Corporation. It is an implementation of the Scheme programming language.


When you import an .ssc guideline SpecBuilder picks all information from a selected HL7 guideline and replaces information that is only available in the .ssc file with content from the selected HL7 guideline. If no information is found for a particular node, then an error message is sent to a log file which is shown in the Import Log wizard step. In this case, some properties have no values generated in the output guideline.


To import an Oracle (SeeBeyond) Monk Format (SSC) file:

1. Open SpecBuilder.
2. On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click Import, and then click Import from a Data Format.
3. In the Choose an import to perform box of the Import Wizard, click SeeBeyond SSC file.
4. Click Next.
5. In the Import Source box on the Import Source screen, type the name of the SSC file, or click ellipsis (...) to locate and select the file, and then click Open.
6. Click Next.
7. On the Oracle (SeeBeyond) Monk Format (SSC) Import Options screen, select import options. The following options are available:
8. Click Next. Proceed to the next step if you want to choose a guideline from the database.
9. On the Standards Database screen, choose a guideline from the Standards Database to be used for importing.
10. Click Next.
11. Click Finish.


Note While the file imports, you can click Close on Finish to automatically close the wizard when the import finishes. In this case, theImport Log wizard step is skipped even if errors are present.

---

<a id="import-hl7-mif-to-ecs"></a>

##### Import HL7 MIF to ECS

> 📄 Source: [Import_HL7_MIF_to_ECS.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_HL7_MIF_to_ECS.htm)

###### Import HL7 MIF to ECS


This module converts HL7 MIF to SpecBuilder Guideline.


To import HL7 MIF to ECS:

1. On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click Import, and then click Import from a Data Format.
2. In the Choose and import to perform section, click HL7 MIF to ECS.
3. In the File to Import box, type a file name for the HL7 MIF or click Browse to navigate to the file location, and then click Next.
4. Click Finish to import the file.


Note To import HL7 MIF to ECS you need to have the HL7 version 3 standards data base installed.


The CMET description is present in an imported file. Apart from the CMET description, the resulted guideline also has all necessary data types and vocabularies from the installed HL7 version 3 standards data base.

---

<a id="import-rosettanet-from-modular-xsd"></a>

##### Import RosettaNet from Modular XSD

> 📄 Source: [Import_RosettaNet_from_Modular_XSD.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_RosettaNet_from_Modular_XSD.htm)

###### Import RosettaNet from Modular XSD


The RosettaNet standard is based on XML and defines message guidelines, business processes interface, and implementation frameworks for interactions between companies.


To import RosettaNet from Modular XSD:

1. On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click Import, and then click Import from a Data Format.
2. In the Choose and import to perform section, click RosettaNet Modular XSD.
3. In the File to Import box, type a file name for the RosettaNet or click Browse to navigate to the file location, and then click Next.
4. In the RosettaNet Modular XSD to ECS Import Options dialog box, type a BPSS file name or click Browse () to navigate to the file location, and then click Next. You may skip this in case you don't want to include available node properties into the generated guideline. If you provide a BPSS file in this page, then node properties are available in the [Properties pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Properties_Pane_Overview.htm).
5. Click Finish to import the file.

---

<a id="import-from-pervasive-xml"></a>

##### Import from Pervasive XML

> 📄 Source: [Import_from_Pervasive_XML.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_from_Pervasive_XML.htm)

###### Import from Pervasive XML


XML is commonly used as a message format in web service interfaces and message queues. The intelligent Pervasive XML data connector easily reads and writes XML, and displays it in the Data Browser in a tree view, or a view of any particular element you choose to spotlight. Pervasive reads DTD files and also creates them based on your specifications.


To import Pervasive XML:

1. On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click Import, and then click Import from a Data Format.
2. In the Choose and import to perform section, click Pervasive XML, and then click Next.
3. In the File to Import box, type a file name for the Pervasive XML or click Browse to navigate to the file location, and then click Next.
4. In the CodeSet file to Import dialog box, type a file name or click Browse to navigate to the file location, and then click Next. You may skip this in case you don't want to include available node properties into the generated guideline. If you provide a CodeSet file in this page, then node properties are available in the [Properties pane](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Properties_Pane_Overview.htm).
5. Click Finish to import the file.

---

<a id="import-from-xml-schema"></a>

##### Import from XML Schema

> 📄 Source: [Import_from_XML_Schema.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_from_XML_Schema.htm)

###### Import from XML Schema


To import XML Schema file:

1. On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click Import, and then click Import from a Data Format. The Import Wizard is displayed.
2. In Choose an import to perform, click XML Schema (XSD), and then click Next.
3. Click Browse and navigate to the file to import or type the path and file name that you want to import, and then click Next.
4. Set import options:
5. Click Next.
6. Click Finish to close the Import Wizard.


Note While the file imports, you can click Close on Finish to automatically close the wizard when the import finishes.

---

<a id="import-from-json-schema"></a>

##### Import from JSON Schema

> 📄 Source: [Import_from_JSON_Schema.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_from_JSON_Schema.htm)

###### Import from JSON Schema


You can import a guideline from JSON schema format.


To import a guideline from JSON Schema:

1. Open your guideline file.
2. On the File menu, click Import, and then click Import from a Data Format.
3. In Choose an import to perform, click JSON Schema, and then click Next.
4. On the Import source page, in File to import, type a path and a file name that you want to import or click Browse to navigate to the file to import, and then click Next.
5. Click Finish to close the Import Wizard page.


Note While the file imports, you can click Close on Finish to automatically close the wizard when the import finishes.

---

<a id="export-guidelines"></a>

#### Export Guidelines

> 📄 Source: [Book_Export_Guidelines.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Export_Guidelines.htm)

##### Export Guidelines


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="export-wizard"></a>

##### Export Wizard

> 📄 Source: [Export_Wizard.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_Wizard.htm)

###### Export Wizard


After you customize your guideline document, with the Export Wizard, you can export the guideline to the following file formats. Please note that the formats available for export vary with the different file types.

- [BizTalk XDR](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_BizTalk_XDR_Format.htm)
- [BizTalk XSD](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_BizTalk_XSD_Format.htm)
- [Comma Separated Values (CSV)](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_Comma_Separated_Values_Format_CSV.htm)
- [CrossWorlds Business Object Definition](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_CrossWorlds_Business_Object_Definition_Format.htm)
- [EDI gXML versions 0.71, 0.8](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_gXML_v0.80_0.71.htm)
- [Gentran (.ddf)](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_Gentran_DDF.htm)
- [Gentran UNIX (IG)](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_Gentran_Unix_IG.htm)
- [gXML version 1.0](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_gXML_v1.0_Format.htm)
- [HTML with Branching Diagram](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_HTML_with_Branching_Diagram.htm)
- [Mendelson Transformation](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_Mendelson_Transformation_Engine_Format.htm) (.xml)
- [Pervasive XML](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_Pervasive_XML.htm)
- [BIC SEEBURGER Message](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_BIC_SEEBURGER_Message.htm)
- [RosettaNet DTD](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_RosettaNet_Messages_to_RosettaNet_DTD.htm)
- [RosettaNet Modular XSD](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_RosettaNet_Messages_to_RosettaNet_Modular_XSD.htm)
- [RosettaNet XSD](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_RosettaNet_Messages_to_RosettaNet_XSD.htm)
- [SAP IDoc](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_SAP_IDoc_Guideline.htm) (For Positional SAP IDOC)
- [SAP XML IDoc](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_SAP_XML_IDoc.htm)
- [Standard Exchange Format](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_Standard_Exchange_Format_SEF.htm) (.sef)
- [TIE Mapping manager Format](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_TIE_Mapping_Manager_Format_EMM.htm) (.emm)
- [XDATA 1.0 XML Schema](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_XDATA_1.0_XML_Schema.htm) (XSD)
- [XDATA 2.0 XML Schema](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_XDATA_2.0_XML_Schema.htm) (XSD)
- [HL7 to BizTalk XSD](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_HL7_to_Biztalk_XSD.htm)
- [JData Schema (JSON)](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_JData_Schema.htm)
- [JSON Schema](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_JSON_Schema.htm) (for JSON guidelines)
- [Avro Data Schema](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_Avro_Data_Schema.htm)


To export the file:

1. Open the guideline to be exported.
2. On the File menu, click Export to a Data Format.
3. In the Choose an export to perform section, click the appropriate export file format, and then click Next.
4. In the Save exported File as box, type a path and file name for the exported document or click Browse to navigate to the location, and then type a name for the file.
5. Click Next.
6. In Options, set any additional options, and then click Next. See specific exporting topics for information about the available options.
7. Click Save.

<a id="export-to-bic-seeburger-message"></a>

##### Export to BIC SEEBURGER Message

> 📄 Source: [Export_to_BIC_SEEBURGER_Message.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_BIC_SEEBURGER_Message.htm)

###### Export to BIC SEEBURGER Message


The BIC SEEBURGER Message is a schema describing the structure and constraining the contents of data fed to or produced by a data processor. Export to BIC SEEBUGER Message is available for X12, EDIFACT, Odette and Tradacoms standards.


To export to BIC SEEBURGER Message: 

1. Open your guideline file.
2. On the File menu, select Export to a Data Format.
3. In Choose an import to perform, click BIC SEEBURGER Message, and then click Next.
4. In Save resulting files to the folder, click Browse and navigate to the file location, and then click Select Folder.
5. Select Save guideline before exporting to save a guideline that matches the exported document, and then click Next.
6. On the Please select BIC SEEBURGER Message Export options wizard page, set the following export options:
7. Click Next.
8. Click Finish to close the Export Wizard.


Note While the file exports, you can click Close on Finish to automatically close the wizard when the export finishes.

---

5. Click Save.
6. Select Save guideline before exporting to save a guideline that matches the exported document, and then click Next.
7. Select from the following options:
8. Click Next.
9. Click Finish to close the Export Wizard.


Note While the file exports, you can click Close on Finish to automatically close the wizard when the export finishes.

5. Click Save.
6. Select Save guideline before exporting to save a guideline that matches the exported document, and then click Next.
7. In the EDI .ecs to Microsoft BizTalk XML Schema Export Options dialog box, do the following:
8. Click Next.

<a id="export-to-comma-separated-values-format-csv"></a>

##### Export to Comma Separated Values Format (CSV)

> 📄 Source: [Export_to_Comma_Separated_Values_Format_CSV.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_Comma_Separated_Values_Format_CSV.htm)

###### Export to Comma Separated Values format (CSV)


You can export your guideline to comma-separated values format (CSV). Then you can open your guideline in applications including Microsoft Excel, where it can be viewed and/or modified.


To export a guideline to CSV format:

1. Open a guideline.
2. On the File menu, click Export to a Data Format.
3. In the Choose an export to perform box, click Comma Separated Values format (CSV), and then click Next.
4. In the Save exported file as box, enter a path and file name for the exported document or click Browse to navigate to the location, and then enter a name for the file.
5. Click Save.
6. Select Save guideline before exporting to save a guideline that matches the exported document.
7. Click Next.
8. In the Export Configuration dialog box, select one of the following options:
9. Click Next.
10. Click Finish to close the Export Wizard.


Note While the file exports, you can click Close on Finish to automatically close the wizard when the export finishes.


To create a new configuration file or modify an existing one:

1. In the Export Configuration dialog box, select New configuration or enter the path to the file, and then click the Customize check box. Click Next.
2. In the Column Options dialog box, select guideline attributes to be exported. Select columns, their names, and order to export. Click Edit Columns Properties on the toolbar to provide column name and value to use if corresponding attribute is not specified in the guideline. Click Next.
3. In the ID Format Options dialog box, select formatting for ID columns. For each node type, select the attributes to be used and appropriate formatting. Use the following options on the toolbar:
4. Click Next.
5. In the Rows Options dialog box, select rows types to be exported. A new row is generated for each node type that is checked. Clear the node to suppress the row creation. Set the end row flag to true, in order to make a row indicate the end of node content. The following options are available:
6. In the Header and Trailer Options dialog box, select the row, and specify content for the row on the Format string field. Use the following options on the toolbar:
7. Click Next.
8. In the Save Export Configuration dialog box, click Save configuration, and then in the Save configuration file as field, provide a file path.
9. Click Next.

---

###### Export to CrossWorlds Business Object Definition Format


SpecBuilder includes an export function to convert files from the .ecs file format into the CrossWorlds Business Object Definition (BODef) file format that can be processed by IBM CrossWorlds software.


To export to CrossWorlds Business Object Definition format:

2. On the File menu, click Export to a Data Format.
3. In Choose an export to perform, click CrossWorlds Business Object Definition, and then click Next.
4. In the Save exported File as box, enter a path and file name for the exported document or click Browse to navigate to the location, and then enter a name for the file.
5. Click Save.
6. Select Save guideline before exporting to save a guideline that matches the exported document.
7. Click Next.
8. Set the following export parameters:
9. Click Finish to close the Export Wizard.


Note While the file exports, you can click Close on Finish to automatically close the wizard when the export finishes.

> 📄 Source: [Export_to_gXML_v0.80_0.71.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_gXML_v0.80_0.71.htm)

###### Export to gXML v0.80/0.71


This routine exports a guideline to Guideline XML Exchange Format (gXML). gXML is the industry standard to allow the exchange of electronic commerce guidelines. A DTD file which validates gXML compliant documents is also created.


To export to gXML v0.8:

1. Open your guideline file.
2. On the File menu, click Export to a Data Format.
3. From the Export Wizard, click EDI gXML v0.8, and then click Next.
4. In the Save exported file as box, enter a path and file name for the exported document or click Browse to navigate to the location, and then enter a name for the file.
5. Click Save.
6. Select Save guideline before exporting to save a guideline that matches the exported document.
7. Click Next.
8. On the Please select version of gXML wizard page, click the version of gXML to create. Click Next.
9. On the Please select export options wizard page, pick from the following options:
10. On the Select Dictionary Export options wizard page, choose from the following options:
11. Click Next.
12. Click Finish to close the Export Wizard.

7. Click Next.
8. Choose from the following options:

This export routine converts EDI guidelines from .ecs format to Pervasive XML format.

2. On the File menu, click Export to a Data Format.
3. In Choose an export to perform, click Pervasive XML, and then click Next.

<a id="export-to-sap-idoc-guideline"></a>

##### Export to SAP IDoc Guideline

> 📄 Source: [Export_to_SAP_IDoc_Guideline.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_SAP_IDoc_Guideline.htm)

###### Export to SAP IDoc Guideline


This function exports Positional Flat File guidelines to the SAP IDoc format that can be imported into a SAP system.


To export to SAP IDoc guideline:

1. Open your guideline file.
2. On the File menu, click Export to a Data Format.
3. In Choose an export to perform, click SAP IDoc guideline, and then click Next.
4. In the Save exported file as box, enter a path and file name for the exported document or click Browse to navigate to the location, and then enter a name for the file.
5. Click Save.
6. Select Save guideline before exporting to save a guideline that matches the exported document.
7. Click Next.
8. In the .ecs to SAP IDoc export dialog box, in the Export Options section, click Export Records Section to include records in the export file. This is available only when exporting SAP Idocs. Records are comparable to envelopes in EDI guidelines.
9. Click Finish to close the Export Wizard.


Note While the file exports, you can click Close on Finish to automatically close the wizard when the export finishes.

---

This function exports Positional Flat File guidelines to the SAP XML IDoc format that can be imported into a SAP system.


To export to SAP XML IDoc:

1. Open your guideline file.
2. On the File menu, click Export to a Data Format.
3. In Choose an export to perform, click SAP XML IDoc, and then click Next.
4. In the Save exported file as box, enter a path and file name for the exported document or click Browse to navigate to the location, and then enter a name for the file.
5. Click **Save**.
6. Select Save guideline before exporting to save a guideline that matches the exported document.

<a id="export-rosettanet-messages-to-rosettanet-dtd"></a>

##### Export RosettaNet Messages to RosettaNet DTD

> 📄 Source: [Export_RosettaNet_Messages_to_RosettaNet_DTD.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_RosettaNet_Messages_to_RosettaNet_DTD.htm)

###### Export RosettaNet Messages to RosettaNet DTD


DTD declares which elements and references may appear in the document of the particular type, and what are the contents and attributes of the elements. You can use XML validation against DTD. Exporting RosettaNet messages as DTDs loses some of the semantics of the standard message. To be able to incorporate all the semantics of the RosettaNet messages, you can [export to XSD](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_RosettaNet_Messages_to_RosettaNet_XSD.htm).


To export a RosettaNet Message to RosettaNet DTD:

1. Open your guideline file.
2. On the File menu, click Export to a Data Format.
3. In Choose an export to perform, click RosettaNet DTD.
4. In the Save exported file as box, enter a path and file name for the exported document or click Browse to navigate to the location, and then enter a name for the file.
5. Click Save.
6. Select Save guideline before exporting to save a guideline that matches the exported document, and then click Next.
7. On the RosettaNet ECS to DTD Options wizard page, choose from the following options:
8. Click Next.
9. Click Finish to close the Export Wizard.


Note You can click Close on Finish to automatically close the wizard when the export is complete.

---

<a id="export-rosettanet-messages-to-rosettanet-modular-xsd"></a>

##### Export RosettaNet Messages to RosettaNet Modular XSD

> 📄 Source: [Export_RosettaNet_Messages_to_RosettaNet_Modular_XSD.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_RosettaNet_Messages_to_RosettaNet_Modular_XSD.htm)

###### Export RosettaNet Messages to RosettaNet Modular XSD


RosettaNet Business Messages are schemas for XML files that partners exchange. DTDs are not powerful enough to allow for all the features of RosettaNet standard messages to be defined properly. Exporting RosettaNet messages as DTDs loses some of the semantics of the standard message. To be able to incorporate all the semantics of the RosettaNet messages, you can export to an XSD. The Modular XSD format was created by RosettaNet developers and is used only with RosettaNet guidelines.


To export a RosettaNet Message to RosettaNet Modular XSD:

1. Open your guideline file.
2. On the File menu, click Export to a Data Format.
3. In Choose an export to perform, select RosettaNet Modular XSD, and then click Next.
4. In the Save exported File as box, enter a path and file name for the exported document or click Browse to navigate to the location, and then enter a name for the file.
5. Click Save.
6. Select Save guideline before exporting to save a guideline that matches the exported document.
7. Click Next.
8. On the RosettaNet ECS to RosettaNet Modular XSD Export Options wizard page, choose from the following options:

---

<a id="export-rosettanet-messages-to-rosettanet-xsd"></a>

##### Export RosettaNet Messages to RosettaNet XSD

> 📄 Source: [Export_RosettaNet_Messages_to_RosettaNet_XSD.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_RosettaNet_Messages_to_RosettaNet_XSD.htm)

###### Export RosettaNet Messages to RosettaNet XSD


RosettaNet Business Messages are schemas for XML files that partners exchange. DTDs are not powerful enough to allow for all the features of RosettaNet standard messages to be defined properly. Exporting RosettaNet messages as DTDs loses some of the semantics of the standard message. To be able to incorporate all the semantics of the RosettaNet messages, you can export to an XSD file.


To export a RosettaNet Message to RosettaNet XSD:

1. Open your guideline file.
2. On the File menu, click Export to a Data Format.
3. In Choose an export to perform, select RosettaNet XSD, and then click Next.
4. In the Save exported file as box, enter a path and file name for the exported document or click Browse to navigate to the location, and then enter a name for the file.
5. Click Save.
6. Select Save guideline before exporting to save a guideline that matches the exported document.
7. Click Show advanced options to set XSD namespace options, and then click Next.
8. Use this wizard to configure XSD namespace generation:
9. Click Next.
10. On the RosettaNet .ecs to XSD Export Options wizard page, choose from the following options:
11. Click Next.
12. Click Finish to close the Export Wizard.


Note While the file exports, you can click Close on Finish to automatically close the wizard when the export finishes.

<a id="export-to-standard-exchange-format-sef"></a>

##### Export to Standard Exchange Format (SEF)

> 📄 Source: [Export_to_Standard_Exchange_Format_SEF.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_Standard_Exchange_Format_SEF.htm)

###### Export to Standard Exchange Format (SEF)


You can export guidelines to the Standard Exchange Format (a plain text format).


The following limitations apply when exporting and/or importing to this format.

- Segments are not exported to SEF files if their position attribute is empty.
- SEF only supports excluding or including of codes - not deleting. If you delete a standard code, export it to SEF, and then re-import it, the code appears as "excluded".


To export SEF Documents:

1. Open your guideline file.
2. On the File menu, click Export to a Data Format.
3. In Choose an export to perform, click Standard Exchange Format (SEF), and then click Next.
4. In the Save exported file as box, enter a path and file name for the exported document or click Browse to navigate to the location, and then enter a name for the file.
5. Select Save guideline before exporting to save a guideline that matches the exported document, and then click Next.
6. On the Set required export options wizard page, choose from the following options:
7. Click Next.
8. Click Finish to close the Export Wizard.


Note While the file exports, you can click Close on Finish to automatically close the wizard when the export finishes.

---

<a id="export-to-xdata-10-xml-schema"></a>

> 📄 Source: [Export_to_XDATA_1.0_XML_Schema.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_XDATA_1.0_XML_Schema.htm)

###### Export to XDATA 1.0 XML Schema


This export routine converts an EDI or PFF guideline from .ecs format to XDATA 1.0 XML Schema format so that it can be used by XML Applications such as Edifecs XEngine.


To export EDI guideline to XDATA Version 1.0:

1. Open your guideline file.
2. On the File menu, click Export to a Data Format.
3. In Choose an export to perform, click XDATA Version 1.0 XML Schema, and then click Next.
4. In the Save exported File as box, enter a path and file name for the exported document or click Browse to navigate to the location, and then enter a name for the file.
5. Select Save guideline before exporting to save a guideline that matches the exported document, and then click Next.
6. On the Export wizard page, choose from the following options:
7. Click Next.
8. Please specify macro nodes wizard page is displayed.
9. Click Next to export the document.
10. Click Finish to close the Export Wizard.


Note While the file exports, you can click Close on Finish to automatically close the wizard when the export finishes.


To export PFF to XDATA Version 1.0:

1. Open a PFF guideline.
2. Repeat steps 2 to 6 of the procedure described earlier.
3. On the Export wizard page, choose node properties, which are included in the exported guideline. Click to show one of the following:

---

<a id="export-to-xdata-20-xml-schema"></a>

##### Export to XDATA 2.0 XML Schema

> 📄 Source: [Export_to_XDATA_2.0_XML_Schema.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Export_to_XDATA_2.0_XML_Schema.htm)

###### Export to XDATA 2.0 XML Schema


To export to XDATA Version 2.0:

1. Open your guideline file.
2. On the File menu, click Export to a Data Format.
3. In Choose an export to perform, click XDATA Version 2.0 XML Schema, and then click Next.
4. In the Save exported file as box , enter a path and file name for the exported document or click Browse to navigate to the location, and then enter a name for the file.
5. Click Save.
6. Select Save guideline before exporting to save a guideline that matches the exported document, select Show advanced options, and then click Next.
7. On the XSD namespace options wizard page, choose from the following options:
8. Click Next.
9. On the Customize element and type name templateswizard page, you can choose to generate node names compatible with XEngine (default), or customize the node naming in the following ways: XML element names must contain a prefix to indicate a node type. This can be a single character or a full name - for example, S or Segment. To apply templates configurations, select Use abbreviated element name prefixes.
10. For each node type in the guideline, click to the right of the node name to define the name as a combination of various properties from the specification.
11. Click OK, and then click Next.
12. On the Please select common conversion options wizard page, choose from the following conversion options:
13. If .NET compatible node naming is selected, Select internal properties you would like to use wizard page is displayed.
14. Click Next.
15. On the Please select document specific conversion options wizard page, choose Don’t use xsd:choice for keyed segments/loops option if your XSD tool does not support the <xsd:choice> feature.
16. Click Next.
17. Please specify macro nodes wizard page is displayed.
18. Click Next to export the document.
19. Click Finish to close the Export Wizard page.


Note While the file exports, you can click Close on Finish to automatically close the wizard when the export finishes.

<a id="export-to-xml-schema-xsd"></a>
