<a id="data-editor-window-for-ncpdp"></a>

#### Data Editor Window for NCPDP

> 📄 Source: [Data_Editor_Window_for_NCPDP.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Editor_Window_for_NCPDP.htm)

##### Data Editor Window for NCPDP


For NCPDP Batch guidelines, {NCPDP Data} nodes containing Telecom transmissions are displayed on the Analyzer/Data pane:


*[Image: Analyzer/Data tab]*


To open Data Editor window, click Browse ():


*[Image: Data Editor]*


The Data Editor window is used to edit the Telecom Transmissions. Its workspace has a three-pane format:

- The Guideline pane on the left with a tree view of the guideline,
- The user-defined data file in the right pane,
- The Data Errors log if the data was analyzed or Data Generator Log if the data was generated.


The following options are available on the toolbar:

- Click Save Data As (), to save data changes to a file.
- Click Search/Replace (), to find specified text and replace it with the data you need. For more information, see [Search and Replace](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Search_and_Replace.htm).
- Click Edit Delimiters (), to change delimiters set. For more information, see [Edit Delimiters](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Edit_Delimiters.htm).
- Click Tree View (), to switch back to the tree view from XData or LDNS Report views.
- Click Business Data View (), to switch to business perspective.

---

<a id="data-business-perspective"></a>

#### Data Business Perspective

> 📄 Source: [Data_Business_Perspective.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Business_Perspective.htm)

##### Data Business Perspective


For EDI, PFF, DFF, HL7, NCPDP, and JSON standards you can view data in a business-friendly form view. This view represents the logical data structure which is optimal for documenting and reviewing.


To switch to business perspective:

1. Open EDI, PFF, DFF, HL7, NCPDP, or JSON guideline.
2. In the Analyzer/Data pane, open an existing data file or generate a new data.
3. On the Analyzer/Data tab toolbar, click Business Data View ().


Note To switch back to the tree data view, click Tree Data View () on the toolbar.


Data nodes are displayed in the form of interactive table, where you can navigate from interchange or functional groups to segments and elements. You can always go to the previous node by using the navigation toolbar.


*[Image: Data in Business Perspective]*


The following option are available for the business data view:


| To | Do this |
| --- | --- |
| Navigate through the nodes | Click Go back () or Go forward (). |
| Add a new child node | Click Add child (). |
| Remove selected child node | Click Remove Child (). |
| Print current data node | Click Print (). |
| Undo or redo your previous action | Click Undo () or Redo (). |

---

<a id="validate-data-files"></a>

### Validate Data Files

> 📄 Source: [Book_Validate_Data_Files.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Validate_Data_Files.htm)

#### Validate Data Files


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="analyzer-overview"></a>

#### Analyzer Overview

> 📄 Source: [Analyzer_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Analyzer_Overview.htm)

| # Analyzer Overview | |
| --- | --- |



Analyzer is an integrated part of the application that tests and validates data files against the corresponding guideline document (.ecs file). In addition, Analyzer tests and analyzes the files against the standard to check for possible mandatory segments or elements that were eliminated from the created guideline.


###### Features and Benefits

- User Interface: Analyzer is part of the state-of-the-art, easy-to-use, and intuitive user interface. Use Analyzer for viewing and editing the data stream and the related guideline as well as checking for possible errors. You can make changes to the data file, and then revalidate it or load a new data file to analyze against the currently-opened guideline.
- Cost Effective: Eliminates the costly process of testing the guideline by running it through the translator and the Value Added Network (VAN) repeatedly to search for errors.
- Automated Testing: Allows the trading partner to send EDI and other data files as a text file to be run through the translator against the guideline to look for mapping errors.


###### Analyzer Architecture


The following diagram shows the process of data analyzing.


*[Image: SpecBuilder Analyzer]*

---

<a id="the-analyzer-interface"></a>

#### The Analyzer Interface

> 📄 Source: [The_Analyzer_Interface.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/The_Analyzer_Interface.htm)

##### The Analyzer Interface


The Analyzer workspace has a three-pane format - the Guideline pane on the left with a tree view of the guideline, the user-defined data file in the right pane, and the error log on the Data Errors tab of the Output pane.


*[Image: Analyzer interface]*


The following options are available on the toolbar:


| To | Do this |
| --- | --- |
| Create new data by using the Data wizard. For more information, see [Create New Data Files](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Create_New_Data_Files.htm). | Click New Data (). |
| Validate a data file by using Analyzer. For more information, see [Analyzer Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Analyzer_Overview.htm). | Click Analyzer (). |
| Save data changes to a file. | Click Save Data As (). |
| Discard the changes you made and go back to the previously saved data. | Click Revert (). |
| Generate data from the guideline. For more information, see [Data Generator Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Overview.htm). | Click Data Generator (). |
| Preview the data file before printing it. | Click Print Preview (). |
| Print the data file. | Click Print (). |
| Find specified text and replace it with the data you need. For more information, see [Search and Replace](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Search_and_Replace.htm). | Click Search/Replace (). |
| Move directly to a specific data node in a data file. For more information, see [Jump to Specific Data Node](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Jump_to_Specific_Data_Node.htm). | Click Go To (). |
| Change delimiters set. For more information, see [Edit Delimiters](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DataExplorer_Edit_Delimiters.htm). | Click Edit Delimiters (). |
| Customize data file options. For more information, see [Data View Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Set_Data_View_Options.htm). | Click Options (). |
| Switch back to the tree view from XData or LDNS Report views | Click Tree View (). |
| View XData (XML) report. For more information about this mode, see [View Generated XData](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/View_Generated_XData.htm). | Click XData (). |
| View LDNS Report. For more information, see [View XML and HTML Logical Data Reports](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/View_XML_and_HTML_Logical_Data_Reports.htm). | Click LDNS Report (). |




For EDI, PFF, DFF, HL7, NCPDP, and JSON guidelines you can view the data in a business-friendly form view. Click Business Data View (), to switch to business perspective. Business View is displayed on the Analyzer/Data tab:


*[Image: Business perspective]*

---

<a id="the-analyzer-wizard"></a>

#### The Analyzer Wizard

> 📄 Source: [Book_The_Analyzer_Wizard.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_The_Analyzer_Wizard.htm)

##### The Analyzer Wizard


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="analyzer-wizard-overview"></a>

##### Analyzer Wizard Overview

> 📄 Source: [Analyzer_Wizard_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Analyzer_Wizard_Overview.htm)

###### Analyzer Wizard Overview


You can use the Analyzer to analyze an EDI, Positional Flat File, Delimited Flat File, NCPDP, HL7, or JSON data file against the related guideline. Analyzer can be accessed directly from the Navigation pane, the Tools tab of the ribbon, or you can click the Analyzer/Data pane to either generate new test data or use an existing data file to test against an open guideline.


The Analyzer Wizard walks you through the following tasks:


###### Analyzer Selecting a Data File


###### Analyzer Setting Preprocessing Options


###### Analyzer Guideline Selection


###### Analyzer Selecting Envelope Segments


###### Analyzer Mode/Outputs

---

<a id="select-a-data-file"></a>

##### Select a Data File

> 📄 Source: [Analyzer_Select_a_Data_File.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Analyzer_Select_a_Data_File.htm)

###### Select a Data File


To analyze a data file directly:

1. On the SpecBuilder toolbar, click Launch Analyzer. The Welcome to the Analyzer Wizard launches.
2. In the Select data file to analyze field, type the name of the data file to use or click Browse to navigate to the location of the file.
3. In the Specify data file type, change the type of file represented by the data file, if required.
4. Select Show Advanced Options if you want to specify any of the following options (the following steps assume that you selected this option):
5. Click Next and proceed to [Analyzer Preprocessing Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Preprocessor_Options.htm).

---

<a id="preprocessor-options"></a>

##### Preprocessor Options

> 📄 Source: [Preprocessor_Options_Analyzer.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Preprocessor_Options_Analyzer.htm)

###### Preprocessor Options


If you want to clean up the data file, you can use the Preprocessor. With the Preprocessor, you can eliminate any terminator string or block characters in the data.

1. In the Clean Up Data File dialog box, select the Use Preprocessor check box.
2. In the Parameters box, select a saved parameter file to use, if applicable.
3. Do one of the following:
4. To save these settings, click Save As and, in the Preprocessor Parameters Name server, enter a name or select an existing parameter name to modify its definition.
5. Enter a path to save the modified data file or click Browse to locate a folder in which to save the modified data file.
6. Click Next. For standards such as PFF, DFF, and NCPDP, go to step 8.
7. In the Parsing result dialog box, do one of the following:
8. For standards such as PFF, DFF, and NCPDP Batch, in the Record Parsing Information dialog box, select the record delimiter used in your data file or click Next to accept the default delimiter.
9. Click Next to go to the [Select properties file wizard page](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Properties_File_Analyzer.htm).

---

<a id="properties-file"></a>

##### Properties File

> 📄 Source: [Properties_File_Analyzer.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Properties_File_Analyzer.htm)

###### Properties File


You can select a file with the list of all data node properties needed to be set for the input data file.


A properties file is a configuration .properties file that is used to add custom properties to the specified data nodes. The parameters are stored as key-value pairs. You can use these properties in Java Script, Java, and .NET rules.


A property file contains lines of the following format that add properties to the required data nodes:


dns.fnm.<data node name>.prp.<name>=<value>

- *dns* - a root value that indicates a data node structure
- *fnm.<data node name>* - moves selection to the data node with the matching name. For example, dns.fnm.Interchange moves the selection to the interchange node.
- *prp.<name>* - creates a new property with the name <name> and the value <value>. If the specified property already exists then its value is overwritten.


Example


dns.fnm.EntireDocument.prp.TransmissionFormat=HIPAA5010A


Note If a data node used in the instruction does not exist, the instruction is ignored.


To select a data node properties file:

1. In the Select properties file dialog box, click to navigate to the required properties file.
2. Click Next to go to the next wizard page.

---

<a id="data-structure"></a>

##### Data Structure

> 📄 Source: [Data_Structure_Analyzer.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Structure_Analyzer.htm)

###### Data Structure


To select a node to validate:

1. In the Data Structure dialog box, select a node to validate. The node's properties are defined in the right pane. By default, the entire document is validated. To limit the validation to specific levels, highlight the appropriate folder.

1. Click Next to go to the next wizard page.

---

<a id="guideline-selection"></a>

##### Guideline Selection

> 📄 Source: [Guideline_Selection_Analyzer.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Guideline_Selection_Analyzer.htm)

###### Guideline Selection

1. In the Guideline selection dialog box, specify the guideline to use for validation. This can be either from the Standards database or from a specific guideline file.

1. Click Next and go to Envelope Segments.


For more information on Envelope Segments, see [Select Envelope Segments](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Select_Envelope_Segments_Analyzer.htm).

---

<a id="record-parsing-information"></a>

##### Record Parsing Information

> 📄 Source: [Data_Generator_Record_Parsing_Information.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Record_Parsing_Information.htm)

###### Record Parsing Information


This wizard step is available for PFF and DFF standards.


*[Image: Specify data parser settings]*


Select one of the following options:

- Create a new message if a data record matches the first record in a guideline: Select to create a new message if a data record of a DFF/PFF file matches the first record in a guideline. In the following example, three messages are created if the option is enabled. If disabled - one message is created.
- File is EBCDIC encoded: Select this check box if a file is EBCDIC encoded.
- In the Open Existing Parser Schema field, to navigate to the file location, provide the file with parser schema or click .
- Generate schema for delimited records: Select this check box to generate parser schema for delimited records. Clear this option for fixed length records, and auto-schema generation mode is used. In the Edit Delimiters field, specify the delimiters which separate records in the data file.

---

<a id="select-envelope-segments"></a>

##### Select Envelope Segments

> 📄 Source: [Select_Envelope_Segments_Analyzer.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Select_Envelope_Segments_Analyzer.htm)

###### Select Envelope Segments


Note Envelopes are not defined for the XML Schema documents, so this option is not available for XML Schema guidelines.


###### Guidelines with envelope segments:


Select one of the following options:

- Use the existing Envelope Segments: Select this option to use the envelope specified in your guideline. Click Next.
- Remove existing and select new Envelope Segments from the database: Select this option to replace envelope segments with ones from the Envelopes database. Click Next, select the envelope segment from the database, click Next, and go to Using Envelope Segments.
- Remove existing and use Envelope Segments from the external guideline file: Select this option to use envelopes stored in an external file. Click Next, select the guideline file to use, and then click Next.
- Remove existing and use Envelope Segments from multiple guideline files: Select this option to use envelopes stored in multiple external files. This option is useful to match the structure of envelope guidelines to that used by the Run-Time engine where Interchange guideline and Functional Group guidelines are stored in separate .ecs files. See "Selecting envelope segments from multiple guideline files" below.


###### Guidelines without envelope segments:

- Select Envelope Segments from the Standards Database: Select this option to add envelope segments from the Standards database. Click Next, select the envelope segment from the database, click Next, and go to Using Envelope Segments.
- Process without Envelope Segments: Select this option to go ahead and analyze the file without envelope segments.
- Use Envelope Segments from the external guideline file: Select this option to add envelope segments stored in an external file.
- Use Envelope Segments from multiple guideline files: Select this option to use envelope segments stored in multiple external files. This option is useful to match the structure of envelope guidelines to that used by the Run-Time engine where Interchange guideline and Functional Group guidelines are stored in separate .ecs files. See "Selecting envelope segments from multiple guideline files" below.


###### Selecting envelope segments from multiple guideline files

1. Specify a guideline file for the Interchange level and one for the Functional Group and then click Next.

1. Click Next to go to the next wizard page.

---

<a id="select-modeoutputs"></a>

##### Select Mode/Outputs

> 📄 Source: [Select_Mode_Outputs_Analyzer.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Select_Mode_Outputs_Analyzer.htm)

###### Select Mode/Outputs

1. In the Analyzer Mode and Outputs dialog box, specify the following Analyzer options:
2. Click Next. After the file has been processed, the Wizard Result dialog box informs you if your file has any errors or not.

---

<a id="select-additional-validation-options"></a>

##### Select Additional Validation Options

> 📄 Source: [Analyzer_Additional_Validation_Options.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Analyzer_Additional_Validation_Options.htm)

###### Select Additional Validation Options


Provide an error severity configuration file for Analyzer to be used. If you leave this field blank, the file specified in the Option dialog box on the Severity tab is used by default. For more information, see [Set Global Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Set_Global_Options.htm).


To provide an error severity configuration file:

1. Click Browse and navigate to the error severity configuration file you want to use.
2. Click one of the following:
3. Click Next and go to the next wizard page.


The specified error severity configuration file is available in the Current option on the Home tab of the ribbon.


When you are validating the XML file, you are able to specify the following options:

- Do not perform Schematron validation: The Schematron validation is skipped.
- Use currently open Schematron: The Schematron rules which are currently displayed on the Schematron tab are used for validation.
- Use specified Schematron: Click browse and navigate to the Schematron file you want to use for validation.

<a id="the-analyzer-wizard-for-ncpdp-batch"></a>

##### The Analyzer Wizard for NCPDP Batch

> 📄 Source: [The_Analyzer_Wizard_for_NCPDP_Batch.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/The_Analyzer_Wizard_for_NCPDP_Batch.htm)

###### The Analyzer Wizard for NCPDP Batch


To analyze an NCPDP Batch data file:

1. Select NCPDP Batch data file in the Select data file to analyze box.
2. Select NCPDP Batch in the Specify data file type box.
3. Select Show Advanced Options check box.
4. [Select how to use Preprocessor](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Preprocessor_Options.htm).
5. View the Data Structure dialog box.
6. Only entire document can be validated. To view the properties of each node, select it under the EntireDocument. To validate the document, click Next.
7. [Specify the guideline to use for validation](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Guideline_Selection.htm).
8. Define significant properties, which the transmission is based on, and then click Next:
9. View the values of the significant properties for the transmission:
10. Click Next.
11. View or select an NCPDP Telecom guideline to use for the correspondent transmission.
12. Click Next.
13. Repeat steps 7 to 9 for all transmissions in a batch.
14. [Select the Analyzer mode and outputs to generate](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Select_Mode_Outputs.htm).

<a id="data-view-options"></a>

#### Data View Options

> 📄 Source: [Book_Data_View_Options.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Data_View_Options.htm)

##### Data View Options


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="set-data-view-options"></a>

##### Set Data View Options

> 📄 Source: [Set_Data_View_Options.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Set_Data_View_Options.htm)

###### Set Data View Options


Use Data View Option dialog box to set data file options.


To set options:

1. Click Analyzer/Data pane.
2. On the toolbar, click Options ().
3. Use the following tabs, to set the options of the data view:

---

<a id="data-view-options-general-tab"></a>

##### Data View Options General Tab

> 📄 Source: [Data_View_Options_General_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_View_Options_General_Tab.htm)

###### Data View Options General Tab


Use the General tab of the Data View Options dialog box, to set one of the following:

- Run wizards with advanced options: When this flag is set the Analyzer wizard shows option to pre-process file and also allows the ability to only validate portion of the data file.
- Show Wizard Result dialog box: When this option is checked, a dialog box appears when Analyzer has processed the file.
- Generate LDNS report: This allows user to indicate logical data report should be generated.
- Generate XData (XML): This allows user to indicate that XML data should be generated.
- Summary Report scope: Format can be set to None, File or Data Node. Default is None, which means no summary report is available. Setting the option to File groups errors at the file level. Setting the option to Data Node groups errors by nodes such as Functional Groups, Transactions, and the rest. Additionally, if Generate Logical Data Node option is set, such as for HIPAA transactions then it groups by items such as Provider, Subscriber, and the rest. When this setting is checked, the report is changed so only one instance of each type of error is reported. In addition to the error, a count is provided to indicate how many times that specific error occurred in the data and what percent this error occupies of the total number of errors found in the data file. This information can be used to quickly determine which errors occur most often and can help you prioritize which errors to resolve.
- Disable Revert option: Select this check box to disable revert ability. In this case, you are not able to go to the previously created data after regeneration or revalidation.
- Display additional information for group nodes in tree: Select this check box to get additional information about the nodes displayed in the data tree view.
- Define a large data file as at least: This allows user to limit large data file mode to files larger than a size provided.
- Display the short version of the HTML report (with large files, the standard HTML report can be very long): Select this check box to display the short version of the HTML report.

---

<a id="data-view-options-data-printing-tab"></a>

##### Data View Options Data Printing Tab

> 📄 Source: [Data_View_Options_Data_Printing_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_View_Options_Data_Printing_Tab.htm)

###### Data View Options Data Printing Tab


From the Data Printing tab, you can select formatting options to use when you print your data file.


All data files can be published to RTF or HTML file format. In addition to setting margins, headers and footers, the following options are available:

- Print line numbers: Gives each segment a unique line number and line numbers are re-started for each Interchange.
- Print grid lines: Prints lines around each column.
- Use alternate shading to highlight the data line: Shades alternate lines for easier viewing.
- Show header nodes: Prints a logical grouping of "header" nodes. Header nodes are nodes that do not appear in the data file but can be explicitly stated in printing. In EDI, header nodes consist of Interchanges Group and Transactions nodes.
- Show header nodes information: Print properties of header nodes.
- Print in indented format: Indents Group and Transaction nodes. Groups are indented with respect to the Interchange, and Transactions are indented with respect to the Group.
- Indent step size: Defines indent of Group and Transaction nodes with respect to their parent node.
- Margins: Sets margins for printed data file.
- Header/footer position: Sets borders of headers and footers.
- Header/footer content: Select from a list of variables, including file name, file path, document version, and standard version, and company name. You can define the Company Name from the Properties page on the File tab.

---

<a id="data-view-options-report-stylesheet-tab"></a>

##### Data View Options Report StyleSheet Tab

> 📄 Source: [Data_View_Options_Report_StyleSheet_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_View_Options_Report_StyleSheet_Tab.htm)

###### Data View Options Report StyleSheet Tab


From the Report StyleSheet tab, you can register or modify style sheets (XSLTs) which can be used to view LDNS XML report and XML data reports.

- New: Allows you to register a new report style sheet which can then be used to view the XML Logical Data Reports and/or XML Data. Clicking this button shows the following dialog box.  
- Modify: Allows you to modify properties of an existing registered style sheet. When creating a new report, the same dialog box and properties are shown, and you can modify the values as needed.
- Remove: Allows you to delete the registered report. You can choose to only remove the registration entry and leave the actual style sheet on the machine or choose to delete both the entry and the style sheet file.
- Move Up/Move Down: Moves the currently selected report either up or down in the list. The order of the registered report is used when you are viewing LDNS XML Data or XData. The first best match based on report type, guideline type and transaction ID as set when the report is registered is used to find the match. For example, if you have created a custom report for 837 transaction and you want it to be the default style sheet used when viewing the LDNS then you should ensure that it is the above any other reports registered for 837 transaction.
- Show StyleSheets of report type: Use this drop-down to filter which registered style sheets to view - all, LDNS or XML Data.

---

<a id="data-view-options-parser-schema-tab"></a>

##### Data View Options Parser Schema Tab

> 📄 Source: [Data_View_Options_Parser_Schema_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_View_Options_Parser_Schema_Tab.htm)

###### Data View Options Parser Schema Tab


Use the Parser Schema tab of the Data View Options dialog box to set the following:


First Record in the Guideline Starts a New Message: Select this check box to indicate that the first record in the guideline starts a new message.

<a id="work-with-errors"></a>

#### Work with Errors

> 📄 Source: [Book_Work_with_Errors.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Work_with_Errors.htm)

##### Work with Errors


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="data-errors-pane"></a>

##### Data Errors Pane

> 📄 Source: [Data_Errors_Pane.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Errors_Pane.htm)

###### Data Errors Pane


The Data Errors pane displays the errors generated after Analyzer has finished validating the data file against the guideline.


| Item | Definition |
| --- | --- |
| # | The order of the error. Errors encountered at the beginning of the document are listed first. |
| Error ID | The ID assigned to the error. Each type of error is assigned an ID. |
| Error Message | Explains the error in terms of what is provided in the document and what Analyzer expected. |
| Error Data | Indicates the actual data within the data file. |
| SNIP Type (HIPAA only) | SNIP Type can be from 1 to 7 as per WEDI (Workgroup for Electronic Data Interchange) definitions. |
| Severity | Indicates the severity level that classifies all errors. See [Error Severity Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Error_Severity_Overview.htm). |



Double-click any error message to view that message in a separate dialog box. Once errors are identified, you can edit the data string, and then click Analyzer on the toolbar to re-analyze the file against the guideline. Analyzer errors listed in the Data Errors pane can also be saved to HTML or RTF format, enabling you to exchange the information with other users and trading partners.


You can [customize this view](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Customize_Data_Error_View.htm), save and print the errors from this pane.

---

<a id="data-error-view"></a>

##### Data Error View

> 📄 Source: [Book_Data_Error_View.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Data_Error_View.htm)

###### Data Error View


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="customize-data-errors-view"></a>

##### Customize Data Errors View

> 📄 Source: [Customize_Data_Error_View.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Customize_Data_Error_View.htm)

###### Customize Data Errors View


On the Data Error View toolbar, click Customize () to customize what types of errors are displayed and to customize error severity.


The following customization can be done:


###### Show/hide/edit SNIP error types (HIPAA only)  


###### Specify type of messages to show

---

<a id="specify-type-of-messages-to-show"></a>

##### Specify Type of Messages to Show

> 📄 Source: [Analyzer_Specify_Type_of_Messages_to_Show.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Analyzer_Specify_Type_of_Messages_to_Show.htm)

###### Specify Type of Messages to Show


You can set options for the data error report by using Data Error View Options.


To specify the type of messages to show:

1. In the Analyzer/Data pane, on the Data View toolbar, click Options ().
2. In the Data Error View Options dialog box, click the Advanced tab.
3. Select from the following options:

---

<a id="correct-errors"></a>

##### Correct Errors

> 📄 Source: [Correct_Errors.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Correct_Errors.htm)

###### Correct Errors


After running the Analyzer Wizard, you can correct any errors that occur between the data file and implementation guideline from the Analyzer/Data pane.


All nodes in the Data View have icons corresponding to the nodes of the guideline in the Guideline View. If there is no match for the data node in the guideline, that node is marked with in the Data View.


To correct errors:

1. On the Data Errors pane, click an error.
2. Select the highlighted data and press F2 (or right-click the data in the, and then click Edit) to edit the data.
3. Correct the data as indicated in the error message.
4. Press Enter.
5. Click the Analyzer button to restart the analysis and verify the modification. The error should no longer appear in the Data Error View.

---

<a id="custom-errors"></a>

##### Custom Errors

> 📄 Source: [Custom_Errors.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Custom_Errors.htm)

###### Custom Errors


###### Custom Error IDs


SpecBuilder and XEngine assign every reported error an error ID, which is included on the error report, and can be used for grouping and summarizing. When adding a custom rule through JavaScript, by default, every new error is given an ID of 60000000.


To customize the ID number assigned to a user-defined error, use ec_ErrorContainer.AddError2 with an _ec_CaseID parameter. CaseID should be a 4 digit number, 6000 or later. 0XXX-5XXX are reserved for system use. For example:


ec_ErrorContainer.AddError2 ("_ec_CaseID", "9876");


This creates an error with an error ID of 60009876. You also need to configure the Error Severity Configuration file to handle this error ID. This file can be edited directly in a text editor or by Creating User-Defined Severity.


<ApplyTo>


<Criteria Name="ErrorID" Value="6000[Case ID]"/>


<SetSeverity SeverityID="[severity ID]"/>


<SetUserErrorMessage>[Error message]</SetUserErrorMessage>


</ApplyTo>


If the previous example was intended as a warning only, the error severity file could be configured in the following way:


<ApplyTo>


<Criteria Name="ErrorID" Value="60009876"/>


<SetSeverity SeverityID="2000"/>


<SetUserErrorMessage>This is a custom warning</SetUserErrorMessage>


</ApplyTo>


###### Using Javascript Variables in Error Severity File Messages


To use a user-defined or system variable value as part of the error message, the variable must be passed to the error object as a parameter of the AddError2 method. AddError2 can support up to 10 variable names as parameters.


The variable is passed as a pair of parameters, the first is a label for the parameter which is used in the error message, and the second is the name of the variable. The variable label is then referenced in the Error Severity Configuration file message by [%Label].


var MyVar = ec_DataLink.m_sData;


ec_ErrorContainer.AddError2 ("_ec_CaseID", "9876", "MyVarAdded", MyVar);


<SetUserErrorMessage>My message with my variable:


'[%MyVarAdded]'.</SetUserErrorMessage>

---

<a id="error-report-formats"></a>

##### Error Report Formats

> 📄 Source: [Error_Report_Format.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Error_Report_Format.htm)

###### Error Report Formats


The Data Error View provides an easy way to view the location and source of errors in a data file. You can use this information to resolve the errors either within the data file or the implementation guideline. Additionally, you can save or print these errors in various formats so that they can be shared with other team members, with trading partners, or for reference purposes.  


To set the error report format:

1. In the Data Errors pane, click the Save As, Print, or Print Preview icon.
2. In the Error Report Type group, select from one of the following types:
3. Set one or more additional report settings:


All report types can be printed to the printer, or be exported to RTF, HTML or PDF formats.

---

<a id="generate-test-data"></a>

### Generate Test Data

> 📄 Source: [Book_Generate_Test_Data.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Generate_Test_Data.htm)

#### Generate Test Data


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="data-generator-overview"></a>

#### Data Generator Overview

> 📄 Source: [Data_Generator_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Overview.htm)

| # Data Generator Overview | | | --- | --- | SpecBuilder enables you to create test data files based on a guideline. These data files can also be modified and validated against the guideline to reveal potential issues that can be corrected. The Data Generator component supports creation of EDI data files, NCPDP Telecom data files, Delimited Flat files, Positional Flat files and XML Instance files from guideline documents.


###### Features and Benefits


The following list presents the features and benefits of Data Generator:

- User Interface: State-of-the-art, intuitive, easy-to-use interface for creating, viewing, and editing data files.
- Data Dictionaries: With data dictionaries, you can store data values and macros that allow generation of customized data files. Data dictionaries are independent of both data files and guidelines so they can be re-used even when new versions of standards are published or when your guideline structure changes.
- Create new test data file from existing data files: With Data Generator, you can create data files totally from scratch or you can provide an existing data file, change values for your specific needs, and easily create new test data files. The changes you make are stored in the data dictionary so you can apply them to other data files.
- Create test data from an external data source: You can use data from external databases or Excel databases to generate test data.
- Data Dictionary Generator: Create data dictionaries from existing data files.
- Edit Data: Easy modification of information at the interchange, group, loop, segment, element, and composite level.


Note When generating data, repeating loops are generated between one and ten times, regardless of the max use value. The number is generated at random, but is always at least one and no more than ten. If you need to generate more loops in the data, you need to create an entry for the loop repeat by using the Data Dictionaries pane.

---

<a id="get-started-with-data-generator"></a>

#### Get Started with Data Generator

> 📄 Source: [Get_Started_with_Data_Generator.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Get_Started_with_Data_Generator.htm)

##### Get Started with Data Generator


To generate a data file for a guideline:

1. Start SpecBuilder.
2. Open the guideline for which to generate the data file.
3. On the toolbar, click Data Generator (). The Data Generator Wizard appears. See Data Generator Wizard Overview for detailed instructions for generating a data file.

- or -

1. Start SpecBuilder.
2. In the Navigation pane, click Data Generator. See Data Generator Wizard Overview for detailed instructions for generating a data file. 


Any errors during data generation are displayed in the Data Errors pane, and the test data file is viewable.

---

<a id="the-data-generator-wizard"></a>

#### The Data Generator Wizard

> 📄 Source: [Book_The_Data_Generator_Wizard.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_The_Data_Generator_Wizard.htm)

##### The Data Generator Wizard


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="welcome-to-the-data-generator-wizard"></a>

##### Welcome to the Data Generator Wizard

> 📄 Source: [Welcome_to_the_Data_Generator_Wizard.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Welcome_to_the_Data_Generator_Wizard.htm)

###### Welcome to the Data Generator Wizard


On the Welcome to the Data Generator Wizard, you can choose whether to create a new test case or use an existing one.

1. Select one of the following:
2. Select Run Analyzer after Generator if you want Analyzer to run immediately after the test data is generated. Analyzer can also be run manually by clicking **Analyzer** on the toolbar.
3. Select Show Advanced Options if you want to specify the following options for the file:
4. Click Next to go to one of the following:

---

<a id="create-or-update-data-file"></a>

##### Create or Update Data File

> 📄 Source: [Data_Generator_Create_or_Update_Data_File.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Create_or_Update_Data_File.htm)

###### Create or Update Data File


In the Create or update data file wizard, you can generate a new data file or update an existing data file.


*[Image: Create or update data file]*

1. In the Please select the action Data Generator should perform group, click Generate to create a new file or Update to modify an existing data file.
2. If you chose Update in step 1, type a file name and choose a data type in the Specify data file type box.
3. Click Next and proceed to [Select a guideline](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Guideline_Selection.htm).


Note The ability to generate data by using an existing data file is extremely useful in the following cases:

- Case 1: You already have a data file with meaningful data values and do not wish to re-specify these values in the Data Generator data dictionary. By having the Data Generator modify the existing file, you need only specify the values you want changed.
- Case 2: The guideline from which the data is being generated contains rules above and beyond the basic standard rules such as EDI Syntax. In this case, the Data Generator creates a valid data file that follows the standard rules such as EDI syntax, but the Data Generator is not able to account for the additional validation rules that may have been added to the guideline, such as for HIPAA guidelines. In these cases it would require significant effort to create a clean data file, so it is extremely useful to start with an existing data file and only modify the fields you are interested in.


Note When using an existing data file to generate new test data, there are some limitations. Specifically, the Repeat set and Skip macros that may be defined in the data dictionary are ignored during data generation so that the new test data file can be as close to the original file as possible.


If you choose to update a data file, logical loops are not supported. The update mode only updates existing data with the matches from the dictionaries and from running dictionary macros.

---

<a id="test-case-selection"></a>

##### Test Case Selection

> 📄 Source: [Data_Generator_Test_Case_Selection.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Test_Case_Selection.htm)

###### Test Case Selection


Use this wizard to choose the test case you want to run while data generation.


*[Image: Test case selection]*


In the test case list, click a test case you want to be used, and then click Next to start data generation.


If you want to modify one of the existing test cases, select this test case in the list and check **Modify Test Case** box. Click Next to go to [Data Generator Guideline Selection](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Guideline_Selection.htm) wizard step.

---

<a id="guideline-selection"></a>

##### Guideline Selection

> 📄 Source: [Guideline_Selection.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Guideline_Selection.htm)

###### Guideline Selection

1. In the Guideline selection dialog box, specify the guideline to use for validation. This can be either from the Standards database or from a specific guideline file.

1. Click Next and go to Envelope Segments.


For more information on Envelope Segments, see [Select Envelope Segments](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Select_Envelope_Segments.htm).

---

<a id="select-envelope-segments"></a>

##### Select Envelope Segments

> 📄 Source: [Select_Envelope_Segments_Data_Generator.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Select_Envelope_Segments_Data_Generator.htm)

###### Select Envelope Segments


Note Envelopes are not defined for the XML Schema documents, so this option is not available for XML Schema guidelines.


###### Guidelines with envelope segments:


Select one of the following options:

- Use the existing Envelope Segments: Select this option to use the envelope specified in your guideline. Click Next.
- Remove existing and select new Envelope Segments from the database: Select this option to replace envelope segments with ones from the Envelopes database. Click Next, select the envelope segment from the database, click Next, and go to Using Envelope Segments.
- Remove existing and use Envelope Segments from the external guideline file: Select this option to use envelopes stored in an external file. Click Next, select the guideline file to use, and then click Next.
- Remove existing and use Envelope Segments from multiple guideline files: Select this option to use envelopes stored in multiple external files. This option is useful to match the structure of envelope guidelines to that used by the Run-Time engine where Interchange guideline and Functional Group guidelines are stored in separate .ecs files. See "Selecting envelope segments from multiple guideline files" below.


###### Guidelines without envelope segments:

- Select Envelope Segments from the Standards Database: Select this option to add envelope segments from the Standards database. Click Next, select the envelope segment from the database, click Next, and go to Using Envelope Segments.
- Process without Envelope Segments: Select this option to go ahead and analyze the file without envelope segments.
- Use Envelope Segments from the external guideline file: Select this option to add envelope segments stored in an external file.
- Use Envelope Segments from multiple guideline files: Select this option to use envelope segments stored in multiple external files. This option is useful to match the structure of envelope guidelines to that used by the Run-Time engine where Interchange guideline and Functional Group guidelines are stored in separate .ecs files. See "Selecting envelope segments from multiple guideline files" below.


###### Selecting envelope segments from multiple guideline files

1. Specify a guideline file for the Interchange level and one for the Functional Group and then click Next.

1. Click Next to go to the next wizard page.

---

<a id="specify-optional-data-generation"></a>

##### Specify Optional Data Generation

> 📄 Source: [Data_Generator_Specify_Optional_Data_Generation.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Specify_Optional_Data_Generation.htm)

###### Specify Optional Data Generation


In the Optional data wizard, you can indicate whether you want nodes that are marked optional in the guideline to be generated and to what extent. You can also specify which usage attributes (User or Standard) in the guideline should be used to determine whether a particular data node is optional or not.


Note This page is not displayed if you choose Update rather than Generate in the [Create or Update Data File](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Create_or_Update_Data_File.htm) dialog box. 


*[Image: Data Generator options]*


To specify optional data generation:

1. Choose from the following data generation options:
2. Choose the option to use when determining whether a node is mandatory or optional:
3. Click Next, and proceed to [Select Element Size Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Select_Element_Size_Options.htm).

---

<a id="select-element-size-options"></a>

##### Select Element Size Options

> 📄 Source: [Data_Generator_Select_Element_Size_Options.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Select_Element_Size_Options.htm)

###### Select Element Size Options


In the Element Size wizard, you can set the size of each of the generated elements. With this option, you can generate data that helps test boundary conditions.


Note This page is not displayed if you choose Update rather than Generate in the [Create or Update Data File](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Create_or_Update_Data_File.htm) dialog box.


*[Image: Element Size]*

1. Choose one of the Element Size options.
2. Click Next and proceed to [Select Index Reset Point](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Select_Index_Reset_Point.htm).

---

<a id="select-index-reset-point"></a>

##### Select Index Reset Point

> 📄 Source: [Data_Generator_Select_Index_Reset_Point.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Select_Index_Reset_Point.htm)

###### Select Index Reset Point


In the Index Reset dialog box, you can determine when to reset a sequential list of dictionary values to the first entry. The resetting can be done at the Transaction, Functional Group, or Interchange level. You can also tell the system not to reset.  For example, if an element in data dictionary has been assigned three values (A, B and C) and they are marked to be used sequentially, this setting dictates when value A should be reused. Setting the reset point to Transaction resets the value to A at the beginning of each transaction.


Note Not available for XML Schema guidelines because XML Schema data documents neither have envelopes nor allow multiple root elements.


For Delimited Flat File, Positional Flat File, and  NCPDP guidelines, the Index Reset option differs. These types of guidelines do not have Transactions, Functional Groups or Interchanges.


The following dialog box is displayed and you can choose to reset on encountering a new message or to not reset:


*[Image: Index Reset]*


Click Next and Proceed to [Select Repeat Counts](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Select_Repeat_Counts.htm).

---

<a id="select-repeat-counts"></a>

##### Select Repeat Counts

> 📄 Source: [Data_Generator_Select_Repeat_Counts.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Select_Repeat_Counts.htm)

###### Select Repeat Counts


In the Repeat Count wizard, indicate how many transactions per functional group to generate, how many functional groups per interchange to generate, and how many interchanges to generate per file. There is a maximum limit for each of the specified nodes.


Not available for XML Schema guidelines because XML Schema data documents neither have envelopes nor allow multiple root elements.


Note This page is not displayed if you choose Update rather than Generate in the [Create or Update Data File](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Create_or_Update_Data_File.htm) dialog box. 


*[Image: Repeat Count]*


For Delimited Flat File, Positional Flat File, and NCPDP guidelines, you can only set repeat counts for messages.


Click Next and proceed to [Select Data Dictionaries](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Select_Data_Dictionaries.htm).

---

<a id="select-data-dictionaries"></a>

##### Select Data Dictionaries

> 📄 Source: [Data_Generator_Select_Data_Dictionaries.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Select_Data_Dictionaries.htm)

###### Select Data Dictionaries


In the Data Dictionaries wizard, in the More Dictionaries pane, click the data dictionaries to be used by the Data Generator. Use the center arrow keys to move them to the Selected Dictionaries pane, and the up and down arrows to arrange the order in which they are used.


*[Image: Data Dictionaries]*


Click Next and proceed to [Select Delimiters](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Set_Delimiters.htm).

---

<a id="set-delimiters"></a>

##### Set Delimiters

> 📄 Source: [Data_Generator_Set_Delimiters.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Set_Delimiters.htm)

###### Set Delimiters


In the Delimiters dialog box, you can set the desired delimiters for the data file.


Note Not available for XML Schema guidelines.


To set delimiters:

1. In the Edit delimiters pane, type either the Ascii Value or Hex Value for each delimiter type.
2. To open a previously-saved delimiter file, click the Open delimiters set.
3. To save the settings, click the Save delimiters set.
4. Check Specify filler characters to set the characters that are used to populate an element when the {$Filler} macro is utilized in the data dictionary.
5. Click Next. The Filler Characters dialog box is displayed.
6. Specify the characters to be used for each data type. You can specify either the ASCII or the Hexadecimal equivalent for each filler character. You can also save the whole set as the default for future re-use.
7. Click Next and proceed to [Set Test Case Parameters](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Set_Test_Case_Parameters.htm).

---

<a id="ncpdp-data-record"></a>

##### NCPDP Data Record

> 📄 Source: [NCPDP_Data_Record.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/NCPDP_Data_Record.htm)

###### NCPDP Data Record


This wizard step is available for NCPDP Batch guidelines.


To specify how to generate an NCPDP Data Record:

1. Click Browse (), and select the Generation Task file .DGT to be used for NCPDP Telecom data Record generation.
2. Click Next.

---

<a id="set-test-case-parameters"></a>

##### Set Test Case Parameters

> 📄 Source: [Data_Generator_Set_Test_Case_Parameters.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Set_Test_Case_Parameters.htm)

###### Set Test Case Parameters


Command Line Execution 


With the Data Generator wizard, you can simply generate an output file and/or to save the options in a test case that can be re-used later to generate additional test data files. The test case is saved in a Generation Task file, typically by using a .dgt file extension. A Generation Task file can contain multiple test cases. 


To set test case parameters:

1. Check the Output Data file name box to specify the name and path for the output file or click Browse to navigate to the location of the file. This is the data file generated by the data generation engine.
2. Check Save Test Case to the Generation Task file to save all the options set in the wizard to a test case. Type the name and path for the Generation Task file or click Browse to navigate to the location of the file.
3. Click Next to begin generating the file and/or the test case.


###### Command Line Execution 


Generation Task files can be executed from a command line, allowing you to generate test data files without using the user interface. The batch execution program is called RunDGT.exe and is located in the SpecBuilder install path, usually "C:\Program Files\SpecBuilder" directory. Program parameters are the path and name of the Generation Task file.


Example


C:\Program Files\SpecBuilder>RunDGT f:\junk\GenerationTaskFile.dgt


Note Test cases in the Generation Task file must specify an output file.

---

<a id="data-generator-scenario"></a>

#### Data Generator Scenario

> 📄 Source: [Book_Data_Generator_Scenario.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Data_Generator_Scenario.htm)

##### Data Generator Scenario


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="data-generator-scenario-external-data-sources"></a>

##### Data Generator Scenario - External Data Sources

> 📄 Source: [Data_Generator_Scenario_External_Data_Sources.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Scenario_External_Data_Sources.htm)

###### Data Generator Scenario - External Data Sources


When using the [Data Generator](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Generator_Overview.htm) to generate test data, you can extract data from external data sources (databases or Excel) to populate the data file.


The following tasks needs to be done to extract the data:

- Create a data source - for example, an Excel database, Access database, SQL database. For this scenario, we use [ExampleData](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/LabFiles/ExampleData.xls) (an Excel database)
- [Set up an ODBC data source](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Set_Up_an_ODBC_Data_Source.htm)
- [Create a data dictionary](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Create_a_Data_Dictionary.htm) - for more information about data dictionaries, see [Data Dictionary Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Dictionary_Overview.htm)
- [Open the ODBC connection](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Open_the_ODBC_Connection.htm)
- [Create the recordset](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Create_the_Recordset.htm)
- [Select the data to include](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Select_the_Data.htm)
- Generate the data
- [Close the connection](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Close_the_ODBC_Connection.htm)

---

<a id="set-up-an-odbc-data-source"></a>

##### Set Up an ODBC Data Source

> 📄 Source: [Set_Up_an_ODBC_Data_Source.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Set_Up_an_ODBC_Data_Source.htm)

###### Set Up an ODBC Data Source


Before you can use an external data source, you need to set up an ODBC connection to the data in Windows.


To create an ODBC connection:

1. Click Start, and then click Control Panel.
2. Click Administrative Tools.
3. Double-click Data Sources (ODBC).
4. Double-click Microsoft Excel Driver.
5. In the ODBC Microsoft Excel Setup dialog box, do the following:

---

<a id="create-a-data-dictionary"></a>

##### Create a Data Dictionary

> 📄 Source: [Create_a_Data_Dictionary.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Create_a_Data_Dictionary.htm)

###### Create a Data Dictionary


The Data Dictionary is used to communicate to the Data Generator where to get the data and how to apply it.


Instructions in the Data Dictionary use the [ADO database macros](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Database_Macros_Overview.htm) to [open](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Open_the_ODBC_Connection.htm) and [close the ODBC connection](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Close_the_ODBC_Connection.htm), to [retrieve the recordset](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Create_the_Recordset.htm), and to [select the data](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Select_the_Data.htm).


To create the data dictionary:

1. On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click New, and then open a HIPAA 4010A1 837 Professional guideline.
2. Click the Data Dictionaries pane.
3. Click the New Dictionary ().
4. In the Name box, type DGExample-SS.
5. Click OK.


A finished version of the dictionary is located in SpecBuilder\Config\Data-Generator\Dictionary\9.4.


Please see the sample [dictionary file](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/LabFiles/ExampleODBC-SS-62.dic).

---

<a id="open-the-odbc-connection"></a>

##### Open the ODBC Connection

> 📄 Source: [Open_the_ODBC_Connection.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Open_the_ODBC_Connection.htm)

###### Open the ODBC Connection


You need to add a macro to the Data Dictionary to open the ODBC connection.


To open the ODBC connection:

1. In the Guideline pane, click the "T" Transaction node and drag it to the Dictionary name.
2. Click Next twice to advance to the Pre-Macro dialog box.
3. Click New.
4. Click ADOCreateConnection, and then click OK.
5. In the Connection Name box, type Sample.
6. In the Connection String box, type DSN=DGExample-SS.
7. Leave UserID and Password blank.
8. Click Apply, and then click Next, and Finish.


To test the connection:

1. Click the Analyzer/Data pane.
2. In the Analyzer wizard, click Generate New Test Data.
3. Click Next to advance to the end of the wizard.


There should be no errors displayed.

---

<a id="create-the-recordset"></a>

##### Create the Recordset

> 📄 Source: [Create_the_Recordset.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Create_the_Recordset.htm)

###### Create the Recordset


In this step, you can run a query on the table to create a recordset to be processed by the Data Generator. Also, the number of records is counted in order to know how often to repeat the subscriber loop.


To create the recordset:

1. In the Guideline pane, click the "T" Transaction node.
2. Click the Data Dictionaries pane.
3. Double-click the line under the dictionary name. The Edit Dictionary Item dialog box opens.
4. Click the Pre-Macro tab.
5. Click New(Ins).
6. Click ADOExecuteSelect.
7. In the Connection box, select {Sample} from the drop-down list.
8. In the SQL select statement box, type select * from ClaimData.
9. In the Result Recordset box, select ClaimMaster from the drop-down list.
10. Click Apply.
11. Click New(Ins).
12. Click RunJScript.
13. In the text box, type ecVariables.Records=ecVariables.ClaimMaster.RecordCount;.
14. Click OK.
15. In the Guideline pane, click loop 2000B and drag it to the Dictionary Name.
16. In the Data Dictionary Node Wizard, click Next.
17. Make sure that the Repeat data set is selected.
18. On the Values toolbar, click the Insert ().
19. In the Values box, type {Records}. This is the variable that was created earlier and contains the record count. The 2000B loop is repeated this many times.
20. Click Next twice, and then click Finish.
21. Open loop 2000B, and then click loop 2300. Drag it to the Dictionary Name.
22. In the Data Dictionary Node Wizard, click Next.
23. Make sure that the Repeat data set is selected.
24. On the Values toolbar, click the Insert ().
25. In the Values box, type 1.
26. Click Next twice, and then click Finish.

---

<a id="select-the-data"></a>

##### Select the Data

> 📄 Source: [Select_the_Data.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Select_the_Data.htm)

###### Select the Data


In this step, you select the data from the columns of the database.


To select the data:


Do the following steps for all elements in the table:

1. In the Guideline pane, drag the element to the Dictionary Name.
2. Click Next until you reach the Post Macros page.
3. Click New(Ins).
4. Click ADOGetColumnValue.
5. In the Recordset box, select {ClaimMaster} from the drop-down list.
6. In the Name of the Column box, type the column name for the element as shown later.
7. In the Result box, select {$Data} from the drop-down list.
8. Click Apply or Finish.


| Element | Name of the Column |
| --- | --- |
| Loop 1000A, NM103 - Submitter Last Name | DoctorLast |
| Loop 1000A, NM104 - Submitter First Name | DoctorFirst |
| Loop 2010BA, NM103 - Subscriber Last Name | PatientLast |
| Loop 2010BA, NM104 - Subscriber First Name | PatientFirst |
| Loop 2000B, 2300, CLM02 Claim Amount | ClaimAmount |

---

<a id="close-the-odbc-connection"></a>

##### Close the ODBC Connection

> 📄 Source: [Close_the_ODBC_Connection.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Close_the_ODBC_Connection.htm)

###### Close the ODBC Connection


After generating the data, you should close the ODBC connection. This is done with the ADOCloseConnection post macro on the transaction node.


To close the ODBC connection:

1. In the Guideline pane, click the "T" Transaction node.
2. Double-click the dictionary entry.
3. Click the Post Macros tab.
4. Click New(Ins).
5. Click ADOCloseConnection.
6. Select {Sample} in the Connection list.
7. Click OK.

---

<a id="compare-and-migrate-data-files"></a>

### Compare and Migrate Data Files

> 📄 Source: [Book_Compare_and_Migrate_Data_Files.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Compare_and_Migrate_Data_Files.htm)

#### Compare and Migrate Data Files


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="data-comparator-overview"></a>

#### Data Comparator Overview

> 📄 Source: [Data_Comparator_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparator_Overview.htm)

| # Data Comparator Overview | |
| --- | --- |



The Data Comparator is used to compare and modify data files. The following are the most common uses of Data Comparator:

- Compare data files from different standard versions to see the differences.
- Modify a data file without breaking its structure.
- Define user rules for comparison.
- Troubleshoot problems with a data file by identifying the changes.


With Data Comparator, you can edit a data file while retaining the data structure. It also compares the data files intelligently by using the referenced guideline structure and user-defined rules.


The Data Comparator can be run from a [user interface](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparator_Interface.htm) or from a [command-line interface](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparator_Command_Line_Operation.htm) .


###### Data Comparator Architecture


The following diagram shows the process of data comparison.


*[Image: SpecBuilder Data Comparator]*

<a id="the-data-comparator-wizard"></a>

#### The Data Comparator Wizard

> 📄 Source: [Book_The_Data_Comparator_Wizard.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_The_Data_Comparator_Wizard.htm)

##### The Data Comparator Wizard


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="select-data-file-and-type"></a>

##### Select Data File and Type

> 📄 Source: [Data_Comparator_Select_Data_File_and_Type.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparator_Select_Data_File_and_Type.htm)

###### Select Data File and Type


Use the Data Comparator Wizard to select the data files you want to compare and set comparison options for them.


To select a data file for comparison: 

1. On the toolbar, click the Launch Data Comparator. The Data Comparison Wizard opens.
2. In the Left data file box, enter the name of the data file to use or click Browse to navigate to the location of the file.
3. Perform the same operation for the Right data file box.
4. In the Data file type, change the type of file represented by the data file, if required.
5. Select Show Advanced Options if you want to specify any of the following options (the following steps assume that you selected this option):
6. Click Use Same Reference Guideline to use the same guideline for both left and right data files. If you click this option you do not have to select a referenced guideline for the right data file.
7. Click Expand Diff Tree to Records (slow for large data files) to expand the diff tree up to the record level after the diff data tree is loaded; otherwise the diff tree is not expanded.
8. Click Next and proceed to the [Data Comparator Clean Up Data File](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Preprocessor_Options.htm) wizard.

---

<a id="preprocessor-options"></a>

##### Preprocessor Options

> 📄 Source: [Preprocessor_Options.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Preprocessor_Options.htm)

###### Preprocessor Options


If you want to clean up the data file, you can use the Preprocessor. With the Preprocessor, you can eliminate any terminator string or block characters in the data.

1. In the Clean Up Data File dialog box, select the Use Preprocessor check box.
2. In the Parameters box, select a saved parameter file to use, if applicable.
3. Do one of the following:
4. To save these settings, click Save As and, in the Preprocessor Parameters Name server, enter a name or select an existing parameter name to modify its definition.
5. Enter a path to save the modified data file or click Browse to locate a folder in which to save the modified data file.
6. Click Next. For standards such as PFF, DFF, and NCPDP, go to step 8.
7. In the Parsing result dialog box, do one of the following:
8. For standards such as PFF, DFF, and NCPDP Batch, in the Record Parsing Information dialog box, select the record delimiter used in your data file or click Next to accept the default delimiter.
9. Click Next to go to the [Select properties file wizard page](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Properties_File_Analyzer.htm).

---

<a id="data-structure"></a>

##### Data Structure

> 📄 Source: [Data_Structure.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Structure.htm)

###### Data Structure


To select a node to validate:

1. In the Data Structure dialog box, select a node to validate. The node's properties are defined in the right pane. By default, the entire document is validated. To limit the validation to specific levels, highlight the appropriate folder.

1. Click Next to go to the next wizard page.

---

<a id="envelope-segments"></a>

##### Envelope Segments

> 📄 Source: [Select_Envelope_Segments.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Select_Envelope_Segments.htm)

###### Select Envelope Segments


Note Envelopes are not defined for the XML Schema documents, so this option is not available for XML Schema guidelines.


###### Guidelines with envelope segments:


Select one of the following options:

- Use the existing Envelope Segments: Select this option to use the envelope specified in your guideline. Click Next.
- Remove existing and select new Envelope Segments from the database: Select this option to replace envelope segments with ones from the Envelopes database. Click Next, select the envelope segment from the database, click Next, and go to Using Envelope Segments.
- Remove existing and use Envelope Segments from the external guideline file: Select this option to use envelopes stored in an external file. Click Next, select the guideline file to use, and then click Next.
- Remove existing and use Envelope Segments from multiple guideline files: Select this option to use envelopes stored in multiple external files. This option is useful to match the structure of envelope guidelines to that used by the Run-Time engine where Interchange guideline and Functional Group guidelines are stored in separate .ecs files. See "Selecting envelope segments from multiple guideline files" below.


###### Guidelines without envelope segments:

- Select Envelope Segments from the Standards Database: Select this option to add envelope segments from the Standards database. Click Next, select the envelope segment from the database, click Next, and go to Using Envelope Segments.
- Process without Envelope Segments: Select this option to go ahead and analyze the file without envelope segments.
- Use Envelope Segments from the external guideline file: Select this option to add envelope segments stored in an external file.
- Use Envelope Segments from multiple guideline files: Select this option to use envelope segments stored in multiple external files. This option is useful to match the structure of envelope guidelines to that used by the Run-Time engine where Interchange guideline and Functional Group guidelines are stored in separate .ecs files. See "Selecting envelope segments from multiple guideline files" below.


###### Selecting envelope segments from multiple guideline files

1. Specify a guideline file for the Interchange level and one for the Functional Group and then click Next.

1. Click Next to go to the next wizard page.

<a id="options-settings"></a>

##### Options Settings

> 📄 Source: [Data_Comparator_Options_Settings.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparator_Options_Settings.htm)

###### Options Settings


Use this wizard to choose a file with settings you want to use.


*[Image: Options Settings]*

- Choose one of the following:
- Last used Options: Click to use the options you set the last time you performed a data comparison.
- Options from specified file: Type the file name with the stored settings or click Browse to navigate to the location of the previously saved configuration. For more information about how to save a configuration, see [Data Comparison Options Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparison_Options_Overview.htm).

- Click Next, to go to [Data Comparator Guideline Comparison Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparator_Guideline_Comparison_Options.htm) wizard page.


Note If you don't use Advanced Options, then this page is the last wizard page.

<a id="guideline-comparison-options"></a>

##### Guideline Comparison Options

> 📄 Source: [Data_Comparator_Guideline_Comparison_Options.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparator_Guideline_Comparison_Options.htm)

###### Guideline Comparison Options


Use the Guideline Comparison Options wizard page to set the options that are used in the comparison process.


*[Image: Guideline Comparison Options]*

- Ignore Whitespace: Select this check box to ignore whitespace when comparing.
- Ignore nodes that are different:
- Never: Click to always show the differences between nodes.
- If both source and destination are excluded or missing: Click to skip different nodes if they are excluded or missing in both source and destination guidelines.
- If either source or destination is excluded or missing: Click to skip different nodes if they are excluded or missing in one of the guidelines.
- Document Options Comparison Settings: Select the properties to be compared.


Select all the node properties you want to compare, and then click Next, to go to [Data Comparator Guideline Match Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparator_Guideline_Match_Options.htm).

---

<a id="guideline-match-options"></a>

##### Guideline Match Options

> 📄 Source: [Data_Comparator_Guideline_Match_Options.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparator_Guideline_Match_Options.htm)

###### Data Comparator Guideline Match Options


Use the Guideline Match Options wizard to select the key attributes that Data Comparator uses to find matches.


*[Image: Guideline Match Options]*


To use Guideline Match Options:

1. Check Case Sensitive to match text by case.
2. Check the node attributes that are used as key attributes to find the match. For example, if you want Data Comparator to establish a match when loops have the same ID, check the box marked ID under the Loop folder as it is shown in the preceding picture. If you want Data Comparator to match by name as well as ID, check both.
3. Click Next to proceed to [Data Comparator Data Comparison Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparator_Data_Comparison_Options.htm).

---

<a id="data-comparison-options"></a>

##### Data Comparison Options

> 📄 Source: [Data_Comparator_Data_Comparison_Options.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparator_Data_Comparison_Options.htm)

###### Data Comparison Options


Use the Data Comparison Options wizard to set the options for comparing data files.


*[Image: Data Comparison Options]*

- Enable case insensitive data comparison: Select this option to enable case insensitive data comparison.
- Enable cross link among repeated data nodes: If you have repeated segments in your guideline, you can select this option to compare the first repeated segment in the source guideline to all the children of the same node in the target guideline.
- Compare all items except those linked to nodes on the listed UPaths: This option compares the differences between items except for items in the List of UPaths.
- Ignore all items except those linked to nodes on the listed UPaths: This option ignores the differences between items except for items in the List of UPaths.
- List of UPaths used to flag data differences: This is the list of UPaths that is used for the previous options.


To modify the set:

1. Do one of the following:
2. Use the following toolbar options, to modify the list of UPaths:
