<a id="data-comparator-interface"></a>

#### Data Comparator Interface

> 📄 Source: [Data_Comparator_Interface.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparator_Interface.htm)

##### Data Comparator Interface


The Data Comparator user interface consists of a double-pane format for each data file. The top pane displays the data structure for the data files being compared organized according to the referenced guideline. The status of the nodes depends on the [Comparison Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparator_Guideline_Comparison_Options.htm).


The Properties pane (at the bottom) displays the properties of the node selected in the Guideline pane and shows actual property differences.


*[Image: Data Comparator]*


Use the following toolbar options, to work with data files:


Click Previous or Next Difference (), to browse through the different data nodes skipping all other nodes.


Click View Guideline Trees (), to view guideline information.


 


Use the Guideline and Properties pane toolbar (in the middle) to work with differences found during the comparison process. 

- shows that the destination node is missing.

- shows that some attributes of the node are different.

- shows that the source node is missing.

- shows that some attributes and child nodes are different.

- shows that some child nodes are different.

---

<a id="data-comparator-properties-dialog-box"></a>

#### Data Comparator Properties Dialog Box

> 📄 Source: [Data_Comparator_Properties_Dialog_Box.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparator_Properties_Dialog_Box.htm)

##### Data Comparator Properties Dialog Box


You can use the Segment Properties dialog box to compare a node's property information in both the source and destination document side-by-side. It is useful to see this information when the Properties pane cannot completely show all information due to the space limitation. 


To launch the Segment Properties dialog box:

- In the Properties pane, double-click the property you want to view.
- Right-click the property and choose View Property Detail. The following dialog box is displayed:
- Use the Properties drop-down list to select the property to display.
- Use the Previous Difference and Next Difference at the bottom of the dialog box to move to the previous or next difference.

---

<a id="publish-reports"></a>

#### Publish Reports

> 📄 Source: [Publish_Reports.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Publish_Reports.htm)

##### Publish Reports


You can create a report showing the differences between source and target data file and you can print or publish it to HTML, RTF, PDF, or Word XML format.


To publish a report:

- On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click Publish, and then click HTML, RTF, PDF, WORD, EXCEl (for Migrator) or X12 Companion Guide.
- In the Save As dialog box, choose where you want to store your document and provide the file name, and then click Save.
- When asked if you want to view the published document, do one of the following:
- Click Yes to launch the appropriate program and view the exported document.
- Click No to go back to the application.

---

<a id="data-comparison-options"></a>

#### Data Comparison Options

> 📄 Source: [Book_Data_Comparison_Options.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Data_Comparison_Options.htm)

##### Data Comparison Options

---

<a id="data-comparison-options-overview"></a>

##### Data Comparison Options Overview

> 📄 Source: [Data_Comparison_Options_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparison_Options_Overview.htm)

###### Data Comparison Options Overview


There are two ways to set comparison options. You can define these options when going through the [Data Comparator wizard](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_The_Data_Comparator_Wizard.htm) or you can use the Data Comparison Configuration dialog box.


To set options by using Data Comparison Configuration dialog box:

1. On the Tools tab, click Options.
2. Click one of the following tabs, to define options for the particular section:


You can save the Data Comparison configuration as an .xml file for further usage in the Data Comparator wizard. You can also import a configuration file.


To save a configuration file:

1. In the Data Comparison Configuration dialog box, click Export Options ().
2. In the Export Options As dialog box, provide a file location and a file name.
3. Click Save.


To import configuration:

1. In the Data Comparison Configuration dialog box, click Import Options ().
2. In the Import Options From dialog box, navigate to the file you want to import.
3. Click Open.

---

<a id="data-comparison-tab"></a>

##### Data Comparison Tab

> 📄 Source: [Data_Comparison_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparison_Tab.htm)

###### Data Comparison Tab


Use this tab to change any of the data comparison options set in the [Data Comparator Data Comparison Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparator_Data_Comparison_Options.htm) wizard.


Use this wizard to set comparison mode. You can change or edit the list of the UPaths, which is used while comparison. Set Data Comparison Mode, to say Data Comparator if you want to compare or ignore the items linked to nodes with UPaths set in the list. For more information on how to do it, see [Data Comparator Data Comparison Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparator_Data_Comparison_Options.htm).

---

<a id="data-printing-tab"></a>

##### Data Printing Tab

> 📄 Source: [Data_Printing_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Printing_Tab.htm)

###### Data Printing Tab


Printing a report provides a useful way to analyze the differences between data files. The size of the report can be controlled by setting the Printing Options. 


To modify printing options:

1. On the Tools tab, click Options.
2. Click the Printing Options tab.
3. Check the items to print and clear the box for those that are not wanted on the printed report.
4. To change a Page Layout option, click one of the values and enter a new value or select one from the drop-down list.

---

<a id="comparison-tab"></a>

##### Comparison Tab

> 📄 Source: [Comparison_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Comparison_Tab.htm)

###### Comparison Tab


Use this tab to change any of the comparison options set in the [Guideline Comparison Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparator_Guideline_Comparison_Options.htm) wizard page. 


To set comparison options:

- On the Tools tab, click Options.
- Click the Data Comparison tab.


In this dialog box you control what properties to compare and which ones not to compare. You can also reduce the comparison report by indicating if comparison between two nodes should be ignored if both the source and destination nodes are excluded or missing. Or if either the source or destination is excluded or missing. For more information, see [Guideline Comparison Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparator_Guideline_Comparison_Options.htm).

---

<a id="match-tab"></a>

##### Match Tab

> 📄 Source: [Match_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Match_Tab.htm)

###### Match Tab


Use this tab to modify the options set in the [Data Comparator Guideline Match Options](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparator_Guideline_Match_Options.htm) wizard.


From the Matching tab, select the key attributes that Data Comparator uses to find matches. For example, if you want Data Comparator to establish a match when loops have the same ID, check the box marked ID under the Loop folder. If you want Data Comparator to match by name as well as ID, select both. You can also set Data Comparator to match text by case by checking the Case Sensitive box. For more information, see [Data Comparator Guideline Match Options.](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Data_Comparator_Guideline_Match_Options.htm)

<a id="search-and-replace"></a>

### Search and Replace

> 📄 Source: [Search_and_Replace.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Search_and_Replace.htm)

#### Search and Replace


To search and replace text in the data file:

1. Load the data file.
2. On the Data View toolbar, click Search/Replace ().
3. In the Data Search & Replace dialog box, in the Search for box, type the text to search for.
4. In the Replace with box, type the text that replaces the searched for text (optional).
5. Select the Direction from the current location to search.
6. Choose from the following options:

- Whole word: Searches for the entire word.
- Match case: Distinguishes between upper and lower case characters.
- Search around: Check to continue the search from the start when you reach the top or the bottom of the guideline.
- Use match patterns: This should be checked if the search is looking for text over multiple elements.  For example, if data file contains the following segments:

<a id="db-connector-overview"></a>

### DB Connector Overview

> 📄 Source: [DB_Connector_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/DB_Connector_Overview.htm)

#### DB Connector Overview


DB Connector is an import module, which performs an import that generates a guideline by database request. This guideline contains a database query which was used to generate a guideline and database connection settings.

---

<a id="import-from-a-database"></a>

### Import from a Database

> 📄 Source: [Import_from_Database.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_from_Database.htm)

#### Import from a Database


To import a file from a database:

1. On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click Import, and then click Import from a Database.
2. Click Next.
3. In the Database Connection dialog box, choose from the following options:
4. Click Test Connection to verify connection succeeds. Click Next.
5. In the Import Settings dialog box, select the scenario you want to use for data processing and provide the output file type.
6. Click Next.
7. The following options are available on the Import Settings dialog box toolbar:
8. Click Next.
9. The following options are available on the Specify SQL Statement dialog box toolbar:
10. Click Next.
11. In the Save Configuration dialog box, check the Save Configuration box, and type a file name or click Browse to navigate to the file location, and then click Next. You may skip this in case you don't want to save the configuration.
12. Click Next.
13. Click Finish to close the Import Wizard.


After completing this wizard by using the Database extraction scenario, you are able to retrieve data from database by using Data wizard. To launch the Data wizard, in the Analyzer / Data pane, click New Data ().

<a id="salesforce-connectivity"></a>

## Salesforce Connectivity

> 📄 Source: [Book_Salesforce_Connectivity.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Salesforce_Connectivity.htm)

### Salesforce Connectivity


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="salesforce-connectivity-overview"></a>

### Salesforce Connectivity Overview

> 📄 Source: [Salesforce_Connectivity_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Salesforce_Connectivity_Overview.htm)

#### Salesforce Connectivity Overview


Salesforce is a SaaS CRM (Customer Relationship Management) system that allows you to store data and develop applications in a cloud storage system.


The Salesforce official documentation says that Salesforce API objects represent database tables that contain information about your organization. For example, the central object in the Salesforce data model represents accounts - companies and organizations involved in your business, such as customers, partners, and competitors. The objects already created for you by Salesforce are called standard objects. The objects you create in your organization are called custom objects. The objects you create, that link to data stored outside of your organization are called external objects.


For detailed information on Salesforce API object concepts, click [here](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.api.meta/api/sforce_api_objects_concepts.htm).


SpecBuilder allows you to interact with [Salesforce Health Cloud](http://www.salesforce.com/industries/healthcare/health-cloud/) through the Salesforce import. To start working with the Salesforce, you should have an account at [www.salesforce.com](https://www.salesforce.com/). For more information on working with the Salesforce REST API, see the [official documentation](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.api_rest.meta/api_rest/).


You can also connect to your [Salesforce sandbox](https://help.salesforce.com/articleView?id=deploy_sandboxes_intro.htm&type=5), isolated from your production environment, to import your test data.


With SpecBuilder, you can create a guideline using objects retrieved from Salesforce. For more information, see [Import from Salesforce.](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_from_Salesforce.htm)

---

<a id="import-from-salesforce"></a>

### Import from Salesforce

> 📄 Source: [Import_from_Salesforce.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Import_from_Salesforce.htm)

#### Import from Salesforce


To import a file from Salesforce:

1. On the File tab, in the Backstage view, click Import, and then click Import from Salesforce.
2. In the Salesforce Connection Settings dialog box, enter the following Salesforce connection settings:
3. Click Finish to close the Import from Salesforce Wizard.


You can view and edit your Salesforce credentials, and view other settings used for the Salesforce import. For this, on the Home tab of the ribbon, in Salesforce, click Configuration. On the Authentication tab of the Salesforce Configuration dialog, you can view credentials used for Salesforce connection. On the Request tab of the Salesforce Configuration dialog, you can view an object and fields used for guideline generation if a guideline was created using the single object extraction, or the query you entered to create a guideline using a custom search query.


When you validate a guideline created using the Salesforce import, you can retrieve test data from Salesforce. For this, click the Data/Analyzer tab and in the Data Wizard, select Retrieve Data from Salesforce. In Object ID, provide an ID of an object to be extracted if a guideline is created by using a single object extraction.

<a id="external-code-lists"></a>

## External Code Lists

> 📄 Source: [Book_External_Code_Lists.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_External_Code_Lists.htm)

### External Code Lists


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="external-code-lists-overview"></a>

### External Code Lists Overview

> 📄 Source: [ECL_External_Code_Lists_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_External_Code_Lists_Overview.htm)

#### External Code Lists Overview


An external code list is a set of code values that are not included in the guideline but are referenced in the guideline through an external file. This external file contains all the code values and there can be multiple external files that are part of one external code list.


In SpecBuilder, [ECL Version 2.0](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Version_2.0.htm) is supported.


You should be aware of the following information regarding external code lists:

- External code lists cannot be mixed with standard code lists or composite code lists.
- Multiple external code lists are allowed.
- The name of the external code list must match the name of one of the registered code lists.
- Once an external code list has been added, its name cannot be changed since it is the key. You must delete the node and insert the proper external code list. The description of the code list can be changed.
- To validate that a value matches the external code list, you must write a rule by using either JavaScript or Java.
- Only one installed version of HIPAA database and SB Sample can be installed on a single computer.


Note Editing External Code Lists requires Java Runtime Engine 11 and properly configured the JAVA_HOME environment variable. If Java is not installed, External Code Lists is available for reading.

---

<a id="ecl-version-20"></a>

### ECL Version 2.0

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Version_2.0.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Version_2.0.htm)

#### ECL Version 2.0


Version 2.0 of external code lists becomes effective with SpecBuilder 6.7 and offers the following benefits:

- Code lists stored in a database repository
- System code lists separated from user code lists (updates to system code lists do not affect user code lists)
- Decreased disk usage
- Support for multiple user repositories
- The ability to copy code lists from one repository to another
- The ability to share repositories on a network
- The ability to assign effective start and end dates to each code
- The ability to assign Not Used dates to each code
- The ability to exclude/include codes as required
- The ability to register codes differently by trading partner - for example, DRG codes
- Monthly updates of system codes
- The ability to override system code list by user codes
- The ability to pick up updates to code lists without system restart


With ECL version 2.0, there are three types of code lists:

- System code lists - available to view only; cannot be modified
- SpecBuilder samples - available to view only; cannot be modified
- User - available for modification


When you install ECL Version 2.0, the following things occur:

- Old system codes are uninstalled and replaced with the updated version.
- If no user code lists exist, an empty user repository is created.


For more information about installing ECL Version 2.0, see [Install External Code Lists](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Install_External_Code_Lists.htm).

---

<a id="install-external-code-lists"></a>

### Install External Code Lists

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Install_External_Code_Lists.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Install_External_Code_Lists.htm)

#### Install External Code Lists


For Version 2.0 of External Code Lists, the multisetup program installs system external code lists for HIPAA.


To install the ECL Version 2.0 external code lists for HIPAA on Windows:

1. Navigate to the ECL2/ECL2_WIX subdirectory.
2. Double-click the Edifecs Standards database External Code Lists for HIPAA.msi file.
3. Download the External Code Lists (ECL) for HIPAA contained in the ECL2InstEdifecs.exe file.
4. Double-click the .exe file to extract the installation files.
5. Install the external code lists by typing the following command:
6. Do one of the following:
7. Click Next.
8. On the License Agreement dialog, review the license agreement and click I accept the terms in the license agreement, and then click Next.
9. On the Destination Folder dialog, click Change and navigate to the folder where you want to install the program (other than the default program location), and then click Next.
10. On the next wizard step, select if you want to setup the complete or custom database version, and then click Next.
11. Click Install to start the installation process.
12. Click Finish when the process is completed.


To install the ECL Version 2.0 external code lists for HIPAA on a UNIX system:

1. Navigate to the ECL2/ECL2_Perl_Unix subdirectory.
2. Download the External Code Lists (ECL) for HIPAA contained in the ECL2InstEdifecs.tar.gz file.
3. Decompress the file from the downloaded distribution archive by executing the following command:
4. Install external code lists by typing the following command:
5. Depending on your installation options, restart the command shell.


###### ECL Installer specifics


Starting from the version 9.2.31, the ECL installer automatically detects the H2 database version of the installed SpecBuilder and XEngine, and:

- If any of these versions is 9.3.3 or later, the ECL installer migrates the H2 database to the version 2.2.222.
- If any of these versions is 9.2.4 or later (but both earlier than 9.3.3), the ECL installer migrates the H2 database to the version 2.1.210.
- If both XEngine or SpecBuilder are not installed or both are older than version 9.2.4, the ECL installer doesn't migrate anything.


###### Updating Items from the Repository


Any items of the projects can be updated from the repository. To do this, on the Project tab of the ribbon, click Update from Repository ().

---

<a id="migrate-code-lists"></a>

### Migrate Code Lists

> 📄 Source: [Migrate_Code_Lists.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Migrate_Code_Lists.htm)

#### Migrate Code Lists


In SpecBuilder 9.3.3 the H2 database has been upgraded to version 2.2.224 to address the vulnerability reported in [CVE-2022-45868](https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2022-45868). If you are upgrading SpecBuilder to 9.3.3, migrate the External Code Lists (ECL).


**Note** As a result of not migrating the External Code Lists, the following error can occur when browsing the External Code Lists or when data analysis is performed: *Caused by: org.h2.mvstore.MVStoreException: The read format 2 is smaller than the supported format 3 [2.2.224/5]*


**To migrate the External Code Lists:**

- [Update](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Install_External_Code_Lists.htm) the system External Code Lists to the version 9.3.10;
- Migrate the user External Code Lists using the H2 Migration Tool available in the [Edifecs Library](https://repository.edifecsfedcloud.com/#/storage/library/Tools/Administration/Edifecs H2 Migration Tool/9.3.3 and later). For the External Code Lists migration, use one of the following scripts:

 [Copy](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/javascript:void(0);)

```
run.bat -mode ecl -migration-report migration-report.csv -scan-folder "%ECRootPath%\Common\UserECLDB\jECL"
:: or
migrate-ecl.bat "%ECRootPath%\Common\UserECLDB\jECL\"
```






where


**"%ECRootPath%\Common\UserECLDB\jECL"** – represents the path to the user ECL repository. A custom path can also be used, e.g., **"C:\UserRepository\jECL"**.


For more information on the H2 Migration Tool, click [here](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/Library/Content/Edifecs_Library/H2_Migration_Tool.htm).


**Note** There's also the possibility to migrate the user External Code Lists automatically while performing SpecBuilder installation by checking the **Migrate User External Code Lists** checkbox in the **Ready to Install the Program** window.

---

<a id="explore-the-workspace"></a>

### Explore the Workspace

> 📄 Source: [Book_Explore_the_Workspace_ECL.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Explore_the_Workspace_ECL.htm)

#### Explore the Workspace ECL


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="external-code-lists-browser-overview"></a>

#### External Code Lists Browser Overview

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Browser_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Browser_Overview.htm)

##### External Code Lists Browser Overview


The External Code Lists browser displays all the repositories, code lists, and codes registered in SpecBuilder in a tree in the left pane. Properties for a selected item are shown in the right pane.


Note You cannot view the old version of user lists in this browser.


External code lists are supported for NCPDP Batch, NCPDP Telecommunication, and NCPDP Medical Subrogation standards. You can also define a new standard for the code lists. For more information about how to define standard, see [Registered Standards tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Code_List_Registered_Standards_tab.htm).


To launch the code lists browser:

- Launch SpecBuilder, and on the Advanced tab, in the Environment group, click External Code Lists.


You can browse through the code and code lists on the left pane to navigate to the item you want to view or edit. The properties of the selected item are displayed on the right pane. The following tabs are shown depending on what item is selected in the tree:

- [Repository Properties Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Repository_Properties_Tab.htm)
- [Code Lists Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Code_Lists_Tab.htm)
- [Codes Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Codes_Tab.htm)
- [Code List Properties Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Code_List_Properties_Tab.htm)
- [Code List Criteria Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Code_List_Criteria_Tab.htm)
- [Code List Trading Partners Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Code_List_Trading_Partners_Tab.htm)

---

<a id="repository-properties-tab"></a>

#### Repository Properties Tab

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Repository_Properties_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Repository_Properties_Tab.htm)

##### Repository Properties Tab


Use this tab to set repository properties.


You can view the following items:

- Path: Displays the path to the folder where the codes are stored.
- Name: The repository name.
- Registration type: The name you provide when registering a repository.
- Version: Code lists version (for system use only).
- Default: Shows if the repository is set as default.


For more information on how to set repository properties, see [Set Repository Properties](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Set_Repository_Properties.htm).

---

<a id="code-lists-tab"></a>

#### Code Lists Tab

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Code_Lists_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Code_Lists_Tab.htm)

##### Code Lists Tab


Use this tab to view system code lists or view and edit user code lists stored within the selected repository. Double-click the code list, to view its properties.


The following properties are available:

- Enabled: The box is checked if the code list is enabled.
- Name: Displays the name of the code list.
- Description: Displays the code list description.
- TP Key: Displays trading partner key.

---

<a id="codes-tab"></a>

#### Codes Tab

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Codes_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Codes_Tab.htm)

##### Codes Tab


Use this tab to view system codes in a list and their properties. You can view and edit user codes.


The following properties are available:

- Index: Displays code index.
- Include/Exclude status: Displays code status.
- ID: Displays code Identification number.
- Name: Displays code name.
- Start Date: Displays the start date when the code is effective. For more information about how to set date range, see [Date Range Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Date_Range_Tab.htm).
- End Date: Displays the end date by which the code is effective. For more information about how to set date range, see [Date Range Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Date_Range_Tab.htm).
- Details: Yes displays in this column for the codes that contain additional information such as User notes and Date Range and No when there is no additional information.

---

<a id="code-list-properties-tab"></a>

#### Code List Properties Tab

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Code_List_Properties_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Code_List_Properties_Tab.htm)

##### Code List Properties Tab


Use this tab to view system code list properties or view and edit user code list properties.


The following properties are available:

- Name: Code list name.
- Description: Code list description.
- Comments: Code list comments.
- Standard: Code list standard.
- Version: Code list version.
- Source Type: Code list source type.
- Source: Code list source filename.
- Override Type: Your code list can be used in place of the system code list or this code list can extend the system list.
- Enabled: Shows if the code list is enabled or disabled.
- Cache Type: The following cache types are available:
- No Cache: The system reads the database each time to check for the code.
- Full (default): All codes are cached in memory at the time that XEServer is started/restarted.
- Refreshable: Codes are cached but the system checks for any updates and reload the codes.
- Licensing Type: Shows if this ECL property can be viewed by user according to license agreement terms with maintaining organization.

---

<a id="code-list-criteria-tab"></a>

#### Code List Criteria Tab

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Code_List_Criteria_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Code_List_Criteria_Tab.htm)

##### Code List Criteria Tab


With the Criteria tab, you can add or display information about the selected code's category or subcategory. This is used when the same code list is used for various types of codes - for example, system code list 5 that is used for countries currencies and subdivisions of countries.


The following options are available:

- Click Commit () to save your changes to the Code list.
- Click Reset () to reset your recent changes.
- Click Add () to add a new criteria to the list.
- Click Delete () to remove the selected criteria from the list.

---

<a id="code-list-trading-partners-tab"></a>

#### Code List Trading Partners Tab

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Code_List_Trading_Partners_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Code_List_Trading_Partners_Tab.htm)

##### Code List Trading Partners Tab


With the Trading Partners tab, you can add or display the Trading Partners associated with the code list.


The following options are available: 

- Click Commit () to save your changes to the Code list.
- Click Reset () to reset your recent changes.
- Click Add () to add a new Trading Partner Name to the list.
- Click Delete () to remove the selected Trading Partner Name from the list.


Note Trading Partner Name should be the TPName referenced in the XEngine selector file.

---

<a id="code-list-registered-standards-tab"></a>

#### Code List Registered Standards Tab

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Code_List_Registered_Standards_tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Code_List_Registered_Standards_tab.htm)

##### Code List Registered Standards Tab


Use this tab to manage registered standards.


The following options are available:

- Click Add () to add a new standard to the list.
- Click Delete () to remove the selected standard from the list.
- Click Edit () to edit the selected standard in the list.

---

<a id="code-list-repositories"></a>

### Code List Repositories

> 📄 Source: [Book_Code_List_Repositories.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Code_List_Repositories.htm)

#### Code List Repositories


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="code-list-repositories-overview"></a>

#### Code List Repositories Overview

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Code_List_Repositories_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Code_List_Repositories_Overview.htm)

##### Code List Repositories Overview


Repositories are special containers, where code lists are stored. They are displayed in the tree view on the left pane of the External Code List. All repositories are grouped by their registration type:

- System
- SpecBuilder Sample
- User


You can have several repositories registered for one type. One repository of the same type can be set as default. Only repositories marked as Default are used for validation.


To set the default repository :

- In the left pane of the browser, click the repository you want to be used as default.
- In the right pane, on the toolbar, click Set as Default ().


The Default status is automatically removed from any other repository of the same registration type.


You can perform the following actions with repositories:

- [Create a repository](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Create_a_Repository.htm)
- [Set repository properties](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Set_Repository_Properties.htm)
- [Register a repository](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Register_a_Repository.htm)
- [Unregister a repository](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Unregister_a_Repository.htm)
- [Delete a repository](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Delete_a_Repository.htm)

---

<a id="create-a-repository"></a>

#### Create a Repository

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Create_a_Repository.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Create_a_Repository.htm)

##### Create a Repository


You may have one local repository for system code lists and multiple remote system code repositories. You can have as many local or remote repositories for user code lists as you wish.


When you create a new repository , it is registered at the same time.


To create a new user repository :

1. Right-click on a user repository , and then click Create New User Repository .
2. In the Name box, type a name for the new repository.
3. In the Folder box, type a folder name or click Browse to navigate to the location of the repository .
4. If you want to make this repository default, click Set as Default.
5. Click OK.

---

<a id="set-repository-properties"></a>

#### Set Repository Properties

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Set_Repository_Properties.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Set_Repository_Properties.htm)

##### Set Repository Properties


After you have registered a repository , you can set its properties. Use the [Properties tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Code_List_Properties_Tab.htm) to modify properties for the repository . This tab displays properties for the currently selected repository .


*[Image: External Code List Properties tab]*


To set repository properties:

1. In the left pane, click the repository name. The Properties tab is displayed.
2. On the toolbar in the right pane, do one of the following:

---

<a id="register-a-repository"></a>

#### Register a Repository

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Register_a_Repository.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Register_a_Repository.htm)

##### Register a Repository


When a new repository is created, the repository is automatically registered.


However, you may want to re-register a repository that was unregistered or created on a different machine.


To register a repository :

1. Right-click any of the repositories, and then click Register Repository .
2. In the Name box, type the name of the repository to register.
3. In the Folder box, type the name of the folder where the repository was created, or click Browse to navigate to the location of the repository .
4. If you want this repository to be used as the default, click Set as Default.
5. Click OK.

---

<a id="unregister-a-repository"></a>

#### Unregister a Repository

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Unregister_a_Repository.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Unregister_a_Repository.htm)

##### Unregister a Repository


If you do not want to use a repository , but do not want to delete it, you can unregister it. XEngine does not use any of the codes in the repository to validate a file.


To unregister a repository :

1. Right-click the name of the repository that you want to unregister.
2. Click Unregister Repository .
3. Click Yes to confirm that you want to unregister the repository or No if you do not.

---

<a id="delete-a-repository"></a>

#### Delete a Repository

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Delete_a_Repository.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Delete_a_Repository.htm)

##### Delete a Repository


When a new repository is created and registered you may want to delete one of the registered user repositories.


To delete a repository:

- Right-click any of the repositories, and then click Delete User Repository .
- Click Yes in the Confirmation screen.

---

<a id="external-code-lists"></a>

### External Code Lists

> 📄 Source: [Book_Code_Lists.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Code_Lists.htm)

#### External Code Lists


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="create-external-code-lists"></a>

#### Create External Code Lists

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Create_External_Code_Lists.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Create_External_Code_Lists.htm)

##### Create External Code Lists


Import codes from an external source Reuse another external code list Create an empty code list Create placeholder


There are two ways to create an external code list:

- By using the external code lists browser.
- By creating your own XML or CSV file containing the external codes and [importing it](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Import_External_Code_Lists.htm). This method is useful for those application developers who already have the content of external code list in an existing database or file and want to use it with SpecBuilder.


To create an external code list by using the external code list browser:

1. On the Advanced tab, in the Environment group, click External Code Lists.
2. In the External Code Lists tree in the left pane, expand User Code Lists.
3. Right-click a user code list and select Add New Code List.
4. Enter a Name, Description, and Version for the code list.
5. In the Standard box, select one of the defined registered standards. The following standards are defined by default: HIPAA, NCPDP Batch, NCPDP Telecommunication, or NCPDP Medical Subrogation. For more information about how to define standards, see [Registered Standards tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Code_List_Registered_Standards_tab.htm).
6. In the Cache Type box, select one of the following types of caching:
7. If you want your code list to be used in place of the system code list to validate files, click Override System. Otherwise, this code list extends the system list.
8. Add a comment, if appropriate.
9. Click Next.
10. On the Specify Criteria for the Code List wizard page, click Add () to add a name/value pair of criteria to the code list. To delete a criteria, click Delete ( ).
11. On the Specify Trading Partner Information for the Code List wizard page, click Add () to add a Trading Partner Name to the code list. To delete a trading partner, click Delete ().
12. On the Specify Source Type for the Code List wizard page, choose one of the following options:
13. Click Next to import the codes or create an empty code list.

---

<a id="modify-code-properties"></a>

#### Modify Code Properties

> 📄 Source: [Book_Modify_Code_Properties.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Modify_Code_Properties.htm)

##### Modify Code Properties


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="modify-external-code-properties"></a>

##### Modify External Code Properties

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Modify_External_Code_Properties.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Modify_External_Code_Properties.htm)

###### Modify External Code Properties


You can modify code's name, data range, user notes and other properties by using the View Code screen.


To modify code properties:

1. In the code list do one of the following:
2. Modify one of the following:
3. Click the Date Range tab, to set the date range for the code. For more information, see [Date Range Tab.](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Date_Range_Tab.htm)
4. Click OK.

---

<a id="date-range-tab"></a>

##### Date Range Tab

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Date_Range_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Date_Range_Tab.htm)

###### Date Range Tab


Use this tab to set date ranges for the code.


Note If there is no date in the code, it means that the code is valid for any date.


*[Image: Date Range tab]*


 


Note The Date Ranges tab is available for HIPAA code lists only.


To add a date range:

1. Click Add (), to add a new date range for the code.
2. Double-click in the Type box to select the type (Effective or Not Used) of date range. Effective is the default.
3. Type a Start Date and End Date for the code to be valid in MM/DD/YYYY format or double-click in the boxes to choose the dates from the calendar.


Note The Not Used date range takes precedence over the Effective date range.

---

<a id="notes-tab"></a>

##### Notes Tab

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Notes_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Notes_Tab.htm)

###### Notes Tab


The Notes tab shows the user notes for codes. Typically the user notes store specific implementation details for the code that are company- or industry-specific. 


*[Image: Notes tab]*


You can [add new notes and edit existing](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Add_or_Edit_a_User_Note.htm) by using this tab.

---

<a id="versions-tab"></a>

##### Versions Tab

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Versions_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Versions_Tab.htm)

###### Versions Tab


Use this tab to specify the version range for the data validation.


*[Image: Versions tab]*


Note The Versions and Rules tabs are available for NCPDP code lists only.


To add a version range:

1. On the Versions tab, click Add (Ins) () to add an Effective version.
2. Double-click in the Start Version or End Version columns to select the version from the drop-down menu.
3. Click Delete () to remove a version range.
4. Click OK.

---

<a id="rules-tab"></a>

##### Rules Tab

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Rules_Tab.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Rules_Tab.htm)

###### Rules Tab


Use this tab to add a rule to the code.


*[Image: Rules tab]*


Note The Versions and Rules tabs are available for NCPDP code lists only.


To create a new rule:

1. Click New () to open the Create a Rule dialog box.
2. In the Rule drop-down menu, select the type of rule you want to create.
3. Specify the parameters of a new rule instance in the Field ID field.
4. Click OK.


Note Only Field Check and Field Version Check rule types are currently available.

---

<a id="work-with-external-code-lists"></a>

#### Work with External Code Lists

> 📄 Source: [Book_Work_with_External_Code_Lists.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Work_with_External_Code_Lists.htm)

##### Work with External Code Lists


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="create-a-new-code"></a>

##### Create a New Code

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Create_a_New_Code.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Create_a_New_Code.htm)

###### Create a New Code


Click here to see the instructions about how to add a new code for the NCPDP Telecommunication standard.


When working with user code lists, you can add codes to the selected code lists.


To add a new code:

1. Click Lock to lock the code list for editing.
2. Click New () to open the New Code Wizard.
3. In the ID box, type an ID for the new code.
4. Click Excluded to be able to override a single code from the system code list.
5. Click Next.
6. On the Specify New Code Properties wizard page, in the Name box, type a name for the code.
7. On the Date Ranges tab, click Add (Ins) to add an Effective or Not Used date range. For more information, see [Date Range Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Date_Range_Tab.htm).
8. Double-click in the Type box to choose Effective or Not Used.
9. Double-click in the Start Date or End Date columns to enter the dates.
10. Click Delete () to remove a date range.
11. Click the Notes tab to add notes about the code.
12. Click New local user note () to add a new note.
13. In the Description column, type a name for the note.
14. In the right pane, type the text of the note.
15. To switch to rich text editing, click the chevron on the right, and then click Rich Text. With this, you can add additional formatting for notes.
16. Continue adding as many notes as you need.
17. To edit a note, click in the right pane and make the required changes.
18. To change the description, click the description to change, and then click Rename ( ). Type the new description.
19. Click Finish to add the code.
20. On the [Codes Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Codes_Tab.htm) toolbar, click Commit () to save your changes for this code list.

 Click here to see the instructions about how to add a new code for the NCPDP Telecommunication standard.


To add a new code:

1. Click Lock to lock the code list for editing.
2. Click New () to open the New Code Wizard.
3. In the ID box, type an ID for the new code.
4. Click Excluded to allow overrides for single codes from the system code list.
5. Click Next.
6. On the Specify New Code Properties wizard page, in the Name, type a name for the code.
7. On the Versions tab, click Add (Ins) () to add an Effective version. For more information, see [Versions Tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Versions_Tab.htm).
8. Double-click in the Start Version or End Version columns to select the version from the drop-down menu.
9. Click Delete () to remove a version range.
10. Click the Notes tab to add notes about the code.
11. Click New local user note () to add a new note.
12. In the Description column, type a name for the note.
13. In the right pane, type the text of the note.
14. To switch to rich text editing, click the chevron on the right, and then click Rich Text. With this, you can add additional formatting for notes.
15. Continue adding as many notes as you need.
16. To edit a note, click in the right pane and make the required changes.
17. To change the description, click the description you want to change, and then click Rename ( ). Type the new description.
18. Click Rules tab to create a new rule.
19. Click New () to open the Create a Rule dialog box.
20. In the Rule drop-down menu, select a type of the rule you want to create.
21. Specify parameters of a new rule instance in the Field ID field.
22. Click OK.
23. Click Finish to add the code.

---

<a id="browse-external-code-lists"></a>

##### Browse External Code Lists

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Browse_External_Code_Lists.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Browse_External_Code_Lists.htm)

###### Browse External Code Lists


You can view [external code lists](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_External_Code_Lists_Overview.htm) that have been registered.


Note You cannot view any old versions of code lists.

1. On the Advanced tab, click External Code Lists.


The External Code Lists window displays the following information:


*[Image: External Code Lists browser]*

- Registered code lists is displayed in the left pane.
- There can be three types of external code lists:
- System -  Provided with the product and cannot be modified.
- SpecBuilder Sample - Sample code lists.
- User - Lists that you create and that you can add to or modify.
- If multiple code lists are present, they can be viewed by expanding the tree selection.
- Properties associated with code repositories, code lists, or codes are displayed in the right pane.

---

<a id="lockunlock-external-code-lists"></a>

##### Lock/Unlock External Code Lists

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Lock_Unlock_External_Code_Lists.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Lock_Unlock_External_Code_Lists.htm)

###### Lock/Unlock External Code Lists


Use the Lock option to edit codes. This option locks all code lists in the repository or one particular code list for editing. Other users are able to view this code list and use it for validation but they are not able to make the changes while the code list is marked as locked.


To lock codes:

1. On the Codes tree, expand the User Code Lists repository.
2. Click the code list or code you want to edit.
3. On the [Code List Properties tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Code_List_Properties_Tab.htm), click Lock ().


You have to unlock codes after you finished editing them. Otherwise, other users are not able to edit the locked code.


To unlock codes:

1. On the left pane, expand the User Code Lists repository.
2. Click the code list or code you locked.
3. On the [Code List Properties tab](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Code_List_Properties_Tab.htm), click Clear Lock For All Code Lists ().


Note Only the user who sets the lock can remove it.

---

<a id="filter-external-code-lists"></a>

##### Filter External Code Lists

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Filter_External_Code_Lists.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Filter_External_Code_Lists.htm)

###### Filter External Code Lists


When working with codes you can use filter the list to easily find codes you need.


To filter codes:

1. Click the repository that contains the necessary codes and expand the codes you need.
2. On the toolbar provide the text or value that you want to use as a filter in the Filter box.

---

<a id="search-external-code-lists"></a>

##### Search External Code Lists

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Search_External_Code_Lists.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Search_External_Code_Lists.htm)

###### Search External Code Lists


You can use search to find a particular code or code list.


To perform searching:

1. On the code list tree, right-click a code list or code, and then click Find.
2. In the Find what box, type the text to search for.
3. Click Match case to match the case of the text when searching.
4. Click Find Next to begin the search and to continue searching.
5. Click Close to stop searching.


All code lists and codes containing the provided text are displayed.


Every external code list or code set is identified by name and a set of criteria. Name is set up based on standards specifications. Criteria is used when code set has multiple types of codes.


Validation is proceeded basing on the name and criteria parameters within JavaScript rules. Every code within a specific code set has one or more effective date ranges.


When validation is completed, code and the date within date range are found in the specific code list. If they are not found, code is considered to be invalid. If code doesn't have any assigned date range, it mean that it is valid for any date.


If there are user defined external code lists registered in the system, validation is done for user defined code list first, and then for the system code list.

---

<a id="include-external-code-lists"></a>

##### Include External Code Lists

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Include_External_Code_Lists.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Include_External_Code_Lists.htm)

###### Include External Code Lists


To insert external code lists into an EDI guideline document:

1. In the Guideline pane, click the element where the new code list is assigned.
2. Right-click the element, point to Insert Child, and then click External Code List.
3. On the Welcome to the New Child Node Wizard wizard page, click one of the following, and then click Next:
4. After you have inserted an external code list you can view the codes and properties in the right pane in the External Code List section.


Note Adding external code list to the guideline does not make it be used for validation. You need to create JavaScript or Java rule for validation.

---

<a id="disable-external-code-lists"></a>

##### Disable External Code Lists

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Disable_External_Code_Lists.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Disable_External_Code_Lists.htm)

###### Disable External Code Lists


You can disable a user code list to prevent the validation engine from checking the data value against that specific code list.


Note While you can only disable a user code list, you can effectively disable a system code list by creating an empty user code list with the same name and placeholder source type.


To disable a code list:

1. On the Advanced tab, click External Code Lists.
2. In the External Code Lists dialog box, browse to the user code list you want to disable.
3. Right-click the list and choose Disable Code List.

---

<a id="copy-an-external-code-list-to-another-repository"></a>

##### Copy an External Code List to Another Repository

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Copy_an_External_Code_List_to_Another_Repository.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Copy_an_External_Code_List_to_Another_Repository.htm)

###### Copy an External Code List to Another Repository


When working with external code lists, you can copy the registry and all code lists to a different repository. This can be used to copy repositories from a test to a production system. 


To copy code lists to another repository:

1. On the left pane, click the repository you are working in.
2. On the toolbar in the right pane, click Copy to ().
3. In the Target Repository drop-down list, click the repository where you want to copy code lists. The drop-down list shows all user repositories registered in the Standards Registry.
4. In the Mode box, select one of the following:
5. Click Unlock Target Repository to unlock the repository and all code lists in it from editing while copying.
6. Click OK to begin copying.


Note You can copy just one code list.

---

<a id="copy-an-external-code-list-from-another-repository"></a>

##### Copy an External Code List from Another Repository

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Copy_an_External_Code_List_from_Another_Repository.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Copy_an_External_Code_List_from_Another_Repository.htm)

###### Copy an External Code List from Another Repository


When working with external code lists, you can copy the registry and all code lists from a different repository to the current one. This can be used to copy repositories from a test to a production system.


To copy code lists from another Repository :

1. In the left pane, click the repository you are working in.
2. On the toolbar in the right pane, click Get From ().


The Get Copy of External Code Lists from Source Repository screen is displayed:


*[Image: Get Copy of External Code Lists from Source Repository]*

1. In the Source Repository drop-down list, click the repository you want to get code lists from. The drop-down list shows all user repositories registered in the Standards Registry.
2. In the Mode box, select one of the following:

- Merge with Current Repository: Click to merge code lists. If the source repository has code lists that are not in the target, they are added. If the target repository has code lists that are not in the source repository , those code lists are retained.
- Replace Current Repository: Click to replace all contents of the repository .

1. Click Lock Source Repository to lock the repository and all code lists in it from editing while copying.
2. Click OK to begin copying.

---

<a id="importexport-code-lists"></a>

#### Import/Export Code Lists

> 📄 Source: [Book_Import_Export_Code_Lists.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Import_Export_Code_Lists.htm)

##### Import/Export Code Lists


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="import-external-code-lists"></a>

##### Import External Code Lists

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Import_External_Code_Lists.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Import_External_Code_Lists.htm)

###### Import External Code Lists


There are two ways of importing code lists:

- [Import External Code Lists from an XML File](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Import_External_Code_Lists_From_an_XML_File.htm)
- [Import External Code Lists from a CSV File](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Import_External_Code_Lists_from_a_CSV_File.htm)


CSV import is not fully supported for non-HIPAA standards.


Import from an XML file for non-HIPAA standards could be performed only if code list standard and exported standard in the XML file are the same and have the same versions.


You can import external code lists when creating a new code list. For more information, see [Create External Code Lists](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Create_External_Code_Lists.htm).

---

<a id="import-external-code-lists-from-an-xml-file"></a>

##### Import External Code Lists from an XML File

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Import_External_Code_Lists_From_an_XML_File.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Import_External_Code_Lists_From_an_XML_File.htm)

###### Import External Code Lists from an XML File


Sample code list XML File:


Note Import from an XML file for non-HIPAA standards could be performed only if code list standard and exported standard in the XML file are the same and have the same versions.


To import an external code list from an XML file:

1. Create an XML file containing the codes to import. The file must comply with the following XSD:
2. To import the file, right-click the repository name, and then click Import Code Lists.
3. Navigate to the location of the XML file, and then click Open.


The codes are imported into the list.


Note It is recommended that you validate your XML code list file and the registry file (standardsregistry.xml) before using them. Invalid XML files can cause the external code list lookup to fail.

---

<a id="import-external-code-lists-from-a-csv-file"></a>

##### Import External Code Lists from a CSV File

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Import_External_Code_Lists_from_a_CSV_File.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Import_External_Code_Lists_from_a_CSV_File.htm)

###### Import External Code Lists from a CSV File


Note CSV import is not fully supported for non-HIPAA standards.


To import an external code list from a CSV file: 

1. Create a .csv file containing the codes to import. The first string must contain layout for the file.
2. Open the External Code Lists browser from the **Advanced** tab: *Skip to step 7 if there are existing Code Lists in the repository. To Add New Code List using the Import from CSV feature, right-click on a Code Lists library and select **Add New Code List**: Alternately, use the button. The New Code List Wizard is displayed. To create a new Code List entry, fill in the required fields, define a set of Criteria and specify a Trading Partner for the Code List by clicking **Next**. To import the CSV file, in the Specify Source Type for the Code List* window select the **CSV File** source for the **Import codes from external source** option and click **Next**: *Select the source file by navigating to the location of the .csv file and click **Next**. The new codes are added. To use the Import from CSV feature on existing Code Lists, click on the Code List, Lock the codes, right-click anywhere in the right pane, and then click Import from CSV: **Note** The Codes have to be Locked* first for the **Import from CSV** option to be available. The Import Wizard is displayed.
3. Click Next.
4. On the Choose source for import screen, navigate to the location of the .csv file, and then click Next.
5. On the Export Configuration screen, do one of the following: - Click New configuration to set configuration options. - Click Existing configuration to select an existing configuration file. Click Browse to navigate to the location of the configuration file. Click Customize to change settings in the existing configuration file. Skip to step 13 if you don't want to customize your configuration file.
6. Click Next.
7. Select columns you want to be imported, and then click Next.
8. If you want to save your configuration settings, select the Save configuration check box and provide the configuration file name and location.
9. Click Next, to import the Code List.

---

<a id="export-external-code-lists-to-an-xml-file"></a>

##### Export External Code Lists to an XML File

> 📄 Source: [ECL_Export_External_Code_Lists_to_an_XML_File.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/ECL_Export_External_Code_Lists_to_an_XML_File.htm)

###### Export External Code Lists to an XML File


You can export user code lists to a file with a .xml extension.


To export a code list to xml:

1. On the left pane, right-click the code list you want to export, and then click Export to XML. The Export wizard is displayed.
2. If you are exporting a single code list, then provide the name for the exported file.
3. If you want to export the whole code list repository, provide name and location for the folder to export file in it, and then click Next.

<a id="smart-trading-cloud-repository"></a>

## Smart Trading Cloud Repository

> 📄 Source: [Book_Smart_Trading_Cloud_Repository.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Smart_Trading_Cloud_Repository.htm)

### Smart Trading Cloud Repository


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="smart-trading-cloud-repository-overview"></a>

### Smart Trading Cloud Repository Overview

> 📄 Source: [Smart_Trading_Cloud_Repository_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Smart_Trading_Cloud_Repository_Overview.htm)

#### Smart Trading Cloud Repository Overview


Smart Trading Cloud Repository is a solution that allows you to store your files in the cloud reliably and securely. If you need to access your files directly from your run-time environment from different locations, and if you do not want to store your working files locally, use the Smart Trading Cloud Repository that is fully integrated with SpecBuilder and allows you to save and access your files any time directly from SpecBuilder without having to copy your data or create backups. Smart Trading Cloud Repository gives you privacy and data integrity during transfers to and from your servers.


With the Smart Trading Cloud Repository, you can improve your team work and facilitate coordinated development of your artifacts in a team as well as move the artifacts from one environment to another. The Smart Trading Cloud Repository ensures you have your artifacts up-to-date and always available.


To use the Smart Trading Cloud Repository in SpecBuilder, you only need a web browser and an account at [https://repository.edifecsfedcloud.com](https://repository.edifecsfedcloud.com/). For more information, see [Work with Smart Trading Cloud Repository](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/SB_Work_with_Smart_Trading_Cloud_Repository.htm).

---

<a id="quick-start"></a>

### Quick Start

> 📄 Source: [Quick_Start.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Quick_Start.htm)

#### Quick Start


The following case study demonstrates the Smart Trading Cloud Repository opportunities to make your work with guideline documents easy and less complicated.


To save a guideline to the Smart Trading Cloud Repository:

1. Create a new guideline in SpecBuilder. For more information, see [Create a New Guideline Document](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Create_a_New_Guideline_Document.htm).
2. To save your guideline to repository, click File, click Save, and then click Save to Cloud. The Account Settings dialog box is displayed.
3. In the Account Settings dialog box, enter your Login and Password, and then click Test connection to verify your connection.
4. Click OK.
5. In the Save to Cloud dialog, in Path, navigate to the location where you want to save your guideline and enter the guideline's name.
6. Click Save.


The next time you need to make changes to your guideline, you can open it directly from the repository, modify it, and save it in the repository.


Before you start working with the repository, make sure you are signed in.


To open a guideline document from the Smart Trading Cloud Repository:

1. Click File, click Open, and then click Open from Cloud.
2. Navigate to the guideline document you saved previously, and then click Open.
3. Make the required changes to your guideline file, click File, and then click Save. Smart Trading Cloud automatically saves the changes.


You can access a guideline saved in the Smart Trading Cloud Repository from multiple locations without having to create copies.

---

<a id="work-with-smart-trading-cloud-repository"></a>

### Work with Smart Trading Cloud Repository

> 📄 Source: [SB_Work_with_Smart_Trading_Cloud_Repository.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/SB_Work_with_Smart_Trading_Cloud_Repository.htm)

#### Work with the Smart Trading Cloud Repository


To open, save, and access your files in the Smart Trading Cloud Repository, you must have an account and be able to sign in using your credentials.


Note If you have enabled the multi-factor authentication or SAML Federation for your Smart Trading Account, SpecBuilder cannot connect to Smart Trading Cloud using these authentication mechanisms at the moment. The support for the new authentication modes will be added to SpecBuilder within upcoming hotfixes.


To set the Smart Trading Cloud Repository:

1. Click the File tab, click Account, and then click Account Settings.
2. Enter your Login and Password, if you already have an existing account or if you created one during the SpecBuilder installation.
3. Click Test connection to verify your connection settings.
4. Click OK.


To switch to another Smart Trading Cloud account, click the File tab, click Account, and then click Account Settings and provide another credentials.


To save files to the repository:

1. Open a document you want to save to the Smart Trading Cloud Repository.
2. Click the File tab, click Save to save changes, and then click Save to Cloud.
3. On the Save As dialog box, in the left pane, select the location where you want to save your file, and then click Save to save the file in the repository.


To open files from the repository:

1. Click the File tab, click Open, and then click Open from Cloud.
2. On the Open from Cloud dialog box, select the file you want to upload, and then click Open.


**To set a domain for Cloud Solutions integration**:

1. Go to: ${ECRootPath}/Common
2. In the cloud.properties file, comment out the line containing the domain you don't want to use: Note Switching back to CollabLynk environment is not recommended as it will be completely replaced with FedRAMP in future.
3. Restart your computer.


When you save changes you have made to the document that you opened from the repository, Smart Trading Cloud commits your changes automatically to the repository.


When you configure your maps and save them to the repository, the corresponding to maps guidelines, which are stored in the repository, become available and the paths to them are relative. Guidelines that are stored on your local drive remain unavailable and their paths are absolute.


The Smart Trading Cloud Repository is integrated to the SpecBuilder core components, that allows you to directly work with the SpecBuilder documents stored in the repository, such as guidelines, maps, tag schemas, X12 companion guides, and error severity configurations. You can also process files you store in the repository with Analyzer and Translator.


To make your work with the Smart Trading Cloud Repository more convenient, SpecBuilder offers a shell extension option that extends the abilities of the Windows operating system. It represents a tree-structured hierarchy of the repository and allows you to interact with the files you store in the repository directly from SpecBuilder. When you create the Smart Trading Cloud Repository account, the Smart Trading Cloud folder () is available in the File Explorer and in the Open and Save dialogs of SpecBuilder (or other third-party software, such as Microsoft Word, Excel, PDF viewers and editors, text editors, EDI and non-EDI software) that allows you to navigate to files located in the repository. For more information, view [Smart Trading Cloud Shell Extension Overview](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Smart_Trading_Cloud_Shell_Extension_Overview.htm).

---

<a id="smart-trading-cloud-shell-extension"></a>

### Smart Trading Cloud Shell Extension

> 📄 Source: [Book_Smart_Trading_Cloud_Shell_Extension.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Book_Smart_Trading_Cloud_Shell_Extension.htm)

#### Smart Trading Cloud Shell Extension


This section contains the following information:

---

<a id="smart-trading-cloud-shell-extension-overview"></a>

#### Smart Trading Cloud Shell Extension Overview

> 📄 Source: [Smart_Trading_Cloud_Shell_Extension_Overview.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Smart_Trading_Cloud_Shell_Extension_Overview.htm)

##### Smart Trading Cloud Shell Extension Overview


The Smart Trading Cloud Shell Extension broadens the capabilities of the Windows operating system and provides you with direct access to the Smart Trading Cloud Repository, a solution that allows you to store files in the cloud reliably and securely. For more information on the Smart Trading Cloud Repository, visit [Smart Trading Cloud Help Center](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/SmartTradingCloud/Content/Home.htm).


Note If you have enabled the multi-factor authentication or SAML Federation for your Smart Trading Account, the Shell Extension cannot connect to Smart Trading Cloud using these authentication mechanisms at the moment. The support for the new authentication modes will be added within upcoming hotfixes.


Content of the Smart Trading Cloud Repository is available as the Smart Trading Cloud folder in the File Explorer navigation pane. You can view a tree-structured hierarchy of the repository and work with the files and folders stored in the repository the way you do it with any other local file in Windows: you can open, create, move, rename, and delete them. You can also access the repository using Windows Explorer address bar. For that, in the address bar, enter repository:/files.


With the shell extension, you can view the version of a file and the name of the person who created or modified the file. You can also roll a file back to any previous version.


A shell extension also allows you to save/download documents to/from the Smart Trading Cloud Repository using other software, such as Microsoft Word, Excel, PDF viewers and editors, text editors (such as Notepad), EDI and non-EDI third-party software. When you work with files stored in the Cloud Repository and want to save changes you make using the third-party software, make sure you have full access to the required directory. You cannot save your modified files to the read-only location. In this case, in the notification area, a pending upload notification appears and has to be resolved using the following options:

- Save a Copy: Click to save the document to another location.
- Discard: Click to remove the document from the pending upload notifications list and cancel the upload.
- Retry All to Upload: Click to upload all pending documents again.

---

<a id="install-smart-trading-cloud-shell-extension"></a>

#### Install Smart Trading Cloud Shell Extension

> 📄 Source: [Install_Smart_Trading_Cloud_Shell_Extension.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Install_Smart_Trading_Cloud_Shell_Extension.htm)

##### Install Smart Trading Cloud Shell Extension


The standalone Smart Trading Cloud Shell Extension is designed as an add-on component for SpecBuilder 8.8 or earlier, as well as for the systems not having SpecBuilder installed.


Starting from SpecBuilder 8.8.1, the Smart Trading Cloud Shell Extension is included to the SpecBuilder installation package, so the standalone Shell Extension cannot be installed.


To install Smart Trading Cloud Shell Extension, complete the following steps:


| To | Do this |
| --- | --- |
| Download SpecBuilder | To get the download instructions, contact our support team. Copy ShellExtension94.exe to your workstation. |
| Install SpecBuilder | Double-click the SpecBuilder installer. The Setup Wizard appears. Click Next. Review and accept the license agreement, and then click Next. Click Change to specify the folder where you want to install Edifecs SpecBuilder, or click Next to accept the default location. Click Install. Click Register for Smart Trading Repository Account to create an account on Smart Trading Cloud Repository -or- Click Next to register later. When the installation is complete, click Finish. |



After the installation, the Smart Trading Cloud directory appears in your Windows File Explorer.

---

<a id="work-with-context-menu-options"></a>

#### Work with Context Menu Options

> 📄 Source: [Work_with_Context_Menu_Options.htm](https://help.edifecsfedcloud.com/svc/product/specbuilder/9.4/Content/Work_with_Context_Menu_Options.htm)

##### Work with Context Menu Options


Use the context menu of the files located in the Smart Trading Cloud folder to perform the following options:


| Operation | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Select | Allows you to select a file. |
| Open | Allows you to open a selected folder or a file. |
| Rollback | Allows you to restore a file to a previous version. |
| Show in browser | Allows you to view a selected file in the repository using a browser. |
| Copy as path | Allows you to copy a link that you can use in your run-time environment. You can use this link when you work with files that have been shared with you. |
| Cut | Allows you to cut and paste a file to another location. |
| Copy | Allows you to copy and paste a file to another location. |
| Delete | Allows you to delete a file from the repository. |
| Rename | Allows you to rename a file or a folder. |

---
